Project {
  database_type: 'Databricks Unity Catalog'
  Note: 'Lakehouse data model for Manufacturing - Version 1'
}

Table production.production_work_order {
  production_work_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production work order. Primary key for this entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for Cost of Goods Sold reporting; each work order must be allocated to a cost center for accurate product costing.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account for make-to-order production, if applicable.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: The primary PLC/device executing the work order is tracked for traceability and maintenance scheduling.']
  eco_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: Work orders must be traceable to the engineering change order that triggered production for compliance and cost impact analysis.']
  engineering_bom_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific BOM revision used for this production run, defining the component structure and quantities.']
  engineering_revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.revision. Business justification: Captures the specific component revision manufactured, enabling quality traceability and regulatory reporting.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Required for OEE and safety reporting to capture the physical location where each production work order is executed.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finished or semi-finished good being produced in this work order.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Order‑to‑Production report requires linking each work order to the originating sales opportunity for fulfillment tracking.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Order‑driven work‑order execution requires linking each work order to its sales order for scheduling and cost allocation.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Work orders that involve regulated activities must reference the required permit to ensure legal compliance.']
  planned_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.planned_order. Business justification: MRP creates planned orders then converts them to production work orders; linking enables traceability in the Production Order Execution Report.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_line. Business justification: A work order runs on a specific production line; the FK replaces the free‑text line identifier.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: PROJECT COST ALLOCATION: Work orders must be tied to the capital project for cost tracking and reporting in the Project Cost Summary report.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Required for traceability of external component procurement to a work order, used in scheduling and cost reporting.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.recipe. Business justification: Each work order follows a defined recipe; production planning and quality assurance reports need the recipe reference.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Regulatory compliance tracking per work order requires linking each work order to its primary regulatory requirement for audit reports.']
  sales_order_intake_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: Production‑to‑Sales reconciliation needs a direct FK from work order to the sales order intake that generated it, replacing the denormalized sales_order_number.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: A work order is scheduled for a particular shift; the FK provides a proper relational link.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Outbound Shipping Process: each completed work order generates a shipment record for finished goods; linking enables shipment creation and tracking directly from the work order.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Work orders must be linked to the SKU produced for inventory, costing, and sales order fulfillment reporting.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific storage location where finished goods from this work order will be received.']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Work order supervision requires assigning a supervisor employee for approval and oversight, used in Work Order Approval Report.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Work Order Supplier Performance Report requires linking each work order to its primary supplier for quality and delivery metrics.']
  version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.version. Business justification: A production work order is executed using a specific production version, line, and shift. Adding these FKs normalizes the model and removes the redundant string fields.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or plant location where production is executed.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual total cost incurred for this work order, including material, labor, and overhead.']
  actual_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when production execution was completed on the shop floor.']
  actual_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of finished goods produced and confirmed, in base unit of measure.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when production execution began on the shop floor.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number assigned to the output of this work order for traceability and quality control.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this work order was formally closed and all confirmations finalized.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of work order completion based on actual quantity versus planned quantity (0.00 to 100.00).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this work order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this work order.']
  cycle_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual cycle time in minutes to produce one unit, used for OEE calculation and capacity planning.']
  downtime_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total unplanned downtime in minutes during work order execution, impacting OEE availability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this work order record was last modified.']
  oee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated OEE percentage for this work order, combining availability, performance, and quality metrics (0.00 to 100.00).']
  planned_finish_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when production is planned to be completed for this work order.']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Target quantity of finished goods to be produced by this work order, in base unit of measure.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when production is planned to begin for this work order.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Scheduling priority assigned to this work order, determining its sequence in the production queue.']
  production_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special instructions, issues, or observations during work order execution.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the work order was released to the shop floor for execution.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the production routing that defines the sequence of operations for this work order.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material scrapped or rejected during production, in base unit of measure.']
  scrap_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of material scrapped or rejected during production (0.00 to 100.00).']
  setup_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual time in minutes required to set up equipment and tooling before production begins.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned standard cost per unit for this production run, used for variance analysis.']
  takt_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target takt time in minutes per unit, representing the rate at which products must be completed to meet customer demand.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Base unit of measure for quantities (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter).']
  wip_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current financial value of work in progress for this order, including material, labor, and overhead costs.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the work order, typically generated by ERP or MES systems.']
  work_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the work order in the manufacturing execution workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|released|in_progress|completed|closed|cancelled|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  work_order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the work order by production purpose: standard production, rework, prototype, maintenance, or sample run.']
  yield_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of good units produced versus total units started, used for OEE quality calculation (0.00 to 100.00).']

  Note: 'Core manufacturing work order representing a discrete production job issued to the shop floor. Authorizes manufacture of a specific quantity of finished or semi-finished goods by linking a routing, BOM revision, and production version. Tracks planned vs. actual dates, priority, order status, WIP value, and completion percentage. The SSOT for all production execution activity within this domain. Sourced from ERP production orders (e.g., SAP PP) and MES work order management (e.g., Siemens Opcenter).'
}

Table production.order_confirmation {
  order_confirmation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for order_confirmation']
  asset_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent work order against which this confirmation is reported. Links to the production order master record in ERP (SAP PP) or MES (Siemens Opcenter).']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Order confirmations are posted to cost accounting; linking to cost_center enables proper cost allocation per confirmation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shop floor operator or production worker who performed and confirmed this operation. Used for labor tracking, skill-based routing, and performance analysis.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or product being produced in this operation. Links to the material master record for inventory posting and cost calculation.']
  reversed_confirmation_order_confirmation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original confirmation record that this reversal confirmation is canceling. Null if this is not a reversal transaction.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production shift during which this confirmation was recorded. Links to shift calendar for shift-level output reporting and labor cost allocation.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Order confirmations must be tied to the SKU to report actual yield, scrap, and cost per product.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center (production resource) where this operation was performed. Links to the work center master data defining capacity, cost center, and resource type.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'The cost accounting activity type for this confirmation (e.g., machine setup, machine run, manual labor, quality inspection). Used for activity-based costing and rate calculation.']
  actual_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual cost amount incurred for this confirmation based on actual quantities and actual rates. Used for variance analysis and profitability reporting.']
  actual_labor_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total labor hours consumed for this operation confirmation. Used for labor cost calculation and efficiency analysis against standard or planned hours.']
  actual_machine_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total machine runtime hours consumed for this operation confirmation. Used for machine cost allocation and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The production batch or lot number assigned to the output of this confirmation. Used for traceability, quality control, and recall management in batch-managed materials.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the confirmation transaction. Externally visible confirmation document number generated by ERP (SAP CO11N/CO15) or MES system.']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the confirmation record indicating whether it is in draft, submitted for approval, posted to inventory and cost accounting, reversed due to error, or cancelled.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the production activity was confirmed on the shop floor. Represents the actual business event time of goods receipt or operation completion.']
  confirmation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the confirmation transaction indicating whether it represents a final completion, partial progress, automatic backflush, milestone achievement, rework activity, or scrap reporting.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or system account that created this confirmation record. Used for audit trail and accountability in production reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this confirmation record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this confirmation record. Typically the plant or company code currency.']
  final_confirmation_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator specifying whether this confirmation represents the final completion of the operation. When true, the operation is closed for further confirmations and the work order may proceed to the next operation or completion.']
  goods_movement_type varchar [note: 'The inventory movement type code triggered by this confirmation (e.g., 101 for goods receipt, 261 for goods issue, 531 for scrap). Aligns with ERP goods movement transaction codes.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'The quality inspection lot number created for the output of this confirmation. Links to the quality management inspection lot record for usage decision and certificate of conformance.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or system account that last modified this confirmation record. Used for audit trail and change accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this confirmation record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'The material document number generated in ERP inventory management when this confirmation posted goods movements. Used for reconciliation between production and inventory systems.']
  mes_transaction_code varchar [note: 'The unique transaction identifier from the source MES system (e.g., Siemens Opcenter) that originated this confirmation. Used for system integration reconciliation and traceability.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'The specific operation sequence number within the work order routing for which this confirmation is recorded. Identifies the production step (e.g., 0010, 0020, 0030) in the manufacturing process.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The manufacturing plant or production facility code where this confirmation was recorded. Used for multi-site reporting and cost center assignment.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the confirmation was posted to inventory and financial ledgers. Used for period-based cost settlement and variance analysis.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator specifying whether the output from this confirmation requires quality inspection before it can be released to inventory or the next operation. Drives inspection lot creation in QM module.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this confirmation is a reversal of a previously posted confirmation. Used to correct errors and maintain audit trail of production reporting changes.']
  rework_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of output that requires rework or reprocessing due to quality issues. Tracked separately from scrap to measure process capability and rework costs.']
  scada_event_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the SCADA system event or data collection record that triggered or validated this confirmation. Used for automated confirmation workflows and real-time monitoring integration.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of defective or non-conforming output that was scrapped during this operation. Used for scrap rate calculation and variance analysis.']
  serial_numbers varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of serial numbers for serialized output units produced in this confirmation. Used for unit-level traceability in high-value or regulated products.']
  setup_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The time spent on machine setup, changeover, and preparation activities before production started. Tracked separately from run time for SMED (Single-Minute Exchange of Die) analysis.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the originating system for this confirmation record (ERP for SAP PP, MES for Siemens Opcenter, SCADA for Aveva, MANUAL for manual entry). Used for data lineage and integration monitoring.']
  standard_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The standard cost amount for this confirmation based on planned quantities and standard rates. Used as the baseline for variance calculation in cost accounting.']
  teardown_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The time spent on machine teardown, cleanup, and post-production activities after the operation completed. Used for total cycle time calculation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for all quantity fields in this confirmation (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, L for liters, M for meters). Must align with the material master UOM.']
  variance_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments explaining variances, quality issues, or other notable events during the operation. Provides context for exception analysis and CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) investigations.']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for any variance between planned and actual quantities or times. Used for root cause analysis and continuous improvement initiatives.']
  yield_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of good output produced and confirmed for this operation. Represents conforming units that passed quality inspection and are available for subsequent operations or finished goods inventory.']

  Note: 'Shop floor confirmation record capturing actual production output reported against a work order operation. Records confirmed yield quantity, scrap quantity, rework quantity, actual labor hours, machine hours, and operator ID. Represents the operation-level goods receipt event and drives actual vs. planned variance analysis for cost settlement and performance monitoring. Sourced from ERP order confirmations (e.g., SAP CO11N/CO15) and MES activity reporting (e.g., Siemens Opcenter).'
}

Table production.production_schedule {
  production_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production schedule record. Primary key.']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bill of materials used for this scheduled production. Defines the material components and structure.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Associates production schedule with the plant location to enable plant‑level capacity planning and compliance reporting.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or product being scheduled for production.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer order driving this production schedule, if make-to-order. Null for make-to-stock schedules.']
  primary_production_employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID of the production planner responsible for creating and maintaining this schedule. Used for accountability and workload tracking.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_line. Business justification: A production schedule is defined for a particular line; the FK replaces the free‑text line code.']
  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order generated from this schedule. Links planning to execution.']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production routing or process plan used for this schedule. Defines the sequence of operations and work centers.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Master production schedules are created per SKU; linking enables MPS reporting and demand planning.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center or production line assigned to execute this schedule.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule requires management approval before release. True for high-value, high-risk, or exception schedules; false for routine schedules.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this schedule was cancelled. Null if the schedule was not cancelled.']
  capacity_requirement_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total machine or labor hours required to complete this schedule. Used for capacity planning and load leveling.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when production for this schedule was completed. Used for schedule performance analysis and cycle time measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and schedule age analysis.']
  firmed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule is firmed (locked) and should not be automatically rescheduled by planning systems. True means the schedule is manually controlled; false means it can be adjusted by MRP/APS.']
  freeze_horizon_date date [note: 'Date beyond which this schedule is frozen and cannot be changed without formal approval. Protects near-term execution from planning volatility.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this schedule record was last updated. Tracks schedule volatility and planning changes.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Total lead time from schedule release to completion, including queue time, setup, run, and move time. Used for order promising and planning.']
  lot_size_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Standard lot or batch size for this production schedule. May be driven by economic order quantity, equipment constraints, or process requirements.']
  lot_sizing_rule varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to determine production lot sizes. Fixed lot uses a constant quantity; lot-for-lot matches demand exactly; EOQ optimizes ordering costs; POQ aggregates demand over periods.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'Code identifying the MRP controller or planner responsible for this schedule. Used for accountability and workload distribution.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments from planners regarding special instructions, constraints, or considerations for this schedule.']
  pegging_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the demand source (sales order, forecast, safety stock) that this schedule is pegged to. Enables traceability from supply to demand.']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Target quantity of the material to be produced according to this schedule. Expressed in the base unit of measure for the material.']
  planning_bucket varchar [note: 'Time granularity of the schedule planning horizon. Daily for short-term detailed scheduling, weekly for medium-term planning, monthly for long-term capacity planning.']
  planning_horizon_weeks varchar [note: 'Number of weeks into the future that this schedule covers. Defines the forward visibility window for production planning.']
  planning_strategy varchar [note: 'Manufacturing strategy governing how this schedule is planned and executed. Make-to-stock builds for inventory; make-to-order builds against customer orders; assemble-to-order configures from standard components; engineer-to-order designs and builds custom products.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Relative priority of this schedule compared to other schedules. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for resource allocation and sequencing decisions.']
  released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this schedule was released for execution. Marks the transition from planning to active production.']
  run_time_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated time required to produce the planned quantity, excluding setup and teardown. Used for cycle time analysis.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Buffer stock quantity maintained to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. Influences schedule timing and quantities.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the production schedule. Externally visible schedule reference number used in planning and execution systems.']
  schedule_source varchar [note: 'System or process that generated this schedule. MRP run for material requirements planning; APS optimization for advanced planning and scheduling; manual planning for planner-created schedules; demand forecast for forecast-driven schedules; customer order for order-driven schedules.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production schedule. Draft schedules are under planning; released schedules are active; frozen schedules are locked for execution; revised schedules have been updated; cancelled schedules are voided; completed schedules are finished.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the schedule by planning level. MPS for top-level finished goods; FAS for final assembly operations; RCCP for high-level capacity validation; MRP for detailed component planning.']
  scheduled_finish_date date [note: 'Planned date when production for this schedule is expected to be completed. Used for order promising and delivery planning.']
  scheduled_finish_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when production execution is scheduled to finish, used for detailed capacity loading and sequencing.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned date when production for this schedule is expected to begin. Key input for capacity planning and material availability checks.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when production execution is scheduled to start, including shift and time-of-day information for detailed shop floor scheduling.']
  setup_time_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated time required to set up equipment and tooling before production can begin. Part of total lead time calculation.']
  shift_assignment varchar [note: 'Shift during which this production schedule is assigned to run. Used for labor planning and shift-level capacity allocation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the planned quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, L for liters, M for meters).']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the schedule. Incremented each time the schedule is revised or replanned.']

  Note: 'Master production schedule (MPS) record defining planned production quantities, start/finish dates, and shift assignments for a given production item across a planning horizon. Tracks schedule version, freeze horizon, planning bucket (daily/weekly), and schedule status (draft, released, frozen, revised). Represents the output of APS/MRP II scheduling runs. Sourced from ERP production planning (e.g., SAP PP) and APS systems (e.g., Microsoft Dynamics 365 Supply Chain).'
}

Table production.work_center {
  work_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the work center. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center for financial tracking and allocation of work center expenses.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or supervisor responsible for managing this work center.']
  maintenance_strategy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preventive maintenance strategy applied to equipment at this work center.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where this work center is located.']
  work_center_group_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_center_group. Business justification: A work center belongs to a work center group; adding work_center_group_id creates the parent relationship and eliminates the need for ad‑hoc grouping logic.']
  available_capacity_per_shift decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard available capacity of the work center per shift, measured in the capacity category unit.']
  capacity_category varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for capacity planning (e.g., machine hours, labor hours, throughput units).']
  capacity_planning_group varchar [note: 'Grouping code for aggregating work centers in capacity planning and leveling activities.']
  control_key varchar [note: 'Control key defining how operations are processed at this work center (e.g., internal processing, external processing, inspection).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this work center record was first created in the system.']
  efficiency_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard efficiency rate of the work center expressed as a percentage, representing the ratio of actual output to theoretical maximum output.']
  formula_key varchar [note: 'Formula key used for calculating operation times and capacity requirements at this work center.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this work center record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this work center record was last modified.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Physical location description of the work center within the plant (e.g., Building 2, Floor 3, Bay 5).']
  mes_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work center is integrated with the MES for real-time shop floor control and data collection.']
  number_of_machines varchar [note: 'Count of individual machines or equipment units within this work center.']
  number_of_operators varchar [note: 'Standard number of operators or workers assigned to this work center during normal operation.']
  oee_baseline_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OEE baseline for this work center, calculated as Availability × Performance × Quality. Used for capacity planning and performance benchmarking.']
  plc_address varchar [note: 'Network address or identifier of the PLC controlling this work center, used for SCADA and MES integration.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether operations at this work center require mandatory quality inspection before proceeding to the next step.']
  scada_tag_prefix varchar [note: 'SCADA tag prefix used to identify real-time data points from this work center in the process control system.']
  scheduling_type varchar [note: 'Scheduling strategy used for operations at this work center (forward scheduling, backward scheduling, or capacity-only).']
  shift_sequence_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shift sequence or shift calendar defining operating hours for this work center.']
  standard_processing_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard processing or cycle time in minutes per unit for operations performed at this work center.']
  standard_queue_time_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard queue time in hours representing the typical wait time before an operation begins at this work center.']
  standard_setup_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard setup or changeover time in minutes required to prepare the work center for a new production run.']
  standard_teardown_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard teardown time in minutes required to complete and clean up after an operation at this work center.']
  utilization_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target utilization rate of the work center expressed as a percentage, representing the ratio of actual operating time to available time.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this work center configuration is effective and available for production planning.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this work center configuration is effective. Null indicates indefinite validity.']
  work_center_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the work center type indicating the nature of the production resource.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the work center used in ERP and MES systems. Externally-known unique code for capacity planning and routing assignments.']
  work_center_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the work center (e.g., Assembly Line 3, CNC Machining Cell A, Welding Station 5).']
  work_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the work center indicating its operational availability.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this work center record in the system.']

  Note: 'Master data entity representing a physical or logical production resource on the shop floor — a machine, assembly cell, production line segment, or labor group — at which manufacturing operations are performed. Captures work center code, plant, cost center linkage, capacity category, available capacity per shift, efficiency rate, utilization rate, and OEE baseline target. The SSOT for capacity planning and routing assignments within the production domain. Sourced from ERP work center master (e.g., SAP CR01).'
}

Table production.routing {
  routing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the manufacturing routing. Primary key.']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Bill of Materials (BOM) that defines the material components consumed by this routing. Links routing to BOM for integrated production planning.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or finished good that this routing produces. Links to the material master in inventory domain.']
  planner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Routing planning is owned by a planner employee, needed for Routing Change Management report.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility where this routing is executed. Links to plant master data.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'FK to production.production_line']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this routing was approved for production use.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the routing. Pending routings await review; approved routings are authorized for production; rejected routings require rework; under_review routings are in the approval workflow.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved this routing for production use.']
  base_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Standard lot size or batch quantity for which the routing times and resource requirements are defined. All operation times are calculated relative to this base quantity.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the base quantity. Standard ISO unit codes such as EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), M (meter).']
  change_number varchar [note: 'Engineering Change Notice (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) number that authorized the creation or last modification of this routing. Supports traceability of process changes.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard cost amount.']
  counter varchar [note: 'Sequential counter within the routing group. Together with routing_group, forms an alternative business key.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this routing record was first created in the system.']
  is_default_routing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this routing is the default routing for the material. True if this is the primary routing; false if it is an alternative.']
  is_phantom_routing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a phantom routing that is automatically consumed without generating a separate production order. Used for subassemblies that are immediately incorporated into parent assemblies.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this routing record was last modified.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which this routing was used in a production order or work order. Used to identify obsolete or inactive routings.']
  lot_size_from decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum lot size for which this routing is applicable. Supports lot-size-dependent routing selection.']
  lot_size_to decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum lot size for which this routing is applicable. Null indicates no upper limit.']
  planner_group varchar [note: 'Production planning team or planner responsible for maintaining and scheduling this routing.']
  routing_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the routing purpose, scope, and special instructions. Provides context for production planners and shop floor operators.']
  routing_group varchar [note: 'Grouping code for related routings. Used to organize routings by product family, process type, or manufacturing cell.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the routing. Externally visible routing code used in production planning and shop floor documentation.']
  routing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the routing. Draft routings are under development; released routings are approved for use; active routings are currently in production; inactive routings are temporarily suspended; obsolete routings are retired; blocked routings are prohibited from use.']
  routing_text varchar [note: 'Extended free-form text field for detailed routing notes, special handling instructions, safety warnings, and quality requirements.']
  routing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the routing purpose. Production routings define standard manufacturing sequences; inspection routings define quality check sequences; rework routings define repair processes; universal routings apply across multiple materials; rate routings define continuous process flows; reference routings serve as templates.']
  scheduling_type varchar [note: 'Scheduling strategy for this routing. Forward scheduling starts from the earliest start date; backward scheduling works back from the required finish date; midpoint scheduling balances around a target date.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record from which this routing was sourced. SAP_PP indicates SAP S/4HANA Production Planning; TEAMCENTER indicates Siemens Teamcenter PLM; OPCENTER indicates Siemens Opcenter MES; MANUAL indicates manually entered routing.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this routing in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  standard_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard cost to execute this routing for the base quantity. Includes labor, machine, overhead, and tooling costs. Used for cost accounting and variance analysis.']
  total_labor_time_minutes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cumulative labor time in minutes across all operations for the base quantity. Labor time may scale differently than machine time.']
  total_lead_time_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cumulative lead time in hours required to complete all operations in this routing for the base quantity. Includes setup, machine, labor, and queue times.']
  total_machine_time_minutes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cumulative machine processing time in minutes across all operations for the base quantity. Machine time scales with lot size.']
  total_operation_count varchar [note: 'Total number of operations (steps) defined in this routing. Derived from routing operation line items.']
  total_setup_time_minutes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cumulative setup time in minutes across all operations for the base quantity. Setup time is independent of lot size.']
  usage varchar [note: 'Intended application context for the routing. Standard routings are the default production method; alternative routings provide backup processes; trial routings support pilot runs; prototype routings support R&D; emergency routings address contingency scenarios.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this routing has been used in production orders or work orders. Indicates routing popularity and stability.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Effective start date from which this routing is valid and can be used in production planning and execution.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Effective end date after which this routing is no longer valid. Null indicates indefinite validity.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the routing. Supports versioning of routing definitions to track engineering changes and process improvements.']

  Note: 'Manufacturing routing master defining the ordered sequence of operations required to produce a finished or semi-finished item. Captures routing number, routing type (production, inspection, universal), base quantity, status, and validity dates. Each routing is composed of individual operations (modeled as line items) linked to work centers with standard times for setup, machine run, and labor. The SSOT for standard production process definitions. Sourced from ERP routing master (e.g., SAP CA01) and PLM process plans (e.g., Siemens Teamcenter).'
}

Table production.version {
  version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production version record. Primary key.']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific BOM (Bill of Materials) that defines the component structure and material requirements for this production version.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master that this production version is defined for. Links to the finished good or semi-finished product being manufactured.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where this production version is valid and executable.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'FK to production.production_line']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the routing that defines the sequence of operations, work centers, and process steps for manufacturing this material under this production version.']
  version_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  version_production_scheduler_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this production version was formally approved for use in production by authorized personnel or quality management.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved this production version for production use.']
  bom_alternative varchar [note: 'Alternative BOM indicator when multiple BOMs exist for the same material. Allows selection of different component lists for different production scenarios.']
  bom_usage varchar [note: 'BOM usage indicator defining the purpose of the BOM (e.g., \'1\' for production, \'3\' for engineering). Determines which BOM alternative is selected.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'Engineering Change Number (ECN) or Engineering Change Order (ECO) that authorized the creation or modification of this production version. Links to change management process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this production version record was first created in the system.']
  distribution_key varchar [note: 'Key that controls how production quantities are distributed across multiple production versions when more than one version is valid for the same material and lot size.']
  is_costing_relevant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this production version is used for product costing calculations. True means this version is included in standard cost estimates.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this production version is locked and cannot be used for new production orders. True means locked, False means available for use.']
  is_mrp_relevant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this production version is considered during MRP (Material Requirements Planning) runs. True means MRP will use this version for planning.']
  issue_storage_location varchar [note: 'Default storage location from which components are issued for production orders created with this production version. 4-character alphanumeric code.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this production version record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this production version record was last modified in the system.']
  lot_size_from decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum production lot size (quantity) for which this production version is valid. Defines the lower bound of the lot size range.']
  lot_size_to decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum production lot size (quantity) for which this production version is valid. Defines the upper bound of the lot size range. Null indicates no upper limit.']
  lot_size_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the lot size range (e.g., \'EA\' for each, \'KG\' for kilogram, \'L\' for liter). ISO standard unit codes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this production version. May include setup requirements, quality considerations, or operational constraints.']
  production_version_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the production version within the material-plant combination. Typically 1-4 characters. Business identifier for the production version.']
  production_version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production version indicating its operational state and usability for production planning and execution.']
  receipt_storage_location varchar [note: 'Default storage location where finished goods are received after production completion for this production version. 4-character alphanumeric code.']
  task_list_type varchar [note: 'Type of task list used in this production version. N=Standard routing, R=Rate routing, Q=Inspection plan. Determines the structure of operations.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date from which this production version becomes effective and can be used for production planning and execution.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'End date until which this production version remains effective. Null indicates indefinite validity.']
  version_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the production version explaining its purpose, characteristics, or usage scenario (e.g., \'Standard Production\', \'High Volume Line\', \'Alternate Routing for Capacity Constraint\').']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this production version record in the system.']

  Note: 'Production version master linking a specific BOM and routing combination valid for manufacturing a material within a defined quantity range and validity period. Captures production version code, description, BOM usage, routing group, lot size range (minimum/maximum), valid-from/valid-to dates, and MRP relevance flag. Sourced from SAP PP material master production version (MRP4 view). Determines which BOM and routing are used when a work order is created.'
}

Table production.shift {
  shift_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shift definition record. Primary key.']
  calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the factory calendar that defines working days, holidays, and plant shutdowns for this shift. Used to exclude non-working days from capacity calculations.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Links shift to its site location, enabling shift‑level labor cost and safety incident aggregation per plant.']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Shift supervisor employee oversees shift operations, needed for Shift Performance and Safety reports.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific work center or production line where this shift is applicable. Nullable if the shift applies plant-wide. Used for capacity planning and machine-specific scheduling.']
  break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Sum of all scheduled break periods (lunch, rest breaks) within the shift, in minutes. Subtracted from gross duration to calculate net available time for production.']
  break_schedule varchar [note: 'Textual description of the break structure, including start times and durations (e.g., \'10:00-10:15 (15 min), 12:00-12:30 (30 min)\'). Used for communication to shop floor personnel and HMI display.']
  changeover_allowance_minutes varchar [note: 'Standard time reserved within the shift for product changeovers, tool changes, or setup activities. Used to adjust net available time for multi-product shifts and to calculate planned downtime for OEE.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shift definition record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  crew_size varchar [note: 'Standard number of operators or workers assigned to this shift pattern. Used for labor capacity planning and cost allocation. Nullable if crew size varies by work center.']
  days_of_week varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of weekday codes (MON, TUE, WED, THU, FRI, SAT, SUN) indicating which days this shift pattern applies to. Used for weekly capacity planning and workforce rostering.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this shift definition is no longer valid. Nullable for open-ended shift patterns. Used to retire obsolete shift definitions while preserving historical data.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this shift definition becomes active and can be used for production scheduling. Supports versioning of shift patterns over time.']
  end_time timestamp [note: 'Time of day when the shift ends, in HH:mm:ss format (24-hour clock). May be less than start_time for shifts that cross midnight. Used to calculate gross shift duration.']
  gross_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time of the shift from start to end, in minutes, including all breaks and non-productive time. Used as the denominator for availability calculations.']
  handover_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Planned overlap time between consecutive shifts for handover communication, equipment status review, and safety briefing. Subtracted from net available time if included in shift boundaries. Nullable for non-continuous operations.']
  is_continuous_operation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift is part of a 24/7 continuous manufacturing operation (True) or a discrete batch operation (False). Used to determine handover requirements and WIP tracking rules.']
  labor_rate_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base labor rates for this shift type (e.g., 1.0 for regular, 1.5 for overtime, 2.0 for holiday). Used for labor cost calculation and variance analysis.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this shift definition record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shift definition record was last updated. Used for audit trail and to track the currency of master data.']
  net_available_minutes varchar [note: 'Net productive time available for manufacturing execution, calculated as gross_duration_minutes minus break_duration_minutes. Used as the planned production time for OEE availability calculation and takt time planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information about the shift pattern, special instructions, or exceptions. Used for communication to planners and supervisors.']
  number_of_breaks varchar [note: 'Count of distinct break periods scheduled within the shift (e.g., 2 for a shift with one lunch break and one rest break). Used for workforce scheduling and labor compliance reporting.']
  planned_output_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Target production volume for this shift, in base unit of measure. Used as the denominator for performance OEE calculation and as input to takt time calculation. Nullable for non-production shifts.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for shift selection when multiple shift definitions are valid for the same time period (lower number = higher priority). Used by APS systems to resolve scheduling conflicts.']
  rotation_pattern varchar [note: 'Description of the multi-day or multi-week rotation cycle this shift belongs to (e.g., \'2-2-3 Continental\', \'4-on-4-off\', \'DuPont 12-hour\'). Used for long-term workforce scheduling and fatigue management.']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this shift within a daily rotation (e.g., 1 for first shift, 2 for second shift, 3 for third shift). Used for shift handover reporting and continuous production tracking.']
  shift_category varchar [note: 'Broad categorization of shift purpose. Production shifts are used for manufacturing execution and OEE calculation; non-production shifts (e.g., maintenance, training) are excluded from throughput metrics; mixed shifts include both activities.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the shift pattern (e.g., DAY_1, NIGHT_A, SWING_2). Used as the externally-known reference in scheduling systems and MES.']
  shift_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the shift (e.g., Day Shift, Night Shift, Morning Shift). Used for display in reports and HMI screens.']
  shift_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the shift definition. Active shifts are available for scheduling; inactive shifts are temporarily disabled; draft shifts are under review; archived shifts are retained for historical reference only.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift pattern indicating the nature of the working period. Regular shifts are standard production periods; overtime, weekend, and holiday shifts may carry premium labor rates; maintenance and emergency shifts are non-production periods.']
  start_time timestamp [note: 'Time of day when the shift begins, in HH:mm:ss format (24-hour clock). Used to calculate shift boundaries for production tracking and OEE reporting.']
  takt_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target cycle time per unit to meet customer demand, calculated as net_available_minutes * 60 / planned_output_quantity. Used for production pacing and line balancing. Nullable if not applicable to this shift type.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the plant location where this shift operates (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/Berlin, Asia/Shanghai). Used to convert shift boundaries to UTC for global reporting and to handle daylight saving time transitions.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for planned_output_quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, M for meters). Must align with material master UOM for the products manufactured during this shift.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this shift definition record. Used for audit trail and data governance.']

  Note: 'Shift definition master representing a named working period pattern for a plant or work center. Defines shift code, start/end times, break structure, net available minutes, and shift type (regular, overtime, weekend). Used as the time-boundary reference for capacity planning, OEE shift-level reporting, takt time calculation, and workforce scheduling integration.'
}

Table production.shift_report {
  shift_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shift report record. Primary key.']
  asset_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production work order executed during this shift.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisor or shift leader responsible for this production shift.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Allows shift reports to be tied to the plant location for accurate reporting of downtime and quality metrics per site.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shift definition (e.g., Day Shift, Night Shift, Swing Shift).']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product or material that was produced during this shift.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center or production line where this shift occurred.']
  actual_good_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of conforming units produced during the shift that passed quality inspection.']
  actual_production_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual time the work center was actively producing during the shift, excluding downtime.']
  availability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of planned production time that the equipment was available and operating, calculated as (Planned Time - Downtime) / Planned Time × 100.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Production batch or lot number assigned to output from this shift for traceability.']
  changeover_count varchar [note: 'Number of production changeovers or setups performed during the shift.']
  changeover_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total time spent on changeovers and setups during the shift.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments from the shift supervisor documenting notable events, issues, or observations during the shift.']
  cycle_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time in seconds to complete one production cycle or unit during the shift.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the primary source system from which shift data was captured (MES, SCADA, ERP, or manual entry).']
  downtime_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total unplanned downtime during the shift due to equipment failures, material shortages, or other interruptions.']
  energy_consumption_kwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total electrical energy consumed by the work center during the shift, measured in kilowatt-hours.']
  escalation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shift report requires management escalation due to performance issues, quality problems, or safety concerns.']
  material_consumed_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of raw materials or components consumed during the shift.']
  material_waste_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material wasted or lost during production beyond normal scrap allowances.']
  oee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall Equipment Effectiveness calculated as Availability × Performance × Quality, expressed as a percentage.']
  operator_count varchar [note: 'Number of operators assigned to the work center during this shift.']
  performance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of maximum possible output achieved during operating time, calculated as (Actual Output / Theoretical Maximum Output) × 100.']
  planned_production_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total planned available production time for the shift, excluding scheduled breaks and maintenance windows.']
  planned_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Target production quantity planned for this shift based on production schedule and capacity.']
  quality_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether production output from this shift is on quality hold pending inspection or investigation.']
  quality_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of good units produced out of total units, calculated as (Good Quantity / Total Produced Quantity) × 100.']
  report_closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shift report was officially closed and finalized in the MES system.']
  report_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shift report record was first created in the system.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the shift report, typically formatted as YYYYMMDD-SHIFT-WORKCENTER.']
  rework_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of units requiring rework or reprocessing to meet quality standards.']
  safety_incident_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a safety incident occurred during this shift requiring documentation and follow-up.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of units scrapped during the shift due to defects or non-conformance.']
  scrap_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total produced quantity that was scrapped, calculated as (Scrap Quantity / Total Produced Quantity) × 100.']
  shift_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the shift occurred.']
  shift_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the shift in minutes, calculated from start to end timestamp.']
  shift_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the shift ended production activities.']
  shift_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the shift started production activities.']
  takt_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target production rate in seconds per unit, calculated from customer demand and available production time.']
  throughput_rate_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average number of units produced per hour during the shift.']
  total_produced_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity produced during the shift, including good, scrap, and rework units.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for all quantity fields in this report (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M|M2|M3|TON|LB|GAL|FT — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  yield_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'First-pass yield rate representing the percentage of units that passed quality inspection without rework.']

  Note: 'Shift-level production performance report capturing actual results for a specific shift at a work center or line. Records planned vs. actual good quantity, scrap, rework, downtime minutes, OEE (availability × performance × quality), cycle time, takt time, and throughput rate. The SSOT for shift-level OEE and production KPIs. Drives daily production meetings, continuous improvement actions, and management escalation triggers. Sourced from MES shift close procedures (e.g., Siemens Opcenter) and SCADA data aggregation.'
}

Table production.wip_lot {
  wip_lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the work-in-progress lot or batch. Primary key for the WIP lot tracking entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: WIP lot valuation requires assignment to a cost center for inventory costing and financial statements.']
  current_work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center or production resource where this lot is currently located and being processed.']
  eco_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: Lots produced under a specific engineering change order need linkage for compliance and cost reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production operator or technician currently responsible for processing this lot at the current work center.']
  engineering_revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.revision. Business justification: Lot tracking must record the component revision to ensure traceability for quality investigations.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Links WIP lot to its plant location for lot traceability and site‑specific quality control.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record representing the product or component being manufactured in this lot.']
  material_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_requirement. Business justification: WIP lots originate from material requirements; linking enables requirement fulfillment analysis and variance reporting in the WIP Requirement Traceability report.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Lot traceability to the sales order is required for compliance and customer‑specific quality tracking.']
  parent_lot_wip_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent WIP lot if this lot was split or derived from another lot. Enables genealogy and traceability tracking.']
  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: A WIP lot is generated from a specific production work order; linking removes the ambiguous order number field.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: WIP TRACKING PER PROJECT: Associates each WIP lot with its project for progress reporting and earned value calculations.']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production routing defining the sequence of operations this lot must traverse.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: WIP lot tracking requires the SKU identifier for traceability, quality inspection, and regulatory compliance.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: WIP lot tracking includes source supplier to support recall management and supplier quality analysis.']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the lot actually completed all operations and was confirmed as finished. Null if lot is still in process.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for process industries where multiple lots may be grouped into a single batch for traceability or quality purposes.']
  current_operation_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number of the operation currently being performed on this lot within the routing. Used to track progress through the production process.']
  current_operation_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the lot arrived at and began processing at the current operation. Used for operation cycle time tracking.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Shelf life expiration date for this lot, applicable for materials with limited shelf life such as chemicals, adhesives, or perishable components.']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason why this lot is on hold, if applicable. Examples include quality issue, material shortage, engineering change, equipment failure.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'Quality inspection lot number assigned by the QM system if this WIP lot is subject to inspection. Links to quality inspection records.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this WIP lot record was last updated in the system. Used for audit trail and data synchronization.']
  lot_creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this WIP lot record was created in the MES system, marking the start of lot tracking.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the lot or batch. Human-readable unique code used for tracking and traceability across the shop floor and in MES systems.']
  lot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the WIP lot. Indicates whether the lot is released for production, actively in process, on hold, completed, scrapped, or cancelled.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this WIP lot that do not fit structured fields.']
  original_lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot number of the original lot if this is a rework or split lot. Maintains traceability to the source lot for genealogy purposes.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Production priority level assigned to this lot. Determines scheduling precedence and resource allocation on the shop floor.']
  production_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when physical production of this lot began at the first operation. Used for lead time and cycle time calculation.']
  project_number varchar [note: 'Project identifier if this lot is being produced for a specific engineering or customer project. Used for project-based manufacturing tracking.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot requires quality inspection before proceeding to the next operation or before final goods receipt.']
  quantity_completed decimal(15,3) [note: 'Cumulative quantity of material that has successfully completed all operations in the routing and is ready for goods receipt.']
  quantity_in_process decimal(15,3) [note: 'Current quantity of material actively being processed at the current operation. Represents material that has not yet completed the current step.']
  quantity_on_hold decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material in this lot that is currently on hold pending quality inspection, engineering review, or other disposition decision.']
  quantity_ordered decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of material originally planned to be produced in this lot as specified in the production order.']
  quantity_scrapped decimal(15,3) [note: 'Cumulative quantity of material that has been rejected or scrapped due to quality defects or process failures during production.']
  rework_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot is a rework lot created to reprocess previously rejected or non-conforming material.']
  scheduled_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which this lot is scheduled to complete all operations and be available for goods receipt. Used for capacity planning and delivery commitment.']
  scrap_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the primary reason for any scrapped quantity in this lot. Used for root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  serial_number_profile varchar [note: 'Serial number profile code indicating whether and how individual units within this lot are serialized for traceability.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production shift during which this lot is currently being processed. Used for shift-level performance reporting.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'Code indicating if this lot represents special stock such as consignment, project stock, or customer-owned material.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Storage location or staging area code where this WIP lot is currently physically located within the plant.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for all quantity fields in this lot record (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, M for meters).']

  Note: 'Work-in-progress (WIP) lot or batch tracking entity representing a discrete quantity of material currently being processed through the production routing. Captures lot number, material number, current operation, current work center, quantity in process, quantity completed, quantity scrapped, lot creation timestamp, and lot status (in-process, on-hold, completed, scrapped). Enables real-time WIP visibility, genealogy tracing, and shop floor material flow tracking. Sourced from MES lot tracking (e.g., Siemens Opcenter).'
}

Table production.production_downtime_event {
  production_downtime_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production downtime event record. Primary key.']
  alarm_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.alarm_definition. Business justification: Root‑cause analysis links downtime events to the specific alarm definition that triggered them, required for OEE loss reporting.']
  asset_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance work order created to address the downtime event. Null for non-maintenance related downtime.']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: Downtime root‑cause analysis often references an engineering change notice; linking enables systematic CAPA tracking.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific equipment or machine that experienced downtime. Links to equipment register.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Captures the exact plant location of each downtime event, required for safety incident analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  primary_production_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the maintenance technician or operator assigned to resolve the downtime event. Links to employee master data.']
  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production order that was interrupted by this downtime event. May be null for unplanned stoppages outside scheduled production.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: DOWNTIME COST ATTRIBUTION: Downtime events are allocated to the owning project for KPI dashboards and project profitability analysis.']
  safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.safety_incident. Business justification: Downtime root‑cause analysis links downtime events to recorded safety incidents for safety performance reporting.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production shift during which the downtime event occurred. Links to shift schedule master data.']
  tertiary_production_recorded_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator or technician who initially recorded this downtime event. Links to employee master data.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or production line where the downtime occurred. Links to the work center master data.']
  alarm_code varchar [note: 'SCADA or DCS alarm code that triggered the downtime event. Used to correlate downtime with specific equipment faults.']
  alarm_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the SCADA alarm that initiated the downtime event.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the downtime event record. Pending indicates awaiting supervisor review, approved indicates validated, rejected indicates disputed or incorrect entry.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the downtime event record was approved by a supervisor or manager.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free-text comments or notes about the downtime event from operators, technicians, or supervisors.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for production loss value (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  downtime_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the downtime event: breakdown (unplanned equipment failure), planned_maintenance (scheduled PM), changeover (product/tool change), material_shortage (feedstock unavailable), quality_hold (production stopped for quality issue), operator_absence (staffing gap).']
  downtime_event_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the downtime event, typically generated by MES or SCADA system.']
  downtime_reason varchar [note: 'Operator or supervisor entered reason for the downtime event. Free-text field capturing immediate observed cause.']
  downtime_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the downtime was planned (scheduled maintenance, changeover) or unplanned (breakdown, material shortage, quality hold).']
  duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the downtime event in minutes. Calculated as the difference between end_timestamp and start_timestamp, or current time if still ongoing.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when production resumed after the downtime event. Null if downtime is still ongoing.']
  impact_on_oee decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated percentage impact of this downtime event on the work center or line OEE for the shift or day. Expressed as percentage points lost.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this downtime event record is currently active. False indicates the record has been logically deleted or superseded.']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this downtime event is part of a recurring pattern at this work center or equipment. True if similar events have occurred within the past 30 days.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this downtime event record was last updated. Audit trail field.']
  mttr_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mean time to repair for this type of downtime event, calculated from historical data. Used for benchmarking and performance tracking.']
  notification_sent boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether automated notifications were sent to supervisors, maintenance team, or management when this downtime event occurred.']
  preventive_action varchar [note: 'Description of preventive measures implemented to avoid recurrence of this type of downtime event.']
  production_loss_units decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated quantity of production units lost due to this downtime event. Calculated based on standard cycle time and downtime duration.']
  production_loss_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial value of production lost due to this downtime event. Calculated using standard cost per unit and production loss units.']
  recorded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this downtime event record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.']
  recurrence_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this type of downtime event has occurred at this work center or equipment within the past 30 days. Used for chronic loss identification.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action taken to resolve the downtime event and restore production.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department accountable for resolving the downtime event: production (operator-related), maintenance (equipment repair), quality (inspection/testing), materials (supply chain), engineering (design/process), operations (planning/scheduling).']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the root cause of the downtime event. Typically references a root cause taxonomy maintained in the CMMS or MES system.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the root cause analysis findings for this downtime event.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business impact severity of the downtime event: critical (full line stoppage, safety risk), high (major capacity loss), medium (partial degradation), low (minor impact).']
  shift_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the shift during which the downtime occurred. Used for daily OEE reporting and trend analysis.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that originated this downtime event record: MES (Siemens Opcenter), SCADA (Aveva), ERP (SAP PP), CMMS (Maximo), or Manual (operator entry).']
