  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this downtime event in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the downtime event began. Captured from SCADA alarm logs or MES operator entry.']

  Note: 'Downtime event capturing a planned or unplanned production stoppage at a work center or line. Records start/end timestamps, duration, downtime category (breakdown, planned maintenance, changeover, material shortage, quality hold, operator absence), root cause code, and responsible department. Critical input for OEE availability calculation, TPM analysis, and changeover time (SMED) tracking. Sourced from SCADA alarm logs and MES downtime tracking (e.g., Siemens Opcenter).'
}

Table production.production_goods_receipt {
  production_goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production goods receipt event. Primary key for this entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center responsible for the production activity. Used for cost allocation and variance analysis.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production operator or technician who confirmed the goods receipt on the shop floor. Used for accountability and performance tracking.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Connects goods receipt to the receiving plant location, supporting traceability and inventory valuation per site.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lot or batch number assigned to the received goods for traceability and quality control purposes.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for the finished or semi-finished good that was received. Links to the material being produced.']
  mrp_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.mrp_run. Business justification: Goods receipts are posted as a result of an MRP run; linking enables receipt verification against the originating run in the Goods Receipt Reconciliation report.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_line. Business justification: Replace string line code with a proper foreign key to the production line entity for referential integrity.']
  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: Link goods receipt to the work order it finalizes, enabling traceability from receipt to production work order.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: PROJECT INVENTORY RECEIPT: Goods receipts are linked to the project to reconcile received materials against project budgets.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.shift. Business justification: Replace string shift identifier with a foreign key to the shift entity, allowing accurate shift‑level reporting.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Goods receipt records need the SKU to validate received quantity against the product master and enable downstream inventory updates.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the storage location within the plant where the received goods were posted to inventory. Links to warehouse storage location.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Goods receipt must record the supplying vendor to enable invoice matching and supplier performance tracking.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number assigned to the received goods for traceability. May be system-generated or manually assigned based on batch management configuration.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'The production confirmation number from the MES or ERP system that triggered this goods receipt. Links shop floor execution to inventory posting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The system timestamp when this goods receipt record was first created in the data warehouse. Used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Reference number from the physical delivery documentation or packing slip accompanying the goods. Used for reconciliation with physical documents.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date on which the goods receipt document was created or initiated. May differ from posting date in cases of backdated transactions.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the goods receipt was posted. Used for monthly financial closing and reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the goods receipt was posted. Used for period-based financial reporting and inventory valuation.']
  gr_document_number varchar [note: 'The unique document number generated by the ERP system for this goods receipt transaction. This is the business identifier used for tracking and audit purposes.']
  gr_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the goods receipt document. Indicates whether the receipt has been successfully posted, reversed, cancelled, or is awaiting quality inspection.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'The quality inspection lot number created for this goods receipt if quality inspection is required. Links to quality management inspection results.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The system timestamp when this goods receipt record was last updated in the data warehouse. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP movement type code that classifies the type of inventory transaction. For production goods receipts, typically 101 (goods receipt to stock) or 131 (goods receipt from production order).']
  note varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by the operator or warehouse personnel regarding the goods receipt. Captures exceptions, observations, or special handling instructions.']
  order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The total quantity originally planned to be produced on the work order. Used to calculate yield and completion percentage.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the goods receipt was posted to inventory in the ERP system. This is the accounting-effective date for inventory valuation.']
  posting_user varchar [note: 'The system user ID who posted the goods receipt transaction in the ERP system. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  production_version varchar [note: 'Identifies the specific production version (combination of BOM and routing) used to manufacture the goods. Used for process traceability and costing.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received goods must undergo quality inspection before being released to unrestricted stock. Drives QM workflow.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the goods were physically received and confirmed on the shop floor or warehouse. Captures the real-world event time for production completion.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of finished or semi-finished goods received and posted to inventory. Measured in the base unit of measure for the material.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'The material reservation number that was created for the work order and fulfilled by this goods receipt. Links production output to material planning.']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number of the reversal transaction if this goods receipt was reversed. Provides audit trail for corrections.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this goods receipt has been reversed or cancelled. Used to filter active vs. reversed transactions in reporting.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for reversing the goods receipt (e.g., quantity error, wrong material, quality issue). Used for root cause analysis.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material scrapped or rejected during the production run that resulted in this goods receipt. Used for yield calculation and quality analysis.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'Code indicating if the goods receipt is for special stock types such as consignment, project stock, or sales order stock. Affects inventory valuation and availability.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'The type of stock to which the goods were received. Unrestricted stock is available for use, quality inspection stock is awaiting QA approval, blocked stock cannot be used.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which the received quantity is expressed (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, L for liters). Must match the material master base UOM.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Classification code used for split valuation of materials when the same material is valued differently based on procurement type or quality grade.']
  vendor_batch_number varchar [note: 'External batch number from a supplier or contract manufacturer, if the goods receipt is for subcontracted production. Used for external traceability.']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of good output relative to the total input or planned quantity. Calculated as (received_quantity / order_quantity) * 100. Key OEE component.']

  Note: 'Goods receipt event recording formal completion and stock posting of finished or semi-finished goods produced against a work order. Captures GR document number, posting date, material, plant, storage location, received quantity, batch number, and movement type (101). Sourced from SAP PP/WM (MIGO). This is the production domain\'s handoff point — it triggers inventory update in the inventory domain and signals work order completion. Does NOT own ongoing stock balances (inventory domain owns those).'
}

Table production.bom_consumption {
  bom_consumption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for bom_consumption']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shop floor operator who performed or confirmed the material consumption. Enables accountability and labor tracking.']
  engineering_bom_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific BOM line item that defines the planned component requirement. Links actual consumption to engineering BOM specification.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality inspection lot if the component underwent inspection before consumption. Links consumption to quality control records.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the component material consumed from inventory. Identifies the specific raw material, sub-assembly, or component issued to production.']
  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent work order for which materials were consumed. Links consumption to the manufacturing execution context.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Material consumption entries need the SKU to roll up costs and efficiency metrics to the final product.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific storage location or bin within the warehouse from which material was picked. Provides granular inventory tracking.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse from which the component material was issued. Identifies the physical storage facility.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual cost per unit of the component material based on moving average price or FIFO/LIFO valuation. Reflects real inventory valuation at consumption time.']
  actual_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual quantity of component material issued from inventory to the work order. Represents the real consumption recorded via goods issue or backflush.']
  backflush_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consumption was automatically backflushed by the MES system upon work order confirmation (true) or manually posted (false).']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number of the component material consumed. Enables traceability for quality control, recall management, and shelf-life tracking.']
  consumption_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, observations, or special instructions related to the consumption event. Captures operator notes and exception details.']
  consumption_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the consumption record: posted (finalized in ERP), pending (awaiting confirmation), reversed (cancelled), cancelled (voided), confirmed (validated by supervisor).']
  consumption_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the consumption event: planned (per BOM), unplanned (ad-hoc issue), scrap (defective material), rework (reprocessing), or sample (quality testing).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center to which the material consumption cost is allocated. Used for internal cost accounting and variance analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consumption record was first created in the data warehouse. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all cost amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Ensures consistent financial reporting across global operations.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The expiration or shelf-life date of the consumed component material. Critical for perishable materials, chemicals, and time-sensitive components.']
  goods_issue_number varchar [note: 'The ERP-generated document number for the material goods issue transaction. Typically corresponds to SAP movement type 261 for production consumption.']
  goods_issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the material goods issue transaction was posted in the ERP system. Represents the official consumption event time.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consumption record was last updated in the data warehouse. Tracks data change history for audit and reconciliation.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'Three-digit SAP movement type code indicating the nature of the goods issue. Common values: 261 (goods issue to order), 262 (reversal of 261).']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'The routing operation sequence number at which the component was consumed. Links consumption to the specific production step in the routing.']
  original_goods_issue_number varchar [note: 'If this is a reversal transaction, the goods issue document number of the original transaction being reversed. Enables audit trail for corrections.']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'The quantity of component material planned to be consumed according to the BOM specification and work order quantity. Basis for variance analysis.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the consumption transaction was posted to the general ledger. May differ from goods issue timestamp for period-end adjustments.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consumed component required quality inspection before use (true) or not (false). Supports quality assurance compliance tracking.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for the consumption or variance. Examples: normal production, rework, quality testing, engineering change, process improvement.']
  reservation_item_number varchar [note: 'The line item number within the material reservation document. Provides granular linkage to the specific reserved component.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'The ERP reservation document number that allocated the material to the work order. Links consumption to the material requirements planning reservation.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents a reversal of a previous goods issue (true) or an original consumption posting (false). Used for correction and adjustment tracking.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'The quantity of component material scrapped or wasted during production. Subset of actual quantity representing non-conforming or damaged material.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'The unique serial number of the component if serialized inventory control is used. Provides unit-level traceability for critical components.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'The shift during which the material was consumed (e.g., day shift, night shift, weekend shift). Supports shift-level performance analysis.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard cost per unit of the component material at the time of consumption. Used for cost variance calculation and financial reporting.']
  total_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total cost of the consumption transaction (actual quantity multiplied by actual unit cost). Represents the financial impact of the material issue.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for all quantity fields (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, L for liters, M for meters). Must align with material master base UOM.']
  variance_quantity decimal(18,6) [note: 'The difference between actual and planned consumption quantity (actual minus planned). Positive values indicate over-consumption; negative values indicate under-consumption.']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Specific code explaining the root cause of consumption variance when actual differs from planned. Used for continuous improvement and cost control analysis.']
  vendor_batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number assigned by the component supplier. Enables upstream traceability to vendor quality records and certificates of conformance.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the work center or production resource where the component was consumed. Enables cost center allocation and capacity analysis.']

  Note: 'Component material consumption record capturing the actual goods issue of raw materials and components from inventory to a work order during production execution. Tracks component material number, planned vs. actual issued quantity, variance quantity, unit of measure, storage location, batch number, and backflush indicator. Enables actual vs. planned BOM consumption variance analysis for cost control and material planning feedback. Sourced from ERP goods issue postings (e.g., SAP movement type 261) and MES backflush events.'
}

Table production.resource_tool {
  resource_tool_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for resource_tool']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Tools are calibrated and allocated per component type; linking supports tool‑component compatibility and maintenance planning.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the equipment register in the asset management domain if this production resource tool is also tracked as a capital asset. Links to Computerized Maintenance Management System (CMMS) for maintenance lifecycle details.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record if this production resource tool is also managed as an inventory item (e.g., consumable cutting tools). Links to inventory domain for stock tracking.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tool custodianship assigns a responsible employee, required for Tool Calibration and Maintenance schedule.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Tool Allocation process requires exact stock location of each tool; the Tool Management report joins resource_tool with stock_location to show current location.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Tool assets are procured from vendors; linking enables warranty management and supplier performance monitoring.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center or production line where this production resource tool is primarily assigned. Used for capacity planning and scheduling.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Original purchase or fabrication cost of the production resource tool in the specified currency. Used for capital expenditure (CapEx) tracking and asset valuation.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date when the production resource tool was acquired or placed into service. Used for depreciation calculations and lifecycle tracking.']
  calibration_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the most recent calibration certificate issued by the calibration laboratory. Used for quality audit trails and traceability.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days between calibration events for this production resource tool. Used to automatically calculate next calibration due date.']
  calibration_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production resource tool requires periodic calibration to maintain measurement accuracy and quality compliance. True = calibration required; False = no calibration needed.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current calibration compliance status. Current = within calibration interval; Due = approaching calibration date; Overdue = past calibration date and blocked from use; Not Applicable = calibration not required for this tool type.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production resource tool record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the acquisition cost (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  current_usage_cycles varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of production cycles or operations this production resource tool has performed to date. Used to calculate remaining useful life and trigger replacement.']
  drawing_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the engineering drawing or Computer-Aided Design (CAD) model that defines the production resource tool design. Links to Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) system.']
  expected_useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated useful life of the production resource tool in years. Used for depreciation calculations and replacement planning.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the production resource tool was last calibrated. Used to calculate next calibration due date and track calibration history.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production resource tool record was last updated. Used for audit trails and change tracking.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) who produced this production resource tool. Used for spare parts sourcing and technical support.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Original equipment manufacturer (OEM) part number or model number for this production resource tool. Used for procurement and technical documentation.']
  maximum_usage_cycles varchar [note: 'Maximum number of production cycles or operations this production resource tool can perform before requiring replacement or refurbishment. Used for preventive maintenance planning.']
  next_calibration_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next calibration event. Used for preventive maintenance planning and tool availability forecasting. Tool may be blocked from use if this date is exceeded.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional operational information, special handling instructions, or historical context for this production resource tool.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility where the production resource tool is physically located and managed. Aligns with ERP plant master data.']
  prt_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the production resource tool (jig, fixture, mold, die, NC program, or specialized equipment). Used for scheduling, capacity planning, and shop floor control.']
  prt_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the production resource tool by functional category. Determines usage patterns, calibration requirements, and scheduling logic.']
  resource_tool_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the production resource tool, including its purpose, specifications, and operational characteristics.']
  resource_tool_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production resource tool. Determines availability for scheduling and capacity planning. Available = ready for use; In Use = assigned to active work order; Maintenance = under repair; Calibration = undergoing calibration; Retired = no longer in service; Quarantine = blocked pending quality review.']
  revision_level varchar [note: 'Current engineering revision level of the production resource tool design. Used to track Engineering Change Orders (ECO) and Engineering Change Notices (ECN).']
  safety_certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the current safety certification. Tool may be blocked from use if this date is exceeded.']
  safety_certification_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the current safety certification or inspection certificate. Used for regulatory compliance and audit trails.']
  safety_certification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production resource tool requires safety certification or inspection per Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) or other regulatory requirements. True = certification required; False = no certification needed.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer to this specific production resource tool instance. Used for asset tracking and warranty management.']
  technical_specification varchar [note: 'Detailed technical specifications of the production resource tool, including dimensions, tolerances, materials, and performance characteristics. Used for engineering and quality control.']
  usage_quantity_per_operation decimal(15,3) [note: 'Standard quantity of this production resource tool required per routing operation. Used for tool availability checks during scheduling and capacity planning.']
  usage_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the usage quantity (e.g., EA for each, SET for set, HR for hour). Aligns with ISO standard UOM codes.']

  Note: 'Production resource and tool (PRT) master representing jigs, fixtures, molds, dies, NC programs, and specialized equipment required during routing operations but not consumed in production. Tracks PRT number, type, description, plant, usage quantity per operation, calibration status, next calibration date, and availability status. Enables tool availability checks during scheduling and capacity planning. Maintenance lifecycle details are owned by the asset domain; this entity owns the production-scheduling view of tool readiness. Sourced from ERP PRT master (e.g., SAP PP).'
}

Table production.run {
  run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for run']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Bill of Materials used for this production run, defining the component structure and recipe.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account for whom this production run is being executed, applicable for make-to-order scenarios.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary equipment or machine asset assigned to execute this production run.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary material or product being manufactured in this production run.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Run‑level reporting and profitability analysis need a direct reference to the originating sales order.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'FK to production.production_line']
  sales_order_intake_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: Run scheduling dashboards rely on the originating sales order intake to allocate runs; the FK replaces the redundant sales_order_number field.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'FK to production.shift']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Each production run must reference the SKU to calculate yield, OEE, and cost per product.']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Run supervision links a supervisor employee to each production run for accountability in Run Efficiency analysis.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Production runs need supplier reference to calculate cost, quality impact, and compliance per material batch.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or storage location where finished goods from this run are received.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual total cost incurred for this production run, including material, labor, and overhead costs.']
  actual_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the production run execution was completed or terminated.']
  actual_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total actual quantity produced during this run, representing the sum of good output across all work orders.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the production run execution began on the shop floor.']
  availability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Equipment availability during the production run, calculated as (operating time / planned production time) * 100.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number assigned to the output of this production run for traceability and quality tracking purposes.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Campaign or project code associated with this production run, used for grouping related runs for reporting and analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this production run record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all cost and financial values in this production run.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this production run record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about the production run, capturing operational observations, issues, or special instructions.']
  oee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall Equipment Effectiveness metric for this production run, calculated as availability * performance * quality, expressed as a percentage.']
  performance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Equipment performance efficiency during the production run, calculated as (actual output / target output) * 100.']
  planned_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the production run is planned to complete execution.']
  planned_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total planned production quantity for this run across all included work orders.']
  planned_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the production run is planned to begin execution.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this production run for scheduling and resource allocation purposes.']
  quality_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quality rate during the production run, calculated as (good units / total units produced) * 100.']
  rework_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of material sent for rework or reprocessing during this production run.']
  routing_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production routing or process sequence followed during this run, defining the operations and work centers.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the production run campaign, externally visible and used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production run indicating its operational state.']
  run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the production run indicating the purpose or nature of the execution (standard production, pilot run, rework campaign, validation run, trial batch, or extended campaign).']
  scrap_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of scrap or rejected material generated during this production run.']
  scrap_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall scrap rate for the production run, calculated as (scrap quantity / (actual quantity + scrap quantity)) * 100.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard cost per unit for this production run based on the costing model and BOM.']
  takt_time_minutes decimal(12,4) [note: 'Target production rate or takt time for this run, representing the available production time divided by customer demand, measured in minutes per unit.']
  throughput_rate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average throughput rate achieved during the production run, measured as units produced per hour.']
  total_cycle_time_minutes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cycle time for the production run, representing the sum of all processing time across all operations, measured in minutes.']
  total_downtime_minutes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total unplanned downtime during the production run, measured in minutes.']
  total_setup_time_minutes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total setup and changeover time for the production run, measured in minutes.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for all quantity fields in this production run (e.g., EA, KG, L, M).']
  work_order_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual work orders included or consolidated within this production run campaign.']
  yield_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall yield rate for the production run, calculated as (actual quantity / (actual quantity + scrap quantity)) * 100.']

  Note: 'Production run entity representing a continuous execution campaign on a production line or work center, potentially spanning multiple work orders for the same or similar products. Captures run number, production line, start timestamp, end timestamp, total planned quantity, total actual quantity, total scrap quantity, overall yield rate, number of work orders included, and run status (active, completed, cancelled). Sourced from Siemens Opcenter MES campaign/run management. Enables campaign-level performance analysis beyond individual work orders.'
}

Table production.production_line {
  production_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production line. Primary key for the production line master entity.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.control_system. Business justification: Line configuration & maintenance uses a specific control system; OEE and maintenance reports require linking line to its control system.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Production line overhead is budgeted and reported per cost center; the link supports line‑level cost reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee responsible for supervising operations on this production line.']
  historian_config_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.historian_config. Business justification: Performance analytics and historical data retrieval require linking each line to its historian configuration.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.asset_location. Business justification: Needed to associate each production line with its plant location for capacity planning and regulatory compliance.']
  modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system account that last modified this production line master record.']
  network_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.network_segment. Business justification: Cybersecurity compliance and network topology reports need to know which network segment each line resides in.']
  work_center_group_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center group that this production line belongs to for capacity planning and resource allocation.']
  actual_oee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Most recent calculated Overall Equipment Effectiveness percentage for this production line based on actual performance data.']
  automation_level varchar [note: 'Degree of automation implemented on the production line, ranging from manual operations to fully automated lights-out manufacturing.']
  capacity_constraint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production line is identified as a bottleneck or capacity constraint in the manufacturing process requiring special attention in scheduling.']
  changeover_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total time from last good piece of previous run to first good piece of new run, including setup and stabilization time.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the production line was first commissioned and put into operational service.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production line master record was first created in the system.']
  cycle_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual average time required to complete one production cycle on this line. Used to compare against takt time for performance analysis.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system from which this production line master data originated or is synchronized.']
  design_throughput_rate decimal(12,3) [note: 'Theoretical maximum production rate of the line measured in units per hour under ideal conditions. Used for capacity planning and OEE calculations.']
  energy_consumption_kwh_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Average energy consumption in kilowatt-hours required to produce one unit of output on this production line. Used for energy management and cost analysis.']
  environmental_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this production line is subject to environmental compliance monitoring and reporting requirements.']
  erp_work_center_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding work center code in the ERP system for integration with production planning and costing modules.']
  last_major_upgrade_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent major upgrade or modernization performed on the production line equipment or control systems.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this production line master record was most recently updated or modified.']
  layout_diagram_url varchar [note: 'Reference URL or document path to the physical layout diagram or CAD drawing of the production line configuration.']
  line_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the production line used across MES and ERP systems. Externally-known identifier for scheduling, capacity planning, and shop floor control.']
  line_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the production line for identification and reporting purposes.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the production line based on its primary manufacturing function. Determines the type of operations performed on this line.']
  mes_line_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this production line in the Manufacturing Execution System used for shop floor control and production tracking.']
  mtbf_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time between equipment failures on this production line, used for reliability analysis and preventive maintenance planning.']
  mttr_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to repair and restore this production line to operational status after a failure event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or operational considerations specific to this production line.']
  number_of_stations varchar [note: 'Total count of work stations or process steps configured on this production line. Defines the line configuration and complexity.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the production line indicating availability for production scheduling and capacity planning.']
  planned_availability_hours_per_day decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total hours per day that the production line is scheduled to be available for production, excluding planned maintenance windows.']
  planned_decommission_date date [note: 'Planned date for decommissioning or retirement of this production line from active service.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether output from this production line requires mandatory quality inspection before release to inventory.']
  safety_certification_required varchar [note: 'List of safety certifications or compliance requirements applicable to this production line based on the products manufactured and jurisdictional regulations.']
  scada_system_tag varchar [note: 'Unique tag identifier in the SCADA system used to monitor and control this production line in real-time.']
  setup_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to change over the production line from one product or batch to another, including tooling changes and adjustments.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Operating shift pattern configured for this production line defining daily operational hours and crew rotation.']
  standard_operating_procedure_url varchar [note: 'Reference URL or document path to the standard operating procedures governing operation of this production line.']
  takt_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target time interval between completion of successive units, calculated as available production time divided by customer demand. Critical metric for lean manufacturing and production scheduling.']
  target_oee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness percentage for this production line, representing the goal for availability, performance, and quality combined.']
  throughput_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the throughput rate indicating how production output is quantified for this line.']

  Note: 'Production line master entity representing a named, configured manufacturing line within a plant, composed of an ordered set of work centers and machines. Captures line code, line name, plant, line type (assembly, machining, fabrication, painting, testing), design throughput rate (units/hour), takt time target, number of stations, automation level (manual, semi-automated, fully automated), and current operational status. The SSOT for production line configuration used in scheduling, OEE reporting, and capacity planning.'
}

Table production.work_order_allocation {
  work_order_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the work_order_allocation association']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the order line']
  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the production work order']
  allocated_quantity decimal [note: 'Quantity of the order line that this work order is allocated to produce']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the allocation (e.g., Pending, Confirmed, Completed)']
  allocation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the allocation was created or last updated']

  Note: 'Represents the allocation of production work orders to sales order lines. Each record links one production_work_order to one order_line and captures allocation-specific data such as quantity, status, and timestamps.. Existence Justification: A production work order can be allocated to multiple order lines, and a single order line can be fulfilled by multiple work orders when production is split across runs. Planners actively create, update, and delete allocation records that capture allocated quantity, status, and timestamps, making the allocation a managed business entity.'
}

Table production.work_center_group {
  work_center_group_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for work_center_group']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location or plant where the group operates.']
  parent_work_center_group_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on work_center_group (parent_work_center_group_id)']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.plant. Business justification: Work center groups are defined within a plant; linking to plant provides hierarchy and enables plant‑level reporting.']
  capacity_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum production capacity of the group expressed in units per hour.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center associated with the group.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the group record was first created in the system.']
  default_shift_length_minutes varchar [note: 'Typical length of a shift for the group in minutes.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the work center group becomes operational.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the work center group is retired or no longer valid (nullable).']
  group_code varchar [note: 'External code or tag used to identify the group across systems.']
  group_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the work center group.']
  group_type varchar [note: 'Category that defines the primary purpose of the group (e.g., production, maintenance).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this group is the default selection for new work orders.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the group manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the manager responsible for the group.']
  manager_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number of the group manager.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the group.']
  oee_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OEE value the group aims to achieve, expressed as a percentage.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department that owns the work center group.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Standard shift arrangement used by the group.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the group.']
  total_work_centers varchar [note: 'Count of individual work centers that belong to this group.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the group record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for work_center_group. Referenced by work_center_group_id.'
}

Table production.plant {
  plant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for plant']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the plant.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, building).']
  capacity_mw decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum electrical output capacity of the plant in megawatts.']
  carbon_emission_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total CO₂ equivalent emissions for the plant in kilograms.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the plant is located.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date the plant was permanently shut down, if applicable.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the plant.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plant record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual description of the plant\'s purpose and characteristics.']
  energy_consumption_mwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total electricity consumption for the reporting period in megawatt‑hours.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plant record is currently active in the system.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety or regulatory inspection.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the plant site.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the plant site.']
  maintenance_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Standard interval in days between routine maintenance events.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the plant manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the plant manager.']
  manager_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the plant manager.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the plant.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next major maintenance activity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary information or remarks about the plant.']
  oee_actual decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured OEE percentage achieved.']
  oee_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned OEE percentage target for the plant.']
  operational_since date [note: 'Date the plant began commercial operations.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the plant in ERP/MES systems.']
  plant_type varchar [note: 'Category of the plant based on its primary function.']
  region varchar [note: 'Higher‑level region (e.g., North America, EMEA) for reporting.']
  safety_incident_count varchar [note: 'Number of recorded safety incidents in the reporting period.']
  safety_incident_last_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety incident.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the plant location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the plant.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the plant location.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the plant record.']
  waste_generated_tons decimal(10,2) [note: 'Quantity of waste produced by the plant in metric tons.']
  water_consumption_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total water usage for the reporting period in cubic metres.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for plant. Referenced by plant_id.'
}

Table production.calendar {
  calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for calendar']
  base_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on calendar (base_calendar_id)']
  calendar_type varchar [note: 'Type of calendar definition applied to the row.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calendar record was first inserted.']
  date date [note: 'The calendar day represented by this row.']
  day_of_month varchar [note: 'Numeric day within the month (1‑31).']
  day_of_week varchar [note: 'Name of the weekday (e.g., Monday, Tuesday). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday|Saturday|Sunday — promote to reference product]']
  day_of_year varchar [note: 'Ordinal day within the year (1‑366).']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'Fiscal month (1‑12) for the date.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter (1‑4) for the date.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the date belongs, based on the company\'s fiscal calendar.']
  fiscal_year_start_date date [note: 'First day of the fiscal year to which the row belongs.']
  holiday_name varchar [note: 'Name of the holiday when is_holiday is true; null otherwise.']
  holiday_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the holiday (national, regional, or observance).']
  is_business_day boolean [note: 'True if the date is a regular working day (not weekend or holiday).']
  is_holiday boolean [note: 'True if the date is a recognized public or company holiday.']
  is_leap_year boolean [note: 'True if the year of the date is a leap year.']
  is_weekend boolean [note: 'True if the date falls on a Saturday or Sunday.']
  iso_week_number varchar [note: 'ISO‑8601 week number (1‑53) for the date.']
  iso_year varchar [note: 'ISO‑8601 year associated with the week number.']
  month varchar [note: 'Numeric month of the year (1‑12).']
  month_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the month (e.g., January). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: January|February|March|April|May|June|July|August|September|October|November|December — promote to reference product]']
  month_start_date date [note: 'First calendar day of the month for the row.']
  quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter of the year (1‑4).']
  quarter_name varchar [note: 'Label for the quarter (Q1‑Q4).']
  quarter_start_date date [note: 'First calendar day of the quarter for the row.']
  shift_indicator varchar [note: 'Designated shift for the date in production planning (day, night, or swing).']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier for the date (e.g., UTC, America/New_York).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the calendar record.']
  week_of_year varchar [note: 'ISO week number within the year (1‑53).']
  week_start_date date [note: 'Date of the Monday (or first day of week) for the week containing the row.']
  year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit calendar year.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for calendar. Referenced by calendar_id.'
}

Table production.shift_sequence {
  shift_sequence_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for shift_sequence']
  rotation_shift_sequence_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on shift_sequence (rotation_shift_sequence_id)']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or production line where this shift sequence is applicable.']
  break_count varchar [note: 'Number of scheduled breaks within the shift.']
  break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total break time in minutes per shift.']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the shift sequence in systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shift sequence record was created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the shift sequence purpose and characteristics.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the shift in minutes.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the shift sequence becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the shift sequence expires (null if indefinite).']
  end_time varchar [note: 'Scheduled end time of the shift (HH:mm, 24‑hour clock).']
  is_overtime_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates if overtime work is permitted during this shift.']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Primary labor category assigned to this shift.']
  max_workers varchar [note: 'Maximum number of workers allowed simultaneously on this shift.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the shift sequence (e.g., \'Day Shift\', \'Night Shift\').']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text notes regarding the shift sequence.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Category of shift based on time of day or work pattern.']
  start_time varchar [note: 'Scheduled start time of the shift (HH:mm, 24‑hour clock).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shift sequence.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shift sequence record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for shift_sequence. Referenced by shift_sequence_id.'
}

Table engineering.component {
  component_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the component. Primary key for the component master record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Component cost tracking for budgeting and cost allocation per cost center; required for cost of goods sold reporting.']
  design_engineer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for Component Design Responsibility report, linking each component to the engineer accountable for its design.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Component expense posting to a GL account enables accurate financial statements and inventory valuation.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Inventory Management tracks stock of each engineered component via Material Master for procurement and WIP planning.']
  substitute_component_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of an approved substitute component that can be used interchangeably. Supports supply chain flexibility and risk mitigation.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the preferred supplier for purchased components. Links to supplier master for procurement planning.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'Inventory classification by value contribution. A items are high-value requiring tight control, C items are low-value with relaxed management.']
  cad_model_reference varchar [note: 'File path or URI reference to the authoritative CAD model in PLM. Links to 3D geometry for design, simulation, and manufacturing.']
  ce_marking_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates CE marking compliance for European market access. Confirms conformity with EU health, safety, and environmental protection standards.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Procurement commodity classification code. Groups components by material family for sourcing strategy and supplier alignment.']
  component_description varchar [note: 'Detailed technical description of the component including functional characteristics, application context, and distinguishing features.']
  component_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the component. Primary human-readable identifier used in engineering documentation and BOMs.']
  component_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the component. Human-readable unique code used across engineering, procurement, and manufacturing.']
  component_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the component by procurement and manufacturing strategy. Determines BOM explosion logic and sourcing approach.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for standard cost. Enables multi-currency cost management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the component record was first created in PLM. Audit trail for lifecycle tracking.']
  dfm_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative assessment of component manufacturability. Higher scores indicate easier, lower-cost manufacturing.']
  dfmea_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the DFMEA document analyzing potential design failure modes and mitigation strategies.']
  drawing_number varchar [note: 'Engineering drawing number for 2D technical documentation. References detailed manufacturing and inspection drawings.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current component revision becomes effective for use in new designs and BOMs. Supports ECO implementation.']
  functional_group varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the component within the product architecture. Used for design reuse and modular engineering.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the component contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, storage, and disposal procedures.']
  height_mm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum height dimension of the component envelope in millimeters. Part of dimensional specification for packaging and assembly planning.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Procurement or manufacturing lead time in calendar days. Used for MRP planning and order scheduling.']
  length_mm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum length dimension of the component envelope in millimeters. Part of dimensional specification for packaging and assembly planning.']
  lifecycle_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase in the component lifecycle from concept through obsolescence. Governs release status and usage authorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|design|prototype|validation|production|phase_out|obsolete — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lot_size decimal(12,3) [note: 'Standard production or procurement lot size. Defines batch quantity for manufacturing execution and inventory replenishment.']
  make_or_buy varchar [note: 'Strategic sourcing decision indicating whether the component is manufactured in-house, purchased from suppliers, or both.']
  material_specification varchar [note: 'Material composition and grade specification. Defines raw material requirements for manufactured components.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Minimum order quantity required by supplier or manufacturing process. Constraint for procurement and production planning.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the engineer who last modified the component record. Audit trail for change management.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the component record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking and data currency.']
  obsolescence_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when the component will be obsoleted and no longer available for new designs. Supports lifecycle planning.']
  pfmea_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the PFMEA document analyzing potential manufacturing process failure modes and controls.']
  plm_item_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this component in Siemens Teamcenter PLM system. External system reference for PLM integration.']
  reach_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with EU REACH regulation for chemical substance registration and safety assessment.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Engineering release status indicating approval state for manufacturing and procurement use.']
  reorder_point decimal(12,3) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers replenishment action. Calculated from lead time demand and safety stock.']
  revision varchar [note: 'Current engineering revision level of the component. Tracks design changes through ECO/ECN processes.']
  rohs_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates compliance with EU RoHS directive restricting use of hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Minimum inventory buffer quantity to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions. Input to MRP calculations.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard unit cost for the component in base currency. Used for BOM costing, inventory valuation, and financial planning.']
  technology_family varchar [note: 'Engineering technology classification grouping components by functional domain (e.g., electrification, automation, control systems).']
  tolerance_class varchar [note: 'Manufacturing tolerance classification defining acceptable dimensional variation. Drives manufacturing process selection and quality inspection requirements.']
  ul_certification_number varchar [note: 'UL certification identifier for product safety compliance. Required for electrical components in North American markets.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for inventory, procurement, and BOM quantity calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|G|M|CM|L|ML|SET|KIT — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  weight_kg decimal(12,4) [note: 'Net weight of a single component unit in kilograms. Used for logistics planning, shipping cost calculation, and product specifications.']
  width_mm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum width dimension of the component envelope in millimeters. Part of dimensional specification for packaging and assembly planning.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the engineer who created the component record in PLM. Audit trail for data governance.']

  Note: 'Master record for every discrete engineered part, sub-assembly, or raw material managed in PLM. Serves as the SSOT for component identity, revision state, lifecycle phase, engineering classification, material specification, weight, dimensional envelope, tolerance class, CAD model reference, PLM item ID, and approved substitutes. Covers all manufactured automation system components including electrification modules, PLCs, HMIs, drives, sensors, and structural enclosures. Includes classification taxonomy (commodity, technology family, functional group) for BOM analytics and sourcing alignment.'
}

Table engineering.bom {
  bom_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Bill of Materials record. Primary key for the BOM master data entity.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Customer‑specific BOMs are created for contract fulfillment; linking BOM to customer_account enables contract‑BOM reconciliation reports.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Needed for Project BOM Management process, associating each BOM with the specific capital project it supports for cost and material planning.']
  alternative_bom_indicator varchar [note: 'Identifier for alternative BOM variants of the same product, used to represent different manufacturing methods, material substitutions, or regional variations. Blank for primary BOM.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the BOM in the engineering change workflow. Pending indicates awaiting review, Approved means released for use, Rejected means not accepted, Conditional means approved with restrictions or prerequisites.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or role who approved this BOM revision. Supports audit trail and accountability for engineering change management.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this BOM revision was formally approved for release. Critical for traceability and compliance with engineering change control processes.']
  base_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The fundamental unit of measure in which the finished product is stocked and sold, independent of the BOM quantity basis. Used for inventory and sales order processing.']
  bom_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the BOM structure. Material BOM lists physical components, Document BOM references technical drawings and specifications, Equipment BOM defines installed assets, Variant BOM supports product families, Configurable BOM enables customer-specific selections.']
  bom_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the BOM, providing additional context about the product structure, manufacturing method, or special handling requirements. Supplements the BOM number and type.']
  bom_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the BOM, typically a human-readable code used across engineering, manufacturing, and procurement systems. Serves as the externally-known unique reference.']
  bom_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the BOM. Draft indicates work in progress, In Review means under engineering approval, Approved is ready for release, Active is in production use, Obsolete is no longer valid, Superseded has been replaced by a newer revision, and Frozen is locked for regulatory or contractual reasons. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_review|approved|active|obsolete|superseded|frozen — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  bom_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the BOM by its intended business purpose. Engineering BOM (EBOM) represents design intent, Manufacturing BOM (MBOM) reflects shop floor assembly, Service BOM supports after-sales maintenance, Sales BOM defines customer-facing configurations, Planning BOM is used for MRP, and As-Maintained BOM tracks actual installed configurations.']
  configuration_profile varchar [note: 'Identifier for a variant configuration profile or product family definition. Used in configurable BOMs to define which components are included based on customer selections or feature options.']
  cost_estimate_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the BOM cost estimate. Supports multi-currency costing for global manufacturing operations.']
  cost_estimate_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total cost of the BOM including all material, labor, and overhead components. Rolled up from component costs and routing operations. Used for product costing and pricing decisions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this BOM record was first created in the PLM system. Provides audit trail for data governance and compliance.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this BOM revision becomes valid and can be used for production planning, procurement, and manufacturing execution. Supports time-phased BOM management for product transitions.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this BOM revision remains valid. Nullable for open-ended BOMs. Used to manage phase-out of legacy product structures and ensure correct BOM selection in MRP and MES systems.']
  engineering_change_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the ECO that created or last modified this BOM revision. Provides traceability to the engineering change management process and links to ECO approval workflows.']
  explosion_type varchar [note: 'Defines how the BOM structure should be expanded for planning and execution. Single Level shows only immediate children, Multi Level recursively expands all sub-assemblies, Summarized aggregates quantities across all levels.']
  is_configurable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this BOM supports variant configuration, allowing customer-specific or order-specific component selection. True for configurable BOMs, False for fixed BOMs.']
  is_critical_bom boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this BOM is for a critical or high-value product requiring special handling, approval workflows, or regulatory compliance. True for critical BOMs, False for standard BOMs.']
  is_phantom_bom boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is a phantom (transient) BOM that is not stocked as a discrete item but is exploded through to its components during MRP and production. True for phantom BOMs, False for standard stocked assemblies.']
  lot_size decimal(18,6) [note: 'Standard production lot or batch size for which this BOM is optimized. Used in MRP calculations to determine component requirements and setup costs. Nullable if not applicable.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or system that last modified this BOM record. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this BOM record was last updated. Distinct from approval and effectivity dates. Provides audit trail for data governance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or instructions related to this BOM. May include manufacturing guidelines, quality requirements, or special handling instructions for production planning and shop floor execution.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the manufacturing plant or facility where this BOM is applicable. Supports multi-plant BOM management where different sites may have different manufacturing BOMs for the same product.']
  plm_item_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the owning PLM item in Siemens Teamcenter that this BOM structure represents. Links the BOM to its parent product or assembly definition.']
  production_version varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this BOM to a specific production version in SAP PP, which combines a BOM with a routing to define a complete manufacturing process. Supports multiple production methods for the same product.']
  quantity_basis decimal(18,6) [note: 'The base quantity for which this BOM is defined, typically 1 for single-unit BOMs or a batch size for process manufacturing. All component quantities in the BOM structure are expressed relative to this basis.']
  revision varchar [note: 'Revision level or version identifier for this BOM structure. Tracks engineering changes and ensures traceability across ECO and ECN processes. Critical for change management and configuration control.']
  scrap_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected scrap or waste percentage at the BOM header level, applied to all components. Used in MRP to inflate material requirements. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5%).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system from which this BOM record originated, typically Siemens Teamcenter PLM. Supports data lineage and integration traceability across the enterprise data landscape.']
  source_system_key varchar [note: 'The primary key or unique identifier of this BOM record in the source system. Enables bidirectional traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse and operational PLM system.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity basis, such as EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), M (meter). Must align with the PLM item and material master UOM definitions.']
  usage varchar [note: 'Defines the business context in which this BOM is intended to be used. Production for shop floor execution, Costing for standard cost calculation, Engineering for design and development, Maintenance for service and repair, Sales Order for customer-specific configurations, Project for one-time builds.']
  weight_total decimal(18,6) [note: 'Total weight of the finished assembly including all components, calculated from the BOM structure. Used for logistics planning, freight cost estimation, and product specifications.']
  weight_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the total BOM weight. KG (kilogram), LB (pound), G (gram), OZ (ounce), MT (metric ton).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or system that originally created this BOM record. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']

  Note: 'Bill of Materials master record defining the structured product hierarchy for a given component or finished assembly at a specific revision. Captures BOM type (engineering BOM, manufacturing BOM, service BOM), effectivity dates, quantity basis, unit of measure, and the owning PLM item. Acts as the SSOT for product structure consumed by MRP, MES, and production planning.'
}

Table engineering.engineering_bom_line {
  engineering_bom_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the BOM line item. Primary key for the BOM line entity representing a single parent-child component relationship within a bill of materials structure.']
  bom_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent BOM header that this line belongs to. Links the line item to its containing bill of materials assembly structure.']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: bom_line represents a line item belonging to a BOM; adding bom_id creates the required parent-child relationship and eliminates the BOM silo.']
  primary_engineering_component_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent assembly or product that contains this component. Represents the \'parent\' side of the parent-child relationship in the BOM structure.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: PROCUREMENT: Each BOM line must record the supplier providing that part for purchase order creation and cost tracking.']
  tertiary_engineering_substitute_component_id varchar [note: 'Reference to an approved alternate component that can be used in place of the primary component without requiring an Engineering Change Order (ECO). Enables flexible sourcing and supply chain continuity.']
  assembly_instruction varchar [note: 'Specific instructions for assembling or installing this component into the parent assembly. May reference work instructions, torque specifications, or special tooling requirements.']
  bulk_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component is a bulk material (e.g., paint, adhesive, lubricant) that is consumed in variable quantities and may not be tracked at individual unit level.']
  change_number varchar [note: 'The Engineering Change Order (ECO) or Engineering Change Notice (ECN) number that introduced or last modified this BOM line. Provides traceability to engineering change governance processes.']
  co_product_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line represents a co-product or by-product that is produced alongside the main assembly. Co-products have negative quantities in the BOM and represent outputs rather than inputs.']
  cost_rollup_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost of this component should be included in the parent assembly cost rollup calculation. Some components (e.g., reference items, tooling) may be excluded from product costing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this BOM line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  critical_component_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component is critical to product functionality, safety, or regulatory compliance. Critical components may require additional quality controls, traceability, or supply chain management.']
  effectivity_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this component is no longer effective in the parent assembly. Used to manage engineering changes and phase-out of obsolete components. Null indicates the component is currently effective with no planned end date.']
  effectivity_serial_number_end varchar [note: 'The ending serial number of the parent assembly for which this component is effective. Null indicates the component remains effective for all subsequent serial numbers.']
  effectivity_serial_number_start varchar [note: 'The starting serial number of the parent assembly for which this component is effective. Enables serial-number-based effectivity control for mid-production engineering changes.']
  effectivity_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this component becomes effective in the parent assembly. Used to manage engineering changes and phase-in of new components without creating new BOM versions.']
  engineering_bom_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this BOM line. Indicates whether the component relationship is currently in use, pending approval, or has been superseded.']
  engineering_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes from engineering regarding special assembly instructions, design considerations, quality requirements, or other technical information relevant to this component usage.']
  find_number varchar [note: 'Reference designator or callout number used to locate this component on engineering drawings, assembly diagrams, and technical documentation. Also known as item number or balloon number.']
  fixed_quantity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the component quantity is fixed regardless of parent assembly batch size. When true, the quantity does not scale with production order quantity (e.g., setup materials, tooling).']
  installation_point varchar [note: 'The specific location or mounting point within the parent assembly where this component is installed. Used in assembly instructions and maintenance documentation.']
  lead_time_offset_days varchar [note: 'The number of days before the parent assembly start date that this component must be available. Used in production scheduling and material requirements planning (MRP) to ensure timely component availability.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The user or system account that last modified this BOM line record. Used for audit trail and change management purposes.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this BOM line record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  phantom_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this component is a phantom (transient) assembly that is not stocked or tracked in inventory. Phantom items are exploded through to their child components during MRP processing.']
  position_number varchar [note: 'Sequential position or line number of this component within the parent assembly BOM. Used for ordering and identifying components in engineering drawings and manufacturing instructions.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates how this component is obtained: manufactured in-house (make), purchased from suppliers (buy), transferred from another plant (transfer), or subcontracted to external manufacturers.']
  quantity_per_assembly decimal(18,6) [note: 'The number of units of this component required to build one unit of the parent assembly. Used for material requirements planning (MRP) calculations and cost rollup.']
  reference_designator varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the specific location or function of the component in the assembly (e.g., R1, C5, U3 for electronics; A-01, B-12 for mechanical). Used in assembly instructions and maintenance documentation.']
