  failure_mode varchar [note: 'Specific manner in which the product or component failed as identified during complaint investigation. Aligned with FMEA failure mode taxonomy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for standardized failure mode codes]']
  is_regulatory_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this complaint must be reported to a regulatory authority (e.g., OSHA, EPA, UL, CE Marking body). Triggers compliance workflow and documentation requirements.']
  is_safety_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the complaint involves a potential safety hazard to personnel or end users. Triggers mandatory escalation to product safety and regulatory teams per OSHA and CE Marking requirements.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility where the affected product was produced. Enables complaint analysis by production site.']
  preventive_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of systemic preventive actions taken to prevent recurrence of similar complaints across other products or processes, corresponding to 8D Step D7.']
  production_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP production order number under which the affected product was manufactured. Enables traceability to shop floor execution records in Siemens Opcenter MES.']
  quantity_complained varchar [note: 'Number of units reported as defective or non-conforming in the complaint. Used for severity assessment and potential recall scope estimation.']
  quantity_returned varchar [note: 'Number of units physically returned by the customer under a Return Material Authorization (RMA). May differ from quantity complained if partial returns occur.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the complaint was received and logged in the system. Used for SLA response-time measurement.']
  reported_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the customer formally reported or submitted the complaint. Used as the principal business event date for SLA and aging calculations.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Type of resolution provided to the customer. Drives financial impact tracking (credit notes, replacements) and customer satisfaction measurement.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'Return Material Authorization number issued to the customer for physical return of defective units. Links complaint to logistics return process.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Standardized category of the identified root cause. Enables Pareto analysis of complaint drivers across design, process, material, and supplier dimensions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: design|process|material|supplier|handling|measurement|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the verified root cause of the complaint, corresponding to Step D4 of the 8D methodology. May reference 5-Why analysis, fishbone diagram, or PFMEA findings.']
  salesforce_case_number varchar [note: 'Cross-reference identifier from Salesforce Service Cloud case record linked to this complaint, enabling traceability between CRM and quality management systems.']
  sap_qn_number varchar [note: 'SAP QM Quality Notification number corresponding to this complaint, used for traceability within SAP S/4HANA QM module.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the complaint based on customer impact, safety risk, and regulatory implications. Drives escalation rules and response SLA targets. Aligned with FMEA severity ranking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System audit timestamp recording the most recent modification to this complaint record.']

  Note: 'Customer complaint and field quality issue record capturing reported defects, failures, or dissatisfaction from OEM customers, distributors, or end users. Tracks complaint description, affected product/serial number, failure mode, 8D problem-solving steps, containment actions, root cause, corrective actions, and customer response. Integrates with Salesforce Service Cloud and SAP QM QN.'
}

Table quality.spc {
  spc_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for spc']
  control_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.control_plan. Business justification: SPC chart is defined for a specific Control Plan; replace string reference with FK.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Statistical process control monitoring incurs expenses; linking SPC charts to cost center enables SPC cost tracking.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: SPC charts are generated from data of a specific machine; linking enables equipment‑level capability monitoring.']
  inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP QM inspection plan or Opcenter MES inspection specification that governs the characteristic being monitored by this SPC chart.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or product being monitored by this SPC chart. Links to SAP MM material master. Enables product-specific SPC analysis and capability reporting.']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific manufacturing operation or process step within the routing where this SPC chart monitors quality. Links to SAP PP routing operations and Opcenter MES operation definitions.']
  tag_definition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.tag_definition. Business justification: Each SPC chart monitors a specific tag/parameter defined in Tag Definition; essential for SPC‑Tag mapping in quality dashboards.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing work center or production line where this SPC chart is applied. Aligns with SAP PP work center master data and Siemens Opcenter MES resource definitions.']
  active_rule_set varchar [note: 'Identifies the specific set of out-of-control detection rules configured for this chart. Determines which pattern tests are evaluated during real-time monitoring in Siemens Opcenter MES.']
  auto_recalculate_limits boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the system should automatically recalculate control limits when a specified number of new subgroups have been collected. Supports dynamic SPC limit management in Siemens Opcenter MES.']
  baseline_sample_count varchar [note: 'Number of subgroups used to establish the initial control limits (UCL, LCL, CL) during the chart setup phase. Typically a minimum of 25 subgroups is required per ANSI/ASQ standards to ensure statistically valid control limits.']
  capability_assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the current Cp, Cpk, Pp, Ppk indices were last calculated and recorded. Used to determine whether capability data is current and whether recalculation is required per the control plan review cycle.']
  center_line decimal(18,6) [note: 'The process mean or grand average used as the center line of the SPC chart. Represents the expected process level when in statistical control. For X-bar charts this is the grand mean; for p-charts this is the average proportion nonconforming.']
  characteristic_criticality varchar [note: 'Criticality classification of the monitored characteristic based on its impact on product safety, function, or regulatory compliance. Critical characteristics (safety/regulatory) require tighter capability targets (Cpk ≥ 1.67). Aligns with DFMEA/PFMEA severity ratings.']
  characteristic_name varchar [note: 'Name of the quality characteristic or process parameter being monitored (e.g., \'Shaft Outer Diameter\', \'Surface Roughness Ra\', \'Weld Tensile Strength\', \'Solder Joint Void Percentage\'). Aligns with the inspection characteristic in SAP QM.']
  characteristic_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the monitored characteristic is a variable (continuous measurement, e.g., dimension, weight, temperature) or an attribute (discrete count/proportion, e.g., pass/fail, defect count). Determines applicable chart types.']
  chart_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive human-readable name for the SPC chart, identifying the monitored characteristic and process (e.g., \'Shaft Diameter X-bar R — Line 3 CNC\'). Used in operator dashboards and quality reports.']
  chart_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique identifier for the SPC chart, used on shop floor documentation and in Siemens Opcenter MES. Follows the plant-defined numbering convention (e.g., SPC-WC001-00042).']
  chart_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SPC chart configuration. Active charts are live on the shop floor; suspended charts are temporarily halted; archived charts are retained for historical reference only.']
  chart_type varchar [note: 'Type of Statistical Process Control (SPC) chart used for monitoring. Determines the statistical method applied: X-bar R (variables, small subgroups), X-bar S (variables, large subgroups), p-chart (proportion nonconforming), c-chart (count of defects, constant area), u-chart (defects per unit, variable area), CUSUM (cumulative sum), EWMA (exponentially weighted moving average). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: xbar_r|xbar_s|p_chart|c_chart|u_chart|cusum|ewma — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  control_limit_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the control limits for this SPC chart. Standard is 3-sigma (99.73% confidence); 2-sigma may be used for warning limits; probability-based limits are used for non-normal distributions; custom allows manually specified limits.']
  cp_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Process capability index Cp measuring the ratio of the specification tolerance width to the process spread (6-sigma). Indicates the potential capability of the process assuming it is centered. Cp ≥ 1.33 is typically required for critical characteristics per AIAG PPAP.']
  cpk_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Process capability index Cpk measuring the actual process capability accounting for process centering. Represents the minimum of (USL - mean)/(3σ) and (mean - LSL)/(3σ). Cpk ≥ 1.33 is the standard acceptance threshold for critical characteristics per AIAG PPAP.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SPC chart configuration record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail requirements under ISO 9001:2015 Clause 7.5 for documented information control.']
  cusum_decision_interval decimal(18,6) [note: 'Decision interval (h) for CUSUM charts, representing the threshold at which the CUSUM statistic triggers an out-of-control signal. Typically set to 4 or 5 times the process standard deviation. Applicable only when chart_type = \'cusum\'. Null for other chart types.']
  cusum_reference_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Reference value (k) for Cumulative Sum (CUSUM) charts, representing the allowable slack or reference value used in the CUSUM statistic calculation. Typically set to half the shift magnitude to be detected. Applicable only when chart_type = \'cusum\'. Null for other chart types.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this SPC chart configuration is valid and active on the shop floor. Supports versioning of SPC configurations following engineering changes (ECO/ECN) or process improvements.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which this SPC chart configuration is valid. Null for open-ended configurations. Populated when a chart is superseded by a new version following an Engineering Change Order (ECO) or process revision.']
  ewma_lambda decimal(6,4) [note: 'Smoothing parameter (λ) for Exponentially Weighted Moving Average (EWMA) charts, ranging from 0 to 1. Lower values give more weight to historical data (better for detecting small shifts); higher values give more weight to recent data. Applicable only when chart_type = \'ewma\'. Null for other chart types.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this SPC chart configuration record. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  lower_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Statistically derived lower control limit for the SPC chart, calculated as the process mean minus three standard deviations (3-sigma) or equivalent. Signals an out-of-control condition when breached. May be zero or null for attribute charts where negative values are not meaningful.']
  lower_spec_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Engineering lower specification limit for the monitored characteristic as defined in the product drawing, design specification, or customer requirement. Distinct from the statistical LCL. Used to calculate process capability indices (Cp, Cpk).']
  min_cpk_required decimal(6,3) [note: 'Minimum acceptable Cpk value required for this characteristic as specified by the customer, engineering, or quality standard. Typically 1.33 for standard characteristics and 1.67 for critical/safety characteristics per AIAG PPAP requirements.']
  nelson_rules_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Nelson rules (8 rules for detecting non-random patterns) are active for this SPC chart in addition to or instead of Western Electric rules. Nelson rules extend WECO with additional run and trend tests.']
  out_of_control_action_plan varchar [note: 'Reference to or description of the Out-of-Control Action Plan (OCAP) that operators must follow when an SPC violation is detected. Defines the escalation path, containment actions, and notification requirements.']
  pp_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Process performance index Pp calculated using the overall (long-term) standard deviation rather than the within-subgroup standard deviation. Used in PPAP submissions to assess long-term process performance potential.']
  ppap_submission_level varchar [note: 'PPAP submission level associated with this SPC characteristic, indicating the depth of documentation required for customer approval. Level 3 (full submission) is the default; levels 1–5 vary by customer requirement.']
  ppk_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Process performance index Ppk measuring actual long-term process performance accounting for both process spread and centering using overall standard deviation. Used alongside Cpk in PPAP submissions and ongoing process monitoring.']
  recalculation_trigger_count varchar [note: 'Number of new subgroups that must be collected before an automatic recalculation of control limits is triggered. Applicable only when auto_recalculate_limits is true. Null otherwise.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version or revision identifier for the SPC chart configuration, incremented when control limits, specification limits, or chart parameters are updated following an Engineering Change Order (ECO) or process improvement event.']
  sampling_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which subgroup samples are collected for SPC monitoring, expressed as a time interval or production quantity (e.g., \'every 30 minutes\', \'every 50 parts\', \'every batch\'). Defined in the inspection plan and control plan.']
  sampling_frequency_minutes varchar [note: 'Numeric representation of the sampling interval in minutes, used for automated scheduling in Siemens Opcenter MES and for SPC monitoring dashboards. Null if sampling is quantity-based rather than time-based.']
  subgroup_size varchar [note: 'Number of individual measurements collected per subgroup sample for the SPC chart. Determines the applicable control chart constants (A2, D3, D4, B3, B4) and the statistical power of the chart. Typically 2–10 for X-bar R charts.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The nominal or target value for the monitored characteristic, typically the midpoint between USL and LSL or as specified by engineering. Used in CUSUM and EWMA chart calculations and for Cpk asymmetry analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Engineering unit of measure for the monitored characteristic (e.g., mm, µm, kg, °C, %, N/mm²). Applies to variable characteristics. Aligns with SAP QM inspection characteristic unit of measure.']
  upper_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Statistically derived upper control limit for the SPC chart, calculated as the process mean plus three standard deviations (3-sigma) or equivalent for the chart type. Signals an out-of-control condition when exceeded. Stored as a computed configuration value.']
  upper_spec_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Engineering upper specification limit for the monitored characteristic as defined in the product drawing, design specification, or customer requirement. Distinct from the statistical UCL. Used to calculate process capability indices (Cp, Cpk).']
  western_electric_rules_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the Western Electric (WECO) sensitizing rules for detecting non-random patterns are active for this SPC chart. When true, the chart evaluates all four Western Electric zone tests in addition to the basic 3-sigma rule.']

  Note: 'Statistical Process Control (SPC) chart configuration and monitoring record defining chart type (X-bar R, X-bar S, p-chart, c-chart, u-chart, CUSUM, EWMA), monitored characteristic, associated process/work center, control limits (UCL, LCL, CL), specification limits (USL, LSL), subgroup size, sampling frequency, Western Electric/Nelson rule configuration, and process capability indices (Cp, Cpk, Pp, Ppk). Stores SPC violation events and out-of-control signals as detail records including violation type, timestamp, operator response, and corrective action taken. Drives real-time SPC monitoring on the shop floor via Siemens Opcenter MES integration.'
}

Table quality.quality_audit {
  quality_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality audit record. Primary key for the audit entity.']
  checklist_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit checklist or audit criteria template used to guide the audit and document conformance.']
  lead_auditor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee record of the lead auditor responsible for planning, conducting, and reporting the audit.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the internal department being audited if the audit is an internal audit. Null for external audits.']
  previous_audit_quality_audit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous audit of the same entity, enabling audit history tracking and trend analysis.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the annual or periodic audit program under which this audit was scheduled, linking to the master audit schedule and risk-based audit planning.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier master record if the audit is a supplier audit. Null for internal or customer audits.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when the audit was completed, including closing meeting and final observations.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when the audit commenced, which may differ from the planned start date due to scheduling changes.']
  audit_category varchar [note: 'Categorization of the audit by the relationship to the audited party: internal (own departments/processes), external supplier (supplier facilities), external subcontractor (subcontractor sites), external customer (customer-mandated), certification (third-party certification body), surveillance (ongoing certification monitoring), or special (triggered by incident or complaint). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: internal|external_supplier|external_subcontractor|external_customer|certification|surveillance|special — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  audit_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the audit is conducted: on-site (physical presence at audited location), remote (virtual audit via video/document sharing), hybrid (combination of on-site and remote), or document review (desk audit of documentation only).']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the audit, externally visible and used for tracking and reference across systems and documentation.']
  audit_result varchar [note: 'Overall result or conclusion of the audit: conforming (no major issues), non-conforming (major issues found), conditional pass (minor issues requiring follow-up), fail (critical issues), acceptable, or unacceptable.']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the audit scope, including the processes, departments, product lines, or ISO clauses covered. Defines the boundaries and focus areas of the audit.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit: planned (in audit program), scheduled (dates confirmed), in progress (audit underway), report draft (findings being documented), report issued (formal report distributed), CAPA pending (corrective actions awaited), CAPA verification (actions being verified), closed (audit complete and verified), or cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|scheduled|in_progress|report_draft|report_issued|capa_pending|capa_verification|closed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit by its focus area: system audit (QMS compliance), process audit (manufacturing process conformance per VDA 6.3), product audit (finished goods verification), layered process audit (LPA short-interval checks), supplier audit (external provider assessment), or customer audit (customer-mandated assessment).']
  audited_entity_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the audited entity location.']
  audited_entity_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or address of the audited entity, including city, state/province, and country.']
  audited_entity_name varchar [note: 'Name of the department, supplier, facility, or site being audited.']
  audited_entity_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity being audited: internal department, supplier facility, subcontractor site, customer site, manufacturing plant, warehouse, or laboratory. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: internal_department|supplier|subcontractor|customer_site|manufacturing_plant|warehouse|laboratory — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  auditee_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the auditee contact for audit coordination and communication.']
  auditee_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person at the audited entity responsible for coordinating the audit and providing access to information.']
  auditor_independence_verified boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether auditor independence and impartiality have been verified per ISO 19011 requirements, ensuring no conflict of interest.']
  capa_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which corrective and preventive actions must be completed and submitted for verification.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether corrective and preventive actions are required based on the audit findings.']
  capa_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the CAPA process: not required, pending (awaiting submission), submitted, under review, approved, rejected, verified (effectiveness confirmed), or closed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_required|pending|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|verified|closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  capa_submission_date date [note: 'Actual date when the auditee submitted the CAPA plan or evidence of corrective actions taken.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the audit was formally closed after all findings were addressed, CAPAs verified, and re-audit (if required) completed.']
  conforming_items_count varchar [note: 'Number of checklist items found to be in full conformance with the audit criteria.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was first created in the system.']
  duration_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total duration of the audit in days, calculated from actual start to actual end date.']
  evidence_collected varchar [note: 'Description or reference to the objective evidence collected during the audit, such as documents reviewed, records examined, interviews conducted, and observations made.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Executive summary of the audit findings, highlighting key non-conformances, observations, strengths, and areas for improvement.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit record was last updated, supporting audit trail and change tracking.']
  lead_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead auditor for quick reference and reporting.']
  lead_auditor_qualification varchar [note: 'Certification or qualification held by the lead auditor, such as ISO 9001 Lead Auditor, VDA 6.3 Process Auditor, or internal auditor certification.']
  major_ncr_count varchar [note: 'Number of major non-conformances identified during the audit. Major NCRs represent significant deviations that affect the QMS effectiveness or product quality.']
  minor_ncr_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor non-conformances identified during the audit. Minor NCRs represent isolated or less significant deviations that do not critically impact the QMS.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Statement of the audit\'s purpose and intended outcomes, such as QMS conformance verification, process effectiveness assessment, supplier qualification, or compliance validation.']
  observation_count varchar [note: 'Number of observations or opportunities for improvement noted during the audit that do not constitute non-conformances but warrant attention.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date for the audit as defined in the audit program or audit plan.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date for the audit as defined in the audit program or audit plan.']
  re_audit_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a follow-up re-audit is required to verify the effectiveness of corrective actions or to address major non-conformances.']
  re_audit_scheduled_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the follow-up re-audit to verify corrective action effectiveness.']
  report_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document management system reference or file path to the formal audit report document.']
  report_issued_date date [note: 'Date when the formal audit report was issued and distributed to relevant stakeholders.']
  score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score or rating assigned to the audit based on conformance level, typically expressed as a percentage or on a defined scale (e.g., VDA 6.3 scoring).']
  standard varchar [note: 'The quality standard or framework against which the audit is conducted, such as ISO 9001:2015, IATF 16949, VDA 6.3, AS9100, ISO 13485, or customer-specific requirements.']
  team_members varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of audit team member names or employee IDs participating in the audit alongside the lead auditor.']
  total_checklist_items varchar [note: 'Total number of checklist items or audit criteria evaluated during the audit.']

  Note: 'Quality audit record documenting all scheduled and unscheduled audits regardless of target — internal departments, supplier facilities, subcontractor sites, or customer-mandated assessments. Captures audit type (system audit, process audit, product audit, layered process audit), target scope (internal department/process, supplier site, subcontractor, remote), audit program/schedule reference, checklist criteria and results, findings classification (major non-conformance, minor non-conformance, observation, opportunity for improvement), audit score/rating, auditor qualification and independence verification, evidence collected, corrective action requirements with CAPA linkage, re-audit schedule, and closure status. Supports ISO 9001 clause 9.2 (internal audit), clause 8.4 (externally provided processes — supplier control), VDA 6.3 process audit methodology, and SAP Ariba supplier qualification workflows.'
}

Table quality.apqp_project {
  apqp_project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the APQP project record. Primary key.']
  apqp_sku_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.sku_master']
  primary_apqp_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer for whom this APQP project is being executed. Links to the customer master.']
  primary_apqp_program_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the program manager responsible for overall APQP project execution and coordination.']
  primary_apqp_sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or component being launched or changed under this APQP project.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the APQP project was completed and all deliverables were approved.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when the APQP project activities commenced.']
  apqp_phase varchar [note: 'Current APQP phase: Phase 1 (Plan and Define), Phase 2 (Product Design and Development), Phase 3 (Process Design and Development), Phase 4 (Product and Process Validation), Phase 5 (Feedback Assessment and Corrective Action).']
  control_plan_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the control plan was completed and approved.']
  control_plan_status varchar [note: 'Completion status of the control plan deliverable, defining the process controls, inspection methods, and reaction plans for the manufacturing process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this APQP project record was first created in the system.']
  cross_functional_team_members varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or text description of cross-functional team members involved in the APQP project (engineering, manufacturing, quality, supply chain, etc.).']
  customer_approval_status varchar [note: 'Overall customer approval status for the APQP project and production launch authorization.']
  design_fmea_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the Design FMEA was completed and approved by the cross-functional team.']
  design_fmea_status varchar [note: 'Completion status of the Design FMEA deliverable, a critical Phase 2 output identifying potential design failure modes.']
  gate_review_1_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Phase 1 gate review, assessing readiness to proceed to product design and development.']
  gate_review_2_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Phase 2 gate review, assessing product design completion and readiness for process design.']
  gate_review_3_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Phase 3 gate review, assessing process design completion and readiness for validation.']
  gate_review_4_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Phase 4 gate review, assessing product and process validation completion and readiness for production launch.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this APQP project record was last updated.']
  lessons_learned_documented boolean [note: 'Indicates whether lessons learned from this APQP project have been documented for continuous improvement (Phase 5 activity).']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Target date for completing all APQP phases and achieving customer approval for production launch.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for the APQP project to commence, typically aligned with the product development timeline.']
  ppap_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the customer approved the PPAP submission, authorizing full production.']
  ppap_level varchar [note: 'PPAP submission level required by the customer, ranging from Level 1 (warrant only) to Level 5 (full submission with samples and complete documentation).']
  ppap_status varchar [note: 'Status of the PPAP submission and approval process, the final gate for customer approval to begin production.']
  ppap_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the PPAP package was submitted to the customer for approval.']
  process_flow_diagram_status varchar [note: 'Completion status of the process flow diagram deliverable, documenting the manufacturing process steps and sequence.']
  process_fmea_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the Process FMEA was completed and approved by the cross-functional team.']
  process_fmea_status varchar [note: 'Completion status of the Process FMEA deliverable, a critical Phase 3 output identifying potential manufacturing process failure modes.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the APQP project, typically reflecting the product or component being launched.']
  project_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional notes, issues, or context related to the APQP project execution.']
  project_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the APQP project, typically assigned by the quality or engineering department for external reference and tracking.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the APQP project indicating its progress through the quality planning process.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the APQP project based on the nature of the change or launch being managed.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment for the APQP project based on product complexity, process capability, and customer requirements.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system from which this APQP project record originated (e.g., SAP QM, Siemens Teamcenter PLM, quality management application).']
  target_production_date date [note: 'Planned date for the product to enter full production following successful APQP completion and customer approval.']

  Note: 'Advanced Product Quality Planning (APQP) project record managing the structured quality planning process for new product launches and engineering changes. Tracks APQP phase (1-5), project milestones, deliverable completion status (design FMEA, process flow, control plan, PPAP), gate review outcomes, responsible engineers, and customer approval status. Aligned with AIAG APQP 2nd Edition.'
}

Table quality.compliance_test {
  compliance_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compliance test record. Primary key for the compliance test entity.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this compliance test to a formal CAPA record if corrective or preventive actions were initiated based on test results.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the customer who requested or requires this compliance test, applicable for customer-specific qualification tests or contractual compliance requirements.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Regulatory compliance tests are executed on specific product SKUs; linking supports test result aggregation per SKU.']
  test_engineer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier for the test engineer or quality engineer responsible for overseeing and validating the compliance test.']
  applicable_standard varchar [note: 'The regulatory standard, certification requirement, or industry specification that this compliance test is designed to verify, such as UL 508, IEC 61131, CE marking directives, or customer-specific qualification standards.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch or lot number of the product or material tested, enabling traceability to specific production runs.']
  certificate_issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance certificate was officially issued by the certifying body or testing laboratory.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the compliance certificate issued upon successful completion of the test, such as UL listing number, CE certificate number, or other regulatory certification identifier.']
  certificate_valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date of the validity period for the compliance certificate, indicating when the certification becomes effective.']
  certificate_valid_until_date date [note: 'End date of the validity period for the compliance certificate, after which re-testing or re-certification may be required.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether corrective actions are required to address test failures or non-conformances before the product can be certified or released.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance test record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  customer_notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer was formally notified of the compliance test results and any associated actions or implications.']
  customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the customer must be formally notified of the compliance test results, particularly in cases of failures or conditional passes.']
  customer_specification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to customer-specific technical specifications, qualification requirements, or contractual testing obligations that this compliance test addresses.']
  failure_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of any failures, non-conformances, or deviations observed during the compliance test, including specific failure modes and conditions.']
  laboratory_accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Accreditation number or certification identifier for the testing laboratory, demonstrating its authorization to perform compliance testing under relevant standards.']
  laboratory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific laboratory or testing facility that performed the compliance test, including internal lab names or external accredited laboratory names.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the compliance test record was last updated, supporting change tracking and audit trail requirements.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility code where the tested product was produced, linking to the organizational structure.']
  ppap_submission_level varchar [note: 'PPAP submission level associated with this compliance test, indicating the extent of documentation and testing required for customer approval in automotive or regulated industries.']
  product_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the product, component, or material being tested, providing context for the compliance test scope.']
  regulation_reference varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory requirement, directive, or legal framework reference that mandates this compliance test, including clause or section numbers where applicable.']
  regulatory_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this compliance test has regulatory implications that could affect product market authorization, safety certifications, or legal compliance status.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or comments related to the compliance test, including special conditions, deviations from standard procedures, or contextual information.']
  retest_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a retest is required due to test failure, conditional pass, certificate expiration, or product design changes.']
  retest_scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned date for conducting a retest to address failures, verify corrective actions, or renew expired certifications.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number of the individual unit or sample subjected to compliance testing, providing item-level traceability.']
  test_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance test activities were completed and final results were determined.']
  test_conditions varchar [note: 'Environmental and operational conditions under which the compliance test was performed, including temperature, humidity, voltage, frequency, load conditions, and any other relevant parameters.']
  test_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for conducting the compliance test, including laboratory fees, material costs, and internal resource costs.']
  test_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the test cost amount, such as USD, EUR, or CNY.']
  test_engineer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the test engineer or quality engineer responsible for the compliance test execution and validation.']
  test_laboratory varchar [note: 'Type of laboratory where the compliance test was conducted, indicating whether it was performed internally, at an accredited external laboratory, at a customer facility, or by a third-party testing organization.']
  test_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the compliance test, typically a human-readable test reference number used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  test_procedure_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the documented test procedure, test plan, or test method used to conduct the compliance test, ensuring traceability and repeatability.']
  test_report_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the formal test report document that contains detailed results, observations, and conclusions from the compliance test.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the compliance test indicating whether the product or component passed, failed, conditionally passed, was not applicable, or is still in progress.']
  test_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of what is being tested, including the specific product components, assemblies, materials, or systems covered by this compliance test.']
  test_start_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance test activities commenced at the testing laboratory or facility.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the compliance test indicating whether it is planned, in progress, completed, passed, failed, or conditionally passed pending additional verification.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Category of compliance test performed, such as safety testing, electrical testing, environmental testing, mechanical testing, performance testing, electromagnetic compatibility (EMC), chemical analysis, or material testing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: safety|electrical|environmental|mechanical|performance|emc|chemical|material — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Product compliance and regulatory testing record for certifications such as UL, CE marking, IEC standards, and customer-specific qualification tests. Captures test type, applicable standard/regulation, test scope, test laboratory (internal or accredited external), test conditions, pass/fail results, test report reference, certificate number, validity period, and re-test schedule.'
}

Table quality.notification {
  notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for notification']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated CAPA record if one was created from this notification. Links notification to corrective action workflow.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who reported the quality issue (for Q1 customer complaint notifications). Links to customer master data.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or facility where the quality issue was detected or originated.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or product affected by the quality issue. Links to material master data.']
  ncr_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the associated NCR if one was created from this notification. Links notification to non-conformance management workflow.']
  notification_reported_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who reported or created the quality notification. Links to human resources master data.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for managing and resolving the quality notification. Current assignee for workflow tracking.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier responsible for the defective material (for Q2 supplier defect notifications). Links to supplier master data.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or production line where the quality issue occurred. Enables root cause analysis by location.']
  actual_closure_date date [note: 'Actual date when the quality notification was closed. Used for performance measurement and cycle time analysis.']
  affected_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material or units affected by the quality issue. Used for impact assessment and containment planning.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number of the affected material. Critical for traceability, containment, and recall management.']
  capa_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a formal CAPA is required for this quality notification. Triggers CAPA workflow when true.']
  containment_action varchar [note: 'Immediate actions taken to contain the quality issue and prevent further impact (e.g., quarantine, production stop, customer notification, recall). Critical for risk mitigation.']
  containment_completed_date date [note: 'Date when containment actions were completed and verified. Tracks response effectiveness.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quality notification record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  customer_complaint_number varchar [note: 'External complaint reference number provided by the customer. Used for cross-reference and customer communication.']
  customer_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the customer was formally notified of the quality issue. Compliance and relationship management tracking.']
  customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether customer notification is required for this quality issue. Triggers customer communication workflow.']
  defect_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification grouping of the defect type (e.g., dimensional, functional, cosmetic, material, assembly). Used for trend analysis and reporting.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code classifying the type of defect or quality issue. Links to defect catalog for consistent categorization and analysis.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code of the department responsible for investigating and resolving the quality issue. Used for organizational reporting.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'Source or stage where the quality issue was detected (e.g., incoming inspection, in-process inspection, final inspection, customer site, field service). Used for process improvement analysis.']
  detection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quality issue was first detected or observed. Critical for response time analysis and containment planning.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the affected material or product for quick reference and reporting.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the affected material. Human-readable code used across procurement, production, and quality systems.']
  notification_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the quality issue, including symptoms, observations, and context. Provides comprehensive information for investigation and resolution.']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the quality notification, externally visible and used for tracking and reference across systems. Typically follows SAP QM numbering convention.']
  notification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quality notification. Tracks progression from initial creation through investigation, resolution, and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|open|in_progress|pending_approval|completed|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quality notification. Q1=Customer Complaint, Q2=Supplier Defect, Q3=Internal Problem, Q4=Audit Finding, Q5=Improvement Request, QA=Quality Alert. Determines workflow and routing.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the employee currently responsible for the quality notification. Used for reporting and escalation.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Business code for the plant or facility. Used for location-based reporting and analysis.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level indicating urgency and impact of the quality issue. Critical issues require immediate action and escalation.']
  production_order_number varchar [note: 'Production order associated with the quality issue. Links notification to manufacturing execution context.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number for supplier-related quality issues. Enables traceability to procurement transaction.']
  quality_standard_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to applicable quality standard or regulation (e.g., ISO 9001 clause, industry specification, customer requirement). Used for compliance tracking.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the affected quantity (e.g., EA, KG, M, L). Aligns with material master UOM.']
  regulatory_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this quality issue has regulatory reporting implications (e.g., safety, environmental, compliance). Triggers regulatory workflow.']
  reported_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the employee who reported the quality issue. Provides quick reference without joining to employee master.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quality issue was officially reported or logged. May differ from creation timestamp if entered retroactively.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'RMA number issued for customer returns related to this quality notification. Tracks return and replacement workflow.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the root cause (e.g., material, process, equipment, human error, design, supplier). Used for trend analysis and prevention.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the identified root cause of the quality issue. Result of root cause analysis investigation.']
  sales_order_number varchar [note: 'Sales order number for customer-related quality issues. Links notification to order fulfillment context.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the specific unit or equipment affected by the quality issue. Enables precise traceability for serialized items.']
  target_closure_date date [note: 'Planned or target date for completing investigation and closing the quality notification. Used for SLA tracking and escalation.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief summary or headline describing the quality issue. Used for quick identification and reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the quality notification record. Audit trail for change tracking.']

  Note: 'SAP QM Quality Notification (QN) record serving as the unified quality event management entity for internal defects, customer complaints, supplier defects, and audit findings. Captures notification type (Q1 customer complaint, Q2 supplier defect, Q3 internal), priority, affected material, quantity, defect description, tasks assigned, and resolution status. Central workflow hub linking NCRs, CAPAs, and complaints.'
}

Table quality.rma_disposition {
  rma_disposition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the RMA disposition record. Primary key for tracking quality disposition decisions on returned products.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account that initiated the return. Links to the customer master data.']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the quality inspector who performed the incoming inspection and failure verification.']
  rma_authority_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the authorized person who made the disposition decision, ensuring accountability.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: RMA disposition needs to reference the storage location where returned material is held, required for inventory reconciliation and warranty processing.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier responsible for the defect, if supplier responsibility is confirmed.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Production batch or lot number of the returned product, used for batch-level quality analysis and potential recall actions.']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this RMA disposition requires initiation of a formal CAPA process for systemic issue resolution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this RMA disposition record was first created in the data system.']
  credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary credit amount issued to the customer if disposition was credit, in the transaction currency.']
  credit_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the credit amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  customer_reference_number varchar [note: 'Customer\'s own reference or tracking number for this return, used for cross-reference in customer communications.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the returned material, determining the action to be taken and financial impact. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: repair|replace|scrap|credit|return_to_customer|use_as_is|rework — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disposition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the disposition decision was made and authorized.']
  failure_code_confirmed varchar [note: 'Standardized failure code assigned by quality inspection based on actual findings, used for defect trending and FMEA analysis.']
  failure_code_reported varchar [note: 'Standardized failure code selected by customer or service representative at time of return initiation.']
  failure_description_confirmed varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the actual failure or defect as confirmed by quality inspection, which may differ from the customer-reported failure.']
  failure_description_reported varchar [note: 'Customer\'s description of the failure or defect as reported at the time of RMA initiation.']
  incoming_inspection_result varchar [note: 'Result of the incoming quality inspection performed upon receipt of the returned product.']
  inspection_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the incoming inspection and failure analysis were completed.']
  quantity_returned decimal(15,3) [note: 'Number of units returned under this RMA. May be fractional for bulk materials measured by weight or volume.']
  receiving_plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code of the facility that received the returned material for inspection and disposition.']
  receiving_warehouse_location varchar [note: 'Specific warehouse location or storage bin where the returned material is held pending disposition.']
  repair_action_performed varchar [note: 'Description of the repair or rework actions performed on the returned product, if disposition was repair or rework.']
  repair_completed_date date [note: 'Date when repair or rework activities were completed and the product was ready for return or reuse.']
  replacement_material_number varchar [note: 'Material number of the replacement product sent to the customer, if disposition was replace.']
  replacement_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the replacement unit shipped to the customer, enabling traceability of the replacement.']
  return_initiated_date date [note: 'Date when the RMA was authorized and the return process was initiated by the customer or service team.']
  return_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the returned product was physically received at the inspection facility or warehouse.']
  return_shipment_date date [note: 'Date when the repaired or replacement product was shipped back to the customer.']
  return_to_customer_status varchar [note: 'Status of the return shipment to the customer for repaired or replaced products.']
  return_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier tracking number for the return shipment to the customer, enabling delivery confirmation.']
  returned_material_description varchar [note: 'Full text description of the returned material for human readability and reporting.']
  returned_material_number varchar [note: 'Material number (SKU) of the product being returned. References the product master in SAP MM.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the return authorization. Externally-known reference number used by customers and service teams to track the return.']
  rma_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RMA process, tracking progression from initiation through final disposition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initiated|in_transit|received|inspection_in_progress|disposition_complete|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rma_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the return reason category, determining handling procedures and cost allocation.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the root cause of the failure, used for quality improvement prioritization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: design|manufacturing|material|handling|installation|operation|maintenance|no_defect — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the root cause determined through failure analysis, supporting CAPA and continuous improvement.']
  salesforce_case_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating service case in Salesforce Service Cloud that initiated this RMA.']
  sap_qm_notification_number varchar [note: 'Quality notification number generated in SAP QM module for tracking the quality issue associated with this RMA.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number of the specific unit being returned, enabling traceability to manufacturing batch and production history.']
  supplier_responsibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the root cause is attributable to a supplier defect, triggering supplier quality notification and potential cost recovery.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the returned quantity (each, kilogram, meter, liter, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|LB|M|FT|L|GAL|M2|M3 — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this RMA disposition record was last modified, supporting audit trail and data lineage.']
  warranty_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this RMA is covered under product warranty terms, affecting cost allocation and supplier recovery.']

  Note: 'Return Material Authorization (RMA) quality disposition record for products returned from customers or field service due to reported defects or failures. Captures RMA number, returned product/serial number, customer reference, failure description reported vs confirmed, incoming inspection results upon receipt, root cause determination, disposition decision (repair/replace/scrap/credit/no-fault-found), repair actions performed, and return-to-customer shipment status. Integrates with Salesforce Service Cloud for case tracking and SAP QM for quality notification generation.'
}

Table quality.certificate_of_conformance {
  certificate_of_conformance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for certificate_of_conformance']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer to whom this certificate is issued. Links to customer master data.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Certificate of Conformance is issued for a specific Inspection Lot; replace string with FK.']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the inspector who performed the quality inspection. Links to HR master data for competency verification.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or product for which this certificate is issued. Links to master material data.']
  authorized_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the authorized person who signed and approved the certificate for release. Typically a quality manager or authorized representative.']
  authorized_signatory_title varchar [note: 'Job title or position of the authorized signatory (e.g., Quality Manager, Plant Manager, Authorized Representative).']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch or lot number for which this certificate applies. Critical for traceability and recall management.']
  certificate_language varchar [note: 'Language in which the certificate is issued (ISO 639-1 two-letter code or full language name). May be required for international shipments.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique certificate number assigned to this Certificate of Conformance or Certificate of Analysis. Used for customer reference and traceability.']
  certificate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certificate. Draft = under preparation, Issued = released to customer, Revised = updated version issued, Voided = cancelled, Superseded = replaced by newer version, Archived = historical record.']
  certificate_type varchar [note: 'Type of quality certificate issued. CoC = Certificate of Conformance, CoA = Certificate of Analysis, CoC_CoA = Combined certificate, Material_Test_Report = MTR, Mill_Test_Certificate = MTC for raw materials, Inspection_Certificate = third-party inspection.']
  conformance_statement varchar [note: 'Formal statement declaring that the material conforms to specified requirements. Typically a standardized declaration text.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certificate record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  customer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the customer receiving this certificate. Denormalized for certificate document completeness.']
  customer_order_number varchar [note: 'Customer\'s purchase order number or reference number for which this certificate is issued. Enables customer-side traceability.']
  customer_specific_requirements varchar [note: 'Additional customer-specific requirements or clauses that must be included in the certificate per customer contract or specification.']
  delivery_number varchar [note: 'Shipment or delivery document number for the material covered by this certificate. Links certificate to logistics execution.']
  digital_signature_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or hash of the digital signature applied to the certificate for authenticity and non-repudiation. Used for electronic certificates.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the PDF or electronic document of the certificate stored in the document management system.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the quality inspection or testing was performed for this certificate.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Name of the quality inspector or technician who performed the inspection and testing.']
  issued_date date [note: 'Date when the certificate was officially issued and released to the customer or recipient.']
  issued_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the certificate was officially issued, including time zone information for global traceability.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Production lot number associated with the certified material. May differ from batch number depending on manufacturing process.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Full description of the material or product covered by this certificate. Provides human-readable identification.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Material number (SKU) of the product or material covered by this certificate. Corresponds to SAP material master.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility code where the certified material was produced or inspected.']
  plant_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the manufacturing plant or facility that produced the certified material.']
  ppap_level varchar [note: 'PPAP submission level if this certificate is part of a PPAP package. Levels 1-5 define the documentation requirements per AIAG PPAP standard.']
  ppap_submission_number varchar [note: 'PPAP submission identifier if this certificate is part of a PPAP package submitted to the customer.']
  production_order_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing order number under which the certified material was produced. Links certificate to production execution records.']
  quantity_certified decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material covered by this certificate. Represents the amount tested and certified as conforming.']
  regulatory_compliance_statement varchar [note: 'Statement of compliance with applicable regulatory requirements (e.g., RoHS, REACH, FDA, CE marking). Lists regulations the product conforms to.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent revision to this certificate. Null for initial issue.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the certificate. Increments with each revision or correction. Initial issue is typically 0 or 1.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the certificate was revised. Documents the nature of corrections or updates made.']
  sales_order_number varchar [note: 'Internal sales order number associated with the shipment covered by this certificate.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number of the individual unit or assembly covered by this certificate. Used for serialized products requiring unit-level traceability.']
  signature_date date [note: 'Date when the authorized signatory signed and approved the certificate.']
  specification_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the product specification, standard, or drawing against which conformance is certified (e.g., ASTM A36, DIN 1234, customer spec XYZ-001).']
  specification_version varchar [note: 'Version or revision of the specification document referenced. Ensures traceability to correct specification revision.']
  test_method_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the test methods or standards used for inspection and testing (e.g., ASTM E8, ISO 6892, EN 10002). May list multiple methods.']
  test_results_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of key test results and measurements included in the certificate. May include chemical composition, mechanical properties, dimensional checks, etc.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the certified quantity (e.g., EA, KG, M, L). Follows ISO 31 or SAP UoM standards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certificate record was last modified. Audit trail for record changes.']

  Note: 'Quality certificate (Certificate of Conformance / Certificate of Analysis) issued for manufactured products or material lots shipped to customers. Captures certificate type (CoC, CoA), issued date, product/material, lot/batch number, inspection lot reference, test results summary, specification compliance statement, authorized signatory, and customer-specific certificate requirements.'
}

Table quality.measurement_system {
  measurement_system_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the measurement system record.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.control_system. Business justification: Calibration traceability links each measurement system to the control system that consumes its data; needed for Calibration Compliance reports.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Calibration and maintenance of measurement systems generate costs; linking to cost center enables equipment cost accounting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer responsible for the measurement system\'s performance and compliance.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: Connects measurement instruments to the asset they are installed on, required for calibration scheduling and traceability.']
  reference_measurement_system_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on measurement_system (reference_measurement_system_id)']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Measurement systems must be calibrated and validated against regulatory standards; the FK records the governing regulation.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Measurement System contracts and calibrations are managed per equipment supplier; linking supports supplier contract compliance and calibration schedule reporting.']
  asset_tag varchar [note: 'Internal asset tag used for tracking within the enterprise asset management system.']
  calibration_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the calibration certificate issued by the lab.']
  calibration_interval_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days between mandatory calibrations.']
  calibration_lab varchar [note: 'Name of the accredited laboratory that performed the calibration.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the calibration schedule.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the measurement system is considered in service.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the measurement system is no longer in service (null if open‑ended).']
  gauge_rr_repeatability decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage repeatability component from the Gauge R&R study.']
  gauge_rr_reproducibility decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage reproducibility component from the Gauge R&R study.']
  gauge_rr_study_date date [note: 'Date the most recent Gauge Repeatability & Reproducibility study was performed.']
  gauge_rr_total_variation decimal(5,2) [note: 'Combined variation percentage from the Gauge R&R study.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the measurement system was installed and became operational.']
  last_calibration_date date [note: 'Date the most recent calibration was performed.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date the most recent preventive maintenance was performed.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the measurement system.']
  maintenance_interval_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days between preventive maintenance activities.']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the maintenance schedule.']
  measurement_range_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Highest value the system can accurately measure.']
  measurement_range_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lowest value the system can accurately measure.']
  measurement_system_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the measurement system purpose and application.']
  measurement_system_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the measurement system.']
  measurement_uncertainty varchar [note: 'Stated measurement uncertainty (e.g., ±0.02 mm) as defined by the calibration certificate.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model number assigned by the manufacturer.']
  msa_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used for the MSA (e.g., Gauge R&R, Bias).']
  msa_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the MSA indicating whether the system meets acceptance criteria.']
  msa_study_date date [note: 'Date the most recent Measurement System Analysis was conducted.']
  next_calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next calibration based on the interval.']
  next_maintenance_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next preventive maintenance.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the plant where the measurement system is installed.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the measurement system record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the measurement system record.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number stamped on the device.']
  system_code varchar [note: 'Enterprise-wide unique code assigned to the measurement system (e.g., MS-2023-001).']
  system_type varchar [note: 'Category of measurement technology used by the system.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Physical unit used for the measurement output.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or production line that uses the measurement system.']

  Note: 'Unified measurement system management record covering instrument calibration events, Gauge R&R studies, and measurement system analysis results for quality inspection equipment'
}

Table quality.audit_program {
  audit_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for audit_program']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary owner (person or role) responsible for the audit program.']
  parent_audit_program_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on audit_program (parent_audit_program_id)']
  associated_department varchar [note: 'Business department responsible for owning or executing the audit program.']
  audit_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to conduct the audit.']
  audit_owner_role varchar [note: 'Role of the audit owner within the organization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit program record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the audit program\'s objectives, scope, and methodology.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the audit program expires or is superseded; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the audit program becomes effective and can be scheduled.']
  frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Time unit for the audit recurrence interval.']
  frequency_value varchar [note: 'Numeric part of the audit recurrence interval (e.g., 3).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the audit program is mandatory for the applicable scope.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the audit program.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the audit program in external systems.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit program indicating its purpose or origin.']
  regulatory_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard that the audit program is designed to satisfy.']
  required_resources varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of resources (personnel, equipment, tools) needed to execute the program.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification assigned to the audit program based on impact and likelihood.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Area or entity that the audit program covers.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit program.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the audit program record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit program record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the audit program definition.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the audit program record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for audit_program. Referenced by program_id.'
}

Table quality.audit_checklist {
  audit_checklist_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for audit_checklist']
  parent_audit_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on audit_checklist (parent_audit_checklist_id)']
  applicable_process varchar [note: 'Name of the manufacturing process or functional area to which the checklist applies.']
  audit_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the audit (e.g., pass, fail, observations).']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the boundaries (e.g., plant, line, equipment) covered by the checklist.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current execution state of an audit using this checklist.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the audit (internal, external, or regulatory).']
  category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the checklist (e.g., process, product, system, environment, safety).']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Reference to the external standard or regulation the checklist supports (e.g., ISO 9001, ISO 14001).']
  control_limit_lower decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lower statistical control limit for the measured parameter.']
  control_limit_upper decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper statistical control limit for the measured parameter.']
  control_objective varchar [note: 'The quality or compliance objective that the checklist aims to verify.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline for completing any required corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a corrective action is needed based on the audit result.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the checklist record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose and scope of the checklist.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or URL of the supporting document that defines the checklist.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the checklist version becomes active.']
  evidence_required boolean [note: 'Specifies if supporting evidence must be attached to the audit record.']
  evidence_type varchar [note: 'Preferred format of evidence to be collected.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the checklist is retired (nullable).']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the checklist is required to be executed.']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the checklist has been archived and is no longer active.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the checklist must be performed for the associated process.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when the checklist was last reviewed for relevance.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for any quantitative target in the checklist (e.g., mm, kg, %).']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the audit checklist.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Department that owns and maintains the checklist.']
  related_system varchar [note: 'Name of the equipment, software, or system the checklist pertains to.']
  responsible_role varchar [note: 'Organizational role or job title accountable for performing the checklist.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between mandatory reviews of the checklist.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with the checklist items.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of items to be inspected per audit execution.']
  sampling_method varchar [note: 'Method used to select items for inspection within the checklist.']
  statistical_control boolean [note: 'Indicates whether statistical process control limits are applied.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the checklist definition.']
  target_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric target that must be met or not exceeded during the audit.']
  tolerance varchar [note: 'Allowed deviation from the target value.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the checklist record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the checklist record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the checklist (e.g., v1.2).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the checklist record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for audit_checklist. Referenced by checklist_id.'
}

Table quality.inspection_characteristic {
  inspection_characteristic_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for inspection_characteristic']
  parent_inspection_characteristic_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on inspection_characteristic (parent_inspection_characteristic_id)']
  characteristic_type varchar [note: 'Category of the characteristic indicating the nature of the measurement.']
  code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned by engineering to identify the characteristic across systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the characteristic record was first created.']
