  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this MOQ constraint record originated (e.g., SAP_MM, ARIBA, D365_SCM).']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this MOQ constraint record in the source system, enabling traceability and reconciliation.']
  supplier_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard procurement lead time in days from this supplier for this material, associated with orders meeting the MOQ constraint.']
  supplier_package_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Standard package or container quantity defined by the supplier. Orders must be placed in multiples of this package size.']

  Note: 'Captures Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) and lot-sizing constraints for each material-supplier combination, including MOQ value, order quantity rounding value, maximum order quantity, fixed lot size, and the business justification for the constraint. Tracks MOQ negotiation history and exceptions approved by supply planners. These constraints directly influence MRP lot-size calculations and procurement cost optimization.'
}

Table supply.demand_plan_version {
  demand_plan_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demand plan version record. Primary key for the demand plan version entity.']
  demand_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent demand plan that this version belongs to. Links to the master demand plan entity.']
  primary_demand_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who granted final approval for this demand plan version. Links to the user or employee master for audit trail and accountability in the S&OP approval workflow.']
  superseded_by_version_demand_plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer demand plan version that replaces this version. Null if this is the current active version. Maintains version lineage and audit trail for plan evolution tracking.']
  tertiary_demand_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who most recently modified this demand plan version record. Links to the user or employee master for change tracking and accountability.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this demand plan version was formally approved by authorized stakeholders. Null if version has not yet been approved. Marks the transition to executable plan status.']
  approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this demand plan version has received formal approval through the S&OP governance process. True if approved for execution, false if still in draft or review.']
  baseline_version_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this version serves as the baseline reference for variance analysis and scenario comparison. True if this is the authoritative baseline, false for alternative scenarios or working versions.']
  bias_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Systematic tendency of this demand plan version to over-forecast (positive bias) or under-forecast (negative bias) actual demand. Calculated as the sum of forecast errors divided by sum of actuals, used to detect and correct persistent forecasting errors.']
  confidence_level_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level associated with the demand forecast in this version. Represents the probability that actual demand will fall within the forecasted range, typically 80%, 90%, or 95% confidence intervals used for safety stock calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this demand plan version record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking.']
  demand_time_fence_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from the current date within which demand changes are frozen or restricted. Defines the period where MRP (Material Requirements Planning) will not automatically adjust planned orders based on forecast changes, protecting near-term production stability.']
  forecast_accuracy_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured accuracy of this demand plan version compared to actual demand realization. Calculated as 100 minus the Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), used for continuous improvement of forecasting methods and model selection.']
  forecast_model_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the statistical or algorithmic model used to generate demand forecasts in this version (e.g., ARIMA, exponential smoothing, machine learning model). Applicable when planning method is statistical or APS.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this demand plan version record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization across systems.']
  material_group_code varchar [note: 'Product family or material group classification for demand aggregation. Used to organize demand planning at the product category level before disaggregation to individual SKUs (Stock Keeping Units).']
  planning_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days covered by this demand plan version. Calculated as the difference between planning horizon end and start dates, used for capacity planning and lead time analysis.']
  planning_horizon_end_date date [note: 'Last date of the planning period covered by this demand plan version. Defines the end of the forecast window, typically aligned with fiscal quarters or annual planning cycles.']
  planning_horizon_start_date date [note: 'First date of the planning period covered by this demand plan version. Defines the beginning of the forecast window for supply planning and MRP (Material Requirements Planning) execution.']
  planning_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to generate this demand plan version. Statistical uses algorithmic forecasting, collaborative incorporates cross-functional input, consensus reflects S&OP agreement, APS (Advanced Planning and Scheduling) uses optimization engines, and manual indicates planner-driven creation.']
  planning_time_fence_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from the current date within which supply planning changes require manual approval. Defines the period where automated MRP adjustments are restricted to prevent disruption to committed production schedules.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or distribution center code for which this demand plan version applies. Links to the plant master in SAP PP or Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM for location-specific demand planning.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Business name for the planning scenario represented by this version. Used for what-if analysis and sensitivity testing in S&OP processes (e.g., Optimistic Growth, Conservative Base, Supply Constrained).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the operational system that created or owns this demand plan version record. Typically Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM, SAP APO (Advanced Planning and Optimization), or specialized demand planning applications for data lineage and integration traceability.']
  total_planned_demand_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate demand quantity across all materials and time buckets within this plan version. Represents the total forecasted demand volume for supply network capacity planning and scenario comparison.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the total planned demand quantity. Typically base inventory unit (EA, KG, L, M) aligned with material master UOM definitions in SAP MM or Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM.']
  version_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text commentary and business context for this demand plan version. Captures assumptions, market conditions, promotional events, supply constraints, or other factors influencing the forecast that are relevant for scenario interpretation and decision-making.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the demand plan version. Human-readable version code used for tracking and referencing specific plan iterations (e.g., V1.0, V2.1, BASELINE-2024Q1).']
  version_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the demand plan version. Tracks the approval workflow state from initial draft through review, approval, and eventual archival or supersession by newer versions.']
  version_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the demand plan version indicating its purpose and stage in the S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning) process. Baseline represents initial statistical forecast, sales-adjusted includes sales team input, consensus reflects cross-functional agreement, approved is the final authorized plan, and working is an in-progress draft.']
  creation_date date [note: 'Date when this demand plan version was created. Represents the business event timestamp for version instantiation in the S&OP cycle.']

  Note: 'Manages versioned demand plan records enabling scenario comparison and consensus planning. Captures plan version identifier, version type (baseline statistical, sales-adjusted, consensus, approved), creation date, planning horizon, total planned demand quantity, and version approval workflow status. Supports S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning) processes by maintaining multiple demand scenarios for supply planning sensitivity analysis in Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM.'
}

Table supply.sop_cycle {
  sop_cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the S&OP cycle record. Primary key.']
  primary_sop_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user responsible for orchestrating and managing this S&OP cycle. Typically the S&OP manager or demand planning manager.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the S&OP cycle was formally approved by executive leadership, marking the transition to execution phase.']
  approved_demand_forecast_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the demand forecast approved during this S&OP cycle. Links to the demand forecast master data in Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM.']
  approved_supply_plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the supply plan approved during this S&OP cycle. Links to the supply plan master data in Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM and SAP PP/MM.']
  business_unit_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary business unit participating in this S&OP cycle (e.g., AUTO for Automation, ELEC for Electrification, INFRA for Smart Infrastructure).']
  capacity_utilization_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target capacity utilization percentage for manufacturing plants during the planning period. Used to assess supply plan feasibility and identify capacity constraints.']
  cost_plan_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total planned cost of goods sold (COGS) for the planning period based on the approved supply plan and material/labor cost assumptions. Used for financial reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this S&OP cycle record was first created in the system.']
  cycle_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the S&OP cycle, often including the planning period and business context (e.g., \'January 2024 S&OP Cycle - Automation Division\').']
  cycle_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, special circumstances, or important observations related to this S&OP cycle.']
  cycle_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the S&OP cycle, typically formatted as SOP-YYYY-MM representing the planning period.']
  cycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the S&OP cycle. Tracks progression through demand review, supply review, pre-S&OP meeting, executive S&OP decision, approval, and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|demand_review|supply_review|pre_sop|executive_sop|approved|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  demand_review_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the demand review phase was completed and demand consensus was achieved.']
  demand_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of the demand review phase within this S&OP cycle. Tracks completion of demand consensus building across sales, marketing, and product management.']
  demand_supply_gap_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate quantity gap between demand forecast and supply plan across all materials and time periods within this S&OP cycle. Positive values indicate demand exceeds supply; negative values indicate supply exceeds demand.']
  demand_supply_gap_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate financial value of the demand-supply gap in base currency. Represents revenue at risk (if demand exceeds supply) or excess inventory exposure (if supply exceeds demand).']
  executive_sop_decision varchar [note: 'Summary of executive decisions made during the executive S&OP meeting, including strategic trade-offs, investment approvals, and final plan authorization.']
  executive_sop_meeting_date date [note: 'Date when the executive S&OP meeting was held for final decision-making and plan approval by senior leadership.']
  financial_reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date when financial reconciliation was completed and the S&OP plan was aligned with the financial budget.']
  financial_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of financial reconciliation between the approved S&OP plan and the financial budget. Ensures alignment between operational plans and financial commitments.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year, typically 1-12, used for financial reconciliation.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which this S&OP cycle belongs, used for financial reconciliation and annual planning alignment.']
  gap_resolution_strategy varchar [note: 'Description of the strategy approved to resolve demand-supply gaps, including capacity expansion, supplier escalation, demand shaping, inventory build, or customer allocation decisions.']
  inventory_plan_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target inventory value at the end of the planning period based on the approved supply plan. Used for working capital planning and financial reconciliation.']
  key_assumptions varchar [note: 'Critical assumptions underlying the S&OP plan, including market conditions, customer demand drivers, supplier performance, capacity availability, and economic factors.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this S&OP cycle record was last updated in the system.']
  planning_horizon_months varchar [note: 'Number of months into the future covered by this S&OP cycle planning horizon. Typically 12-18 months for industrial manufacturing.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the planning horizon covered by this S&OP cycle. Typically the last day of the month for monthly S&OP cycles.']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the planning horizon covered by this S&OP cycle. Typically the first day of the month for monthly S&OP cycles.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Primary manufacturing plant code for which this S&OP cycle is executed. Links to SAP S/4HANA plant master data.']
  pre_sop_meeting_date date [note: 'Date when the pre-S&OP meeting was held to reconcile demand and supply plans and prepare recommendations for executive review.']
  pre_sop_meeting_outcome varchar [note: 'Summary of key decisions, gap resolutions, and recommendations from the pre-S&OP meeting. Captures consensus on demand-supply balance and escalation items for executive S&OP.']
  revenue_plan_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total planned revenue for the planning period based on the approved demand forecast and pricing assumptions. Used for financial reconciliation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this S&OP cycle record originated. Typically Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM integrated business planning module.']
  supply_review_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the supply review phase was completed and supply commitment was established.']
  supply_review_status varchar [note: 'Status of the supply review phase within this S&OP cycle. Tracks completion of supply capability assessment across manufacturing, procurement, and logistics.']
  supply_risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall supply risk assessment for this S&OP cycle, considering supplier lead times, capacity constraints, material availability, and geopolitical factors.']
  supply_risk_mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Description of actions planned to mitigate identified supply risks, including supplier diversification, safety stock increases, or alternative sourcing strategies.']

  Note: 'Represents a Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP) cycle record for the industrial manufacturing business, capturing the monthly integrated business planning process execution. Records planning cycle period, participating business units (automation, electrification, smart infrastructure), demand review status, supply review status, pre-S&OP meeting outcomes, executive S&OP decisions, approved supply plan version, approved demand forecast version, key supply/demand gap resolutions, and financial reconciliation status. Serves as the governance audit record linking demand consensus to supply commitment for each planning period. Managed through Microsoft Dynamics 365 SCM integrated business planning.'
}

Table supply.allocation {
  allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for allocation']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account receiving the allocation when allocation_recipient_type is customer or oem_partner.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supply chain manager or executive who approved this allocation decision.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Allocation of supply to specific sales order lines is required for the \'Order Fulfillment Allocation\' process, ensuring each line\'s quantity is matched to available supply.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for the automation component, electrification module, or smart infrastructure product being allocated.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_contract. Business justification: Allocation of supply quantities to fulfill obligations under specific service contracts (Contract Allocation dashboard).']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Allocation records allocate available supply to specific SKUs for order fulfillment.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier whose capacity or delivery constraints triggered the allocation decision.']
  supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the constrained supply plan that triggered this allocation decision.']
  allocated_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material allocated to the recipient entity (customer, plant, distribution center, or product line).']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Algorithm or business rule used to distribute constrained supply across competing demand requests.']
  allocation_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique allocation identifier used for tracking and reference in supply chain communications.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the allocation decision in the supply chain workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|proposed|approved|active|partially_fulfilled|fulfilled|cancelled|superseded — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the allocation decision was formally approved for execution.']
  available_supply_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total constrained supply quantity available for allocation across all competing demand requests.']
  communication_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether allocation notification has been sent to the affected customer or recipient entity.']
  communication_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when allocation notification was sent to the recipient.']
  constraint_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the supply constraint circumstances, including supplier names, affected components, and expected resolution timeline.']
  constraint_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the root cause of the supply constraint that necessitated this allocation decision.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the supply contract or SLA that mandates this allocation when contractual_commitment_flag is true.']
  contractual_commitment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation fulfills a contractual supply commitment or service level agreement (SLA) obligation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this allocation record was first created in the system.']
  customer_tier varchar [note: 'Customer segmentation tier used in priority-based or customer-tier allocation methods to determine allocation preference.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the allocation decision was made by the supply planning team or automated allocation engine.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation decision required executive escalation due to high business impact or customer sensitivity.']
  expected_resolution_date date [note: 'Anticipated date when the supply constraint will be resolved and normal supply levels restored.']
  fulfillment_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of requested quantity that was allocated, calculated as (allocated_quantity / requested_quantity) * 100.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this allocation record was last updated or revised.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special considerations, exceptions, or business rationale for the allocation decision.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the time period for which this allocation decision is effective.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the time period for which this allocation decision is effective.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the manufacturing plant or distribution center where the constrained supply originates.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking assigned to this allocation decision, where lower numbers indicate higher priority in fulfillment sequencing.']
  product_line_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the product line receiving the allocation when allocation_recipient_type is product_line.']
  recipient_distribution_center_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the regional distribution center receiving the allocation when allocation_recipient_type is distribution_center.']
  recipient_plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the recipient plant when allocation_recipient_type is plant.']
  recipient_type varchar [note: 'Type of entity receiving the allocated supply, used to determine the appropriate recipient identifier field.']
  requested_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Original quantity requested by the recipient before allocation constraints were applied.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system that created this allocation record (e.g., D365_SCM for Microsoft Dynamics 365 Supply Chain Management, SAP_PP for SAP Production Planning).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the allocated quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter).']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the allocation decision, incremented when allocation is revised or rebalanced.']

  Note: 'Records supply allocation decisions made when available supply is insufficient to meet total demand across the industrial manufacturing portfolio. Specifies how constrained supply of automation components, electrification modules, or smart infrastructure products is distributed across OEM customers, regional distribution centers, plants, or product lines. Captures allocation quantity, allocation priority, allocation method (proportional, priority-based, customer-tier, contractual commitment), allocation period, demand requests being fulfilled or partially fulfilled, and the constraining supply plan reference. Critical for managing semiconductor shortages, long-lead-time component constraints, and force majeure events in industrial manufacturing supply chains.'
}

Table supply.replenishment_proposal {
  replenishment_proposal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the replenishment proposal record. Primary key.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for which replenishment is proposed.']
  mrp_run_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRP run that generated this replenishment proposal.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Replenishment proposals are triggered by individual sales orders; linking to order_header enables the \'Replenishment Proposal Generation\' report.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.plant']
  primary_replenishment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who firmed this proposal, locking it from automatic MRP adjustments.']
  quote_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote_line. Business justification: Replenishment proposals are derived from quoted line items to ensure stock for quoted sales.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.request. Business justification: Replenishment proposals are generated from field service requests for spare parts (Service‑Driven Replenishment process).']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Replenishment proposals are generated per SKU to trigger purchase orders or production.']
  source_plant_id varchar [note: 'FK to supply.plant']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preferred or proposed supplier for external procurement proposals. Null for in-house production proposals.']
  converted_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the purchase order, production order, or stock transport order created from this proposal. Null if not yet converted.']
  converted_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of execution document created when the proposal was converted: purchase_order, production_order, stock_transport_order, or subcontract_order. Null if not yet converted.']
  converted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the proposal was converted into a purchase order, production order, or stock transport order.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this replenishment proposal record was first created in the system, typically during MRP run execution.']
  exception_message_code varchar [note: 'MRP exception or warning code associated with this proposal (e.g., reschedule in, reschedule out, cancel, increase quantity, decrease quantity). Alerts planner to abnormal conditions.']
  exception_message_text varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the MRP exception or planning situation requiring planner attention.']
  firmed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the proposal has been firmed by the planner. Firmed proposals are protected from automatic MRP changes in subsequent runs.']
  firmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the proposal was firmed by the planner.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this replenishment proposal record was last updated, capturing planner actions, status changes, or subsequent MRP run adjustments.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Total procurement or production lead time in days used to calculate the proposed order date from the requirement date. Includes processing, manufacturing, and transit time.']
  lot_sizing_procedure varchar [note: 'Algorithm used by MRP to determine the proposed quantity: lot_for_lot (exact requirement), fixed_lot_size (standard batch), economic_order_quantity (EOQ optimization), period_lot_size (aggregate period demand), or replenish_to_max (fill to maximum stock level).']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum order quantity constraint applied during MRP calculation, typically driven by supplier requirements or production batch minimums.']
  mrp_controller_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the MRP controller or buyer/planner responsible for reviewing and actioning this proposal.']
  planner_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text comments entered by the planner documenting decisions, overrides, or special handling instructions for this proposal.']
  planner_override_date date [note: 'Manually adjusted delivery date entered by the planner, overriding the MRP-calculated proposed delivery date. Null if no override applied.']
  planner_override_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Manually adjusted quantity entered by the planner, overriding the MRP-calculated proposed quantity. Null if no override applied.']
  planning_time_fence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this proposal falls within the planning time fence period where automatic MRP changes are restricted and planner approval is required for modifications.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Urgency level assigned to the proposal: critical (immediate action required), high (expedited processing), normal (standard lead time), or low (can be delayed if needed).']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Method of procurement for the material: external (purchased from supplier), in_house (manufactured internally), or both (mixed procurement strategy).']
  proposal_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for the replenishment proposal, typically system-generated or following organizational numbering convention.']
  proposal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the replenishment proposal: open (awaiting planner review), firmed (approved by planner but not yet converted), converted (transformed into purchase order or production order), rejected (declined by planner), cancelled (voided due to changed requirements), or on_hold (temporarily suspended).']
  proposal_type varchar [note: 'Type of replenishment action proposed by MRP: purchase requisition for external procurement, planned production order for in-house manufacturing, stock transfer request for inter-plant movement, subcontract order for external processing, or external procurement for direct vendor shipment.']
  proposed_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the material should be available to meet demand requirements, calculated by MRP based on lead time and requirement date.']
  proposed_order_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase requisition or production order should be created to meet the proposed delivery date, accounting for procurement or manufacturing lead time.']
  proposed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material recommended by MRP for replenishment, calculated based on net requirements, lot sizing rules, and safety stock policies.']
  purchasing_group_code varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for procurement activities for this material category. Used for purchase requisition routing.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the planner rejected this proposal (e.g., obsolete material, demand cancelled, alternative source selected). Null if not rejected.']
  rejection_reason_text varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the proposal was rejected by the planner. Null if not rejected.']
  reorder_point_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers replenishment proposal generation in reorder point planning scenarios.']
  requirement_date date [note: 'Original date when the material is needed to satisfy customer orders, production schedules, or safety stock targets. Drives the MRP calculation.']
  rounding_value decimal(18,3) [note: 'Rounding increment applied to the proposed quantity to align with packaging, pallet, or container standards (e.g., round to nearest 10, 50, or 100 units).']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Safety stock level maintained for this material at this location. MRP generates proposals to maintain this buffer against demand variability.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system that generated this replenishment proposal (e.g., SAP_PP, D365_SCM, APS_SYSTEM).']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Specific storage location within the plant where the replenished material will be received and stored.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the proposed quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter, M for meter).']

  Note: 'Consolidates MRP-generated replenishment proposals for buyer/planner review before conversion to purchase orders or production orders. Captures proposal type (purchase requisition, planned production order, stock transfer), proposed quantity, proposed dates, supplying source, proposal status (open, firmed, converted, rejected), and planner override details. Represents the actionable output of MRP runs requiring human review in SAP MM/PP.'
}

Table supply.planning_parameter {
  planning_parameter_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for planning_parameter']
  planning_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Factory calendar identifier that defines working days, holidays, and shifts used to schedule planned order dates and calculate lead times.']
  primary_planning_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the planner or administrator who created this planning parameter configuration record.']
  primary_planning_material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for which these planning parameters apply.']
  process_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.process_parameter. Business justification: Connects planning parameters to defined process parameters, enabling the Production Planning‑Execution Alignment report that validates parameter consistency.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Planning parameters (lot size, lead time) are stored per SKU to drive MRP calculations.']
  abc_indicator varchar [note: 'Classification of the material based on consumption value or strategic importance: A=High Value/Critical, B=Medium Value/Moderate, C=Low Value/Routine, used to prioritize planning attention and inventory policies.']
  assembly_scrap_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected scrap or yield loss percentage during assembly or production, used to inflate gross requirements to account for anticipated waste.']
  availability_check_group varchar [note: 'Defines the scope and logic of ATP (Available-to-Promise) checking: which stock types, planned receipts, and reservations are considered when confirming customer orders or production orders.']
  backward_consumption_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the past that actual demand (sales orders, shipments) can consume forecast quantities, reducing planned independent requirements retroactively.']
  consumption_mode varchar [note: 'Defines how actual demand consumes forecast: 1=Backward Only, 2=Backward and Forward, 3=Forward Only, 4=Forecast Reduction by Material.']
  conversion_indicator varchar [note: 'Controls whether planned orders are automatically converted to production or purchase orders: 1=Automatic Conversion, 2=Manual Conversion Required.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this planning parameter configuration record was first created in the system.']
  discontinuation_indicator varchar [note: 'Flags materials in phase-out: 1=Material to be Discontinued, 2=Successor Material Exists, Z=Discontinuation Effective, used to trigger follow-up material substitution in planning.']
  effective_out_date date [note: 'Date on which this material is discontinued and should no longer be planned or procured, triggering transition to successor materials.']
  forward_consumption_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the future that actual demand can consume forecast quantities, allowing early orders to reduce future planned independent requirements.']
  goods_receipt_processing_time_days varchar [note: 'Time in days required for receiving, inspection, and posting of goods after physical arrival, extending the total procurement lead time.']
  in_house_production_time_days varchar [note: 'Total lead time in days required to manufacture the material in-house, from order release to goods receipt, including queue, setup, processing, and move times.']
  individual_collective_indicator varchar [note: 'Defines whether requirements are managed individually per sales order (1=Individual Requirements) or pooled across orders (2=Collective Requirements) for planning purposes.']
  jit_delivery_schedules_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this material is managed under JIT delivery schedules with suppliers, enabling frequent small-lot deliveries synchronized with production consumption.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this planning parameter configuration record was most recently updated, supporting change tracking and audit compliance.']
  last_mrp_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent MRP run that processed this material-plant combination using these planning parameters, enabling audit and troubleshooting.']
  lot_sizing_procedure varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate order quantities: EX=Lot-for-Lot, HB=Replenish to Maximum Stock Level, FX=Fixed Lot Size, WB=Weekly Lot Size, LS=Lot Size Key, ZB=Periodic Lot Size, EW=Economic Order Quantity, FB=Fixed Bin Quantity, LZ=Lot Size with Rounding. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EX|HB|FX|WB|LS|ZB|EW|FB|LZ — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  maximum_lot_size_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Maximum order quantity that can be procured or produced in a single lot, driven by storage capacity, equipment constraints, or supplier limitations.']
  minimum_lot_size_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum order quantity that must be procured or produced in a single lot, enforcing MOQ constraints from suppliers or production efficiency requirements.']
  mixed_mrp_indicator varchar [note: 'Controls whether subassemblies are planned with the header material or separately: 1=Plan Subassemblies Separately, 2=Plan with Header, 3=No Mixed MRP.']
  mrp_controller_code varchar [note: 'Three-character code identifying the planner or planning group responsible for managing MRP exceptions, firming planned orders, and coordinating supply for this material-plant combination.']
  mrp_type varchar [note: 'MRP procedure that controls how the planning run processes this material: PD=MRP, VB=Manual Reorder Point, VM=Automatic Reorder Point, VV=Forecast-Based Planning, ND=No Planning, R1=Time-Phased Planning, R2=Reorder Point Planning with External Requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PD|VB|VM|VV|ND|R1|R2 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  parameter_effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this planning parameter configuration becomes active and is used by MRP runs, enabling time-phased parameter changes.']
  parameter_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this planning parameter configuration expires and is no longer used by MRP runs, supporting planned transitions to new parameter sets.']
  parameter_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this planning parameter record: active=In Use by MRP, inactive=Disabled, pending=Awaiting Approval, archived=Historical Record.']
  period_indicator varchar [note: 'Defines the planning period granularity for this material: D=Daily, W=Weekly, M=Monthly, controlling how requirements are bucketed and planned orders are generated.']
  planned_delivery_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected procurement lead time in days from purchase order creation to goods receipt for externally procured materials, based on supplier lead time agreements.']
  planning_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Total number of days into the future that the MRP run will generate planned orders and evaluate requirements, defining the outer boundary of the planning window.']
  planning_strategy_group varchar [note: 'Strategy that defines the planning approach: 10=Make-to-Stock, 11=Make-to-Stock with Final Assembly, 20=Make-to-Order, 25=Make-to-Order with Planning, 30=Production by Lot Size, 40=Planning at Assembly Level, 50=Planning without Final Assembly, 52=Planning with Final Assembly, 54=Planning with Planning Material, 56=Planning with Planning Material and Final Assembly, 59=Phantom Assembly, 60=Planning with Collective Requirements, 70=Planning at Sales Order Item Level, 82=Assembly Processing, 89=Configure-to-Order. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 10|11|20|25|30|40|50|52|54|56|59|60|70|82|89 — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  planning_time_fence_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from today defining the firm zone where MRP will not automatically create, reschedule, or delete planned orders without planner approval, protecting near-term execution stability.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character alphanumeric code identifying the manufacturing or distribution plant where these planning parameters are effective.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Defines how the material is obtained: E=In-house Production, F=External Procurement, X=Both (production and procurement possible).']
  quota_arrangement_usage varchar [note: 'Defines how quota arrangements distribute procurement across multiple sources: 1=Quota Arrangement Active, 2=Quota Arrangement Inactive, 3=Quota Arrangement for Subcontracting.']
  rounding_value_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity increment to which planned order quantities are rounded up to align with packaging units, pallet sizes, or standard container quantities.']
  scheduling_margin_key varchar [note: 'Key that defines opening period, float before production, and float after production used in backward and forward scheduling to buffer planned order dates.']
  special_procurement_type varchar [note: 'Special procurement rule: 10=Withdrawal from Alternative Storage Location, 20=Production in Alternative Plant, 30=External Procurement from Alternative Plant, 40=Stock Transfer, 50=Phantom Assembly, 52=Collective Requirements, 60=Cross-Project Material, 70=Consignment, 80=Pipeline, 90=Subcontracting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 10|20|30|40|50|52|60|70|80|90 — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  strategy_group_counter varchar [note: 'Sequence number used in conjunction with planning strategy group to differentiate multiple planning variants for the same material-plant combination.']
  takt_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target production rate in minutes per unit, defining the pace at which this material must be produced to meet customer demand without overproduction or underproduction.']

  Note: 'Stores the configurable planning parameters that govern MRP and APS behavior for each material-plant combination, including planning strategy (make-to-stock, make-to-order, assemble-to-order), planning horizon, time fence (firm zone, trade-off zone, planning zone), lot-sizing procedure, planning calendar, and MRP controller assignment. These parameters directly control how the supply planning engine generates planned orders and requirements.'
}

Table supply.aps_scenario {
  aps_scenario_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for aps_scenario']
  baseline_aps_scenario_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on aps_scenario (baseline_aps_scenario_id)']
  backorder_allowed boolean [note: 'Whether backorders are permitted when demand exceeds supply.']
  capacity_planning_method varchar [note: 'Methodology applied to allocate production capacity in the scenario.']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to reference the scenario in systems and reports.']
  convergence_tolerance decimal(5,4) [note: 'Numeric tolerance for stopping criteria of the optimizer.']
  cost_model_version varchar [note: 'Version of the cost model applied to calculate scenario economics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scenario record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code for monetary values used in the scenario.']
  demand_forecast_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to generate demand forecasts within the scenario.']
  demand_source varchar [note: 'Origin of demand data used in the scenario.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the scenario purpose, scope and assumptions.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the scenario is no longer valid (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the scenario becomes valid for planning.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the scenario is currently active for planning runs.']
  lead_time_buffer_days varchar [note: 'Additional safety buffer added to supplier lead times.']
  lot_sizing_rule varchar [note: 'Rule used to determine production lot sizes.']
  max_iterations varchar [note: 'Upper bound on solver iterations for convergence.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the planning scenario.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments from planners.']
  optimization_objective varchar [note: 'Primary objective the optimizer seeks to achieve.']
  overtime_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates if overtime labor can be scheduled in the scenario.']
  planning_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithmic approach used by the optimizer.']
  planning_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of future days the scenario plans for.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority of the scenario (1 = highest).']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the scenario adheres to applicable industry regulations (e.g., environmental, safety).']
  related_product_family varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product family that the scenario primarily addresses.']
  safety_stock_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing safety stock calculations.']
  scenario_type varchar [note: 'Broad category of planning the scenario supports.']
  status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the scenario.']
  supplier_inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supplier constraints are considered in the scenario.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the scenario.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the scenario record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for change management of the scenario definition.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the scenario.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for aps_scenario. Referenced by aps_scenario_id.'
}

Table supply.planning_calendar {
  planning_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for planning_calendar']
  base_planning_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on planning_calendar (base_planning_calendar_id)']
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the holiday calendar that defines non‑working days for this planning calendar.']
  calendar_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used to reference the calendar in downstream systems.']
  calendar_type varchar [note: 'Category of the calendar indicating its primary planning purpose.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the calendar record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing context or notes about the calendar.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the calendar becomes effective for planning activities.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the calendar ceases to be effective (nullable for open‑ended calendars).']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar day that is included in the planning horizon.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'Granularity at which planning cycles repeat.']
  is_holiday_calendar boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the calendar includes holiday definitions (true) or not (false).']
  last_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent planning run that used this calendar.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the planning calendar (e.g., Fiscal 2025, Production Shift Calendar).']
  next_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the next planning run that will use this calendar.']
  planning_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days covered by the calendar for forward planning.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar day that is included in the planning horizon.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the calendar.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier (e.g., America/Chicago) used for timestamp calculations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the calendar record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the calendar record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for planning_calendar. Referenced by planning_calendar_id.'
}

Table supply.plant {
  plant_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for plant']
  capacity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum production capacity of the plant expressed in the unit defined by capacity_unit.']
  capacity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the production capacity.']
  carbon_emission_factor decimal(8,4) [note: 'CO₂ emission factor (kg CO₂ per unit of production) for the plant.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the plant is located.']
  closing_date date [note: 'Date the plant ceased operations, if applicable.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of certifications (e.g., ISO 9001, ISO 14001) held by the plant.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the plant location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plant record was first created in the data lake.']
  energy_source varchar [note: 'Main source of energy used by the plant.']
  is_primary_plant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this plant is the primary manufacturing site for the company.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent scheduled maintenance.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the plant.']
  location_address varchar [note: 'Street address of the plant facility.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the plant.']
  maintenance_window varchar [note: 'Typical time window (e.g., 02:00‑04:00) when maintenance activities are performed.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the plant manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary manager responsible for the plant.']
  manager_phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number for the plant manager.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the plant.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled maintenance.']
  number_of_shifts varchar [note: 'Number of production shifts operated per day.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date the plant began operations.']
  operational_start_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the plant began its current operational run.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the plant in ERP and supply‑chain systems.']
  plant_owner varchar [note: 'Legal entity that owns the plant.']
  plant_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status of the plant.']
  plant_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the primary function of the plant.']
  power_capacity_mw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum electrical power capacity of the plant in megawatts.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region where the plant resides.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety performance rating of the plant.']
  shift_hours decimal(4,2) [note: 'Length of each production shift in hours.']
  site_area_sqm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total built‑up area of the plant in square meters.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the plant location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the plant.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the plant location.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the plant record.']
  waste_management_type varchar [note: 'Primary waste management approach employed at the plant.']
  water_usage_cubic_m decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total water consumption of the plant per reporting period.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for plant. Referenced by plant_id.'
}

Table logistics.shipment {
  shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment record. Primary key.']
  carrier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier_contract. Business justification: Associate each shipment with the carrier contract that governs rates and terms.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Link shipment to carrier for authoritative carrier data; carrier_scac_code and carrier_name become redundant.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Shipments incur costs that must be charged to a cost center. Adding cost_center_id (FK → finance.cost_center.cost_center_id) provides the necessary financial linkage.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Shipment Creation Audit requires recording which employee created the shipment record for compliance and traceability.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Required for outbound shipping confirmation and customer billing report linking each shipment to the billed customer.']
  engineering_revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.revision. Business justification: Shipment Traceability Report requires linking each shipment to the engineering revision of the component shipped for compliance and recall tracking.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Shipment execution is driven by a sales order; linking enables order fulfillment tracking and status reporting.']
  origin_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.node. Business justification: Replace raw origin address fields with a foreign key to node, enabling reuse of node master data.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability reporting per shipment requires linking each shipment to its profit_center for margin analysis.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Project Delivery Tracking report requires each shipment to be assigned to its project for status and cost aggregation.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Required for shipment manifest and product traceability; enables recall, compliance, and cost allocation reports linking each shipment to the shipped SKU.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: INBOUND_SHIPMENT_REPORT requires linking each shipment to its originating supplier for performance tracking and inbound logistics KPI reporting.']
  supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: Shipment creation is driven by an approved supply plan; linking enables the \'Supply Plan to Shipment\' execution traceability report.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the shipment was delivered to the destination location.']
  actual_pickup_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the carrier picked up the shipment from the origin location.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number serving as the legal document and receipt for the shipment. Issued by the carrier.']
  commercial_invoice_number varchar [note: 'Invoice number associated with the shipment for customs and billing purposes.']
  consolidation_group_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the consolidation group used in load planning. Multiple shipments with the same consolidation group may be combined into a single load.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was first created in the system.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the customs declaration document required for cross-border shipments. Used for import/export compliance.']
  destination_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address for the destination location.']
  destination_city varchar [note: 'City name of the destination location.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the destination location (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN).']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the destination location (customer site, distribution center, plant) where the shipment is delivered.']
  destination_location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the destination location.']
  destination_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the destination location.']
  destination_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the destination location.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is inbound (supplier to plant) or outbound (plant to customer or distribution center).']
  freight_class varchar [note: 'Freight class assigned to the shipment based on density, stowability, handling, and liability. Used for LTL pricing. Valid classes range from 50 to 500.']
  freight_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total freight cost for the shipment in the specified currency. Includes base transportation charges but may exclude accessorial fees.']
  freight_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the freight cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'UN hazard class for hazardous materials in the shipment (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquids, Class 8 Corrosives). Populated only if hazmat_flag is true.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, documentation, and compliance with safety regulations.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'INCOTERMS code defining the responsibilities, costs, and risks between buyer and seller for international shipments (e.g., EXW, FOB, CIF, DDP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  insurance_value_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Declared value of the shipment for insurance purposes. Used to determine coverage limits and premiums.']
  insurance_value_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the insurance value amount.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipment record was last updated in the system.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Progressive number assigned by the carrier to track the shipment. Used for LTL and FTL shipments.']
  scheduled_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date when the shipment is scheduled to be delivered to the destination location.']
  scheduled_pickup_date date [note: 'Planned date when the carrier is scheduled to pick up the shipment from the origin location.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Contracted service level for the shipment indicating speed and priority: standard, expedited, priority, or same-day delivery.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the shipment, used across systems and by carriers. Typically follows format SHP followed by 10 digits.']
  shipment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipment: planned (not yet tendered), tendered (assigned to carrier), in-transit (en route), delivered (completed), exception (issue occurred), cancelled (voided).']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment requires temperature-controlled transportation (refrigerated or heated).']
  temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments. Populated only if temperature_controlled_flag is true.']
  temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments. Populated only if temperature_controlled_flag is true.']
  tms_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned by the Transportation Management System for internal tracking and integration.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total volume of the shipment in cubic meters, used for capacity planning and freight calculation.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment in kilograms, including packaging and pallets.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for the shipment: LTL (Less Than Truckload), FTL (Full Truckload), parcel, rail, air, or ocean.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number identifying the hazardous substance in the shipment. Populated only if hazmat_flag is true.']

  Note: 'Core transactional entity representing an individual shipment of goods — inbound (supplier to plant) or outbound (plant to customer/DC). Tracks shipment number, direction (inbound/outbound), mode (LTL/FTL/parcel/rail/air/ocean), origin and destination locations, scheduled and actual departure/arrival dates, total weight, total volume, freight class, hazmat flag, shipment status (planned/tendered/in-transit/delivered/exception), TMS reference number, carrier SCAC code, PRO number, BOL number, service level (standard/expedited/priority), and consolidation group for load planning. Includes embedded multi-leg routing structure: leg sequence, leg origin/destination nodes, per-leg carrier assignment, per-leg transport mode, intermodal transfer points, leg distances (km/miles), per-leg scheduled/actual timestamps, leg status, per-leg freight costs, and cross-dock/transshipment/relay indicators. Serves as the central transactional entity and SSOT for all transport execution within the logistics domain, linking freight orders, tracking events, delivery documents, customs declarations, and carrier contracts.'
}

Table logistics.shipment_leg {
  shipment_leg_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment leg. Primary key for this entity representing a single transport segment within a multi-leg shipment journey.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier assigned to execute this leg. References the logistics service provider responsible for moving goods.']
  destination_location_logistics_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the arrival location for this leg. May reference a warehouse, plant, distribution center, customer site, cross-dock facility, or transshipment hub.']
  destination_location_node_id varchar [note: 'FK to logistics.logistics_node']
  operator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Leg Handling Accountability records the employee operating the vehicle or equipment for each leg, required for safety incident investigations.']
  origin_location_logistics_node_id varchar [note: 'FK to logistics.logistics_node']
  primary_shipment_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the departure location for this leg. May reference a warehouse, plant, distribution center, cross-dock facility, or transshipment hub.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent shipment that this leg belongs to. Links this transport segment to the overall shipment journey.']
  tertiary_shipment_transfer_point_location_node_id varchar [note: 'FK to logistics.logistics_node']
  accessorial_charges_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of additional charges beyond base freight cost, such as liftgate service, inside delivery, residential delivery, detention, or special handling fees.']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the carrier arrived at the destination location. Captured from TMS or carrier proof-of-delivery systems.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the carrier departed from the origin location. Captured from Transportation Management System (TMS) or carrier tracking systems.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number issued by the carrier for this leg. Legal document serving as receipt of goods and contract of carriage.']
  carrier_service_level varchar [note: 'Service tier or speed class provided by the carrier for this leg. Defines delivery speed and priority.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shipment leg record was first created in the Transportation Management System (TMS). Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  customs_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of customs clearance process for this leg. Tracks progression through import/export documentation and inspection.']
  customs_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this leg crosses international borders and requires customs clearance documentation.']
  delay_duration_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total duration of delay in hours, calculated as the difference between scheduled and actual arrival timestamps when a delay occurs.']
  delay_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary cause of delay if the leg did not meet scheduled arrival time. Supports root cause analysis and carrier performance evaluation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weather|traffic|mechanical|customs|loading|driver|accident|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  equipment_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific equipment unit (truck, trailer, container, railcar) used for this leg. Enables asset tracking and utilization analysis.']
  fuel_surcharge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional fuel surcharge applied by the carrier for this leg. Typically calculated as a percentage of base freight cost and varies with fuel prices.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this leg transports hazardous materials requiring special handling, documentation, and regulatory compliance.']
  is_cross_dock boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this leg involves a cross-docking operation where goods are transferred directly from inbound to outbound transport without warehousing.']
  is_transshipment boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this leg involves transshipment where goods are transferred between different carriers or transport modes at an intermediate hub.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this shipment leg record was most recently modified. Tracks changes to status, timestamps, or other leg attributes.']
  leg_distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total distance traveled for this leg measured in kilometers. Used for route optimization, cost calculation, and carbon footprint analysis.']
  leg_distance_miles decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total distance traveled for this leg measured in miles. Provided for regions using imperial measurement systems.']
  leg_freight_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total freight cost charged by the carrier for this specific leg. Supports leg-level cost allocation and profitability analysis.']
  leg_freight_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the leg freight cost (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  leg_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this leg within the parent shipment journey. Indicates the position in the multi-leg routing plan (1 for first leg, 2 for second, etc.).']
  leg_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this transport leg in its lifecycle. Tracks progression from planning through execution to completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|scheduled|in_transit|arrived|completed|cancelled|delayed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Classification of shipment load configuration. FTL (Full Truckload) indicates dedicated vehicle, LTL (Less Than Truckload) indicates shared capacity with other shipments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional operational notes, special instructions, or exceptions related to this leg execution.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Progressive number assigned by Less Than Truckload (LTL) carriers to track shipments. Industry-standard identifier for LTL freight.']
  route_optimization_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated score representing the efficiency of this leg routing based on distance, cost, transit time, and service level. Used for continuous improvement analysis.']
  scheduled_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the carrier is scheduled to arrive at the destination location. Used for receiving planning and SLA commitments.']
  scheduled_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the carrier is scheduled to depart from the origin location. Used for planning and Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number applied to the container or trailer for this leg. Used for tamper detection and customs compliance.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this leg requires temperature-controlled transport (refrigerated or heated) to maintain product integrity.']
  temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments on this leg. Used for cold chain compliance monitoring.']
  temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments on this leg. Used for cold chain compliance monitoring.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for real-time shipment visibility and proof-of-delivery. Used for customer self-service tracking portals.']
  transit_time_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned or actual duration of this leg measured in hours. Calculated as the difference between departure and arrival timestamps.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation used for this leg. Supports intermodal routing scenarios where different legs use different transport methods.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Type of vehicle or equipment used to transport goods on this leg. Provides granular detail within the transport mode.']

  Note: 'Represents an individual transport leg within a multi-leg shipment, capturing leg sequence, origin and destination nodes, carrier assignment, mode of transport, scheduled and actual departure/arrival timestamps, leg distance (km/miles), leg freight cost, leg status, and intermodal transfer point details. Supports complex routing scenarios including cross-docking, transshipment, and relay trucking.'
}

Table logistics.freight_order {
  freight_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the freight order. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or freight forwarder assigned to execute this freight order.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Freight Order Creation Audit records which employee originated the order, supporting cost allocation and compliance reporting.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Allows order management to associate each freight order with the originating customer for tracking and cost allocation.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Freight order transports goods for a specific sales order; linking allows freight cost allocation to that order.']
  primary_freight_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility, warehouse, or site where goods will be picked up by the carrier.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Freight Order Creation process for a manufacturing project needs a FK to the project to allocate freight costs to the project budget.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Freight order is created to transport goods for a specific PO; needed for cost allocation and PO fulfillment tracking.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Freight order processing must ensure order‑level regulatory reporting (e.g., export controls), so each order references the governing regulatory requirement.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment(s) associated with this freight order. Links the freight order to the physical shipment being transported.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Needed for freight cost allocation to specific SKU; supports profitability analysis and freight‑to‑product cost reporting.']
  transport_route_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the planned transportation route for this freight order. Links to route optimization and network planning data.']
  accessorial_charges_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total amount of accessorial charges applied to this freight order. Accessorials include additional services such as liftgate, inside delivery, residential delivery, detention, fuel surcharge, and other non-standard services.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the carrier delivered the goods to the destination. Null until delivery is completed. Used for on-time performance measurement and SLA compliance tracking.']
  actual_pickup_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the carrier picked up the goods. Null until pickup is completed. Used for on-time performance measurement and SLA compliance tracking.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the Bill of Lading document associated with this freight order. The BOL serves as a receipt, contract of carriage, and document of title for the goods being transported.']
  carrier_acceptance_status varchar [note: 'Status of the carrier\'s response to the freight order tender. Pending indicates awaiting response, Accepted means carrier confirmed, Rejected means carrier declined, Expired means tender window closed without response.']
  carrier_acceptance_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the carrier accepted or rejected the freight order tender.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person or system that created this freight order record. Audit field for accountability and compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this freight order record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance tracking.']
  customs_required_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this freight order requires customs clearance documentation for cross-border shipment.']
  delivery_window_end timestamp [note: 'Latest date and time when the carrier must deliver the goods. Defines the end of the acceptable delivery time window and may be tied to Service Level Agreement (SLA) commitments.']
  delivery_window_start timestamp [note: 'Earliest date and time when the carrier may deliver the goods. Defines the beginning of the acceptable delivery time window.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of transportation equipment required for this freight order. Dry Van for standard enclosed trailer, Refrigerated for temperature-controlled, Flatbed for oversized or open cargo, Tanker for liquid bulk, Container for intermodal shipping, Box Truck for local delivery.']
  freight_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the freight order, typically generated by the Transportation Management System (TMS) or SAP TM module. Used for external communication with carriers and internal tracking.']
  freight_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the freight order. Draft indicates order is being prepared, Tendered means sent to carrier, Accepted means carrier confirmed, Rejected means carrier declined, In Transit means shipment is moving, Delivered means goods received, Cancelled means order voided, Closed means order completed and invoiced. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Draft|Tendered|Accepted|Rejected|In Transit|Delivered|Cancelled|Closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  freight_rate_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed base freight rate amount for transporting the goods, excluding accessorial charges and taxes. Represents the core transportation cost negotiated with the carrier.']
  freight_rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the freight rate amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this freight order contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, documentation, and compliance with transportation safety regulations.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter Incoterms code defining the responsibilities, costs, and risks between buyer and seller for international shipments. Examples: EXW (Ex Works), FOB (Free On Board), CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person or system that last modified this freight order record. Audit field for change accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this freight order record was last modified. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  package_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual packages or handling units included in this freight order.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets included in this freight order. Used for handling unit tracking and warehouse space planning.']
  pickup_window_end timestamp [note: 'Latest date and time when the carrier must pick up the goods. Defines the end of the acceptable pickup time window.']
  pickup_window_start timestamp [note: 'Earliest date and time when the carrier may pick up the goods. Defines the beginning of the acceptable pickup time window.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for this freight order. Standard for normal service, Expedited for faster-than-normal, Rush for urgent same-day or next-day, Critical for emergency shipments requiring immediate attention.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned Progressive Rotating Order number used to track the shipment through the carrier\'s network. Commonly used in LTL freight for tracking and proof of delivery.']
  sap_tm_freight_order_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from SAP Transportation Management (TM) module linking this freight order to the source system record. Used for traceability and reconciliation with the operational TMS.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of freight service contracted for this order. LTL (Less Than Truckload) for partial loads, FTL (Full Truckload) for dedicated truck capacity, Intermodal for multi-mode transport, Parcel for small package, Air Freight for expedited air transport, Ocean Freight for maritime shipping.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field containing special handling instructions, delivery requirements, or other important notes for the carrier. May include temperature requirements, fragile handling, appointment requirements, or site-specific access instructions.']
  temperature_controlled_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this freight order requires temperature-controlled transportation (refrigerated or heated).']
