  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments. Null if temperature control is not required.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments. Null if temperature control is not required.']
  tender_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the freight order was tendered to the carrier. Spot indicates one-time market rate, Contract indicates pre-negotiated agreement rate, Auction indicates competitive bidding process, Direct Award indicates sole-source assignment.']
  tender_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the freight order was tendered to the carrier. Represents the business event when the carrier was formally requested to provide transportation service.']
  total_freight_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total cost of the freight order including base freight rate, accessorial charges, and any applicable surcharges. Represents the complete contracted cost for this transportation service.']
  volume_m3 decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total volume of the freight shipment in cubic meters. Used for dimensional weight calculation and capacity planning.']
  weight_kg decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total weight of the freight shipment in kilograms. Used for rate calculation, capacity planning, and compliance with weight restrictions.']

  Note: 'Operational freight order issued to a carrier or freight forwarder, representing the contractual instruction to move goods. Captures freight order number, linked shipment(s), carrier SCAC, service type (LTL/FTL/intermodal), pickup and delivery windows, agreed freight rate, accessorial charges, special instructions, tender method (spot/contract/auction), tender timestamp, carrier acceptance status, and SAP TM freight order reference.'
}

Table logistics.delivery_note {
  delivery_note_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delivery note. Primary key for this entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Transportation carrier or logistics service provider handling the shipment.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Delivery Note must record the specific component being issued/received to reconcile inventory and support quality traceability.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Ship-to party for outbound deliveries. The customer receiving the goods.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating sales order for outbound deliveries. Links delivery execution to customer order.']
  plant_data_id varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or distribution center handling the delivery. For inbound: receiving plant. For outbound: shipping plant.']
  primary_delivery_ship_to_address_id varchar [note: 'Destination address for outbound deliveries. Links to customer site or delivery location.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating purchase order for inbound deliveries. Links goods receipt to procurement.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the consolidated shipment if this delivery is part of a multi-delivery shipment.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Links delivery note to the delivered SKU for inventory reconciliation, warranty activation, and after‑sales service tracking.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Specific storage location within the plant for goods receipt or goods issue.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Vendor or supplier for inbound deliveries. The party shipping goods to the organization.']
  supply_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.allocation. Business justification: Delivery notes are generated from supply allocation records; the link supports the Allocation‑to‑Delivery Note audit.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Warehouse facility executing the pick, pack, and ship operations for this delivery.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date when goods were delivered to the destination or received at the plant.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of lading number issued by the carrier as a receipt and contract for transportation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery note record was first created in the system.']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Customs declaration or entry number for international shipments requiring customs clearance.']
  delivery_direction varchar [note: 'Discriminator indicating whether this is an inbound delivery (goods receipt from supplier) or outbound delivery (shipment to customer).']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the delivery note. Used for tracking and reference across systems and with external parties.']
  delivery_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the delivery for warehouse and transportation planning.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery note in the warehouse and logistics execution workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|planned|picking|packed|shipped|in_transit|delivered|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control classification for goods subject to export regulations and licensing requirements.']
  freight_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total freight or transportation cost for the delivery.']
  freight_cost_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for freight cost amount. ISO 4217 three-letter currency code.']
  goods_issue_date date [note: 'Date when goods were physically issued from inventory for outbound deliveries. Triggers inventory reduction.']
  goods_issue_status varchar [note: 'Status of goods issue posting for outbound deliveries. Indicates whether inventory has been reduced.']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'Date when goods were physically received into inventory for inbound deliveries. Triggers inventory increase.']
  goods_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Status of goods receipt posting for inbound deliveries. Indicates whether inventory has been increased.']
  harmonized_tariff_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized System code for customs classification and duty calculation.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms code defining the responsibilities and risks between buyer and seller for transportation and delivery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'Named place or port specified in the Incoterms agreement where risk transfers between parties.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery note record was last updated.']
  loading_date date [note: 'Date when goods were loaded onto the carrier vehicle for transportation.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP material document number generated upon goods issue or goods receipt posting. Links delivery to inventory transaction.']
  number_of_packages varchar [note: 'Total count of packages, cartons, or handling units in the delivery.']
  packing_date date [note: 'Date when goods were packed and prepared for shipment.']
  packing_status varchar [note: 'Status of packing operations indicating readiness for shipment.']
  picking_date date [note: 'Date when warehouse picking operations were completed for outbound deliveries.']
  picking_status varchar [note: 'Status of warehouse picking operations for outbound deliveries.']
  planned_delivery_date date [note: 'Scheduled or promised delivery date communicated to the customer or expected from the supplier.']
  proof_of_delivery_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether proof of delivery documentation has been received and confirmed.']
  route_code varchar [note: 'Predefined route identifier used for transportation planning and optimization.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for the delivery.']
  shipping_service_level varchar [note: 'Service level or speed of delivery selected for the shipment.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions for special handling requirements such as fragile, hazardous, temperature-controlled, or security requirements.']
  total_gross_weight_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of all goods including packaging, measured in kilograms.']
  total_net_weight_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total net weight of all goods in the delivery, measured in kilograms. Excludes packaging weight.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total volume of all goods in the delivery, measured in cubic meters.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for shipment visibility and proof of delivery.']

  Note: 'Delivery document serving as the SSOT for all inbound and outbound physical goods movement authorization and tracking within the logistics domain. For outbound: delivery note number, sales order reference, ship-to party, delivery date, picking status, goods issue status, packing list details, incoterms, and export control classification. For inbound: inbound delivery number, purchase order reference, supplier ID, plant/storage location destination, expected and actual goods receipt dates, quantity ordered vs. received, over/under delivery tolerance, GR status, and material document number. Captures direction discriminator (inbound/outbound), total net/gross weight, packing contents, and MRP integration for inventory replenishment. Serves as the primary document for warehouse pick/pack/ship execution (outbound) and goods receipt processing (inbound). No other entity in this domain models delivery documents.'
}

Table logistics.inbound_delivery {
  inbound_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inbound delivery document. Primary key for this entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the logistics carrier or freight forwarder responsible for transporting the inbound delivery.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the MRP controller responsible for planning and monitoring this inbound delivery.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Inbound delivery process creates an inspection lot; linking enables the Inbound Delivery Inspection Summary to pull lot results.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility receiving the inbound delivery.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order that triggered this inbound delivery.']
  replenishment_proposal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.replenishment_proposal. Business justification: Inbound deliveries are the physical receipt of goods proposed in a replenishment proposal; needed for the \'Proposal‑to‑Receipt\' tracking report.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Associates inbound delivery with received SKU; essential for goods receipt, quality inspection, and stock update processes.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific storage location within the plant where materials will be received.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier delivering the materials or components.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date when the inbound delivery physically arrived at the receiving location.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of lading document number issued by the carrier, serving as receipt and contract for transportation.']
  blocked_stock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received goods are placed in blocked stock pending quality inspection or other clearance.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the materials were manufactured or produced.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inbound delivery document was first created in the system.']
  customs_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Status of customs clearance for international inbound deliveries.']
  customs_entry_number varchar [note: 'Customs entry or declaration number for imported materials, required for regulatory compliance.']
  delivery_complete_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inbound delivery is considered complete and no further receipts are expected for this document.']
  delivery_note_text varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this inbound delivery, capturing special instructions or observations.']
  delivery_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this inbound delivery for scheduling and resource allocation.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inbound delivery document in the procurement and receiving workflow.']
  delivery_variance_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Difference between quantity ordered and quantity received (positive for over-delivery, negative for under-delivery).']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date when the inbound delivery is expected to arrive at the receiving plant.']
  freight_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total freight or transportation cost associated with this inbound delivery.']
  freight_cost_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the freight cost amount.']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'Date when the goods receipt transaction was posted in the system, confirming inventory receipt.']
  goods_receipt_posted_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the warehouse operator who posted the goods receipt transaction.']
  goods_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether goods have been physically received and posted to inventory.']
  inbound_delivery_number varchar [note: 'Business document number for the inbound delivery as generated by SAP MM. Externally visible identifier used for tracking and reference.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'International commercial term defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for delivery, risk, and cost (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether quality inspection is required before the goods can be posted to unrestricted inventory.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inbound delivery document was last updated or modified.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP material document number generated upon goods receipt posting, linking the physical receipt to inventory movement.']
  over_delivery_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Allowable percentage by which the received quantity may exceed the ordered quantity without triggering an exception.']
  packing_slip_number varchar [note: 'Packing slip or delivery note number provided by the supplier, listing the contents of the shipment.']
  quantity_ordered decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of material or components ordered as per the purchase order.']
  quantity_received decimal(18,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of material or components received and posted to inventory via goods receipt.']
  receiving_dock varchar [note: 'Specific dock or receiving bay at the plant where the inbound delivery was unloaded.']
  shipment_number varchar [note: 'Tracking number or shipment identifier provided by the carrier for this inbound delivery.']
  shipping_point varchar [note: 'Location or facility from which the supplier shipped the materials.']
  under_delivery_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Allowable percentage by which the received quantity may fall short of the ordered quantity without triggering an exception.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantities ordered and received (e.g., EA, KG, M, L).']

  Note: 'Inbound delivery document (SAP MM) tracking the expected arrival of purchased materials or components from suppliers. Captures inbound delivery number, purchase order reference, supplier ID, plant and storage location destination, expected delivery date, actual goods receipt date, material document number, quantity ordered vs. received, over/under delivery tolerance, and goods receipt status. Feeds MRP and inventory replenishment processes.'
}

Table logistics.bill_of_lading {
  bill_of_lading_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bill of lading record. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier organization responsible for transporting the cargo.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: B/L issuance audit needs the employee identifier who generated the bill of lading for legal and customs compliance.']
  primary_bill_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party (supplier or internal plant) sending the cargo.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent shipment record that this BOL documents.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date when the cargo was delivered to the consignee and signed for.']
  bill_of_lading_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the BOL document and associated shipment.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique document number issued by the carrier for this shipment. Required for customs and proof of delivery.']
  bol_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the BOL document type indicating transferability and ownership terms.']
  commodity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the goods being shipped, including product names, materials, and characteristics.']
  consignee_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the consignee destination location.']
  consignee_city varchar [note: 'City where the consignee destination is located.']
  consignee_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the consignee destination.']
  consignee_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the consignee party receiving the cargo.']
  consignee_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the consignee destination.']
  consignee_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the consignee destination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this BOL record was first created in the system.']
  declared_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the cargo declared by the shipper for liability and insurance purposes.']
  declared_value_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the declared value amount.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned or estimated date when the cargo is expected to be delivered to the consignee.']
  freight_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total freight transportation charges for this shipment.']
  freight_charge_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the freight charge amount.']
  freight_class varchar [note: 'NMFC freight class code (50-500) used to determine LTL shipping rates based on density, handling, stowability, and liability.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms indicating who is responsible for freight charges (prepaid by shipper, collect from consignee, or third party).']
  handling_unit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of handling units (pallets, crates, boxes, drums) in the shipment.']
  handling_unit_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging or handling unit used for the cargo.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and documentation.']
  hazmat_un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number identifying the hazardous material classification (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline).']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the bill of lading was issued by the carrier acknowledging receipt of cargo.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the BOL was issued by the carrier. Principal business event timestamp.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this BOL record was last modified in the system.']
  pickup_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date when the carrier picked up the cargo from the shipper.']
  proof_of_delivery_signature varchar [note: 'Name of the person who signed for receipt of the cargo at the destination.']
  shipper_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the shipper location.']
  shipper_city varchar [note: 'City where the shipper location is situated.']
  shipper_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the shipper location.']
  shipper_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the shipper party originating the cargo.']
  shipper_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the shipper location.']
  shipper_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the shipper location.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for special handling, loading, or delivery instructions for the carrier.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment requires temperature-controlled transportation (refrigerated or heated).']
  total_weight decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment including packaging.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation used for this shipment (Less Than Truckload, Full Truckload, etc.).']
  weight_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the total weight (pounds, kilograms, tons, metric tons).']

  Note: 'Legal transport document (BOL) issued by a carrier acknowledging receipt of cargo for shipment. Stores BOL number, issue date, shipper and consignee details, carrier name and SCAC, origin and destination addresses, commodity description, freight class, declared value, number of handling units, total weight, special handling instructions (hazmat UN number, temperature requirements), terms of carriage, and signature/seal information. Required for customs and proof of delivery.'
}

Table logistics.carrier {
  carrier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the transportation carrier. Primary key for the carrier master record.']
  api_endpoint_url varchar [note: 'Base URL for the carriers API integration for real-time rate quotes, shipment booking, and tracking. Used for TMS connectivity.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the transportation carrier or freight forwarder as registered with regulatory authorities.']
  carrier_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the carrier in the approved carrier network. Controls whether carrier can be selected for new shipments.']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the carrier based on primary mode of transportation. Determines applicable regulations and service capabilities.']
  claims_ratio decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of cargo claims to total shipments handled by the carrier. Used for carrier quality assessment and risk evaluation.']
  contract_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the current carrier contract becomes effective. Used for rate validation and contract compliance tracking.']
  contract_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current carrier contract expires. Triggers contract renewal workflow and rate renegotiation.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Contractual relationship status with the carrier. Determines pricing structure and service level commitments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carrier record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for carrier invoicing and payment. Determines currency for freight charges and accessorial fees.']
  customs_broker_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier provides customs brokerage services for international shipments. True if customs brokerage available.']
  dot_number varchar [note: 'US Department of Transportation identification number assigned to commercial motor carriers. Required for interstate commerce in the United States.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit unique identifier for the carrier business entity assigned by Dun & Bradstreet. Used for credit assessment and supplier management.']
  edi_capability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier supports EDI transactions for shipment tendering, status updates, and invoicing. True if EDI-enabled.']
  edi_version varchar [note: 'EDI standard version supported by the carrier (e.g., ANSI X12 4010, EDIFACT D96A). Required for EDI integration setup.']
  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier is certified to transport hazardous materials per DOT regulations. True if HAZMAT-certified.']
  headquarters_address varchar [note: 'Physical street address of the carriers corporate headquarters. Used for legal correspondence and contract administration.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City where the carriers corporate headquarters is located.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the carriers headquarters location. Used for regulatory compliance and tax jurisdiction.']
  headquarters_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the carriers headquarters address.']
  headquarters_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the carriers corporate headquarters is located. Two-letter code preferred.']
  iata_code varchar [note: 'IATA numeric airline code for air cargo carriers. Used for air waybill processing and cargo tracking.']
  icao_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter airline designator assigned by ICAO for air carriers. Used in flight operations and air traffic control.']
  insurance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the carriers liability insurance certificate. Required for carrier qualification and risk management.']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total liability coverage amount in USD provided by the carriers insurance policy. Must meet minimum requirements for cargo value.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the carriers current liability insurance coverage. Carrier cannot be used for shipments after this date without updated certificate.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carrier record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  mc_number varchar [note: 'Motor Carrier operating authority number issued by FMCSA for carriers transporting regulated commodities for hire in interstate commerce.']
  on_time_delivery_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of shipments delivered within the committed delivery window. Key performance indicator for carrier performance management.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated with the carrier. Defines invoice payment due date relative to shipment delivery or invoice date.']
  preferred_lanes varchar [note: 'Geographic lanes or routes where the carrier has preferred capacity, competitive rates, or specialized expertise. Comma-separated origin-destination pairs.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary carrier contact for shipment coordination, claims, and operational communication.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the carrier for operational coordination and issue resolution.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary carrier contact for urgent shipment issues and operational escalation.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'FMCSA safety fitness rating based on compliance review and safety performance history. Impacts carrier selection and risk assessment.']
  safety_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite safety performance score based on accident history, violations, and compliance metrics. Scale 0-100, higher is better.']
  scac_code varchar [note: 'Unique two-to-four letter code used to identify transportation companies. Required for EDI transactions and customs documentation in North America.']
  service_coverage_area varchar [note: 'Geographic regions or countries where the carrier provides service. Used for carrier selection based on shipment origin and destination.']
  service_mode varchar [note: 'Primary service mode offered by the carrier. LTL (Less Than Truckload), FTL (Full Truckload), or other specialized services.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number for the carrier. Used for tax reporting and compliance. Format varies by jurisdiction.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier provides temperature-controlled (refrigerated or heated) transportation services. True if capable.']
  tms_integration_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the carriers integration with the enterprise TMS. Determines availability for automated shipment tendering and tracking.']
  tracking_url_template varchar [note: 'URL template for shipment tracking on the carriers website. Placeholder tokens replaced with tracking number for customer self-service.']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'Internal vendor identifier for the carrier in the ERP system. Used for purchase order creation and invoice matching.']

  Note: 'Master record for approved transportation carriers (trucking companies, freight forwarders, rail operators, ocean carriers, air cargo carriers, parcel couriers). Captures carrier SCAC code, carrier name, carrier type, DOT/MC number, IATA/ICAO code, insurance certificate expiry, safety rating, contract status, preferred lanes, payment terms, EDI capability flag, and TMS integration status. Serves as the SSOT for carrier identity within the logistics domain.'
}

Table logistics.carrier_contract {
  carrier_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the carrier contract. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier party with whom this contract is established.']
  contract_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Contract Ownership Tracking links the employee responsible for negotiating and managing the carrier contract, essential for governance and audit.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Carrier contracts generate revenue/expense tracked against a profit center for performance measurement.']
  accessorial_charges_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether accessorial charges (liftgate, inside delivery, residential) are included in base rates or charged separately.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the contract received internal approval from Manufacturing management.']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the Manufacturing executive or manager who approved this contract.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews upon expiry if not terminated.']
  base_rate_type varchar [note: 'Primary rate basis used in this contract for freight charges. CWT (hundredweight).']
  carrier_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the carrier contact.']
  carrier_contact_name varchar [note: 'Primary contact name at the carrier organization for this contract.']
  carrier_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the carrier contact.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the signed contract document stored in the document management system.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the carrier contract, used in communications with carriers and internal references.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the carrier contract. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|expired|terminated|renewed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the carrier contract based on its structure and commitment level.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this contract.']
  damage_claim_liability_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum liability amount per shipment that the carrier accepts for damage or loss claims.']
  detention_charge_per_hour decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hourly detention charge applied after free time expires.']
  detention_free_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Number of minutes allowed for loading/unloading before detention charges apply.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the contract terms become binding and rates become applicable.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the contract terms cease to be binding. Nullable for open-ended contracts.']
  fuel_index_source varchar [note: 'External fuel price index used as the basis for fuel surcharge calculation, e.g., DOE National Diesel Average.']
  fuel_surcharge_method varchar [note: 'Method by which fuel surcharges are calculated and applied to shipments under this contract.']
  geographic_coverage varchar [note: 'Geographic scope of the contract, e.g., North America, USA domestic, specific state/province list.']
  insurance_coverage_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier is required to maintain specific cargo insurance coverage under this contract.']
  insurance_minimum_coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum cargo insurance coverage amount required from the carrier.']
  last_modified_by_name varchar [note: 'Name of the user who last modified this carrier contract record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier contract record was last updated.']
  minimum_volume_commitment decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum shipment volume (in commitment unit) that Manufacturing commits to tender to the carrier during the contract period.']
  negotiation_date date [note: 'Date when the contract terms were finalized and agreed upon by both parties.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional contract terms, special conditions, or operational instructions.']
  on_time_delivery_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractual target percentage for on-time delivery performance, used for SLA compliance measurement.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms agreed with the carrier, e.g., net_30, net_45, net_60.']
  penalty_clause_description varchar [note: 'Description of penalties applicable for service failures, late deliveries, or volume shortfalls.']
  rate_adjustment_trigger varchar [note: 'Conditions or events that trigger a rate adjustment, e.g., fuel price change exceeding 10%, volume variance exceeding 20%.']
  rate_review_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which contract rates are reviewed and potentially adjusted.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required to prevent auto-renewal or to initiate renewal negotiation.']
  service_level_standard varchar [note: 'Guaranteed service level commitment, e.g., next-day delivery, 2-day delivery, standard ground.']
  service_mode varchar [note: 'Primary transportation mode covered by this contract. LTL (Less Than Truckload), FTL (Full Truckload).']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the contract.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for contract termination if status is terminated, e.g., poor performance, cost reduction, carrier exit.']
  volume_commitment_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum volume commitment.']

  Note: 'Negotiated rate and service agreement between Manufacturing and a carrier, serving as the single source of truth (SSOT) for ALL carrier pricing within the logistics domain — including contract rates, spot rates, and benchmark rates. Captures contract-level terms (effective/expiry dates, volume commitments, service level guarantees, penalty clauses, payment terms) and granular lane-level rate records (per cwt, per mile, per shipment, per pallet by origin-destination zone/postal code range, freight class, weight break, and service level). Includes fuel surcharge schedules, accessorial charge tables, rate basis (weight break/flat/distance), rate currency, rate effective date ranges, rate source classification (contract/spot/benchmark), minimum volume thresholds, and rate validity periods. Used by TMS for automated carrier selection, freight cost calculation, spot rate benchmarking, and freight invoice audit. No other entity in this domain stores rate data.'
}

Table logistics.freight_rate {
  freight_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the freight rate record. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier offering this rate.']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation lane (origin-destination pair) for which this rate applies.']
  accessorial_charges_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether additional accessorial charges (liftgate, inside delivery, residential delivery, etc.) may apply to shipments using this rate.']
  base_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Base freight rate amount per unit as defined by rate_basis, before surcharges and adjustments.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the carrier contract under which this rate is negotiated, if applicable.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the rate is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  destination_postal_code_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending postal code of the destination range for which this rate applies.']
  destination_postal_code_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting postal code of the destination range for which this rate applies.']
  destination_zone_code varchar [note: 'Geographic zone or postal code range identifier for the shipment destination covered by this rate.']
  distance_max_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum distance in kilometers for which this rate applies, used in distance-based pricing.']
  distance_min_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum distance in kilometers for which this rate applies, used in distance-based pricing.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this freight rate expires and is no longer valid for new shipments. Null indicates open-ended validity.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this freight rate becomes effective and can be used for shipment cost calculation.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of transportation equipment required for this rate (e.g., dry van, refrigerated, flatbed, container size).']
  freight_class varchar [note: 'National Motor Freight Classification (NMFC) freight class code (e.g., 50, 55, 60, 65, 70, 77.5, 85, 92.5, 100, 110, 125, 150, 175, 200, 250, 300, 400, 500) determining rate based on density, handling, stowability, and liability.']
  fuel_surcharge_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fuel surcharge percentage applied to the base rate to account for fuel cost fluctuations.']
  hazmat_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate is applicable to shipments containing hazardous materials.']
  maximum_charge decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum charge cap for a shipment under this rate, if applicable.']
  minimum_charge decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum charge amount for a shipment under this rate, regardless of weight or distance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding special conditions, restrictions, or clarifications for this freight rate.']
  origin_postal_code_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending postal code of the origin range for which this rate applies.']
  origin_postal_code_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting postal code of the origin range for which this rate applies.']
  origin_zone_code varchar [note: 'Geographic zone or postal code range identifier for the shipment origin covered by this rate.']
  rate_basis varchar [note: 'Basis on which the rate is calculated: per hundredweight (cwt), per mile, per shipment, per pallet, per unit, or flat rate.']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code for this freight rate, used for external reference and carrier communication.']
  rate_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this freight rate record was first created in the system.']
  rate_last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this freight rate record was last modified in the system.']
  rate_published_date date [note: 'Date on which this rate was published or made available by the carrier.']
  rate_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or Transportation Management System (TMS) from which this rate was loaded or synchronized.']
  rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the freight rate record.']
  rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate source: contract (negotiated agreement), spot (market rate), benchmark (industry standard), or tariff (published rate).']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Service level or speed tier offered by the carrier for this rate (standard, expedited, express, economy).']
  transit_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected transit time in days for shipments using this rate and service level.']
  transportation_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation for this rate: Less Than Truckload (LTL), Full Truckload (FTL), parcel, air freight, ocean freight, rail, or intermodal. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LTL|FTL|parcel|air|ocean|rail|intermodal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  volume_commitment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this rate requires a minimum volume commitment from the shipper.']
  weight_break_max_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum weight in kilograms for this rate tier in a weight-break pricing structure.']
  weight_break_min_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum weight in kilograms for this rate tier in a weight-break pricing structure.']

  Note: 'Granular freight rate record defining the cost per unit (per cwt, per mile, per shipment, per pallet) for a specific lane, carrier, mode, and service level combination. Captures origin and destination zone/postal code ranges, freight class, rate basis (weight break, flat, distance), base rate, fuel surcharge percentage, effective date range, rate source (contract/spot/benchmark), and currency. Used by TMS for automated carrier selection and freight cost calculation.'
}

Table logistics.freight_invoice {
  freight_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the freight invoice record. Primary key.']
  carrier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier contract governing rates, terms, and service levels for this shipment.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier who issued this freight invoice and provided the transportation service.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Freight invoices must be charged to a cost center for internal cost accounting and budgeting.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Needed for generating customer‑facing freight invoices and finance reconciliation linking invoice to the customer account.']
  destination_location_logistics_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipment destination location (customer site, plant, warehouse, or distribution center).']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the freight audit on this invoice.']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the freight order that authorized this shipment. Used for three-way match validation (freight order vs BOL vs invoice).']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Mapping freight expense to a GL account enables proper financial posting and audit trail.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Freight invoice reconciliation with the originating sales order ensures accurate financial matching and audit compliance.']
  primary_freight_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the shipment origin location (supplier site, plant, warehouse, or distribution center).']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Three‑way match and project cost accounting require linking each freight invoice to the originating project.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Freight invoice must be matched to the PO for three‑way match, financial reconciliation, and variance analysis.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment record that this invoice covers. Links invoice to physical goods movement.']
  accessorial_charges decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional charges for special services such as liftgate, inside delivery, residential delivery, detention, storage, or redelivery.']
  approved_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final amount approved for payment after audit and dispute resolution. This is the amount that will be paid to the carrier.']
  audit_completed_date date [note: 'Date the freight audit and payment (FAP) process was completed for this invoice.']
  audited_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount determined to be correct after freight audit and payment (FAP) process validation. May differ from invoiced amount due to rate discrepancies or billing errors.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number associated with this freight invoice. Used for three-way match validation and shipment traceability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this freight invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this invoice (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  customs_declaration_number varchar [note: 'Customs declaration or entry number for international shipments. Required for cross-border freight audit and compliance.']
  customs_duties decimal(15,2) [note: 'Customs duties and import/export fees charged for cross-border shipments. Applicable for international freight.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Date the carrier delivered the shipment to the destination location.']
  disputed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount under dispute due to billing errors, service failures, or contract rate discrepancies. Subject to carrier negotiation and resolution.']
  distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total distance traveled for this shipment in kilometers. Used for mileage-based rate validation and route optimization analysis.']
  freight_class varchar [note: 'National Motor Freight Classification (NMFC) freight class assigned to the shipment. Determines LTL pricing based on density, handling, stowability, and liability.']
  fuel_surcharge decimal(15,2) [note: 'Variable fuel surcharge applied by carrier based on current fuel prices and agreed fuel surcharge schedule.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) defining the division of costs and responsibilities between buyer and seller for international shipments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the freight invoice was issued by the carrier. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-issued invoice number. Externally-known unique identifier for this freight invoice used for payment reconciliation and audit.']
  invoiced_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount invoiced by the carrier, including line haul, fuel surcharge, accessorials, customs duties, and taxes. Gross invoice total.']
  line_haul_charge decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base transportation charge for moving freight from origin to destination, excluding fuel surcharges and accessorial fees.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this freight invoice, including special handling instructions, dispute details, or audit findings.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date payment was issued to the carrier. Null if invoice is not yet paid.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is due to the carrier per agreed payment terms.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to pay the carrier for this freight invoice (wire transfer, ACH, check, credit card, or prepaid account).']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Payment transaction reference number or check number used for payment reconciliation and audit trail.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment lifecycle status of the freight invoice. Tracks progression from receipt through approval, dispute, and payment.']
  pickup_date date [note: 'Date the carrier picked up the shipment from the origin location.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of transportation service provided: Less Than Truckload (LTL), Full Truckload (FTL), parcel, intermodal, air freight, ocean freight, or rail. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: LTL|FTL|parcel|intermodal|air|ocean|rail — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the freight invoice (VAT, GST, sales tax) per applicable tax jurisdiction.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Result of three-way match validation comparing freight order, bill of lading (BOL), and freight invoice. Matched indicates all three documents align; variance or failed indicates discrepancies requiring resolution.']
  transit_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in transit from pickup to delivery. Used for carrier performance evaluation and SLA compliance tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this freight invoice record was last modified in the system.']
  variance_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of discrepancies identified during three-way match or freight audit process (e.g., rate mismatch, weight discrepancy, unauthorized accessorials).']
  volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of the shipment in cubic meters. Used for dimensional weight calculations and capacity planning.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total weight of the shipment in kilograms as recorded on the freight invoice. Used for rate validation and freight class determination.']

  Note: 'Carrier-issued freight invoice submitted for payment of transportation services rendered. Tracks invoice number, carrier ID, invoice date, payment due date, referenced freight orders and BOLs, invoiced charges (line haul, fuel surcharge, accessorials), audited amount, disputed amount, approved amount, payment status, and three-way match result (freight order vs. BOL vs. invoice). Feeds accounts payable in SAP FI and supports freight audit and payment (FAP) processes.'
}

Table logistics.transport_route {
  transport_route_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the transport route. Primary key for the transport route entity.']
  preferred_carrier_id varchar [note: 'FK to logistics.carrier']
  alternate_carrier_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of alternate carrier codes that can be used for this route if the preferred carrier is unavailable or for load balancing.']
  carbon_emission_factor_kg_per_km decimal(10,6) [note: 'Average carbon dioxide equivalent emissions per kilometer for this route, used for sustainability reporting and carbon footprint calculation.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard freight cost.']
  cost_per_km decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average freight cost per kilometer for this route, used for variable cost modeling and route comparison.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transport route record was first created in the system.']
  customs_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customs clearance is required for this route (typically true for cross-border international routes).']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the destination location, used for customs and regulatory compliance.']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the destination node (customer site, distribution center, plant, or port) where the shipment ends. Aligns with internal location master or UN/LOCODE for ports.']
  destination_location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the destination location for reporting and operational visibility.']
  distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total distance of the route in kilometers, used for cost calculation, transit time estimation, and route optimization.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this route configuration becomes effective, used for contract lifecycle management and rate validity.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this route configuration is valid. Null indicates an open-ended route with no expiration.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of transportation equipment required for this route (e.g., dry van, refrigerated, flatbed, tanker, 20ft container, 40ft container).']
  fuel_surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a fuel surcharge is applicable to shipments on this route, based on carrier contract terms.']
  hazmat_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route is approved for transporting hazardous materials, based on carrier certification and regulatory compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transport route record was last updated, used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this route was last reviewed for performance, cost, and optimization opportunities.']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Classification of shipment load type for this route: FTL (Full Truckload), LTL (Less Than Truckload), parcel (small package), container (ocean/rail), or bulk (loose cargo).']
  maximum_transit_time_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum expected transit time in days, accounting for delays and worst-case scenarios, used for safety stock and buffer planning.']
  minimum_transit_time_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum achievable transit time in days for expedited or express service on this route.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next route performance review and optimization assessment.']
  optimization_priority varchar [note: 'Primary optimization objective for route selection: cost (lowest freight cost), speed (fastest transit), reliability (on-time performance), sustainability (lowest carbon footprint), or balanced (multi-objective).']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the origin location, used for customs and regulatory compliance.']
  origin_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the origin node (plant, distribution center, supplier facility, or port) where the shipment begins. Aligns with internal location master or UN/LOCODE for ports.']
  origin_location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the origin location for reporting and operational visibility.']
  primary_transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation used for this route: road (truck), rail, air (cargo flight), ocean (container ship), inland waterway (barge), or pipeline.']
  route_capacity_constraint varchar [note: 'Description of any capacity constraints on this route (e.g., weight limits, volume limits, frequency limits, carrier capacity).']
  route_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the transport route or lane, used for operational reference and TMS (Transportation Management System) routing logic.']
  route_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the transport route, typically including origin and destination for human readability.']
  route_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, or considerations for this route.']
  route_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the transport route in the network.']
  route_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the route based on transportation strategy: direct (point-to-point), relay (driver handoff), intermodal (multiple transport modes), milk run (multi-stop collection/delivery), cross-dock (consolidation hub), or backhaul (return leg optimization).']
  seasonal_restriction_details varchar [note: 'Detailed description of seasonal restrictions, including affected months, reasons, and alternative routing recommendations.']
  seasonal_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route has seasonal restrictions (e.g., winter road closures, monsoon disruptions, peak season capacity limits).']
  secondary_transport_mode varchar [note: 'Secondary or alternate mode of transportation for intermodal routes, if applicable.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Service level classification for the route, defining speed and priority: express (fastest), standard (normal), economy (cost-optimized), dedicated (exclusive vehicle), or shared (consolidated shipments).']
  standard_freight_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard or baseline freight cost for this route in the company\'s reporting currency, used for budgeting and cost estimation.']
  standard_transit_time_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected transit time in days for shipments on this route under normal conditions, used for delivery promise calculation and planning.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route supports temperature-controlled (refrigerated or heated) transportation for sensitive goods.']

  Note: 'Defined transportation route or lane between an origin node (plant, DC, supplier) and a destination node (customer, DC, port), capturing route code, origin and destination location codes, primary transport mode, standard transit time (days), distance (km), preferred carrier, alternate carriers, route type (direct/relay/intermodal), seasonal restrictions, and route optimization parameters. Used by TMS for automated routing and load planning.'
}

Table logistics.node {
  node_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the logistics node. Primary key for the logistics node master record.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.control_system. Business justification: Control system assigned to a specific warehouse node for automated material handling, needed for system health reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Node operating costs are allocated to a cost center; remove denormalized cost_center_code.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Node Management assigns a responsible employee to each logistics node, used in operational dashboards and incident response.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: PROCUREMENT_MASTER_PLAN records a primary supplier per node to drive sourcing decisions and allocation of purchase orders.']
  vendor_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_site. Business justification: SUPPLIER_SITE_PLANNING uses logistics nodes to represent supplier locations; linking node to supplier_site enables routing and dock scheduling.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the logistics node location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or unit information. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  carrier_access_restrictions varchar [note: 'Description of any carrier access restrictions or requirements (e.g., pre-registration, security clearance, appointment-only).']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the logistics node is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the logistics node contact. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person or site manager for the logistics node.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the logistics node contact. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the logistics node location (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the logistics node record was first created in the system.']
  customs_bonded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the logistics node is a customs bonded warehouse authorized to store imported goods before duty payment.']
  dock_door_count varchar [note: 'Total number of loading/unloading dock doors available at the logistics node for capacity planning.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date when the logistics node became operational and available for network routing.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date when the logistics node was decommissioned or ceased operations. Null for currently active nodes.']
  erp_location_code varchar [note: 'Location code used in SAP S/4HANA or other ERP systems for material movement and inventory tracking.']
  free_trade_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the logistics node is located within a designated free trade zone or foreign trade zone.']
  handling_equipment_available varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of material handling equipment types available at the node (e.g., forklift, crane, conveyor, automated_guided_vehicle).']
  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node is certified to handle and store hazardous materials per regulatory requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the logistics node record was last updated in the system.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate in decimal degrees for GPS-based route optimization and geospatial analytics.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate in decimal degrees for GPS-based route optimization and geospatial analytics.']
  max_vehicle_length_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum vehicle length in meters that can access the logistics node due to physical constraints (e.g., turning radius, gate clearance).']
  max_vehicle_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum gross vehicle weight in kilograms permitted at the logistics node due to road or dock weight limits.']
  network_tier varchar [note: 'Hierarchical tier classification of the node within the distribution network strategy for routing optimization.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the logistics node. Externally-known unique code used across TMS and ERP systems for network location identification.']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the logistics node (e.g., \'Chicago Distribution Center\', \'Port of Hamburg Entry Point\').']
  node_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the logistics node in the distribution network lifecycle.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the logistics node indicating its primary function in the distribution network. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: manufacturing_plant|distribution_center|cross_dock|port_of_entry|customs_warehouse|customer_site|supplier_site — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, or constraints related to the logistics node.']
  operating_hours_weekday varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekdays in format HH:MM-HH:MM (e.g., 08:00-17:00) for delivery window planning.']
  operating_hours_weekend varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekends in format HH:MM-HH:MM. Null if node does not operate on weekends.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the logistics node ownership model for cost allocation and network strategy.']
  port_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the logistics node has direct access to maritime port facilities for ocean freight.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the logistics node address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  rail_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the logistics node has direct rail siding access for intermodal freight operations.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or regional administrative division for the logistics node location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  storage_capacity_sqm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total indoor storage capacity in square meters for inventory planning and network optimization.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node has temperature-controlled storage facilities for cold chain logistics.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the logistics node location (e.g., America/Chicago, Europe/Berlin) used for delivery appointment scheduling.']
  tms_location_code varchar [note: 'External identifier used by the Transportation Management System to reference this logistics node.']
  yard_capacity_trailers varchar [note: 'Maximum number of trailers that can be staged in the yard simultaneously for yard management and detention planning.']

  Note: 'Master record for all physical nodes in the distribution network — manufacturing plants, distribution centers, cross-dock facilities, ports of entry, customs bonded warehouses, customer delivery points, and supplier pickup locations. Captures node code, node type, address, GPS coordinates, operating hours, dock door count, handling equipment available, customs bonded status, free trade zone flag, TMS location ID, and yard capacity. Serves as the SSOT for network location data within the logistics domain and the reference entity for transport route origin/destination definitions and delivery appointment scheduling.'
}

Table logistics.shipment_tracking_event {
  shipment_tracking_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment tracking event record.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the transportation carrier reporting this event.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent shipment being tracked.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the shipment was delivered (populated only for delivered events).']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading number associated with the shipment.']
  carrier_status_code varchar [note: 'Carrier-specific status code reported for this event (e.g., X1, D1, AF, etc.).']
  carrier_status_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the carrier status code.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'ISO container number for ocean or intermodal shipments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tracking event record was first created in the system.']
  customs_clearance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event represents a customs clearance checkpoint for international shipments.']
  customs_entry_number varchar [note: 'Customs entry number assigned by customs authorities for international shipments.']
  data_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tracking event data was received by the Transportation Management System (TMS) or data platform.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system or method from which the tracking event was captured (EDI 214, carrier API, IoT/telematics, manual entry, TMS).']
  delay_duration_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Duration of delay in hours if the event represents a delay or exception.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Updated estimated delivery date if the shipment has been rescheduled due to delay or exception.']
  estimated_delivery_time timestamp [note: 'Updated estimated delivery timestamp with time precision if available from carrier.']
  event_city varchar [note: 'City where the tracking event occurred.']
  event_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the tracking event occurred.']
  event_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the event location.']
  event_location_name varchar [note: 'Name of the facility, terminal, or location where the event occurred.']
  event_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the event location.']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments provided by the carrier or logistics personnel regarding the event.']
  event_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the location where the tracking event occurred.']
  event_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this event within the shipment lifecycle.']
  event_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the tracking event occurred.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the tracking event occurred in the shipment lifecycle.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of tracking event captured (pickup, in-transit, out-for-delivery, delivered, exception, delay).']
  exception_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for an exception or delay event (e.g., weather, customs hold, address issue).']
  exception_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the exception or delay reason.']
  humidity_reading decimal(5,2) [note: 'Humidity percentage reading captured at the time of the event for sensitive shipments.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tracking event record was last modified.']
  on_time_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event occurred within the expected time window per the Service Level Agreement (SLA).']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Progressive number assigned by LTL (Less Than Truckload) carriers for shipment identification.']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who received the shipment (for delivered events).']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number applied to the container or trailer.']
  signature_obtained_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a signature was obtained at delivery.']
  signature_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a signature was required for delivery.']
  temperature_reading decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature reading captured at the time of the event for temperature-controlled shipments (in Celsius).']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned tracking number for the shipment.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Trailer or truck unit number transporting the shipment.']

  Note: 'Real-time or near-real-time tracking event captured for a shipment in transit, recording event type (pickup, in-transit, out-for-delivery, delivered, exception, delay), event timestamp, event location (city, state, country, GPS coordinates), carrier-reported status code, exception reason (if applicable), estimated new delivery date (if rescheduled), and data source (EDI 214, carrier API, IoT/telematics, manual entry). Enables end-to-end shipment visibility and on-time delivery monitoring.'
}

Table logistics.customs_declaration {
  customs_declaration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customs declaration record. Primary key.']
  customs_broker_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the licensed customs broker handling the declaration and clearance process.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance officer responsible for reviewing and approving this declaration.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment associated with this customs declaration.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Ensures HS code, origin, and compliance data are tied to the exact SKU; required for export/import regulatory reporting.']
  applicable_regulations varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of applicable export control and trade compliance regulations (e.g., EAR, ITAR, EU Dual-Use, OFAC).']
  bond_number varchar [note: 'Customs bond or guarantee number securing payment of duties and compliance with regulations.']
  clearance_date date [note: 'Date when customs authority granted clearance and released the goods.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance officer completed the trade compliance review.']
  compliance_screening_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome of the comprehensive trade compliance screening workflow.']
  consignee_name varchar [note: 'Name of the party receiving the goods at destination.']
  consignor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the party shipping the goods from origin.']
  country_of_destination varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the final destination country for the goods.']
  country_of_export varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country from which goods are being exported.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country where the goods were manufactured or produced.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customs declaration record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the declared value.']
  declaration_number varchar [note: 'Official customs declaration number issued by customs authority. Externally-known unique identifier for this declaration.']
  declaration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the customs declaration in the clearance workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|cleared|held|rejected|released — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  declaration_type varchar [note: 'Type of customs declaration indicating the nature of cross-border movement.']
  declared_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total declared customs value of the goods in the specified currency.']
  denied_party_screening_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of screening against denied parties lists, sanctioned entities, and restricted party databases.']
  dual_use_classification varchar [note: 'Classification determination for goods that have both civilian and military applications.']
  duty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total customs duty assessed and payable on the imported or exported goods.']
  eccn varchar [note: 'Export Control Classification Number assigned under the Commerce Control List for dual-use items.']
  embargo_check_result varchar [note: 'Result of screening against embargoed countries and restricted destinations.']
  entry_date date [note: 'Date when the goods entered the customs territory or when the declaration was lodged with customs authority.']
  export_license_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued export license number authorizing the export of controlled goods.']
  exporter_of_record varchar [note: 'Legal entity responsible for ensuring exported goods comply with export regulations and licensing requirements.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment including packaging, measured in kilograms.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the shipment is under regulatory hold.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized System tariff classification code for the goods. Primary classification for customs duty calculation.']
  import_license_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued import license number authorizing the import of controlled or restricted goods.']
  importer_of_record varchar [note: 'Legal entity responsible for ensuring imported goods comply with local laws and for paying duties and taxes.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for delivery, risk, and cost. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  license_type varchar [note: 'Type of export or import license required (e.g., individual, blanket, temporary, general).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customs declaration record was last modified.']
  net_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Net weight of the goods excluding packaging, measured in kilograms.']
  number_of_packages varchar [note: 'Total count of packages, cartons, or containers in the shipment.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging used for the shipment (e.g., pallet, carton, crate, container).']
  port_of_entry varchar [note: 'Customs port or border crossing where the goods entered the destination country.']
  port_of_exit varchar [note: 'Customs port or border crossing where the goods exited the origin country.']
  regulatory_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment is under regulatory hold pending additional review or documentation.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes documenting the resolution of compliance issues, holds, or flagged items.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total of all taxes, duties, and fees assessed on this customs declaration.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation used for the cross-border shipment.']
  vat_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Value Added Tax or Goods and Services Tax assessed on the imported goods.']

  Note: 'Customs and trade compliance master document for cross-border shipments, serving as the SSOT for ALL customs declarations AND trade compliance screening within the logistics domain. Captures declaration number, declaration type (import/export/transit), shipment reference, country of origin, country of destination, HS tariff codes, declared value, currency, incoterms, customs broker ID, entry date, customs clearance status, duty amount, VAT amount, bond number, export license number, regulatory hold flags. Embeds full compliance screening workflow: denied party screening results, export control classification (ECCN), applicable regulations (EAR, ITAR, EU Dual-Use), screening outcome (cleared/flagged/blocked), compliance officer ID, resolution notes, license type and number, embargo check results, and dual-use classification determinations. No other entity in this domain stores trade compliance or export control screening data.'
}

Table logistics.load_plan {
  load_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the load plan record. Primary key for the load plan entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Load planning expenses are charged to a cost center for OPEX tracking.']
  destination_facility_logistics_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse, plant, distribution center, or customer site to which the load is destined.']
  planned_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the carrier or logistics service provider selected to execute this load plan.']
  primary_load_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created this load plan record, used for audit trail and accountability.']
  primary_load_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse, plant, or distribution center from which the load originates.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Load Planning for a project’s equipment shipment must reference the project to coordinate schedules and resources.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Tie a load plan to the shipment it is planning, enabling direct navigation from load plan to shipment.']
  transport_route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the planned transportation route associated with this load plan, linking to route optimization and network planning.']
  actual_load_date date [note: 'The actual date when the load was physically assembled and loaded, used for performance tracking against planned date.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the bill of lading document issued for this load, serving as the legal contract of carriage and receipt.']
