Captures certifying body, certificate number, issue date, expiry date, applicable markets/regions, product scope, and renewal status. Critical for market access compliance and customer-facing documentation. Supports ISO 9001 quality records and regulatory reporting to UL, CE, and OSHA.'
}

Table product.substitution {
  substitution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for substitution']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: REQUIRED: Substitution approval process logs approved_by_employee_id to capture who authorized a substitution.']
  primary_sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Link primary SKU to master record for substitution; removes free‑form primary_sku column']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Substituted parts must satisfy the same regulatory requirement as the original part for compliance approval.']
  application_restriction varchar [note: 'Free-text description of any limitations or restrictions on where the substitute can be used. May reference specific customer segments, geographic regions, or application types.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the substitution was formally approved by engineering, quality, or product management.']
  automatic_substitution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substitution can be applied automatically by MRP or order management systems (true) or requires manual approval (false).']
  compliance_certification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substitute product requires additional compliance certifications (UL, CE, IEC) before it can be used in regulated applications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the substitution record was first created in the system.']
  customer_notification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customer approval or notification is required before applying the substitution in order fulfillment.']
  customer_segment_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of customer segment codes where this substitution is restricted or not recommended.']
  eco_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the Engineering Change Order that authorized or documented this substitution. Links to PLM system ECO records.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the substitution becomes valid and can be used in order management, MRP, and customer service processes.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the substitution is no longer valid. Null indicates indefinite validity.']
  form_fit_function_match varchar [note: 'Indicates the degree of form-fit-function equivalence between primary and substitute. Exact means identical in all dimensions; partial means some differences exist; none means significant differences.']
  geographic_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where this substitution is NOT permitted due to regulatory, certification, or business policy reasons.']
  interchangeable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substitution is bidirectional (true) or unidirectional (false). When true, both SKUs can substitute for each other.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user or system that last modified the substitution record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the substitution record was last updated.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'Most recent date when this substitution was applied in a business transaction.']
  lead_time_variance_days varchar [note: 'Difference in standard lead time between substitute and primary SKU, measured in days. Positive values indicate longer lead time for substitute.']
  price_variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage difference in list price between the substitute and primary SKU. Positive values indicate the substitute is more expensive; negative values indicate cost savings.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking indicating preference order when multiple substitutes exist for the same primary SKU. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by MRP and order management fallback logic.']
  quality_equivalence_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether quality assurance has verified that the substitute meets the same quality standards as the primary SKU through testing or certification.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for establishing the substitution relationship. Captures why the primary SKU requires a substitute. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: discontinued|end_of_life|allocation|supply_shortage|obsolete|cost_reduction|quality_improvement|design_change — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the substitution relationship was originally created or maintained.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this substitution record in the source system of record.']
  substitute_sku varchar [note: 'The replacement or substitute SKU that can be used in place of the primary SKU.']
  substitution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the substitution record. Active indicates the substitution is in effect; pending approval awaits engineering or quality review; approved is authorized but not yet effective; rejected was not approved; expired is no longer valid; suspended is temporarily inactive.']
  substitution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the substitution relationship. Direct replacement indicates identical form-fit-function; functional equivalent provides same capability with potential differences; upward compatible offers enhanced features; temporary substitute is short-term solution; cross-reference indicates OEM equivalence; alternate is approved alternative.']
  technical_notes varchar [note: 'Engineering or technical notes describing differences between primary and substitute SKUs, installation considerations, or compatibility information.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this substitution has been applied in order management or MRP processes. Used for analytics and substitution effectiveness tracking.']

  Note: 'Defines approved substitute and alternate products for SKUs that are discontinued, on allocation, or unavailable. Captures the primary SKU, substitute SKU, substitution type (direct replacement, functional equivalent, upward compatible), effective date, approval status, and any application restrictions. Supports order management fallback logic in SAP S/4HANA SD, MRP alternative item processing, and customer service resolution for end-of-life product transitions.'
}

Table product.bundle {
  bundle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for bundle']
  approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: REQUIRED: Bundle approval tracking uses approved_by_user_employee_id to identify the employee approving the bundle.']
  product_family_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.family']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Bundle profitability analysis aggregates revenue and cost under a dedicated profit center.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status for new or modified bundles requiring management or product committee approval before activation.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bundle was approved for sale. Null if approval_status is not \'approved\'.']
  assembly_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the bundle requires factory assembly or integration before shipment (True) or is shipped as separate components (False).']
  availability_end_date date [note: 'Date when this bundle is no longer available for new orders. Null for ongoing offerings. Used for promotional bundles and product phase-out planning.']
  availability_start_date date [note: 'Date when this bundle becomes available for sale. Used for new product introduction (NPI) and promotional campaign timing.']
  bundle_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the bundle, used in sales orders and quotations. Typically follows company SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) pattern for composite offerings.']
  bundle_description varchar [note: 'Detailed commercial description of the bundle, including key features, benefits, and typical use cases. Used in catalogs, quotations, and sales documentation.']
  bundle_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable commercial name of the bundle as presented to customers and used in sales materials (e.g., \'Automation Cell Starter Kit\', \'Smart Factory Solution Package\').']
  bundle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the bundle: draft (under development), active (available for sale), inactive (temporarily unavailable), discontinued (no longer sold but supported), obsolete (end-of-life).']
  bundle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bundle structure: fixed (pre-defined components, no substitution), configurable (customer can select options), promotional (time-limited marketing offer), solution (engineered system package), service (includes maintenance/support).']
  certification_requirements varchar [note: 'List of industry certifications or compliance standards the bundle meets (e.g., \'CE\', \'UL\', \'IEC 61508\', \'ATEX\'). Critical for regulated industries and export compliance.']
  component_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) components included in this bundle. Used for complexity assessment and inventory planning.']
  configuration_rules varchar [note: 'Business rules governing component selection and substitution for configurable bundles. Describes constraints, dependencies, and valid option combinations.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the primary country of manufacture or assembly for the bundle. Used for customs, tariffs, and trade compliance.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who created this bundle record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bundle record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the list price (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard discount percentage applied to the sum-of-parts price when pricing_method is \'discounted\'. Null for other pricing methods.']
  distribution_channel_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the distribution channel through which this bundle is sold (e.g., direct sales, distributor, OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer), e-commerce).']
  eccn_code varchar [note: 'Export Control Classification Number assigned to the bundle for U.S. export control compliance. Determines licensing requirements for international shipments.']
  energy_efficiency_rating varchar [note: 'Energy efficiency classification or rating for the bundle if applicable (e.g., \'A+++\', \'Energy Star\', \'IE4\'). Used for sustainability reporting and customer decision-making.']
  environmental_compliance varchar [note: 'Environmental compliance certifications and declarations for the bundle (e.g., \'RoHS\', \'REACH\', \'WEEE\', \'ISO 14001\'). Critical for EU and global environmental regulations.']
  hazmat_classification varchar [note: 'Hazardous material classification code if the bundle contains components subject to dangerous goods regulations. Used for shipping and handling compliance.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized System tariff classification code for the bundle. Used for international trade, customs declarations, and duty calculation.']
  image_url varchar [note: 'URL to the primary product image or rendering of the bundle. Used in e-commerce, catalogs, and sales presentations.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the person who last modified this bundle record. Used for change management and audit trail.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bundle record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard lead time in days from order placement to delivery for this bundle. Accounts for component availability, assembly, and logistics.']
  list_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Standard list price for the bundle in the base currency. Represents the published price before any customer-specific discounts or negotiations.']
  marketing_description varchar [note: 'Extended marketing copy and value proposition for the bundle, used in promotional materials, web catalogs, and sales presentations. Distinct from technical bundle_description.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of bundle units that must be ordered in a single transaction. Used to enforce volume thresholds for bundled offerings.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Description of the standard packaging method for the bundle (e.g., \'Wooden Crate\', \'Cardboard Box\', \'Pallet\', \'Custom Container\'). Impacts shipping and handling requirements.']
  pricing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate bundle price: sum_of_parts (total of component prices), fixed_price (single bundle price regardless of components), discounted (percentage off sum-of-parts), tiered (volume-based pricing), custom (negotiated per deal).']
  replacement_bundle_code varchar [note: 'Bundle code of the successor or replacement bundle when this bundle is discontinued. Used for product transition planning and customer migration.']
  sales_organization_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the sales organization responsible for this bundle. Used in multi-division enterprises to segment product portfolios by business unit or geography.']
  service_level_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the standard service level or SLA (Service Level Agreement) tier included with the bundle (e.g., \'BASIC\', \'PREMIUM\', \'24x7\').']
  target_market_segment varchar [note: 'Primary market segment or industry vertical this bundle is designed for (e.g., \'Automotive Manufacturing\', \'Food & Beverage\', \'Pharmaceutical\', \'Discrete Manufacturing\').']
  technical_documentation_url varchar [note: 'URL or document reference to the technical documentation, installation guides, and specifications for the bundle. Used by sales engineering and customer support.']
  volume_m3 decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total shipping volume of the complete bundle in cubic meters, including packaging. Used for warehouse space planning and transportation optimization.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Standard warranty period in months provided with the bundle. Covers all included components under a unified warranty policy.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total shipping weight of the complete bundle in kilograms, including all components and packaging. Used for logistics planning and freight cost calculation.']

  Note: 'Defines commercial product bundles, solution packages, and system kits that combine multiple SKUs into a single orderable offering (e.g., a complete automation cell bundle including PLC, HMI, drives, and cabling). Captures bundle code, bundle type (fixed, configurable, promotional), component SKU list with quantities, bundle pricing method (sum-of-parts, fixed, discounted), availability period, and minimum order quantity. Supports solution selling for industrial automation and electrification systems in Salesforce CRM and SAP S/4HANA SD. Distinct from catalog_entry which represents individual orderable items; bundle represents composite offerings.'
}

Table product.plant_data {
  plant_data_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for plant_data']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Plant‑level cost reporting requires linking plant data to the plant\'s cost center.']
  production_scheduler_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: REQUIRED: Production scheduling assigns production_scheduler_employee_id to link the scheduler employee for labor planning.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Associate plant‑specific data with the SKU it describes; enables plant‑level planning per SKU']
  abc_indicator varchar [note: 'ABC classification for inventory management prioritization. A=High-value items requiring tight control, B=Medium-value items, C=Low-value items with relaxed control.']
  availability_check_group varchar [note: 'Two-character key that controls which stock types are included in ATP (Available-to-Promise) checks for sales orders and production orders.']
  backflush_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether components are backflushed (automatically issued) when production order confirmations are posted. True=Backflush enabled, False=Manual goods issue required.']
  batch_management_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether batch/lot tracking is required for this material at this plant. True=Batch management active, False=No batch tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this plant data record was first created in the system.']
  cycle_counting_indicator varchar [note: 'Single-character code that determines the frequency of cycle counting for this material. Used to schedule physical inventory counts.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'Date on which this material will be discontinued at this plant. Used for phase-out planning and last-time-buy notifications.']
  effective_out_date date [note: 'Date from which this plant data record becomes inactive. Used to manage time-dependent plant data changes.']
  fixed_lot_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'Fixed order quantity used when lot size procedure is set to fixed lot size. MRP will always propose this quantity.']
  gr_processing_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required for goods receipt processing, quality inspection, and stock placement after physical delivery.']
  in_house_production_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of working days required to manufacture this material from raw materials to finished goods at this plant. Used in production scheduling and MRP.']
  inspection_setup_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a quality inspection setup (inspection lot creation) is required for goods receipts of this material at this plant. True=Inspection required, False=No inspection.']
  issue_storage_location varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the default storage location from which materials are issued to production orders or sales orders.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this plant data record was last updated.']
  lot_size_procedure varchar [note: 'Lot-sizing procedure used in MRP to determine order quantities. EX=Lot-for-lot, HB=Replenish to maximum stock level, FX=Fixed lot size, WB=Weekly lot size, PK=Period lot size, LS=Lot size key.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Unique material identifier (SKU) from the material master. Links this plant data record to the global material master.']
  maximum_lot_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum quantity that can be ordered or produced in a single lot for this material at this plant. Used in MRP calculations.']
  maximum_stock_level decimal(15,3) [note: 'Target maximum inventory level for this material at this plant. Used in replenishment planning to determine order quantities.']
  minimum_lot_size decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered or produced in a single lot for this material at this plant. Used in MRP calculations.']
  minimum_remaining_shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days of remaining shelf life required for goods receipt acceptance. Batches with less remaining shelf life are rejected.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified this plant data record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  mrp_controller varchar [note: 'Three-character code identifying the person or group responsible for MRP and procurement activities for this material at this plant.']
  mrp_type varchar [note: 'MRP procedure that controls how material requirements are planned. PD=MRP, VB=Manual reorder point, VM=Forecast-based planning, VV=Time-phased planning, ND=No planning, R1=Reorder point planning, R2=Replenishment planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PD|VB|VM|VV|ND|R1|R2 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  negative_stock_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether negative stock balances are permitted for this material at this plant. True=Negative stock allowed, False=System blocks transactions that would create negative stock.']
  planned_delivery_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from order placement to goods receipt. Used by MRP to schedule procurement proposals and calculate reorder points.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where this material is produced or stocked. Each plant has its own plant data record for a given material.']
  plant_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this material at this plant. Active=Normal production/sales, Blocked=Temporarily unavailable, Discontinued=End-of-life, Phase_out=Being phased out, New_product_introduction=NPI stage.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Indicates how the material is procured at this plant. E=In-house production, F=External procurement (purchasing), X=Both production and external procurement.']
  production_storage_location varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the default storage location within the plant where finished goods from production are received.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'Ten-character code identifying the profit center responsible for this material at this plant. Used for internal profitability analysis.']
  quality_inspection_type varchar [note: 'QM inspection type that determines when quality inspections are triggered. 01=Goods receipt from vendor, 02=Goods receipt from production, 03=Goods issue to customer, 04=Stock transfer, 05=Physical inventory, 08=Recurring inspection, 09=Audit inspection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 01|02|03|04|05|08|09 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reorder_point decimal(15,3) [note: 'Stock level at which MRP triggers a procurement proposal. Calculated based on lead time demand plus safety stock.']
  rounding_value decimal(15,3) [note: 'Rounding value for lot sizes. MRP rounds proposed order quantities up to the nearest multiple of this value.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum stock level maintained as buffer against demand fluctuations and supply disruptions. MRP triggers procurement when stock falls below this level plus reorder point.']
  scheduling_margin_key varchar [note: 'Key that defines float times (opening period, float before production, float after production) used in production scheduling to add buffer time.']
  serialization_level varchar [note: 'Controls serial number management for this material at this plant. 0=No serial numbers, 1=Serial number at goods receipt, 2=Serial number at goods issue, 3=Serial number at goods receipt and issue, 4=Serial number at production.']
  shelf_life_expiration_days varchar [note: 'Total shelf life period in days from production date to expiration date. Used for batch management and FEFO (First Expired First Out) logic.']
  special_procurement_type varchar [note: 'Special procurement key for subcontracting, consignment, stock transfer, or other special procurement scenarios. 10=Withdrawal from warehouse, 20=Production in another plant, 30=External procurement, 40=Subcontracting, 50=Phantom assembly, 51=Phantom assembly with lead time, 52=Collective order, 70=Stock transfer, 80=Consignment, 90=Pipeline. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 10|20|30|40|50|51|52|70|80|90 — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'Four-digit code that determines the general ledger accounts to which inventory movements are posted. Links material movements to financial accounting.']

  Note: 'Plant-specific master data extensions for each SKU, capturing manufacturing plant assignment, MRP type, lot size procedure, safety stock level, reorder point, planned delivery time, production storage location, and quality inspection type. Represents the SAP S/4HANA MM/PP plant view of the material master, enabling plant-level production planning, MRP runs, and inventory management. Each SKU may have different plant data records per manufacturing facility.'
}

Table product.classification {
  classification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for classification']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product being classified. Links to the product master data.']
  application_area varchar [note: 'The functional application area or use case for the product (e.g., Process Automation, Building Management, Motion Control, Power Distribution).']
  assigned_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that assigned this classification. Supports audit trail and data quality accountability.']
  assigned_date date [note: 'The date when this classification was originally assigned to the product. Distinct from effective start date; tracks data lineage.']
  auto_classified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this classification was assigned automatically by a system or AI/ML model (true) or manually by a human user (false). Supports data quality monitoring and exception handling.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'The business unit or division responsible for the product. Supports P&L reporting and organizational accountability.']
  class_code varchar [note: 'The specific classification code or identifier within the chosen scheme. For example, UNSPSC segment-family-class-commodity code, eCl@ss class ID, or HS tariff code.']
  class_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the classification class. Provides business context for the class code.']
  class_level varchar [note: 'The hierarchical level of the classification within the scheme. For example, in UNSPSC: 1=Segment, 2=Family, 3=Class, 4=Commodity. Enables drill-down and roll-up reporting.']
  classification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the classification assignment. Active classifications are used for procurement, catalog, and export documentation. Deprecated classifications are retained for historical reporting.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Internal or industry-specific commodity code used for procurement categorization, spend analysis, and supplier management. Often aligned with SAP Ariba commodity taxonomy.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A confidence score (0-100) indicating the certainty or quality of the classification assignment. Used when classifications are assigned by automated systems or AI/ML models.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the product is manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for customs documentation and trade compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this classification record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  customs_tariff_code varchar [note: 'The Harmonized System tariff classification code used for customs declarations, import/export documentation, and duty calculation. Critical for international trade compliance.']
  data_quality_status varchar [note: 'The data quality status of this classification record. Indicates whether the classification has been verified, requires review, or has quality issues. Supports data stewardship workflows.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date until which this classification assignment is valid. Null indicates an open-ended assignment. Used for managing classification transitions and product reclassifications.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this classification assignment is valid and should be used for business processes. Supports time-based classification changes for product lifecycle management.']
  export_control_classification varchar [note: 'Export control classification number assigned under export control regulations (e.g., US EAR, EU Dual-Use Regulation). Determines licensing requirements for international shipments of controlled goods.']
  industry_segment varchar [note: 'The primary industry segment or vertical market for which the product is designed (e.g., Automotive, Food & Beverage, Pharmaceuticals, Energy, Infrastructure).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this classification record. Supports audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this classification record was last updated. Supports change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date when this classification was last reviewed for accuracy and relevance. Supports data quality management and periodic classification audits.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'SAP material group code used for grouping materials with similar characteristics for procurement, inventory management, and reporting purposes.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'SAP material type classification (e.g., FERT=Finished Product, HALB=Semi-Finished Product, ROH=Raw Material, HAWA=Trading Goods). Determines procurement and inventory handling rules.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next classification review. Enables proactive data quality management and compliance with classification governance policies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the classification assignment. May include rationale for classification decisions, special handling instructions, or data quality observations.']
  parent_class_code varchar [note: 'The classification code of the parent node in the hierarchy. Enables navigation of multi-level taxonomies.']
  primary_classification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the primary classification for the product. A product may have multiple classifications across different schemes, but only one primary classification per scheme.']
  product_category_l1 varchar [note: 'Top-level product category in the internal product hierarchy. Represents the broadest product grouping (e.g., Automation Systems, Electrification Solutions, Smart Infrastructure).']
  product_category_l2 varchar [note: 'Second-level product category in the internal product hierarchy. Provides more granular segmentation within L1 categories.']
  product_category_l3 varchar [note: 'Third-level product category in the internal product hierarchy. Provides detailed product family classification.']
  product_category_l4 varchar [note: 'Fourth-level product category in the internal product hierarchy. Represents the most granular product classification level.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'The product line or brand family to which the product belongs. Used for portfolio management, marketing segmentation, and sales reporting.']
  record_source varchar [note: 'The source system or data feed from which this classification record originated. Supports data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this classification is required for regulatory compliance purposes (e.g., customs, export control, environmental reporting). True if the classification is mandatory for legal/regulatory reasons.']
  scheme varchar [note: 'The standardized classification system or taxonomy used to categorize the product. Common schemes include UNSPSC (United Nations Standard Products and Services Code), eCl@ss (international product classification standard), ETIM (electro-technical information model), HS Code (Harmonized System for customs), and internal category hierarchies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: UNSPSC|eCl@ss|ETIM|HS_CODE|INTERNAL_CATEGORY|NAICS|SIC|GTIN — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  search_keywords varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of search keywords or tags associated with this classification. Enhances product discoverability in e-commerce catalogs, procurement systems, and internal search applications.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The source system or application that assigned or maintains this classification (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Teamcenter PLM, SAP Ariba, MDM).']
  version varchar [note: 'The version of the classification scheme standard being used. Classification schemes are periodically updated; tracking version ensures correct interpretation of codes.']

  Note: 'Assigns products to standardized classification schemes including industry taxonomy codes (UNSPSC, eCl@ss, ETIM), internal product category hierarchy, and customs tariff/HS codes for export compliance. Captures classification scheme, class code, class description, and assignment validity. Supports procurement catalog integration via SAP Ariba, customs documentation in logistics, and product search/filtering in the commercial catalog. Managed in SAP S/4HANA MM classification system.'
}

Table product.change_order {
  change_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for change_order']
  initiator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: REQUIRED: Change order initiation logs initiator_employee_id to identify the employee who raised the change.']
  product_revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_revision. Business justification: Change orders often target a specific product revision; linking change_order to product_revision captures this ownership.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Tie each change order to the primary SKU it affects; removes unstructured affected_sku_list column']
  affected_bom_level varchar [note: 'Indicates which level(s) of the BOM hierarchy are impacted by the change: top-level finished goods, sub-assemblies, individual components, raw materials, or all levels.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the change order received final approval from all required approvers and was authorized for implementation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Overall approval decision status across all required approvers. Conditional indicates approval with stipulations; escalated indicates management review required.']
  approval_workflow_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the multi-level approval workflow. Indicates which functional area is currently reviewing the change order. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engineering|quality|manufacturing|procurement|finance|executive|complete — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive description of the proposed change, detailing what is being modified, the scope of the change, and the technical nature of the modification.']
  change_owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person assigned as the change owner responsible for driving the change through approval and implementation. Accountable for successful execution.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary business driver for the change: quality improvement, cost reduction, regulatory compliance, component obsolescence, customer request, or safety enhancement.']
  change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why the change is being requested, including background context, problem statement, and business justification.']
  change_request_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique identifier for the change order, typically formatted as ECO-NNNNNN or ECN-NNNNNN. This is the business identifier used across PLM, ERP, and MES systems.']
  change_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the change order in the approval and implementation workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through review, approval, implementation, and closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|approved|rejected|implemented|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the change order by the nature of the modification: design (product design changes), process (manufacturing process changes), documentation (drawing/spec updates), material (material substitution), specification (technical spec changes), or BOM (bill of materials structure changes).']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the change order was formally closed after successful implementation and validation. Marks the end of the change lifecycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the change order record was first created in the PLM system. Marks the beginning of the change request lifecycle.']
  customer_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of customer approval for the change, if customer notification is required. Tracks whether the customer has reviewed and approved the proposed modification.']
  customer_notification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customer notification is required for this change. True for changes that affect form, fit, function, or regulatory compliance requiring customer approval or notification.']
  effectivity_date date [note: 'The date on which the change becomes effective and must be implemented in production. This is the cutover date for transitioning from old to new specifications.']
  effectivity_serial_number varchar [note: 'Optional serial number or production lot number at which the change becomes effective. Used for mid-production changes where effectivity is tied to specific units rather than a date.']
  impact_assessment_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of implementing the change, including tooling, material, labor, and rework costs. Used for cost-benefit analysis and approval decision-making.']
  impact_assessment_inventory varchar [note: 'Assessment of the impact on existing inventory, including obsolete stock quantities, rework requirements, scrap decisions, and transition inventory management strategy.']
  impact_assessment_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Estimated impact on product lead time resulting from the change, measured in days. Positive values indicate lead time increase; negative values indicate reduction.']
  impact_assessment_quality varchar [note: 'Assessment of quality implications, including changes to inspection requirements, test procedures, quality control points, and expected quality improvements or risks.']
  implementation_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the change was fully implemented in production and the new specifications became active.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the physical implementation of the change in production. Tracks execution progress after approval has been granted.']
  implementing_plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility responsible for implementing the change. May be a single plant or multiple plants for multi-site rollout.']
  initiator_department varchar [note: 'Department or functional area of the change initiator (e.g., Engineering, Quality, Manufacturing, Procurement).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the change order record. Tracks the latest modification for audit and synchronization purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional comments, implementation notes, lessons learned, or other relevant information not captured in structured fields.']
  plm_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for this change order in the source PLM system (Siemens Teamcenter). Used for system integration and traceability back to the system of record.']
  previous_revision_level varchar [note: 'The product revision level that existed before this change order. Provides traceability to the prior design state.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level for processing and implementing the change. Critical priority indicates safety or regulatory urgency requiring immediate action.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority or certification body whose requirements are impacted by this change (e.g., UL, IEC, OSHA, EPA, CE). Applicable when regulatory_impact_flag is true.']
  regulatory_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change has regulatory compliance implications requiring additional review, testing, or certification updates (e.g., UL, CE, safety certifications).']
  related_capa_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAPA record associated with this change order. Links the change to corrective or preventive action initiatives.']
  related_ncr_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the Non-Conformance Report that triggered this change order, if applicable. Links the change to quality issues or defects that necessitated the modification.']
  revision_level varchar [note: 'The new product revision level resulting from this change order. Tracks version progression of the product design (e.g., Rev A, Rev B, Rev 01, Rev 02).']
  urgency_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator for expedited processing requirements. True indicates the change requires fast-track approval and implementation due to safety, regulatory, or critical business needs.']
  validation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when validation testing was completed and results were documented. Marks the final verification of the change implementation.']
  validation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether post-implementation validation testing is required to verify the change achieved its intended results without introducing new issues.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Status of post-implementation validation activities. Tracks whether the change has been verified to meet requirements and quality standards.']

  Note: 'Engineering Change Order (ECO) and Engineering Change Notice (ECN) records governing approved changes to product designs, BOMs, specifications, and manufacturing processes as they affect the product master record. Captures change request number, change type (design, process, documentation), affected SKUs and BOM levels, change reason, impact assessment (cost, lead time, inventory), approval workflow status, effectivity date, and implementing plant. This product owns the product-facing change governance; the engineering domain owns the broader design change workflow and CAD/drawing changes. Managed in Siemens Teamcenter PLM change management module per ISO 9001 change control requirements.'
}

Table product.order_line {
  order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the order_line association']
  bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: Orders can be for a bundle; adding bundle_id to order_line creates the needed parent relationship without a cycle.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the sales order header']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the SKU master record']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current delivery status of the SKU for this order line']
  discount_amount decimal [note: 'Discount amount applied to the SKU on the order line']
  net_price decimal [note: 'Net unit price of the SKU on the order line after discounts']
  requested_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of the SKU requested on the order line']

  Note: 'Represents the line item linking a SKU to a sales order, capturing quantity, price, discount, and delivery status for each product in an order.. Existence Justification: A SKU (product) can be sold on many sales orders, and each sales order can contain many SKUs. The line item that links a SKU to an order is created and maintained by order entry staff and carries quantity, price, discount, and delivery status, which are attributes of the relationship itself.'
}

Table product.supply_agreement {
  supply_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the supply_agreement association']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the procurement contract']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the SKU master record']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes effective for the specific SKU']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Lead time in days for delivery of the SKU under this contract']
  price decimal [note: 'Negotiated price for the SKU under this contract']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual relationship between a SKU master record and a procurement contract. It captures contract‑specific details that apply to each SKU‑contract pairing, such as the effective date of the agreement, the negotiated price, and the lead time for delivery.. Existence Justification: A SKU can be covered by multiple procurement contracts (e.g., different suppliers, time‑bound agreements) and a single procurement contract typically includes many SKUs. The business actively creates and maintains these links, tracking contract‑specific terms such as price, effective date, and lead time for each SKU‑contract pair.'
}

Table product.option_set {
  option_set_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for option_set']
  parent_option_set_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on option_set (parent_option_set_id)']
  category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping for the option set (e.g., product line, region).']
  code varchar [note: 'Compact alphanumeric code used to reference the option set in downstream systems.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level applied to the option set.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the option set record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the purpose and usage of the option set.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the option set becomes valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the option set expires; null if indefinite.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier used to map the option set to an external vendor or ERP system.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this option set is the default choice for its type.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the option set.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the option set.']
  option_type varchar [note: 'Category that defines the nature of the option (e.g., color, size, material).']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric order used to present options in a consistent sequence.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the option set.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the option set record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the option set definition for change management.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for option_set. Referenced by option_set_id.'
}

Table sales.opportunity {
  opportunity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sales opportunity record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign that influenced or generated this opportunity.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the resulting contract or order if the opportunity was won and converted.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pipeline forecasting allocates expected costs to cost centers for budgeting purposes.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account associated with this opportunity.']
  customer_contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary contact person at the customer account for this opportunity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or user who owns and is responsible for this opportunity.']
  engineering_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.engineering_project. Business justification: New product opportunities trigger an engineering project; linking enables tracking of project initiation and status.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: Enables Opportunity Management to record the exact equipment to be sold, required for quote generation and project planning.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Opportunity tracking of a specific material enables demand forecasting and aligns inventory planning.']
  opportunity_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Opportunity stores territory as free text; adding opportunity_territory_id creates a proper link to sales.territory, enabling consistent reporting and removing redundant string column.']
  product_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Opportunity reporting by product family is essential for portfolio performance analysis and strategic sales planning.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Opportunity stage forecasts profit per profit center, supporting sales‑finance alignment.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Project Execution Planning: tracks which project fulfills a won opportunity, essential for schedule and cost alignment.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Opportunity qualification requires checking applicable regulatory requirements for the product line to ensure legal sellability.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Opportunity is owned by a sales rep; adding sales_rep_id FK enables proper ownership tracking.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Opportunity pipeline and forecasting require linking each opportunity to the exact SKU being pursued, enabling accurate demand planning and revenue projection.']
  synced_quote_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quote that is currently synced with the opportunity amount and line items.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated revenue value of the opportunity in the base currency. Represents the gross deal size before discounts.']
  close_date date [note: 'Target date by which the opportunity is expected to close (win or loss decision).']
  closed_date date [note: 'Actual date when the opportunity was marked as closed (won or lost). Null if still open.']
  competitor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary competitor identified in this sales opportunity.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the opportunity location.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the opportunity amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  delivery_installation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of product delivery and installation for won opportunities. Tracks post-sale fulfillment.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total discount percentage applied to the opportunity amount. Used for pricing and margin analysis.']
  expected_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Probability-weighted revenue calculated as amount multiplied by probability percentage. Used for forecast accuracy.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter in which the opportunity is expected to close (e.g., Q1 FY2024). Used for quota and forecast alignment.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the opportunity is expected to close. Used for annual revenue planning.']
  forecast_category varchar [note: 'Sales forecast classification indicating confidence level for revenue planning and quota tracking.']
  has_open_activity boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether there are open tasks or activities associated with this opportunity. Used for pipeline health monitoring.']
  industry_segment varchar [note: 'Target industry vertical or market segment for this opportunity (e.g., automotive, food and beverage, pharmaceuticals).']
  is_closed boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the opportunity has reached a final closed state (won or lost).']
  is_private boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the opportunity is marked as private with restricted visibility.']
  is_won boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the opportunity was closed as won (deal successfully secured).']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent sales activity (call, meeting, email) logged against this opportunity.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity record was last updated.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'Original source or channel through which the opportunity was generated.']
  loss_reason varchar [note: 'Primary reason why the opportunity was lost. Used for win/loss analysis and competitive intelligence.']
  next_step varchar [note: 'Description of the next planned action or milestone to advance the opportunity.']
  opportunity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the opportunity including customer needs, solution scope, and strategic context.']
  opportunity_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name or title of the sales opportunity describing the potential deal.']
  opportunity_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the opportunity used in sales communications and reporting.']
  opportunity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the opportunity based on customer relationship status (new customer acquisition vs existing customer expansion).']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the order number generated when the opportunity was converted to a sales order in the ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) system.']
  probability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated probability of winning this opportunity expressed as a percentage (0-100). Used for weighted pipeline forecasting.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Primary product line or business unit category for the opportunity (e.g., automation systems, electrification solutions, smart infrastructure).']
  project_end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual completion date for project delivery if the opportunity is won.']
  project_start_date date [note: 'Planned or actual start date for project delivery if the opportunity is won.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region classification for the opportunity (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC).']
  sales_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from opportunity creation to close date. Used for sales velocity analysis.']
  stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the opportunity in the sales pipeline lifecycle indicating progression toward close. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospecting|qualification|needs_analysis|proposal|negotiation|closed_won|closed_lost — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stage_changed_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the opportunity stage was last changed. Used for stage duration and velocity analysis.']

  Note: 'Core sales opportunity record representing a potential deal for industrial automation, electrification, or smart infrastructure products. Tracks the full revenue pipeline from initial identification through close, including estimated value, probability, stage progression, expected close date, product lines of interest, competitive landscape, win/loss outcome, and primary loss reason. Sourced from Salesforce Sales Cloud Opportunity object. SSOT for all pipeline, forecast, and win/loss analysis data.'
}

Table sales.quote {
  quote_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the commercial quotation record. Primary key.']
  compliance_product_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.product_certification. Business justification: Quote generation validates that the quoted SKU holds the necessary product certification (e.g., CE, UL) before pricing is presented.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal contract record created when the quote is accepted and converted to an order.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Quote budgeting ties estimated costs to a cost center for cost‑control before order conversion.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account (prospective or existing) to whom this quote is issued.']
  customer_contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary customer contact person to whom the quote is addressed.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (sales manager, director, or executive) who approved the quote.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: Links Quote to the specific asset being quoted, supporting price calculation, warranty terms, and delivery scheduling.']
  previous_quote_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the prior version of this quote if this is a revision or amendment.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Quote profitability analysis requires profit center linkage to calculate expected margin.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Quote-to-Project conversion: links a quote to the project that will deliver the quoted solution, required for downstream execution.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative responsible for creating and managing this quote.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales territory to which this quote is assigned for pipeline tracking and commission purposes.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Quotes are generated based on a designated supplier\'s pricing; linking supports price validation, supplier cost analysis, and compliance with approved supplier lists.']
  template_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the document template used to generate the customer-facing quote document.']
  accepted_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer formally accepted the quote, triggering order creation.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the quote received final internal approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of internal approval workflow for the quote, particularly for non-standard pricing or terms.']
  competitor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary competitor identified in the sales opportunity for this quote.']
  configuration_summary varchar [note: 'High-level summary of the product configuration details included in the quote (e.g., system specifications, key components, customization notes).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quote record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the quote is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  delivery_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of days from order confirmation to delivery of the quoted products.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of all discounts applied to the quote (standard and non-standard).']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall discount rate applied to the quote, expressed as a percentage of the subtotal.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for delivery, insurance, and risk transfer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quote record was last updated or modified.']
  non_standard_discount_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quote includes discounts that exceed standard approval thresholds and require special authorization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and comments related to the quote, including negotiation history, customer feedback, and special considerations.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual payment terms offered to the customer (e.g., \'Net 30\', \'50% upfront / 50% on delivery\', \'Letter of Credit\').']
  presented_date date [note: 'Date on which the quote was formally presented to the customer.']
  quote_date date [note: 'Date on which the quote was formally issued to the customer.']
  quote_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the quote, typically reflecting the project or product scope (e.g., \'Factory Automation System - Plant 5\').']
  quote_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the quote. Used in customer communications and contract references.']
  quote_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quote in the sales workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|presented|accepted|rejected|expired — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  quote_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quote based on the nature of the commercial transaction.']
  rejected_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer formally rejected or declined the quote.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason provided by the customer for rejecting the quote (e.g., \'Price too high\', \'Lead time unacceptable\', \'Competitor selected\').']
  service_level_agreement varchar [note: 'Service level commitments included in the quote (e.g., response time, uptime guarantees, maintenance windows).']
  shipping_handling_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated cost for shipping and handling of the quoted products to the customer site.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Planned method of shipment for the quoted products (e.g., \'Air Freight\', \'Ocean Freight\', \'Ground Transport\', \'Courier\').']
  special_terms_conditions varchar [note: 'Additional contractual terms, conditions, or clauses specific to this quote that deviate from standard terms.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of all quoted line items before discounts, taxes, and adjustments.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount calculated on the quote based on applicable tax jurisdictions and rates.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final total value of the quote including all line items, discounts, taxes, and fees.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the quoted prices and terms become effective.']
  valid_until_date date [note: 'Expiration date after which the quote is no longer valid and prices/terms may change.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the quote, incremented when revisions are made to pricing, terms, or configuration.']
  warranty_terms varchar [note: 'Description of warranty coverage included with the quoted products (duration, scope, exclusions).']
  win_probability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated probability (0-100%) that this quote will be accepted and converted to an order, based on sales assessment.']

  Note: 'Commercial quotation issued to a prospective or existing industrial customer for automation systems, electrification solutions, or smart infrastructure components. Captures quoted price, discount structure (including approved non-standard discounts), validity period, payment terms, delivery lead time, configuration details, and approval status. Linked to an opportunity and may progress to a formal proposal or order. Sourced from Salesforce CPQ / Sales Cloud Quote object.'
}

Table sales.quote_line {
  quote_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quote line item. Primary key.']
  bundle_parent_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent quote line if this line is part of a product bundle. Enables hierarchical quote structures for complex system quotes.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Quote creation requires exact component master data for pricing, lead time, and compliance; linking ensures accurate component reference.']
  configuration_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product configuration record for complex configurable products. Links to CPQ (Configure Price Quote) configuration details.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Quote line for hardware must be tied to the future device record for tracking warranty and configuration.']
  engineering_revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.revision. Business justification: Quotes must specify the component revision being sold to guarantee correct version delivery and regulatory compliance.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Quote line must reference internal material master for cost, inventory availability, and production planning.']
  primary_quote_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this quote line record. Audit trail for accountability.']
  primary_quote_sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product master record. Identifies the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit), product configuration, or service offering being quoted.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the engineering or implementation project associated with this quote line. Links sales to project execution.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent commercial quotation header. Links this line item to its containing quote document.']
  quote_sku_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.sku_master']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative responsible for this line item. Used for commission calculation and territory management.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preferred supplier or vendor for this line item. Used for procurement planning and supply chain management.']
  approval_level varchar [note: 'The approval authority level required for this line item based on discount depth or special terms. Escalates based on business rules.']
  commission_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The commission rate applicable to this line item for sales compensation. Business confidential.']
  committed_delivery_date date [note: 'The delivery date committed by the manufacturer for this line item. Based on production schedule and capacity planning.']
  configuration_summary varchar [note: 'Human-readable summary of the product configuration options selected. Describes customizations, options, and specifications for this line item.']
  cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total cost to manufacture or procure the items on this line. Used for margin analysis and profitability calculations. Business confidential.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this quote line record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY).']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The absolute monetary discount amount applied to this line item. Alternative to percentage-based discounts.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied to the list price. Represents negotiated price concessions or promotional discounts.']
  is_bundle_parent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is a parent bundle that contains child line items. True for bundle headers, false for standalone or child items.']
  is_optional boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is optional or required. Optional items may be removed by the customer without affecting the core quote.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this quote line record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The estimated lead time in days from order placement to delivery for this line item. Critical for production planning and customer expectations.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the quote. Determines the display order of line items on the quote document.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'The current status of this quote line item in the approval workflow. Tracks the lifecycle state of individual line items.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quote line item type. Distinguishes between hardware products, software, installation services, commissioning, and ongoing support services.']
  list_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The standard catalog or list price per unit before any discounts. Base price from the product master or price book.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'The original equipment manufacturer (OEM) part number for this item. Used for procurement and supply chain traceability.']
  margin_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross margin amount for this line item. Calculated as subtotal amount minus cost amount. Business confidential.']
  margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The gross margin percentage for this line item. Calculated as margin amount divided by subtotal amount. Business confidential.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes or comments about this quote line item. Used for collaboration between sales, engineering, and operations teams.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'The product family or category classification. Groups related products for reporting and analysis (e.g., Automation Systems, Electrification Solutions, Smart Infrastructure).']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of units being quoted for this line item. Supports fractional quantities for materials sold by weight or volume.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The customer-requested delivery date for this line item. May differ from the committed delivery date based on production capacity.']
  special_terms varchar [note: 'Any special terms, conditions, or notes specific to this line item. May include warranty extensions, service level agreements, or custom provisions.']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The extended line amount before taxes and fees. Calculated as quantity multiplied by unit price.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount applicable to this line item. May include VAT, sales tax, or other applicable taxes based on jurisdiction.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total line amount including all taxes and fees. Final amount for this line item that contributes to the quote total.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the quoted quantity. Defines how the product or service is quantified (e.g., each, kilogram, meter, hour for services). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|kg|meter|liter|hour|day|month|year|set — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual selling price per unit after applying discounts and adjustments. This is the negotiated price for this specific quote.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a commercial quotation, representing a specific SKU, product configuration, or service offering with its own quantity, unit price, discount, and extended amount. Supports multi-line complex industrial system quotes including hardware, software, installation, and commissioning services. Sourced from Salesforce CPQ Quote Line object.'
}

Table sales.proposal {
  proposal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the proposal record. Primary key.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account to whom this proposal is submitted.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales opportunity that this proposal supports.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Proposal‑Project handoff: associates an approved proposal with the resulting project, needed for resource planning and reporting.']
  proposal_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Proposal includes a sales_territory string; adding proposal_territory_id provides a normalized reference to sales.territory and removes the redundant column.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preliminary quote that preceded this formal proposal.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Proposal is authored by a sales rep; adding sales_rep_id FK captures responsibility.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the proposal received final internal approval for submission.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Internal approval status tracking cross-functional review by engineering, legal, and commercial teams.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or role who provided final approval for the proposal.']
  assumptions varchar [note: 'Key assumptions underlying the proposal including site conditions, customer responsibilities, and external dependencies.']
  competitor_names varchar [note: 'Names of known competitors also bidding for this project or opportunity.']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'List of applicable compliance certifications and standards that the proposed solution meets (CE, UL, IEC 61131, ISO 13849, IEC 62443).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the proposal record was first created in the system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in the proposal.']
  customer_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary customer contact for proposal correspondence.']
  customer_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary customer contact person to whom the proposal is addressed.']
  customer_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary customer contact for proposal discussions.']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'Incoterms or delivery conditions specifying responsibilities for shipping, insurance, and risk transfer.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Absolute monetary discount amount applied to the proposal.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall discount percentage applied to the proposal value as a commercial concession.']
  estimated_completion_date date [note: 'Proposed date for project completion and final delivery as outlined in the proposal.']
  estimated_start_date date [note: 'Proposed date for project commencement as outlined in the proposal.']
  exclusions varchar [note: 'Items, services, or responsibilities explicitly excluded from the proposal scope to manage expectations and risk.']
  grand_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final total value of the proposal including all charges and taxes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the proposal record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal notes, comments, or special instructions related to the proposal.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed payment terms and conditions including milestones, schedules, and methods.']
  prepared_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the sales engineer or proposal manager who prepared the proposal.']
  pricing_breakdown varchar [note: 'Itemized breakdown of pricing including equipment, engineering, installation, commissioning, training, and support costs.']
  project_duration_days varchar [note: 'Estimated total duration of the project from commencement to completion in calendar days.']
  proposal_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique business identifier for the proposal document.']
  proposal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the proposal in the sales process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|under_review|approved|submitted|accepted|rejected|withdrawn|expired — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  proposal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the proposal based on its nature and scope (technical, commercial, combined, RFP response, EPC, turnkey).']
  rfp_reference_number varchar [note: 'Customer\'s RFP or tender reference number if this proposal is in response to a formal request.']
  risk_clauses varchar [note: 'Legal and commercial risk mitigation clauses including liability limitations, force majeure, and indemnification.']
  scope_of_supply varchar [note: 'Detailed description of all equipment, systems, services, and deliverables included in the proposal.']
  solution_architecture_summary varchar [note: 'High-level technical description of the proposed automation or electrification solution architecture.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the proposal was formally submitted to the customer.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to the proposal based on jurisdiction and tax regulations.']
  technical_specifications varchar [note: 'Detailed technical specifications of the proposed equipment, systems, and components including performance parameters.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the proposal summarizing the project or solution being offered.']
  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the proposal including all line items, services, and charges before taxes.']
  valid_until_date date [note: 'Expiration date after which the proposal terms, pricing, and commitments are no longer binding.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the proposal document to track revisions and amendments.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration of the warranty coverage offered for the proposed solution in months.']
  warranty_terms varchar [note: 'Detailed warranty terms and conditions including coverage scope, exclusions, and claim procedures.']
  win_probability_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated probability of winning the proposal expressed as a percentage based on sales assessment.']

  Note: 'Formal technical and commercial proposal submitted to a customer or prospect for complex industrial automation or electrification projects. Includes solution architecture summary, scope of supply, technical specifications, pricing breakdown, project timeline, warranty terms, compliance certifications (CE, UL, IEC 61131, ISO 13849), and risk/exclusion clauses. Represents a more detailed and binding document than a quote, often requiring cross-functional review (engineering, legal, commercial) and multi-level approval. Linked to an opportunity and may reference one or more quotes. Common in EPC and large capital project sales cycles typical of industrial manufacturing.'
}

Table sales.sales_lead {
  sales_lead_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sales lead record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that generated or influenced this lead.']
  converted_opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the opportunity record created when this lead was converted, establishing traceability from lead to opportunity.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer account record created or matched when this lead was converted.']
  customer_contact_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contact record created when this lead was converted, linking the lead contact to the customer account.']
