  delivery_location varchar [note: 'The customer\'s delivery address or site location for this order. May reference a customer site ID or contain full address details.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied at intake date to convert order value to base currency. Locked at intake for consistent booking valuation.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter in which this order intake was booked. Format Q1-YYYY through Q4-YYYY. Used for quarterly sales performance reporting and quota tracking.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this order intake was booked. Used for annual sales performance reporting and year-over-year growth analysis.']
  handoff_date date [note: 'The date when the order intake was successfully handed off to the order management domain. Used for sales-to-fulfillment cycle time KPI calculation.']
  handoff_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the handoff from sales to order management domain. Tracks the intake record through validation, transfer, and acceptance by the fulfillment organization.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'The Incoterms rule defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and seller. Critical for international trade compliance and logistics planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  industry_segment varchar [note: 'The customer\'s industry segment or vertical market. Used for market analysis, sales territory planning, and industry-specific KPI tracking.']
  intake_date date [note: 'The date when the order was officially received and recorded in the sales system. This is the booking date used for sales quota recognition and revenue pipeline tracking.']
  intake_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the order intake record. Human-readable unique number used for tracking and reference in sales reporting and KPI dashboards.']
  intake_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the order intake record was created in the system. Used for sales-to-fulfillment cycle time measurement and operational analytics.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this order intake record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this order intake record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments related to this order intake. May include customer requests, internal coordination notes, or special handling instructions.']
  order_management_reference varchar [note: 'The order number or reference assigned by the order management system after handoff. Links this sales intake record to the authoritative order record in SAP SD or equivalent ERP.']
  order_priority varchar [note: 'The priority level assigned to this order intake. Influences production scheduling and resource allocation in the fulfillment process.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the order by business type. Distinguishes between new customer acquisition, existing customer expansion, and service orders for sales mix analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: New Business|Renewal|Upgrade|Add-On|Replacement|Service|Spare Parts — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total confirmed order value at intake. This is the gross order amount before any adjustments, used for sales booking recognition and quota attainment calculation.']
  order_value_base_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Order value converted to the company\'s base reporting currency using the exchange rate at intake date. Used for consolidated sales reporting and KPI tracking.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The agreed payment terms for this order. Defines when payment is due relative to delivery or invoice date. Critical for cash flow forecasting and credit risk management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Net 30|Net 45|Net 60|Net 90|Advance Payment|Letter of Credit|Installment|Custom — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days until payment is due per the agreed payment terms. Used for accounts receivable forecasting and working capital planning.']
  product_line varchar [note: 'The primary product line or business unit for this order. Used for sales performance segmentation and product portfolio analysis.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The customer\'s requested delivery date as captured at order intake. Used for production planning coordination and customer expectation management.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'The agreed shipping or transportation method for order delivery. Impacts delivery lead time and logistics cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Air Freight|Sea Freight|Road Transport|Rail|Courier|Customer Pickup|Multimodal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  special_terms_conditions varchar [note: 'Any special commercial terms, conditions, or customer-specific agreements applicable to this order. May include warranty extensions, service level commitments, or pricing exceptions.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the sales user who created this order intake record in the system.']

  Note: 'Sales order intake record capturing the commercial handoff from a won opportunity or accepted quote to the order management domain. Records the intake date, confirmed order value, customer PO reference, requested delivery date, payment terms confirmed, and handoff status. This is NOT the SSOT for order fulfillment data — the authoritative order record lives in the order domain. The sales domain retains this record solely for order intake KPI tracking, sales-to-fulfillment cycle time measurement, and booking recognition against quota.'
}

Table sales.opportunity_component {
  opportunity_component_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the opportunity_component association']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the component master record']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the sales opportunity']
  estimated_quantity decimal [note: 'Quantity of the component expected to be sold in the opportunity']
  interest_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative level of interest (high, medium, low) expressed by the sales team for the component within the opportunity']
  target_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned delivery date for the component as promised in the opportunity']

  Note: 'Represents the association between a component and a sales opportunity. Each record captures the sales interest, quantity estimate, and planned delivery date for a component within a specific opportunity.. Existence Justification: Sales representatives add multiple engineered components to a single sales opportunity, and each component can appear in many different opportunities across customers. The association captures interest level, estimated quantity, and target delivery date, which are specific to the component‑opportunity pairing.'
}

Table sales.project_rep_assignment {
  project_rep_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the project_rep_assignment association']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the project header']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the sales representative']
  allocation_percent decimal [note: 'Percentage of the rep\'s capacity allocated to the project']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the rep\'s assignment to the project ends']
  role varchar [note: 'The functional role of the sales rep on the project (e.g., Lead, Support, Technical)']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the rep\'s assignment to the project begins']

  Note: 'This association captures the assignment of sales representatives to capital or engineering projects. Each record links one sales_rep_id to one project_header_id and stores the role, allocation percentage, and the effective start and end dates of the assignment.. Existence Justification: A sales representative can be assigned to multiple capital or engineering projects, and each project can have multiple sales representatives contributing with defined roles and allocation percentages. The assignment is actively managed, with start/end dates, role definitions, and allocation percentages recorded for each rep‑project pairing.'
}

Table sales.device_contract_assignment {
  device_contract_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the device_contract_assignment association']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the device registry']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the sales contract']
  contract_device_end_date date [note: 'Date when the device coverage under the contract ends']
  contract_device_start_date date [note: 'Date when the device coverage under the contract starts']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Service level agreed for the device under the contract']

  Note: 'This association product represents the contractual coverage relationship between a sales contract and an automation device. It captures the period during which a device is covered by a contract and the service level agreed for that coverage.. Existence Justification: A sales contract can cover multiple automation devices, and a single device may be covered under multiple contracts over its lifecycle (e.g., initial sale, later service extensions). The business actively creates, updates, and deletes these links, tracking start/end dates and service levels for each contract‑device pairing.'
}

Table sales.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for campaign']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal user responsible for the campaign execution and oversight.']
  parent_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on campaign (parent_campaign_id)']
  actual_clicks varchar [note: 'Number of clicks recorded for the campaign.']
  actual_impressions varchar [note: 'Number of times the campaign content was actually displayed.']
  actual_leads varchar [note: 'Number of qualified leads actually generated.']
  actual_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue realized from the campaign.']
  actual_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount actually spent on the campaign to date.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved monetary budget allocated for the campaign.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel through which the campaign is delivered (e.g., email, social media, search, event, direct).']
  code varchar [note: 'External code or reference number assigned to the campaign, often used in marketing systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the campaign purpose, messaging, and scope.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date on which the campaign is scheduled to end or be retired.']
  expected_clicks varchar [note: 'Projected number of clicks generated by the campaign.']
  expected_impressions varchar [note: 'Projected number of times the campaign content will be displayed to the target audience.']
  expected_leads varchar [note: 'Projected number of qualified leads generated by the campaign.']
  expected_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected revenue attributable to the campaign.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the campaign is executed or repeated.']
  is_test_campaign boolean [note: 'True if the campaign is a test or pilot; false otherwise.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two‑letter ISO language code of the primary language used in campaign assets.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the campaign used in reports and dashboards.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Strategic objective the campaign aims to achieve.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the campaign.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO code representing the primary geographic region targeted by the campaign.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date on which the campaign is scheduled to begin.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the campaign.']
  success_metric varchar [note: 'Key performance indicator used to evaluate campaign success (e.g., leads_generated, revenue_attributed).']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the primary audience segment(s) the campaign is intended for.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the campaign record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for campaign. Referenced by campaign_id.'
}

Table sales.sales_team {
  sales_team_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sales_team']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who manages the sales team.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  manager_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  parent_sales_team_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sales_team (parent_sales_team_id)']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual budget allocated to the sales team.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center code associated with the sales team expenses.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sales team record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the sales team\'s purpose, responsibilities, and scope.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the sales team was discontinued or archived, if applicable.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which the budget and quota apply.']
  headcount varchar [note: 'Number of members in the sales team.']
  is_remote boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sales team operates remotely.']
  office_location varchar [note: 'Physical office address of the sales team.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Performance rating of the sales team for the evaluation period.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Main email address for contacting the sales team.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Main phone number for contacting the sales team.']
  product_line_focus varchar [note: 'Primary product line(s) the sales team sells.']
  quota_achieved decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual sales quota achieved by the team.']
  quota_target decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target sales quota assigned to the team for the fiscal period.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the sales team operates.']
  sales_channel_focus varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel focus of the team.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the sales team was established.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sales team.']
  team_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the sales team within the organization.']
  team_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name of the sales team.']
  team_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sales team based on its focus or structure.']
  territory varchar [note: 'Specific sales territory assigned to the team.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the sales team record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for sales_team. Referenced by sales_team_id.'
}

Table sales.discount_schedule {
  discount_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for discount_schedule']
  base_discount_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on discount_schedule (base_discount_schedule_id)']
  applies_to_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment (e.g., enterprise, SMB) for which the discount schedule is valid.']
  applies_to_product_category varchar [note: 'Product category or line to which the discount schedule applies.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether changes to this schedule require managerial approval.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the discount schedule was approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the discount schedule record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary discount values.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the discount schedule purpose and rules.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary discount amount when the schedule is amount‑based.']
  discount_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the discount (percentage, fixed amount, tiered).']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Primary discount percentage applied when the schedule is percentage‑based.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the discount schedule becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the discount schedule expires; null for open‑ended.']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this discount can be combined with other discounts.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent policy or compliance review of the schedule.']
  max_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Upper limit on the discount amount that can be applied per transaction.']
  minimum_order_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum order monetary value required for the discount schedule to be eligible.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter region code indicating geographic applicability.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between mandatory reviews of the discount schedule.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the discount schedule in external systems.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the discount schedule.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the schedule indicating how discounts are calculated.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the discount schedule.']
  tier_max_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum quantity threshold for the tiered discount; null if unlimited.']
  tier_min_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity threshold for the tiered discount to apply.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the discount schedule record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for discount_schedule. Referenced by discount_schedule_id.'
}

Table sales.quote_template {
  quote_template_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for quote_template']
  archived_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the archive action.']
  base_quote_template_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on quote_template (base_quote_template_id)']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who created the template.']
  updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user who last updated the template.']
  applicable_product_line varchar [note: 'Product line(s) for which the template is intended; free‑text list or comma‑separated values.']
  approval_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the pending approval must be completed.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the template must be approved before it can be used to generate quotes.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the template.']
  archived_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the template was archived.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards constraints that must be reflected in quotes generated from this template.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the template record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values in the template.']
  default_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard discount applied by default when the template is used, expressed as a percent.']
  default_price_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Baseline monetary amount used as a starting point when creating a quote from this template.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the purpose and scope of the quote template.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the template becomes valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the template is no longer valid; null indicates indefinite validity.']
  industry varchar [note: 'Industry segment the template targets (e.g., automotive, energy, aerospace).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the template is currently available for use.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the quote template.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes for internal users.']
  pricing_terms varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of pricing rules, payment terms, and conditions embedded in the template.']
  region varchar [note: 'Primary geographic region where the template is used.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number tracking manual revisions of the template content.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the quote template.']
  template_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business category the template belongs to.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the template in downstream processes.']
  template_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business owner responsible for the template.']
  template_version_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of changes introduced in this version.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the template record.']
  validity_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the generated quote remains valid after issuance.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number of the template; higher numbers indicate newer revisions.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for quote_template. Referenced by template_id.'
}

Table order.order_header {
  order_header_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sales order header.']
  account_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Industrial equipment orders need an installation site for field service planning; site details are in customer.account_site.']
  automation_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.automation_project. Business justification: Order‑Project Tracking links a sales order to the automation engineering project delivering the solution, needed for project budgeting and status reporting.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Links sales orders to the \'Contractual Compliance Obligation\' record that defines mandatory regulatory actions the order must satisfy.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Overall order budgeting: assigning a sales order to a cost center supports order‑level expense allocation and budgeting reports.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who placed the order.']
  customer_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Order processing uses a sold‑to contact for invoicing and communication; this contact is stored in customer.contact.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Project linkage: whole sales orders are tied to internal orders for project cost settlement and tracking.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Revenue attribution: linking orders to the originating sales opportunity enables pipeline‑to‑revenue reporting and commission calculations.']
  order_intake_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: Order Management traceability: each order must reference the intake record that created it for audit and fulfillment reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue attribution: linking the order to a profit center enables consolidated profit‑and‑loss reporting per sales order.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Project Management Report requires linking each sales order to its implementation project for schedule and cost tracking.']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Contract compliance: orders are executed under a sales contract; linking ensures legal and pricing terms are enforced per order.']
  sales_rep_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Commission & performance reports require each sales order to be linked to the responsible sales rep employee.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Commission & accountability: each order is credited to the responsible sales rep, supporting commission payout and performance metrics.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Territory reporting: orders must be assigned to a sales territory for quota tracking, regional performance dashboards, and incentive calculations.']
  shipping_address_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address. Business justification: Shipping logistics require a reference to the customer\'s shipping address; address data resides in customer.address.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Single‑source orders may designate a preferred supplier at the header level; used in order approval and compliance reporting.']
  billing_block boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether billing of the order is blocked.']
  credit_status varchar [note: 'Credit check result for the customer at order time.']
  currency_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate from order currency to company code currency at pricing time.']
  customer_account_group varchar [note: 'SAP account group categorizing the customer.']
  customer_purchase_order_date date [note: 'Date on the customer\'s purchase order.']
  delivery_block boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether delivery of the order is blocked.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the order is distributed (e.g., wholesale, retail).']
  division varchar [note: 'Business division handling the order.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Terms governing freight cost responsibility (e.g., prepaid, collect).']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the order including packaging, measured in kilograms.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms defining delivery responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|FOB|CFR|CIF|DAP|DDP — promote to reference product]']
  internal_comments varchar [note: 'Internal notes visible only to company personnel.']
  net_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total net weight of all items in the order, measured in kilograms.']
  order_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the order amounts.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the order as used in customer communications.']
  order_placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer placed the order.']
  order_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the order for processing.']
  order_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why the order was created (e.g., new project, replacement).']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the order. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|released|partially_delivered|completed|cancelled|closed|on_hold — promote to reference product]']
  order_text varchar [note: 'Long free‑text field for additional order instructions or remarks.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the order based on fulfillment rules.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual terms defining when payment is due (e.g., NET30).']
  price_group varchar [note: 'Group determining price level for the customer.']
  price_list varchar [note: 'Price list identifier used for pricing the order.']
  pricing_date date [note: 'Date on which the pricing conditions were determined.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference number supplied by the customer for their internal tracking.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order record was first captured in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the order record.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date requested by the customer for order delivery.']
  route varchar [note: 'Planned transportation route for delivering the order.']
  sales_document_type varchar [note: 'SAP SD document type code (e.g., OR for standard order).']
  sales_group varchar [note: 'Group of sales representatives responsible for the order.']
  sales_office varchar [note: 'Geographic sales office handling the order.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'Code of the sales organization responsible for the order.']
  shipping_condition varchar [note: 'Condition governing the shipping method for the order.']
  shipping_point varchar [note: 'Logistics location from which the order will be shipped.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applied to the order for tax calculation.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of line item amounts before taxes, discounts, and surcharges.']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after taxes, discounts, and surcharges.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax calculated for the order.']
  transportation_group varchar [note: 'Classification of transportation mode and carrier.']
  volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of the order in cubic meters.']

  Note: 'Core master record for customer purchase orders representing the full order commitment in the order-to-cash lifecycle. Captures customer reference, order type (standard, rush, blanket, consignment), requested delivery date, incoterms, payment terms, pricing date, total net value, currency, sales organization, distribution channel, division, and overall order status. Serves as the SSOT for all customer order commitments in the industrial manufacturing order lifecycle, driving downstream delivery, billing, and revenue recognition.'
}

Table order.line {
  line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each order line record.']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bom. Business justification: Enables Production Planning to pull the exact BOM version for the ordered product, essential for material requirement planning (MRP) reports.']
  compliance_product_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.product_certification. Business justification: Enables the \'Product Certification Validation\' step, ensuring each ordered product has an active certification before fulfillment.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Required for Order‑to‑Manufacturing handoff report linking each sold line to the engineered component for traceability and production scheduling.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost accounting: each sales order line must be charged to a cost center for internal cost reporting and profitability analysis.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Regulated products require batch traceability from order line to lot batch for quality, recall, and compliance management.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Order fulfillment requires linking each order line to the material master for inventory reservation, costing, and MRP planning; this is standard in manufacturing ERP systems.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent sales order (transaction header) to which this line belongs.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability tracking: linking order line revenue to a profit center enables margin reporting per product line.']
  quote_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote_line. Business justification: Audit trail: order line must reference the originating quote line to validate pricing, configuration, and warranty obligations.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.recipe. Business justification: Production Recipe Assignment Report requires linking each order line to the recipe used for manufacturing, ensuring traceability and compliance.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Required for the \'Regulatory Compliance Check\' process that validates each ordered material against applicable regulations before shipment, ensuring legal compliance and accurate reporting.']
  serialized_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.serialized_unit. Business justification: Serialized items need direct linkage between order line and specific serialized unit to support warranty, service, and traceability.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Required for Order Fulfillment & Costing report linking each order line to the master product record for pricing, compliance, and warranty tracking.']
  spare_part_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.spare_part. Business justification: Needed for Spare Part Sales process to tie sold line items to the spare‑part master for warranty and inventory tracking.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Picking process assigns a specific storage location to each order line to locate inventory in the warehouse; the link enables pick list generation and inventory accuracy.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Procurement assigns a supplier to each order line for purchasing; needed for supplier performance, cost analysis, and PO generation.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: Cost allocation report maps each order line to a WBS element of the project for budgeting and earned‑value analysis.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the goods were actually received by the customer.']
  backorder_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the line is on backorder, otherwise false.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for traceability of the material.']
  blanket_release_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the blanket order release that generated this line.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity confirmed by the system after availability check.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the order line record was created in the source system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary values on this line.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date the line was delivered to the customer.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the line\'s delivery.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to this line.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the product is sold (e.g., online, direct).']
  division varchar [note: 'Business division responsible for the product.']
  gross_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total price after taxes and before discounts.']
  gross_weight decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total weight of the line item including packaging (kilograms).']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the quality inspection process.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned lead time in days from order to delivery.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the order, used for ordering and reference.']
  net_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net price per unit before taxes and discounts.']
  net_weight decimal(15,3) [note: 'Weight of the product itself without packaging (kilograms).']
  plant varchar [note: 'SAP plant code where the product is stocked or produced for this order line.']
  pricing_condition varchar [note: 'Pricing condition type governing the price calculation.']
  product_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the product or service on the line.']
  promised_date date [note: 'Date promised to the customer for delivery.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing quality assessment (0‑100).']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Result of quality inspection for the line item.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided when the line is rejected or cancelled.']
  requested_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity originally requested by the customer.']
  rma_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to an RMA record if the line is a return.']
  sales_org varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for the sale.']
  sales_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Price per unit used for revenue recognition.']
  sales_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity used for sales reporting, may differ from requested/confirmed units.']
  schedule_line_date date [note: 'Planned date for delivery or production of this line item.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number for serialized items.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse or bin location from which the material will be shipped.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax amount applicable to this line item.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity (e.g., each, kilogram).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the order line record.']
  volume decimal(15,3) [note: 'Physical volume of the line item (cubic meters).']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for volume.']
  weight_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for weight fields.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a customer sales order representing a discrete product, automation system, or service being ordered. Captures material number, ordered and confirmed quantities, unit of measure, schedule line dates, net price, pricing conditions, plant assignment, storage location, delivery status, rejection reason, and blanket order release reference. Serves as the demand signal for MRP and production scheduling. May reference a parent blanket order for scheduling agreement releases.'
}

Table order.schedule_line {
  schedule_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the schedule line record.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent sales order header to which this schedule line belongs.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or material being scheduled for delivery.']
  split_parent_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original schedule line when this line is a split part.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Production scheduling allocates order schedule lines to exact stock locations for material staging; linking supports real-time allocation and capacity planning.']
  backorder_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the schedule line is backordered due to insufficient stock.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for traceability of the produced material.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'Date confirmed by the system for delivery after scheduling.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity confirmed by the system after ATP check and production planning.']
  confirmed_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the confirmed quantity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule line record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the line amount.']
  goods_issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the goods were posted to inventory for shipment.']
  handling_unit varchar [note: 'Identifier of the handling unit (e.g., pallet, container) used for the shipment.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms governing delivery responsibilities.']
  line_net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net monetary value of the confirmed quantity before taxes.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for quality and compliance tracking.']
  mrp_confirmed_availability_date date [note: 'Date when material availability was confirmed by MRP.']
  plant varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant responsible for producing the scheduled quantity.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority of the schedule line for production and delivery planning.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the customer originally requested for delivery.']
  requested_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity originally requested by the customer for this schedule line.']
  requested_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the requested quantity (e.g., PCS, KG, M3).']
  route varchar [note: 'Planned transportation route for the delivery.']
  schedule_line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the schedule line within the order line item.']
  schedule_line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the schedule line.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number for serialized items in the schedule line.']
  shipping_point varchar [note: 'Logistics location from which the goods will be shipped.']
  split_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the original order line has been split into multiple schedule lines.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse location where the goods will be staged before shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the schedule line record.']

  Note: 'Delivery schedule line within a sales order line item defining confirmed delivery quantities and dates for industrial manufacturing fulfillment. Captures schedule line number, confirmed quantity, delivery date, goods issue date, route, shipping point, and MRP-confirmed availability date. Critical for Available-to-Promise (ATP) checks and production scheduling alignment in the order fulfillment process.'
}

Table order.delivery {
  delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for delivery']
  account_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Delivery execution tracks the specific plant/site where goods are delivered; site info lives in customer.account_site.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Logistics expense accounting: delivery freight and handling costs are charged to a cost center for logistics cost analysis.']
  delivery_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who placed the original sales order.']
  delivery_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created the delivery record.']
  delivery_ship_to_party_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party to which the goods are shipped.']
  delivery_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who last modified the delivery record.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Link delivery to its originating order header; enables traceability and removes redundant order number fields.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Logistics KPI links each delivery to the project it serves, enabling on‑time delivery monitoring per project.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Needed for the \'Export/Import Compliance Verification\' report linking each delivery to the governing regulatory requirement for customs and hazardous material handling.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Inbound delivery records the supplying vendor; required for logistics tracking, on‑time delivery KPI, and supplier scorecards.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the delivery was actually received by the customer.']
  actual_goods_issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when goods were actually issued from the warehouse.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the street address for the delivery destination.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Code of the logistics carrier responsible for transportation.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the delivery destination address.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the delivery destination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  delivery_number varchar [note: 'External delivery document number assigned by SAP for tracking and communication.']
  delivery_state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the delivery destination address.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery.']
  delivery_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the delivery, e.g., stock shipment, return, consignment.']
  freight_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base freight charge before taxes and surcharges.']
  freight_tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the freight cost.']
  freight_total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total freight charge including taxes and surcharges.']
  handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Special handling notes for the carrier (e.g., fragile, keep upright).']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery contains hazardous or regulated materials.']
  is_backorder boolean [note: 'True when the delivery contains items that were previously on backorder.']
  is_partial_delivery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery fulfills only part of the sales order quantity.']
  number_of_items varchar [note: 'Count of individual line items included in the delivery.']
  planned_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the delivery is scheduled to arrive at the customer location.']
  planned_goods_issue_date date [note: 'Planned date on which goods are to be issued from inventory.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the delivery destination.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the delivery for scheduling purposes.']
  shipping_condition varchar [note: 'Incoterm defining responsibility and cost allocation between seller and buyer.']
  shipping_point varchar [note: 'Plant or warehouse location code where the delivery originates.']
  special_equipment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if special equipment (e.g., liftgate) is needed for delivery.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'True when the shipment must be kept within a temperature range.']
  total_gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Combined gross weight of all items in the delivery, expressed in kilograms.']
  total_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Combined volume of the delivery items, expressed in cubic meters.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier provided by the carrier to track the shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the delivery record.']
  window_end timestamp [note: 'End timestamp of the agreed delivery time window.']
  window_start timestamp [note: 'Start timestamp of the agreed delivery time window.']

  Note: 'Outbound delivery document created from a sales order authorizing the physical shipment of finished goods or automation systems from a plant or warehouse. Captures delivery number, delivery type, shipping point, planned goods issue date, actual goods issue date, total weight, volume, carrier, tracking number, and delivery status. Links order fulfillment to warehouse execution and logistics operations.'
}

Table order.delivery_item {
  delivery_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the delivery line item.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Provides shipment traceability to the specific engineered component, required for compliance and after‑sales service analysis.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Item‑level shipping cost allocation: each delivery item’s handling cost is assigned to a cost center for detailed expense tracking.']
  delivery_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent outbound delivery document to which this line belongs.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Associate each delivery item with the order line it fulfills, providing clear lineage.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Enables Delivery Traceability and Warranty Claim process by tying each delivered item to its master product record.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Delivery execution tracks the source stock location of shipped items for traceability and logistics reporting.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: Delivery‑to‑WBS tracking assigns each delivered item to a specific WBS element for cost roll‑up and variance analysis.']
  actual_goods_issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goods issue for this line was posted.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for the material, if batch‑managed.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the carrier responsible for transportation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery line record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for the delivery of this line item.']
  goods_movement_status varchar [note: 'Status of the goods issue transaction for this line.']
  handling_unit_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the handling unit (pallet, crate) containing the material.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Result of the quality inspection (e.g., pass, fail, rework).']
  inventory_management_indicator varchar [note: 'Flag indicating whether the line is subject to inventory management.']
  item_category varchar [note: 'Category defining the business purpose of the line (e.g., standard sale, return, consignment).']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the material.']
  movement_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the goods movement, if applicable.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP movement type code that defines the kind of goods movement.']
  pallet_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pallet on which the line item is loaded.']
  picking_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the picking process for this line.']
  plant varchar [note: 'Plant where the material is stocked for this delivery.']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'Customer‑promised delivery date agreed in the sales order.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Status of quality inspection for the delivered material.']
  quantity_delivered decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of material that has been delivered (goods issue).']
  quantity_ordered decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material ordered for this delivery line.']
  quantity_picked decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material that has been physically picked from inventory.']
  route varchar [note: 'Defined transportation route for the delivery.']
  serial_number_end varchar [note: 'Ending serial number of the range allocated to this line.']
  serial_number_start varchar [note: 'Starting serial number of the range allocated to this line, when serial‑managed.']
  shipping_condition varchar [note: 'Condition under which the goods are shipped (e.g., standard, express).']
  shipping_point varchar [note: 'Logistics point from which the goods are shipped.']
  special_stock_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicator for special stock types such as project stock, vendor consignment, etc.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Warehouse storage location from which the material is picked.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit in which quantities are expressed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the delivery line record.']
  volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Physical volume of the line item in cubic meters.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Gross weight of the line item in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within an outbound delivery document specifying the material, quantity, batch, storage location, and picking status for each product being shipped. Captures delivery item number, material number, delivery quantity, picked quantity, batch number, serial number range, storage location, and goods movement status. Supports warehouse picking, packing, and goods issue execution.'
}

Table order.order_status_event {
  order_status_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each immutable status event record.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order to which this status event belongs.']
  triggered_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory audit of order status changes mandates recording the employee who triggered each event.']
  business_reason_code varchar [note: 'Higher‑level business driver for the event (e.g., demand_spike, inventory_shortage).']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the status transition occurred (e.g., customer_request, production_complete).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional context or notes about the status change.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the source document (e.g., sales order number, delivery note) that triggered the event.']
  event_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the event for the given order, starting at 1.']
  event_source varchar [note: 'Originating system or application that generated the event (e.g., SAP SD, Opcenter MES).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the status change or milestone occurred in the order lifecycle.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the event – status transition, SLA breach detection, manual override, or system‑driven update.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'Order status after the event was applied. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|confirmed|released|scheduled|shipped|delivered|cancelled|on_hold|closed — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'Order status before the event was applied. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: created|confirmed|released|scheduled|shipped|delivered|cancelled|on_hold|closed — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this immutable event record was first written to the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the event record (should remain unchanged for immutable logs).']
  sla_actual_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time taken to reach the milestone, in minutes, used to evaluate SLA compliance.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'True when the event indicates a breach of the order Service‑Level Agreement.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Contracted SLA time target for the milestone, expressed in minutes.']
  triggered_by varchar [note: 'Entity that caused the status change – a human user, an automated system, or a scheduled batch.']

  Note: 'Immutable event log capturing every status transition and milestone in the order lifecycle including creation, confirmation, delivery, goods issue, POD, and SLA breach detection. Records event timestamp, previous and new status, triggering user or system, document change reference, business reason code, and breach indicator flag. Enables full order traceability, SLA compliance monitoring, and fulfillment performance analytics.'
}

Table order.order_rma {
  order_rma_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the RMA record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Return processing expense: RMA costs (inspection, restocking) are charged to a cost center for warranty expense reporting.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who initiated the return.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product to be sent as a replacement, if applicable.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original sales order linked to this RMA.']
  primary_order_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the RMA.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Supports the \'Return Compliance Assessment\' workflow, tracking regulatory obligations for returned items such as hazardous waste handling and reporting.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: RMA returns are sent back to the original supplier; essential for warranty claim processing and supplier return metrics.']
  tertiary_order_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User who performed the most recent update to the RMA.']
  actual_return_date date [note: 'Date the returned items were actually received.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the RMA.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RMA was approved.']
  authorized_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of items the system authorizes for return based on the original order.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier handling the return shipment.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total credit to be applied to the customer\'s account.']
  credit_memo_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a credit memo will be issued for this RMA.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  expected_return_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the returned items should arrive at the plant.']
  handling_fee decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fee charged for processing the return.']
  inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the returned items must undergo quality inspection.']
  is_damaged boolean [note: 'True if the returned item was received with damage.']
  is_repairable boolean [note: 'True if the returned item can be repaired rather than replaced.']
  is_warranty_claim boolean [note: 'True if the return is processed under a warranty agreement.']
  is_wrong_item boolean [note: 'True if the returned item does not match the original order.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary amount after tax and fees.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by service or sales staff.']
  order_rma_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the RMA.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RMA record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the RMA record.']
  refund_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount to be refunded to the customer before taxes and fees.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided when an RMA is rejected.']
  replacement_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of replacement units to be shipped.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RMA was initially requested by the customer or service team.']
  return_plant varchar [note: 'Plant or warehouse code where the returned items are to be received.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing why the product is being returned.']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing details about the return reason.']
  returned_quantity varchar [note: 'Actual number of units received back from the customer.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible RMA number assigned by the order management system.']
  rma_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the RMA (e.g., warranty, non‑warranty, repair, replacement).']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Method used to ship the returned product back to the plant.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the refund or credit.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Tracking identifier provided by the carrier for the return shipment.']

  Note: 'Return Material Authorization record managing the end-to-end return process for defective, damaged, or incorrectly shipped industrial products and automation systems. Captures RMA number, originating sales order reference, return reason code (quality defect, shipping damage, wrong item, warranty claim), authorized return quantity, return plant, credit memo indicator, inspection requirement flag, and RMA status. Integrates with returns processing workflows and quality non-conformance reporting.'
}

Table order.rma_line {
  rma_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the RMA line item.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Links returned items to the original engineered component for warranty claim evaluation and root‑cause analysis.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Line‑level return cost allocation: each returned item’s cost is tracked against a cost center for detailed RMA cost analysis.']
  delivery_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the delivery that originally supplied the returned material.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Sales order identifier for the replacement item shipped to the customer.']
  order_rma_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent RMA document to which this line belongs.']
  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work order created for reworking the returned item.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for the returned material.']
  condition_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the physical condition of the returned item (e.g., new, damaged, used).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RMA line record was created in the system.']
  credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary credit to be issued to the customer for this line.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the credit amount (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  disposition_action varchar [note: 'Business decision for the returned item: scrap, rework, restock, or replace.']
  disposition_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for the chosen disposition action.']
  inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned item must undergo quality inspection.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the quality inspection for the returned item.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the RMA document.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the returned material.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for additional comments or special handling instructions.']
  original_delivery_date date [note: 'Date on which the original delivery was posted.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date the returned material was received at the warehouse.']
  replace_flag boolean [note: 'True if the returned item will be replaced with a new unit.']
  replacement_part_number varchar [note: 'Material number of the replacement part, if a replace action is selected.']
  restock_bin varchar [note: 'Specific bin or storage location within the warehouse.']
  restock_location varchar [note: 'Logical location where the item will be restocked (e.g., plant, zone).']
  restock_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Quantity of the item to be returned to inventory after disposition.']
  restock_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the restocking operation.']
  restock_warehouse varchar [note: 'Warehouse identifier for the restocked inventory.']
  return_date date [note: 'Date the customer initiated the return request.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing why the material is being returned.']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the return reason.']
  returned_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Quantity of the material being returned on this line.']
  rework_flag boolean [note: 'True if the item requires rework before it can be restocked.']
  scrap_flag boolean [note: 'True if the returned item is to be scrapped.']
  scrap_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the item is being scrapped.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the returned unit, if applicable.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the returned quantity (e.g., EA, KG, L).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the RMA line record.']
  warranty_claim_flag boolean [note: 'True if the return is covered under a warranty claim.']
  warranty_claim_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warranty claim associated with this return.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within an RMA document specifying the material, quantity, and disposition instructions for each returned product. Captures RMA line number, material number, returned quantity, original delivery reference, serial numbers, batch number, condition code, disposition action (scrap, rework, restock, replace), and credit value. Supports quality inspection routing and inventory reintegration decisions.'
}

Table order.fulfillment_sla {
  fulfillment_sla_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the SLA record.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer to which this SLA applies; null if SLA is not customer‑specific.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Tie SLA directly to the order it governs, removing duplicate order number.']
  applicable_product_category_code varchar [note: 'Code of the product category that the SLA governs; null if not product‑category specific.']
  breach_action varchar [note: 'Action taken when the SLA is breached.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the SLA expires or is superseded; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the SLA becomes binding.']
  expedite_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SLA permits expedited processing for the associated orders.']
  fulfillment_sla_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the SLA purpose, scope, and any special conditions.']
  fulfillment_sla_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SLA.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the SLA was last reviewed for relevance and compliance.']
  max_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum quantity of items allowed per order under this SLA.']
  measurement_window_days varchar [note: 'Rolling period in days over which SLA performance is measured.']
  min_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity of items required per order to qualify for this SLA.']
  on_time_delivery_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum percentage of orders that must be delivered on or before the target lead time to meet the SLA.']
  order_confirmation_turnaround_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed time in hours between order receipt and order confirmation.']
  penalty_terms varchar [note: 'Textual description of financial or service penalties applied when the SLA is breached.']
  sla_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the SLA, used in contracts and reporting.']
  sla_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the SLA.']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SLA based on the entity it applies to (e.g., customer‑specific, order‑type, product‑category, geographic region).']
  sla_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the SLA, incremented on each change.']
  target_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Promised number of calendar days from order receipt to delivery.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SLA record.']

  Note: 'Service Level Agreement master record defining order fulfillment performance targets for customer accounts, order types, or product categories. Captures SLA code, target lead time days, on-time delivery threshold percentage, order confirmation turnaround hours, expedite eligibility, penalty terms, measurement window, and validity period. Used for automated breach detection against order_status_event timestamps and fulfillment KPI dashboards.'
}

Table order.goods_issue {
  goods_issue_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for goods_issue']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer receiving the goods.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who performed the goods issue posting.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Link goods issue to the order header for end‑to‑end tracking, removing redundant sales order number.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Goods issue transaction records the originating stock location, essential for inventory decrement, audit trails, and compliance reporting.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: Earned‑value reporting requires linking material issues to WBS elements to calculate actual cost versus planned value.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date when the goods were actually delivered to the customer.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for the material, if batch‑managed.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center responsible for the goods issue cost.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary values.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for delivery to the customer.']
  delivery_doc_number varchar [note: 'Outbound delivery document linked to the goods issue.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'System‑calculated expected delivery date based on lead times.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to external system such as carrier tracking number.']
  goods_issue_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the goods issue.']
  handling_unit varchar [note: 'Identifier of the handling unit (e.g., pallet) used for the issue.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms governing delivery responsibilities.']
  inventory_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account for inventory posting.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the goods issue was generated automatically by a system.']
  issue_number varchar [note: 'External document number assigned to the goods issue.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Master data identifier of the material issued.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'SAP movement type code indicating the nature of the goods issue.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary amount after tax.']
  plant varchar [note: 'Plant where the goods issue originated.']
  posting_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the goods issue posting.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the goods issue was posted in the source system.']
  profit_center varchar [note: 'Profit center associated with the revenue from this issue.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order associated with the material movement, if applicable.']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Quality inspection result for the issued material.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material issued.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text notes about the goods issue.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this record is a reversal of a previous goods issue.']
  route varchar [note: 'Planned transportation route for the shipment.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the issued item, if serialized.']
  shipping_point varchar [note: 'Logistics shipping point from which the goods are dispatched.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system that created the goods issue record.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Storage location from which the goods were issued.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount applicable to the goods issue, if any.']
  total_value_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the issued goods at standard cost.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the issued quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record.']
  valuation_area varchar [note: 'Organizational area for inventory valuation.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Method used for inventory valuation of the issued material.']

  Note: 'Goods issue posting event recording the physical departure of finished goods, automation systems, or components from a plant or warehouse against an outbound delivery document. Captures goods issue document number, posting date, movement type (standard issue, reversal), plant, storage location, material document number, total value at cost, and goods issue status. Triggers inventory reduction, revenue recognition eligibility, COGS posting, and billing due list creation. Represents the legal transfer of custody from manufacturer to carrier.'
}

Table order.proof_of_delivery {
  proof_of_delivery_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the proof of delivery record.']
  customer_contact_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or party that received the shipment.']
  delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.delivery. Business justification: Connect POD to its delivery, consolidating address info in delivery.']
  billed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount invoiced to the customer as a result of the delivery.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier that performed the shipment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the proof of delivery record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the billed amount.']
  delivery_condition varchar [note: 'Condition of the goods as reported by the recipient at receipt.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes describing any differences between ordered and received quantities or condition.']
  gps_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the delivery location captured at proof of delivery.']
  gps_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the delivery location captured at proof of delivery.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice generated from this proof of delivery.']
  pod_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic proof of delivery document (PDF, image, etc.).']
  pod_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the proof of delivery, often used in customer communications.']
  pod_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the POD record (e.g., captured electronically on device, paper form, or carrier EDI).']
  pod_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the proof of delivery was recorded at the delivery site.']
  proof_of_delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the proof of delivery record.']
  quantity_received decimal(15,3) [note: 'Number of units received by the customer.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the received quantity.']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the individual or entity that accepted the delivery.']
  recipient_signature varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., URL or hash) to the captured signature of the recipient.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Tracking identifier provided by the carrier for the shipment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the proof of delivery record.']

  Note: 'Record confirming the physical receipt and acceptance of shipped industrial goods by the customer at the delivery destination. Captures POD date, recipient name, signature reference, delivery condition (accepted, accepted with damage, rejected), quantity received, discrepancy notes, GPS coordinates of delivery, and POD source (electronic, paper, carrier EDI). Triggers final billing eligibility and transfer of risk confirmation in the order-to-cash process.'
}

Table order.amendment {
  amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the order amendment record.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer associated with the original order.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the approval action.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific order line that the amendment affects, if applicable.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original sales order that is being amended.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable sequential number assigned to the amendment for external reference.']
  amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the amendment.']
  amendment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time when the amendment became effective in the system.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Category of change requested in the amendment.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the amendment was approved or rejected.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the amendment.']
  change_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting documentation (e.g., PDF, SAP change order).']
  change_request_date date [note: 'Date on which the amendment request was submitted.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the requester or approver.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the amendment record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date when the amendment terms become valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date for the amendment validity, if applicable.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the amendment is considered critical for the customer or production schedule.']
  original_address varchar [note: 'Shipping address recorded on the order before amendment.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount before amendment (gross).']
  original_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned delivery date before the amendment.']
  original_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Unit price of the line item before amendment.']
  original_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity value before the amendment.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the amendment for processing.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing the business reason for the amendment.']
  requested_by varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the amendment was initiated by the customer or an internal user.']
  revised_address varchar [note: 'Updated shipping address after amendment.']
