  revised_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount after amendment (gross).']
  revised_delivery_date date [note: 'Updated delivery date after the amendment.']
  revised_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Unit price of the line item after amendment.']
  revised_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity value after the amendment.']
  sla_actual_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time (in minutes) taken to process the amendment.']
  sla_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Target service‑level agreement time (in minutes) for processing the amendment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the amendment originated.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the amendment record.']

  Note: 'Record capturing a customer-requested or internally-initiated change to an existing sales order, including quantity changes, delivery date revisions, address updates, pricing adjustments, or line item additions and deletions. Captures amendment number, amendment type, change request date, requested by (customer or internal), original value, revised value, approval status, approval date, and change document reference. Supports order change management, customer communication workflows, and audit trail requirements.'
}

Table order.blanket_order {
  blanket_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the blanket order.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the customer that owns the blanket agreement.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: A blanket purchase agreement is negotiated with a specific supplier; needed for contract management and spend analysis.']
  agreement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context or special notes about the blanket order.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'External business number assigned to the blanket agreement.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the blanket order received formal approval.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the blanket order.']
  blanket_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the blanket order.']
  contract_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status of the agreement.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of the agreement defining its purpose and governing rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the blanket order record was first created in the system.']
  cumulative_released_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity that has been released to the customer to date.']
  cumulative_released_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of all released quantities to date.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the agreement.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the products will be delivered (e.g., wholesale, direct).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the blanket agreement becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the blanket agreement expires or is terminated (null for open‑ended).']
  is_jit_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement supports JIT delivery scheduling.']
  last_release_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent call‑off release.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Smallest quantity the customer may release in a single call‑off.']
  minimum_order_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum order quantity.']
  next_release_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled release based on the release frequency.']
  plant varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant responsible for fulfilling releases under the agreement.']
  pricing_conditions varchar [note: 'Textual description of pricing terms, discounts, and rebates applicable to the agreement.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the committed quantity (e.g., each, kilogram).']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region (e.g., NA, EU) applicable to the agreement.']
  release_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled interval at which the customer may release call‑offs against the blanket order.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for the sales side of the agreement.']
  total_committed_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Aggregate quantity of product(s) the customer has committed to purchase over the agreement period.']
  total_committed_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the total committed quantity at the agreed pricing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the blanket order record.']

  Note: 'Long-term supply agreement or scheduling agreement from an industrial customer committing to purchase a defined quantity of products over a specified period at agreed pricing. Captures blanket order number, customer account, validity start and end dates, total committed quantity, total committed value, release frequency, minimum order quantity (MOQ), pricing conditions, and cumulative released quantity to date. Contains individual release call-offs as line-level records with release number, release date, quantity, requested delivery date, cumulative released quantity, and release status. Supports demand planning, JIT delivery scheduling, and production scheduling for high-volume OEM customers.'
}

Table order.blanket_order_release {
  blanket_order_release_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the blanket order release record.']
  blanket_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent blanket order (scheduling agreement) to which this release belongs.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product/material being released for delivery.']
  blanket_order_release_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the release.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date on which the blanket order release was created or issued.']
  release_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the release within the blanket order, used for ordering and tracking.']

  Note: 'Individual release call-off against a blanket order or scheduling agreement, specifying the quantity and delivery date for a specific shipment within the long-term supply commitment. Captures release number, blanket order reference, release date, release quantity, requested delivery date, cumulative released quantity, and release status. Enables JIT delivery scheduling and production demand triggering for OEM and high-volume industrial customers.'
}

Table order.pricing_condition {
  pricing_condition_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the pricing condition record.']
  condition_type_id varchar [note: 'Technical identifier linking to the master list of condition types.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order line to which this pricing condition applies.']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract or agreement that defines this pricing condition.']
  calculation_base varchar [note: 'Reference base used for the condition calculation (e.g., net price, gross price, quantity).']
  condition_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the pricing condition purpose or notes.']
  condition_effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the condition became effective for the order line.']
  condition_expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the condition expired or was superseded.']
  condition_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping identifier for related conditions (e.g., volume rebate group).']
  condition_note varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes or comments entered by users regarding the condition.']
  condition_origin varchar [note: 'Source of the condition – manually entered, system generated, or derived from a contract.']
  condition_priority varchar [note: 'Priority order used when multiple conditions could apply; lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  condition_rate decimal(15,6) [note: 'Percentage or rate applied by the condition (e.g., 5% discount).']
  condition_rate_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for the condition rate, such as "%" or "per_unit".']
  condition_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the order of this condition within the pricing procedure for the line.']
  condition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the condition.']
  condition_type varchar [note: 'Category of the pricing condition, such as base price, discount, surcharge, tax, or rebate.']
  condition_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value associated with the condition (e.g., discount amount, surcharge amount).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pricing condition record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the condition value is expressed.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary discount amount granted by the condition.']
  external_condition_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the condition in an external system or contract (e.g., supplier agreement reference).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the condition is currently active (true) or has been deactivated (false).']
  is_expedited boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the condition relates to an expedited delivery surcharge.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Resulting net monetary impact of the condition after applying value, rate, and taxes.']
  pricing_procedure_step varchar [note: 'Step number within the pricing procedure where this condition is applied.']
  scale_quantity decimal(15,2) [note: 'Quantity threshold at which a scale‑based price or discount becomes applicable.']
  scale_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the scale quantity break (e.g., EA, KG).']
  surcharge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary surcharge amount added by the condition.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary tax amount calculated for the condition.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code used to determine applicable tax rates.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage tax rate applied by the tax condition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the pricing condition record.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the pricing condition is no longer valid.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the pricing condition becomes valid.']

  Note: 'Pricing condition record applied to a sales order line capturing individual pricing elements that compose the final net price per the pricing procedure. Captures condition type (base price, material discount, freight surcharge, tax), condition value, currency, calculation base, scale quantity breaks, validity period, and pricing procedure step sequence. Supports complex industrial pricing scenarios including volume rebates, customer-specific discounts, surcharges for expedited delivery, and raw material price escalation clauses. Maintained independently when pricing agreements change mid-order or when retroactive price adjustments are applied.'
}

Table order.hold {
  hold_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the order hold record.']
  hold_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal user or system that created the hold.']
  hold_releasing_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that released the hold.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order to which this hold applies.']
  superseding_hold_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on hold (superseding_hold_id)']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial amount associated with the hold (e.g., credit limit amount).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Optional free‑form notes added by users.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hold is required for regulatory compliance (e.g., export control).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hold record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the hold amount.']
  hold_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the hold type for reporting.']
  hold_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier for the hold, used in communications and reporting.']
  hold_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the hold – whether it was triggered by an automated system rule or a manual user action.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the hold.']
  hold_type varchar [note: 'Category of the hold condition that blocks order fulfillment.']
  is_system_generated boolean [note: 'True if the hold was automatically created by a system rule; false if manually entered.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the hold after processing.']
  placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the hold became effective.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the hold for escalation handling.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description explaining why the hold was placed.']
  release_conditions varchar [note: 'Conditions that must be satisfied for the hold to be released.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the hold was released or cancelled.']
  target_resolution_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the hold should be resolved.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the hold record.']

  Note: 'Record of a hold or block placed on a sales order or order line that prevents fulfillment progression until the hold condition is resolved. Captures hold type (credit limit exceeded, export compliance review, quality quarantine, customer dispute, pricing approval pending), hold reason text, placed-by user or system trigger, hold start timestamp, target resolution date, release conditions, releasing authority, release timestamp, and hold outcome. Supports multiple concurrent holds per order. Enables operations teams to track blocked orders, measure hold duration KPIs, and ensure regulatory compliance gates are enforced before shipment.'
}

Table order.condition_type {
  condition_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for condition_type']
  parent_condition_type_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on condition_type (parent_condition_type_id)']
  amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Default monetary or percentage amount associated with the condition type, if applicable.']
  applicable_to varchar [note: 'Business object to which the condition type can be applied.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the condition value is entered manually, derived by the system, or calculated via a formula.']
  category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the condition type (e.g., pricing, tax, discount).']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to reference the condition type in transactional systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the condition type record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the purpose and usage of the condition type.']
  discount_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the condition type represents a discount.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the condition type becomes valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the condition type is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this condition type is the default for its category.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the condition type.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to resolve conflicts when multiple conditions apply (lower number = higher priority).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the condition type.']
  surcharge_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the condition type represents a surcharge.']
  tax_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the condition type represents a tax.']
  type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the condition value.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit used for the condition value (e.g., percent, USD, units).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the condition type record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for condition_type. Referenced by condition_type_id.'
}

Table billing.invoice {
  invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the invoice record.']
  compliance_product_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.product_certification. Business justification: Certification bodies charge fees; invoices must reference the product certification they cover for traceability.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost‑center assignment enables cost accounting and profitability analysis per invoice in manufacturing.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Audit: internal control requires recording which employee created each invoice for compliance and traceability.']
  credit_note_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the related credit note, if this invoice is a credit note.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer billed by this invoice.']
  debit_note_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the related debit note, if this invoice is a debit note.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: Lease billing process requires each invoice to reference the leased equipment for revenue recognition and asset tracking.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Required for General Ledger posting of invoice revenue; finance GL accounts aggregate revenue for financial statements.']
  hazardous_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.hazardous_substance. Business justification: Handling of hazardous substances incurs fees; invoicing requires linking to the specific substance record.']
  opportunity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.opportunity. Business justification: Revenue recognition ties invoices back to the originating sales opportunity for financial reporting and pipeline analysis.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: REQUIRED: Invoice‑Generation process needs to trace each invoice to its originating sales order for order‑to‑invoice reconciliation and audit reporting.']
  order_intake_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: Order‑to‑cash process maps each invoice to its originating order intake record for fulfillment and billing reconciliation.']
  permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Permit fees are invoiced; linking invoice to the permit records enables compliance audit of fee collection.']
  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: Invoice Generation process requires linking each invoice to its originating production work order for financial reporting and cost allocation; production work order is the natural source of billing.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Links invoice revenue to profit‑center for segment reporting required by internal management reports.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Project financial reporting requires each invoice to be tied to the originating project header for profitability analysis.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.quote. Business justification: Audit trail requires linking each invoice to the quote that defined pricing and terms after quote acceptance.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Filing fees are billed; the invoice must point to the specific regulatory filing it settles.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Tax compliance reporting requires each invoice to reference the governing regulatory requirement for tax calculation.']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying sales or service contract.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Commission reports need each invoice linked to the sales rep who closed the deal; essential for performance and payout calculations.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.request. Business justification: Required for Service Billing Report linking each invoice to the originating service request, enabling revenue tracking per service activity.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Invoice generation for each shipment requires linking invoice to the shipped order for traceability in the \'Shipment Billing\' process.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Primary shipping location per invoice is needed for traceability, hazardous material compliance, and dock scheduling.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse. Business justification: Warehouse of origin is required for logistics cost allocation, export reporting, and inventory valuation per invoice.']
  waste_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.waste_record. Business justification: Waste disposal services are billed; the invoice must reference the waste record for regulatory reporting.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address, if applicable.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City component of the billing address.']
  billing_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code of the billing address.']
  billing_period_end date [note: 'End date of the billing period covered by the invoice.']
  billing_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the billing period covered by the invoice.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the billing address.']
  billing_state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the billing address.']
  collection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the collection process for overdue invoices.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the invoice.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to the invoice.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Discount rate applied to the invoice before tax.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is due.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes, discounts, and adjustments.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice document.']
  is_self_billing boolean [note: 'True if the invoice is generated by the supplier on behalf of the customer (self‑billing).']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the invoice was issued.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after taxes and discounts.']
  number varchar [note: 'Official invoice number assigned by the billing system.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used by the customer to settle the invoice.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment for this invoice.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the payment terms applied to the invoice.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order reference supplied by the customer.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax applied to the invoice.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice is exempt from tax.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage for the invoice.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the invoice record.']

  Note: 'Core billing document representing a formal demand for payment issued to a customer for product sales, service contracts, or project-based engagements. Captures invoice number, issue date, due date, billing period, total amount, tax amount, discount amount, currency, invoice status (draft, issued, partially_paid, paid, overdue, cancelled, disputed, written_off), invoice type (standard, credit_note, debit_note, proforma, self_billing), payment terms code, billing address, PO reference, contract reference, and source transaction reference. Serves as the SSOT for credit notes (returns, pricing corrections, quality disputes, volume rebate settlements) and debit notes (price adjustments, surcharges, underbilling corrections) via the invoice_type discriminator. Also supports self-billing/evaluated receipt settlement (ERS) for consignment and VMI arrangements. Managed in SAP S/4HANA SD/FI billing document types (VF01/VF04).'
}

Table billing.invoice_line {
  invoice_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each invoice line record.']
  asset_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.work_order. Business justification: Detailed billing of labor/materials per work order requires line‑level association for cost allocation and reporting.']
  billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the billing cycle to which this line belongs.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.capa. Business justification: CAPA Expense Billing – expenses for corrective actions are tracked on invoice lines for cost allocation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Line‑level cost‑center allocation needed for detailed cost accounting and variance analysis.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Required for equipment usage charge‑back report linking each billed line to the specific machine that generated the usage.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: Service‑charge line items must identify the exact equipment serviced to allocate costs and support maintenance cost analysis.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Maps each line to the correct GL account for accurate revenue/expense posting.']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Inspection Service Billing – each inspection lot performed for a customer is charged as a line item on the invoice.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent invoice header to which this line belongs.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: REQUIRED: Revenue recognition and audit trails require linking each invoice line to the specific order line it bills.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Invoice line items need to reference the exact material being billed. Adding material_master_id (FK → inventory.material_master.material_master_id) creates a proper parent‑child relationship and remov']
  ncr_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.ncr. Business justification: NCR Cost Recovery – non‑conformance handling costs are billed to the customer via a dedicated invoice line.']
  original_invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original invoice line that this credit memo line reverses.']
  product_bundle_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.bundle. Business justification: When a bundle is sold, the invoice line must point to the bundle definition for bundle pricing, warranty, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Supports profit‑center based margin reporting per invoice line, a standard manufacturing finance practice.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Revenue recognition and product cost allocation require each invoice line to reference the sold SKU; this mapping is essential for financial reporting and compliance.']
  allocation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount allocated to the cost center (derived from allocation_percent).']
  allocation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the line amount allocated to the specified cost center.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the invoice line record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary values on this line.']
  deferred_revenue_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the line amount is deferred revenue (true) or recognized immediately (false).']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the discount applied to this line.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the line gross amount.']
  expense_account varchar [note: 'GL account used for any expense component associated with this line.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier from an external system (e.g., ERP, CRM) linked to this line.']
  invoice_line_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the product, service, or milestone billed.']
  is_bundle_line boolean [note: 'True if this line is part of a product bundle.']
  is_credit_memo boolean [note: 'True if this line represents a credit memo (negative amount).']
  is_royalty_line boolean [note: 'True if the line represents a royalty charge.']
  is_tax_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the unit price already includes tax.']
  line_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount for the line before tax and discount (quantity × unit_price).']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the invoice line.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the line content (product, service, project milestone, fee, or other charge).']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after tax and discount (line_amount + tax_amount – discount_amount).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments entered by users for this line.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code defining the payment terms applicable to this line.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line was posted to the financial ledger.']
  project_milestone_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project milestone or phase associated with this line.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of the product or service delivered (units, hours, meters, etc.).']
  revenue_account varchar [note: 'GL account used for revenue posting of this line.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Method used to recognize revenue for this line, per accounting standards.']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Royalty rate applied to the base amount, expressed as a percentage.']
  service_end_date date [note: 'End date of the service period covered by this line (if applicable).']
  service_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the service period covered by this line (if applicable).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary tax amount calculated for this line.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code that determines the tax rate and rules applicable to this line.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'True if the line is exempt from tax, false otherwise.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per single unit of the product or service before taxes and discounts.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity (e.g., each, kilogram, liter, meter, hour).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the invoice line.']

  Note: 'Individual line item on a customer invoice representing a specific product, service, or project milestone being billed. Captures line number, item description, SKU or service code, quantity, unit of measure, unit price, line amount, tax code, tax amount, discount percentage, discount amount, cost center, profit center, GL account, revenue recognition method, and deferred revenue flag. Supports detailed revenue allocation across product sales, service contracts, and project milestones per SAP S/4HANA CO-PA and revenue recognition standards.'
}

Table billing.payment {
  payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the payment transaction.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Bank reconciliation requires linking each payment to the corporate bank account record.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who made the payment.']
  processed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Payment processing audit: regulatory and internal policies require tracking which employee processed each payment.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Payments are allocated to projects to track cash flow against project budgets and enable project‑level cash reconciliation.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Anti‑money‑laundering and financial‑regulation monitoring ties each payment to the relevant regulatory requirement.']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Payments are recorded against the governing sales contract to monitor contract cash flow and compliance.']
  sales_order_intake_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: Advance or partial payments are tied to a specific order intake for accurate order financing and tracking.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: Commission calculations need each payment linked to the responsible sales rep for payout and performance metrics.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.request. Business justification: Needed for cash application to specific service requests, supporting the Service Payment Reconciliation process.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of the payment that has been allocated to invoices.']
  allocation_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment was allocated.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the payment allocation to invoices.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the allocation (full, partial, advance, on‑account).']
  amount_discount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the payment.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before any discounts, taxes, or fees.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after discounts, taxes, and fees.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the bank handling the payment.']
  bank_value_date date [note: 'Date on which the bank considers the funds available.']
  batch_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the batch that groups this payment with others for processing.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment was submitted.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the clearing document generated for the payment.']
  clearing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment clearing process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the payment.']
  discount_taken decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary discount amount applied to the payment.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the payment was expected according to invoice terms.']
  early_payment_discount_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an early‑payment discount was applied.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied if payment currency differs from company currency.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier from external payment gateway or bank.']
  fee_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any processing fees associated with the payment.']
  is_reconciled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment has been reconciled to invoices.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal notes regarding the payment.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Payment amount in the original currency before conversion.']
  original_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code of the original payment amount.']
  payment_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment was received or recorded.']
  payment_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the payment.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to make the payment.']
  payment_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible reference number assigned to the payment.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment.']
  reconciliation_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment was reconciled.']
  remittance_advice_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the remittance advice supplied by the payer.']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number for multiple payments within a batch or settlement.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the payment record.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current payment status.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the payment.']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the payment terms (e.g., NET30, EOM).']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment transaction.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment record.']

  Note: 'Record of a payment transaction received from a customer against one or more invoices, serving as the SSOT for all incoming customer payments, cash application, and payment-to-invoice allocation in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR. Captures payment reference number, payment date, amount, currency, exchange rate, payment method (wire transfer, ACH, check, credit card, letter of credit), bank account reference, clearing document number, payment status, remittance advice reference, and bank value date. Includes allocation details: per-invoice allocated amount, allocation date, allocation type (full, partial, advance, on_account), discount taken, early payment discount applied, clearing status, and allocation sequence. Supports partial payments, split payments across multiple invoices, and on-account payments pending allocation.'
}

Table billing.billing_account {
  billing_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the billing account record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assigning AR balances to cost centers supports internal cost‑allocation reporting.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Account management: each billing account is assigned a responsible account‑manager employee for relationship management.']
  payment_term_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.payment_term. Business justification: Billing accounts use a payment term; linking to the payment_term master eliminates the free‑text code and enforces referential integrity.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the billing account (e.g., customer or partner name).']
  account_number varchar [note: 'External account number used in invoicing and payment processing.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the account relationship (direct, distributor, OEM, end‑user).']
  auto_payment_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic payment processing is active.']
  billing_account_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or description about the billing account.']
  billing_account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing account.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the billing address.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address (optional).']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City component of the billing address.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code for the billing address.']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'How often invoices are generated for the account.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the billing address.']
  billing_state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the billing address.']
  close_date date [note: 'Date when the billing account was closed or deactivated (nullable).']
  collection_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the collections process for overdue balances.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the billing account record was first created.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure allowed for the account.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Credit rating assigned to the account based on financial risk assessment.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for billing.']
  current_ar_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Outstanding accounts‑receivable balance for the account.']
  dunning_procedure varchar [note: 'Assigned dunning strategy for overdue payments.']
  external_account_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the account in an external system (e.g., ERP, CRM).']
  invoice_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for delivering invoices to the customer.']
  last_payment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent payment received.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next invoice payment.']
  open_date date [note: 'Date when the billing account was created.']
  payment_due_day_of_month varchar [note: 'Numeric day of the month when payment is due (1‑31).']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used for the most recent payment transaction.']
  preferred_payment_method varchar [note: 'Customer’s preferred method for settling invoices.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is exempt from tax.']
  tax_exempt_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for tax exemption (e.g., government entity, nonprofit).']
  tax_region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code defining the tax jurisdiction.']
  tax_registration_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax registration identifier for the account holder.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the billing account record.']
  vat_number varchar [note: 'Value‑Added Tax identifier used for tax reporting.']

  Note: 'Billing-specific account master record representing the financial relationship between Manufacturing and a customer entity for invoicing, credit management, and collections purposes. Captures account number, account name, account type (direct, distributor, OEM, end_user), billing currency, credit limit reference, current AR balance, payment terms code, billing frequency (monthly, milestone, on_delivery), invoice delivery method (email, EDI, portal, paper), tax registration number, VAT ID, dunning procedure assignment, account status (active, blocked, dormant), and preferred payment method. Serves as the billing-domain anchor for all financial transactions — distinct from the customer master in the customer domain which owns identity and relationship data.'
}

Table billing.dispute {
  dispute_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the billing dispute record.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or external party that raised the dispute.']
  disputed_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice that is the subject of the dispute.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the accounts‑receivable analyst responsible for handling the dispute.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales order associated with the disputed invoice, if applicable.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.request. Business justification: Allows dispute management to reference the related service request, essential for the Service Dispute Resolution workflow.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dispute record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the disputed amount.']
  dispute_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer raised the billing dispute.']
  dispute_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the dispute for tracking in finance and customer service systems.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the dispute.']
  disputed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the charge being disputed.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'True if the dispute has been escalated to senior management or a specialized team.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the dispute for handling urgency.']
  reason_category varchar [note: 'Category describing why the dispute was raised. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pricing_error|quantity_discrepancy|quality_issue|delivery_shortfall|duplicate_invoice|contract_mismatch|tax_error — promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text explanation of why the dispute was raised.']
  resolution_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the dispute was resolved.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing the outcome and any actions taken.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Method used to resolve the dispute.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the dispute record.']

  Note: 'Formal record of a customer-raised dispute against an invoice or billing charge. Captures dispute case number, dispute date, disputed invoice reference, disputed amount, dispute reason category (pricing_error, quantity_discrepancy, quality_issue, delivery_shortfall, duplicate_invoice, contract_mismatch, tax_error), dispute status (open, under_review, resolved, escalated, closed), resolution type (credit_issued, payment_confirmed, partial_adjustment, rejected), resolution date, resolution notes, and responsible AR analyst. Supports CAPA-driven billing quality improvement.'
}

Table billing.collections {
  collections_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for collections']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer (debtor) associated with the collections case.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the collections agent responsible for the case.']
  case_close_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the collections case was closed or resolved.']
  case_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the context or notes for the collections case.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the collections case, used in communications and reporting.']
  case_open_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the collections case was initially created in the system.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the collections case.']
  case_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategic approach applied to the case (e.g., standard reminders, intensive follow‑up, legal action, or write‑off candidate).']
  collection_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that originated the dunning data (e.g., SAP S/4HANA FI‑AR).']
  collection_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the collections workflow.']
  communication_method varchar [note: 'Channel used for the most recent dunning communication.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the dunning notice record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the monetary values in the case.']
  customer_response_status varchar [note: 'Indicator of whether the customer has responded to the latest communication.']
  dunning_charges decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fees applied as part of the dunning process for the case.']
  dunning_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent dunning notice was issued.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current escalation level of the dunning process (1‑4).']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the case has been escalated beyond standard reminders.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the dunning notice to the originating SAP document or external ledger entry.']
  gross_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total outstanding receivable amount before any adjustments or dunning charges.']
  legal_action_flag boolean [note: 'True if legal proceedings have been initiated for the case.']
  net_exposure_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Outstanding amount after dunning charges are applied.']
  next_action_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next collection activity or follow‑up.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary remarks entered by the collections agent.']
  payment_arrangement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal payment arrangement is in place.']
  payment_arrangement_type varchar [note: 'Type of payment arrangement agreed with the customer.']
  promise_to_pay_date date [note: 'Date promised by the customer for payment of the outstanding amount.']
  promised_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount the customer committed to pay on the promise‑to‑pay date.']
  response_date date [note: 'Date when the customer provided a response to the dunning communication.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the dunning notice record.']
  write_off_candidate_flag boolean [note: 'True if the case is identified as a potential write‑off.']

  Note: 'Collections management record serving as the unified SSOT for the full receivables collections lifecycle — from initial overdue detection through escalation to legal action or write-off referral. At the case level: captures collections case ID, collections strategy (standard, intensive, legal, write_off_candidate), assigned collections agent, customer promise-to-pay date, promised amount, case status (open, in_progress, escalated, resolved, closed), case open date, and total case exposure amount. At the communication level: captures dunning level (1=reminder through 4=legal_action), dunning date, total overdue amount, dunning charges, communication method (email, letter, phone, legal), customer response status, response date, and next action date. Tracks the full collections escalation lifecycle per SAP S/4HANA FI-AR dunning procedures (FBMP/F150) and supports structured collections management for industrial B2B receivables with typical 30-90 day payment cycles.'
}

Table billing.revenue_recognition_event {
  revenue_recognition_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the revenue recognition event.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Revenue audit: linking the creator employee to each recognition event satisfies SOX‑type controls and audit trails.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or counterparty associated with the contract underlying the revenue event.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the billing document (invoice) linked to this revenue recognition event.']
  performance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific performance obligation within the contract that is being recognized.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Revenue recognition must comply with accounting standards (e.g., IFRS 15); each event references the governing regulatory requirement.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or description for any adjustment applied to the revenue event.']
  cost_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code for the cost of goods sold.']
  cost_of_goods_sold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost associated with the goods or services recognized in this revenue event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the revenue recognition event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary amounts.']
  deferred_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of revenue deferred (not yet recognized) associated with this event.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the recognized revenue ceases to be effective, if applicable.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the recognized revenue becomes effective for reporting.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier or reference number for the revenue recognition event.']
  is_adjusted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the revenue event includes an adjustment.']
  recognition_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the revenue recognition event.']
  recognition_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revenue recognition event was triggered.']
  recognition_type varchar [note: 'Method used to recognize revenue: point in time, over time, milestone, or percentage of completion.']
  recognized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of revenue that has been recognized in this event.']
  revenue_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which recognized revenue is posted.']
  revenue_recognition_event_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description or notes about the revenue recognition event.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system that generated the revenue recognition event.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the contract portion covered by this revenue event (recognized + deferred).']
  transaction_reference varchar [note: 'External reference number linking to related transaction records (e.g., invoice number).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the revenue recognition event record.']

  Note: 'Operational record capturing a revenue recognition event triggered by a contractual milestone, delivery confirmation, or service completion. Captures event ID, recognition date, recognition type (point_in_time, over_time, milestone, percentage_of_completion), recognized amount, deferred amount, contract performance obligation reference, billing document reference, revenue account, cost of goods sold amount, and recognition status (pending, recognized, reversed, adjusted). Supports IFRS 15 / ASC 606 compliance for revenue recognition across product sales, service contracts, and project engagements.'
}

Table billing.credit_limit {
  credit_limit_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the credit limit record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the analyst responsible for approving and maintaining the credit limit.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the credit limit.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit limit was approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the credit limit record was first created in the system.']
  credit_block_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the credit limit is currently blocked from further usage.']
  credit_check_method varchar [note: 'Method used to assess creditworthiness for this limit.']
  credit_horizon_days varchar [note: 'Number of days over which the credit exposure is evaluated.']
  credit_limit_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the credit limit, used in accounting and customer communications.']
  credit_limit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the credit limit record.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the credit limit is denominated.']
  current_exposure decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current amount of credit already utilized by the customer against this limit.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the credit limit becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the credit limit expires; null for open‑ended limits.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the credit limit was last reviewed by the credit analyst.']
  limit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum approved credit exposure amount for the account, expressed in the specified currency.']
  limit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the credit limit as individual (per customer) or group (aggregated across a corporate group).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next credit limit review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional remarks or justification related to the credit limit.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Risk classification of the credit limit: A=low, B=medium, C=high, D=blocked.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the credit limit record.']
  utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the credit limit that is currently used (current_exposure / limit_amount * 100).']

  Note: 'Credit management record defining the approved credit exposure limit for a billing account, including current utilization and risk classification. Captures credit limit amount, currency, credit limit type (individual, group), credit exposure current value, credit utilization percentage, credit risk category (A=low, B=medium, C=high, D=blocked), credit check method, credit horizon days, last review date, next review date, credit analyst ID, approval status, and credit block flag. Managed in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR Credit Management (FD32).'
}

Table billing.billing_schedule {
  billing_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each billing schedule record.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Schedule ownership: billing schedules are created by a specific employee; linking supports accountability and reporting.']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the contract to which this billing schedule line belongs.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Work Breakdown Structure element of the project linked to this billing milestone.']
  actual_billed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount that was actually invoiced for this milestone.']
  actual_billing_date date [note: 'Date on which the billing was actually executed.']
  billing_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the planned billing amount.']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing schedule line.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the billing schedule record was first created in the system.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Discount subtracted from the planned billing amount, if any.']
  is_retention_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a retention amount is associated with this milestone.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the schedule line within the contract billing schedule.']
  milestone_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the billing milestone purpose or deliverable.']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Category of the billing milestone (e.g., advance payment, progress billing, final billing, retention release).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text field for additional comments or special instructions.']
  percentage_complete_trigger decimal(5,2) [note: 'Project completion percentage that triggers this billing milestone.']
  planned_billing_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount planned to be invoiced for this milestone.']
  planned_billing_date date [note: 'Date on which the billing amount is scheduled to be invoiced.']
  retention_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount held as retention for this milestone, if applicable.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the planned billing amount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the billing schedule record.']

  Note: 'Planned billing milestone schedule for project-based or contract-based engagements, defining when specific amounts are to be invoiced based on project progress, delivery milestones, or contractual dates. Captures schedule line number, planned billing date, planned billing amount, billing milestone description, milestone type (advance_payment, progress_billing, final_billing, retention_release), percentage complete trigger, actual billing date, actual billed amount, billing status (planned, ready_to_bill, billed, on_hold, cancelled), contract reference, and project WBS element reference. Supports project billing for industrial automation system installations (typically 12-36 month delivery cycles) and milestone-based revenue recognition per IFRS 15 variable consideration guidance.'
}

Table billing.tax_determination {
  tax_determination_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the tax determination record.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the billing document (invoice) to which this tax determination applies.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or service line item that the tax is calculated for.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the tax determination record was created.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the tax line within the invoice.']
  reverse_charge_indicator boolean [note: 'True if reverse charge mechanism applies for this tax line.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary amount of tax computed for this line.']
  tax_base_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Monetary amount on which the tax rate is applied.']
  tax_calculation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to compute the tax (standard, manual entry, tax engine, rule‑based).']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Internal or regulatory tax code that identifies the specific tax rule applied.']
  tax_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the tax amount (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction is exempt from tax (true = exempt).']
  tax_exempt_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or justification for tax exemption, if applicable.']
  tax_jurisdiction_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the tax jurisdiction (e.g., USA, DEU).']
  tax_jurisdiction_region varchar [note: 'Region, state, or province code within the country where tax is applied (e.g., CA for California).']
  tax_line_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the tax line (e.g., "EU VAT 20% on machinery").']
  tax_line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the tax line.']
  tax_override_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Manually entered tax amount that overrides the calculated value.']
  tax_override_flag boolean [note: 'True if the tax amount has been manually overridden.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage.']
  tax_registration_number varchar [note: 'Vendor or customer tax registration identifier (e.g., VAT number).']
  tax_reporting_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (e.g., 2023Q1) for which the tax is reported.']
  tax_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that generated the tax determination (e.g., SAP Tax Service, Vertex).']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'Category of tax applied (e.g., VAT, GST, sales tax, withholding tax, excise duty).']
  tax_validated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tax calculation has been validated against regulatory rules.']
  tax_validated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the tax validation was performed.']
  taxable_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the product/service that is subject to tax calculation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the tax determination record.']

  Note: 'Tax calculation and compliance record for billing documents, capturing jurisdiction-specific tax codes, rates, exemptions, and computed tax amounts required for multi-country tax reporting. Captures tax country, tax region, tax code, tax type (VAT, GST, sales_tax, withholding_tax, excise_duty), tax base amount, tax rate percentage, tax amount, tax exempt flag, tax exempt reason, tax jurisdiction code, tax registration validation, reverse charge indicator, and tax reporting period. Supports cross-border industrial equipment sales requiring complex tax determination across EU VAT, US sales tax nexus, Indian GST, and withholding tax regimes. Essential for tax audit compliance and automated tax engine integration (Vertex, SAP Tax Service).'
}

Table billing.advance_payment {
  advance_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the advance payment record.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the bank account where the advance payment was deposited.']
  clearing_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice that clears (partially or fully) the advance payment.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who provided the advance payment.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales order associated with the advance payment.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project or contract to which the advance payment is linked.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Advance payments are recorded against a specific purchase order for supplier cash‑flow tracking and PO reconciliation.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_contract. Business justification: Captures advance payments against a service contract, required for contract financial tracking and cash forecasting.']
  advance_payment_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier assigned to the advance payment (e.g., AP-2024-000123).']
  advance_payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the advance payment.']
  advance_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the advance payment purpose.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross amount of the advance payment received.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the advance payment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the advance payment (e.g., EUR, USD).']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any discount applied to the advance payment.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert foreign currency amount to the company code currency, if applicable.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the advance payment amount is subject to tax.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for additional information or remarks about the advance payment.']
  original_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original amount received in the payment\'s foreign currency before conversion.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the payment was submitted.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to make the advance payment.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the payment receipt generated by the finance system.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the advance payment was received.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the request that initiated the advance payment.']
  special_gl_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the payment is posted to a special general ledger account.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the advance payment, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the advance payment record.']

  Note: 'Record of advance or down payments received from customers prior to invoice issuance, typically for large capital equipment orders (>€100K), long-term project contracts, or custom automation system builds. Captures advance payment reference, receipt date, advance amount, currency, advance type (down_payment, progress_payment, retention, security_deposit), associated sales order or project reference, clearing status (open, partially_cleared, fully_cleared), clearing invoice reference, advance payment request reference, and special GL indicator. Distinct from the payment product which handles post-invoice cash application — advance_payment manages pre-invoice receipts requiring special GL treatment and progressive clearing as project milestones are invoiced. Managed in SAP S/4HANA FI-AR as special GL transactions (F-29/F-48).'
}

Table billing.write_off {
  write_off_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the write-off record.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer whose receivable is being written off.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the write‑off.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Write‑off entries must be posted to a GL account for audit trails and financial statements.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the original invoice linked to this write‑off.']
  amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gross monetary amount written off (before any recovery).']
  approval_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level that approved the write‑off.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or justification details for the write‑off.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the write‑off record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the write‑off amount.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the receivable was written off (e.g., bankruptcy, dispute settled, aged debt, legal settlement, goodwill).']
  recovery_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount recovered after the write‑off was posted.']
  recovery_date date [note: 'Date on which the recovery amount was received.']
  recovery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any portion of the written‑off amount has been subsequently recovered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the write‑off record.']
  write_off_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the write‑off for tracking and audit purposes.']
  write_off_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the write‑off (e.g., pending approval, approved, rejected, closed).']
  write_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the write‑off action was executed in the source system.']

  Note: 'Record of a bad debt write-off action taken against an uncollectable receivable balance. Captures write-off reference, write-off date, write-off amount, currency, original invoice references, write-off reason (bankruptcy, dispute_settled, aged_debt, legal_settlement, goodwill), approval level, approver ID, GL account for bad debt expense, recovery flag, recovery amount (if subsequently collected), and write-off status. Supports bad debt provisioning and financial reporting per IFRS/GAAP requirements.'
}

Table billing.intercompany_invoice {
  intercompany_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the intercompany invoice record.']
  billing_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Intercompany invoices belong to a billing account; adding the FK creates the required parent‑child relationship and prevents the billing_account table from being isolated.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Intercompany invoicing requires linking to the underlying PO to reconcile internal procurement and billing transactions.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Transfer‑pricing regulations require each intercompany invoice to be linked to the applicable regulatory requirement.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice was approved for posting.']
  billing_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Gross amount billed before taxes, discounts, or mark‑ups.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional notes or explanations.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center associated with the intercompany transaction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code of the invoice amounts.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total discount granted on the invoice.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment must be received.']
  elimination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice amount should be eliminated during group consolidation.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency exchange rate to the reporting currency at posting time.']
  external_invoice_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the external (customer‑facing) invoice linked to this intercompany invoice.']
  intercompany_markup_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage markup applied to the transfer price for intercompany profit.']
  invoice_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the intercompany invoice was created in the source system.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'External invoice number assigned by the issuing entity, used for legal and audit purposes.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the intercompany invoice.']
  issuing_company_code varchar [note: 'Code of the legal entity that issues the intercompany invoice.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net amount after tax and discount, representing the amount payable.']
  original_currency_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Invoice amount expressed in the original transaction currency before conversion.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms governing when payment is due.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice was posted to the general ledger.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Project identifier if the invoice is linked to a specific internal project.']
  receiving_company_code varchar [note: 'Code of the legal entity that receives the intercompany invoice.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the invoice represents a reversal/credit note.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the invoice was settled.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current settlement state of the invoice.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total tax applied to the invoice.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction code applied to the invoice.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage for the invoice.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Category of the intercompany transaction driving the invoice.']
  transfer_price_basis varchar [note: 'Basis used to calculate the transfer price (e.g., cost, market, list).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the invoice record.']

  Note: 'Billing document issued between legal entities within the Manufacturing group for intercompany product transfers, shared services, or intragroup project charges. Captures intercompany invoice number, issuing company code, receiving company code, transaction type (goods_transfer, service_charge, cost_allocation, royalty, management_fee), billing amount, transfer price basis, currency, intercompany markup percentage, corresponding external invoice reference, elimination flag for consolidation, and posting status. Supports intercompany reconciliation and group financial consolidation.'
}

Table billing.payment_term {
  payment_term_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the payment term record.']
  base_payment_term_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payment_term (base_payment_term_id)']
  allowed_payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channels through which payment can be made (e.g., online portal, mobile app).']
  allowed_payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instruments permitted under this term.']
  applicable_invoice_type varchar [note: 'Invoice categories to which this payment term can be applied.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment term record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency applicable to the payment term.']
  early_payment_discount_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date during which the early‑payment discount is available.']
  early_payment_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied when payment is made within the early‑payment window.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the payment term becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the payment term expires; null for open‑ended terms.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Additional days after the net due date before penalties apply.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this term is the default for new customers or contracts.']
  is_tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether taxes are waived for transactions using this term.']
  max_discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Upper limit on the monetary value of any discount granted under this term.']
  min_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum amount that must be paid per invoice under this term.']
  net_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days after invoice date when payment is due (e.g., Net 30).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special conditions related to the payment term.']
  payment_term_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payment term.']
  penalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Interest or fee percentage charged on overdue amounts.']
  penalty_type varchar [note: 'Method of applying the penalty (percentage of overdue amount or flat fee).']
  tax_exempt_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for tax exemption (e.g., government incentive, nonprofit status).']
  term_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code used to reference the payment term in contracts and invoices.']
  term_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payment term (e.g., Net, Discount, Milestone, Retention, Custom).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment term record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the payment term record for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Payment terms master defining standard and customer-specific payment conditions (Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30) applied to billing documents and customer accounts'
}

Table billing.billing_cycle {
  billing_cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for billing_cycle']
  previous_billing_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on billing_cycle (previous_billing_cycle_id)']
  billing_day_of_month varchar [note: 'Day of month on which billing is generated (1‑31).']
  billing_weekday varchar [note: 'Weekday on which billing occurs for weekly cycles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday|Saturday|Sunday — promote to reference product]']
