  cutoff_day varchar [note: 'Day of month that defines the billing period cutoff.']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'External code used to identify the billing cycle.']
  cycle_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the billing cycle.']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Category of the billing cycle indicating its recurrence pattern.']
  default_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code used for amounts in this cycle.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the billing cycle.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the billing cycle ends; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the billing cycle becomes effective.']
  fixed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary amount charged each billing period when Fixed Amount Flag is true.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the due date before a payment is considered overdue.']
  interval_count varchar [note: 'Number of intervals between billing occurrences.']
  interval_unit varchar [note: 'Time unit for the interval count.']
  is_fixed_amount boolean [note: 'True if the billing cycle uses a fixed amount for each period.']
  is_prorated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the billing cycle supports prorated charges.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the billing cycle record was created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the billing cycle record.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the billing cycle.']
  tax_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether taxes are included in the billed amount.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for billing_cycle. Referenced by billing_cycle_id.'
}

Table service.request {
  request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for request']
  account_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Associates request with specific site; required for Site‑Level Service Metrics and dispatch planning.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: When parts are shipped for a request, the selected carrier is recorded for SLA monitoring and freight cost reconciliation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for internal charge‑back of service request costs; used in cost allocation reports and budgeting.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who owns the asset.']
  customer_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Links service request to the contacting person; needed for Request Ownership report and escalation workflow.']
  delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.delivery. Business justification: After‑delivery service tickets reference the delivery record to verify shipment details and delivery‑based SLA compliance.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Service request handling requires linking to the exact device (device_registry) for remote diagnostics and parts lookup, a core process in field service.']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: Linking a service request to the originating Engineering Change Notice ensures accurate impact analysis and audit trails.']
  eco_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: Service requests generated from an Engineering Change Order need the ECO reference to coordinate field updates and compliance reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the field technician or support engineer assigned to the request.']
  engineering_revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.revision. Business justification: Links service request to the specific engineering revision that introduced the issue, enabling root‑cause analysis and traceability in the Service‑Engineering handoff report.']
  installed_base_id varchar [note: 'FK to service.installed_base.installed_base_id — Service requests must link to the installed base record to identify which customer asset is being serviced, enabling warranty validation and service history tracking.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Service requests are raised for a specific line item; linking to order_line supports root‑cause analysis and warranty validation per product.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Service request part usage requires identifying the material master of the part consumed; the Parts Usage report relies on this link.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Project Service Request Tracking report ties each service request to its originating project for cost and schedule impact analysis.']
  request_asset_equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'FK to asset.equipment_register']
  request_equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the installed equipment or system affected by the request.']
  sales_order_intake_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: Installation or repair requests are triggered by a sales order intake; linking supports the Service Request Fulfillment dashboard that tracks requests against originating orders.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service contract governing the request.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Service request fulfillment often ships replacement parts; linking request to the shipment that fulfills it enables tracking and cost allocation.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Required for Service Request processing: linking each request to the exact product master enables warranty validation, parts lookup, and service performance reporting.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Service Parts Allocation process assigns a stock location to each service request for part pick‑up; linking request to stock_location enables the allocation report.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Service request fulfillment often requires parts from a specific supplier; linking enables parts cost allocation and supplier performance reporting.']
  actual_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final monetary cost incurred for the service after completion.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Originating communication channel through which the request was submitted.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the request reached a closed state.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for communications related to the request.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for the customer contact.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the request was first recorded in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for cost fields.']
  due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the request should be resolved per SLA.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating how many times the request has been escalated.']
  estimated_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected monetary cost of the service before execution.']
  parts_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost of parts consumed for the request.']
  parts_used varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of part numbers used during the service.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Urgency level used to drive routing and escalation.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier assigned to the request (e.g., CASE‑00012345).']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service request.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Category of the request indicating the nature of the support needed.']
  resolution_deadline date [note: 'Calculated deadline based on SLA tier and priority.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of the actions taken to resolve the request.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified underlying cause of the reported issue after investigation.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the service type performed.']
  site_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the site location.']
  sla_actual_hours varchar [note: 'Actual number of hours taken to resolve the request.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours allowed to resolve the request per SLA tier.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Service level agreement tier defining response and resolution targets.']
  symptom_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the issue reported by the customer.']
  travel_distance_km decimal(8,2) [note: 'Estimated distance in kilometers from technician base to site.']
  travel_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated travel time for the technician to reach the site.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the request record.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the asset warranty expires.']
  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset is covered by an active warranty at the time of the request.']

  Note: 'Core transactional entity representing a customer-initiated after-sales service request or support ticket for installed automation systems and equipment. Captures request type (technical support, field service, warranty claim, RMA, complaint), priority, SLA tier, originating channel (phone, email, portal, field), reported symptom or issue description, affected installed base asset, site location, and full lifecycle status (open, in-progress, pending-customer, resolved, closed). Maps to Salesforce Service Cloud Case object. SSOT for all post-sale support interactions.'
}

Table service.service_contract {
  service_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the service contract.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Contracts often cover specific components; linking enables contract scope reporting and component‑level service level agreement tracking.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.control_system. Business justification: Contracts frequently cover specific control systems; linking provides contract‑to‑system traceability for SLA and compliance audits.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Service contracts generate financial transactions. Adding cost_center_id (FK → finance.cost_center.cost_center_id) enables proper cost allocation for service revenue.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Required for Contract Management report linking each service contract to the owning customer account; contracts are always signed with a specific customer.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Service contract revenue and expense postings need a GL account for proper accounting entries.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Contract creation process uses the original sales order as the basis for the service agreement; linking enables contract‑order reporting and compliance tracking.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Contract ownership accountability report links contract to owning employee for HR and compliance tracking.']
  product_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Contracts are defined per product family; the FK supports contract pricing, compliance, and coverage reports that aggregate by family.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis per service contract requires linking to a profit center for financial reporting.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Service Contract Management for Projects links contracts to the project they support, enabling contract compliance reporting.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Contract compliance tracking: each service contract must reference the regulatory requirement it satisfies for audit and reporting.']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Service contract execution depends on the original sales contract to define scope, billing, and warranty; linking enables the Service Contract Management report that reconciles service obligations wit']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.rep. Business justification: A sales representative owns the service contract relationship; linking supports the Service Contract Ownership dashboard for account management.']
  sales_territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.territory. Business justification: Service contracts are managed within sales territories; linking enables the Territory Service Coverage KPI linking contracts to regional performance.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the contract has been amended.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the contract will automatically renew without manual intervention.']
  billing_cycle_day varchar [note: 'Day of month when billing occurs (1‑31).']
  billing_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the customer is invoiced for the contract.']
  contract_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the contract for reporting and analytics.']
  contract_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the contract purpose and scope.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the contract, used in customer communications and invoicing.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of service agreement defining the billing and delivery model.']
  contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the contract before discounts and taxes.']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or functional extent of the contract coverage.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the contract value.']
  discount_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the base contract value.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or is scheduled to end; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes binding.']
  escalation_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person to contact for escalations.']
  escalation_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for escalation contact.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent contract amendment.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the manager overseeing the contract.']
  net_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contract value after discount and tax adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or internal notes related to the contract.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition (e.g., Net 30, Net 45).']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of required regulatory approvals for the contract.']
  renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract is set to auto‑renew at expiry.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Length of the renewal period in months when auto‑renewal is enabled.']
  resolution_time_target_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum time in hours to resolve a service request.']
  response_time_target_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum time in hours to acknowledge a service request.']
  service_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract.']
  service_tier varchar [note: 'Tier of service provided under the contract.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the contract record.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the current contract status, if applicable.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate for the contract value.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract was terminated, if applicable.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for contract termination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  uptime_guarantee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of time the covered equipment is guaranteed to be operational.']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'Date when the warranty period ends.']
  warranty_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a warranty is bundled with the service contract.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the warranty period begins.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing a formal after-sales service agreement between Manufacturing and a customer organization covering installed automation systems or equipment. Captures contract header: type (preventive maintenance, full-service, time-and-material, extended warranty), coverage scope, SLA commitments (response time, resolution time, uptime guarantee), start/end dates, renewal terms, billing frequency, and contract value. Includes contract line items: each line covering a specific installed asset or product family with line-level coverage type, SLA tier, pricing, coverage dates, and renewal flag. Serves as the SSOT for all service entitlement, coverage data, and contract profitability analysis at both header and line-item granularity.'
}

Table service.service_warranty {
  service_warranty_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the warranty record.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Component‑level warranty tracking requires knowing which engineered component the warranty applies to for claim processing.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Warranty claim costs are allocated to a cost center for internal cost tracking and budgeting.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who owns the warranty.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Warranty records must reference the exact device (device_registry) to validate coverage during service events, required by warranty claim processes.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating sales order that generated the warranty.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Warranty Tracking per Project records warranty coverage for assets installed under a specific project.']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Warranty terms are defined in the sales contract; linking enables the Warranty Coverage audit to ensure warranties are honored per contract.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Warranty validation often requires the original shipment record as proof of delivery; linking warranty to that shipment satisfies compliance audits.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the product that the warranty protects.']
  claims_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'True if the warranty permits service claims.']
  claims_remaining varchar [note: 'Number of warranty service claims still available to the customer.']
  coverage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary limit of liability for the warranty.']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty covers a single product, an entire system, or a site installation.']
  coverage_terms varchar [note: 'Textual description of what components or services are covered under the warranty.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for the coverage amount.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the digital copy of the warranty contract.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the warranty coverage becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the warranty coverage expires; null for open‑ended warranties.']
  exclusions varchar [note: 'Textual description of items or conditions not covered by the warranty.']
  extended_until date [note: 'Date to which the warranty has been extended, if applicable.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the warranty in its lifecycle.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by service personnel.']
  product_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number of the product covered by the warranty.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the warranty record was first created.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the warranty record.']
  registration_date date [note: 'Date when the warranty was entered into the warranty management system.']
  registration_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty has been formally registered in the system.']
  renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the warranty is eligible for renewal.']
  renewal_terms varchar [note: 'Textual description of the conditions and pricing for warranty renewal.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Tier of service provided under the warranty.']
  transferability_flag boolean [note: 'True if the warranty can be transferred to a new owner.']
  warranty_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the warranty by the sales or service system.']
  warranty_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the warranty offering.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing warranty coverage records for manufactured products, automation systems, and electrification solutions sold to customers. Captures warranty type (standard, extended, parts-only, labor-included), warranty start and end dates, coverage terms, exclusions, transferability flag, registration status, and originating sales order reference. Serves as the SSOT for warranty entitlement validation across service and field operations.'
}

Table service.service_rma {
  service_rma_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the Return Material Authorization record.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who initiated the RMA.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: RMA processing for returned equipment needs the device_registry reference to track serial numbers, firmware version, and disposition.']
  inbound_delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.inbound_delivery. Business justification: RMA returns are processed as inbound deliveries; linking the RMA to its inbound delivery enables traceability of returned goods.']
  primary_service_sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or component being returned.']
  processed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: RMA audit requires recording which employee processed the return for traceability.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: RMA Processing linked to Project records returns and repairs as part of the project\'s warranty management.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: RMA process may be subject to specific regulatory reporting (e.g., hazardous material return), requiring linkage to the governing regulatory requirement.']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: RMA processing must reference the sales contract that provided the product; linking supports the RMA Reconciliation report for contract‑based returns.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service contract governing the RMA, if applicable.']
  service_warranty_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warranty contract covering the returned item, if applicable.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: RMA processing must track which supplier handles the returned or replacement product for warranty claims and cost recovery.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics provider handling the return shipment.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the RMA process complies with relevant regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RMA record was first created in the system.']
  credit_note_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the credit note issued for the RMA, if any.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  disposition varchar [note: 'Final action taken for the returned item.']
  disposition_date timestamp [note: 'Date when the disposition decision was recorded.']
  fault_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the defect or issue reported by the customer.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes captured during inspection.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the post-receipt inspection.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RMA is considered critical for business operations.']
  is_under_warranty boolean [note: 'True if the returned item is covered by an active warranty.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form information related to the RMA.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units being returned under this RMA.']
  received_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the returned items were received at the service center.']
  refund_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount refunded to the customer, if applicable.']
  regulatory_report_flag boolean [note: 'True if this RMA must be reported to a regulatory body.']
  repair_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cost incurred to repair the returned item.']
  replacement_serial_number varchar [note: 'Serial number of the replacement unit, if applicable.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer submitted the RMA request.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why the item is being returned.']
  return_shipment_date timestamp [note: 'Date the customer shipped the item back to the service center.']
  return_shipment_method varchar [note: 'Logistics method used for the return shipment.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the RMA request.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number of the specific unit returned.']
  service_rma_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RMA.']
  shipment_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking identifier for the return shipment.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the returned quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the RMA record.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the associated warranty expires.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity representing a Return Material Authorization record for defective, damaged, or warranty-eligible equipment and components returned by customers. Captures RMA number, return reason code, fault description, return authorization date, disposition decision (repair, replace, credit, scrap), return shipment tracking, inspection outcome, and resolution status. Manages the full RMA lifecycle from customer request through disposition and closure.'
}

Table service.field_service_order {
  field_service_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the field service work order.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: The carrier used to transport items for a field service order is needed for performance reporting and carrier cost tracking.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.control_system. Business justification: Field service orders often target a specific control system for maintenance; the order must reference that control system for scheduling and compliance reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Field service order expenses must be charged to a cost center for regional budgeting and cost tracking.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who owns the equipment or requested the service.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific asset or equipment to be serviced.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Field Service Order Allocation to Capital Projects ensures labor and parts costs are charged to the correct project budget.']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Field service orders generate revenue against the sales contract; linking powers the Field Service Billing report aligning service orders with contract terms.']
  service_engineer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the field service engineer assigned to execute the work.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.shipment. Business justification: Field service orders may generate shipments of tools or spare parts; associating the order with its shipment supports logistics coordination.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real end date‑time when the technician completed the work.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real start date‑time when the technician began work on site.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the service site address.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Overall result of the work order execution.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the service site.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  customer_signature_status varchar [note: 'Status of the customer\'s sign‑off on the completed work.']
  labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cost of labor hours billed for the service.']
  labor_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total billable labor hours recorded for the work order.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the service location.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the work order in its processing lifecycle.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the service location.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible identifier assigned to the work order for tracking and customer communication.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the service activity.']
  outcome_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing the outcome of the service activity.']
  parts_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cost of parts consumed during the service.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the service site.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Indicates the urgency for scheduling and resource allocation.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the work order record was first persisted.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the work order record.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the service request was initially created by the customer or internal system.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of how the issue was resolved.']
  root_cause_code varchar [note: 'Categorized reason for any service failure or deviation.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned end date‑time for the service visit.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start date‑time for the service visit.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the service request based on urgency and purpose.']
  service_contract_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier of the service contract governing the work.']
  service_level_agreement_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the SLA terms applicable to this work order.']
  site_address varchar [note: 'Street address of the customer site where service is performed.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the service site address.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the work order total.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate discount applied to the work order.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of all charge components before discounts and taxes.']
  total_net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after discounts and taxes.']
  travel_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost associated with travel time and distance.']
  travel_distance_km decimal(8,2) [note: 'Distance traveled by the technician to the service site, measured in kilometers.']
  travel_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Billable travel time incurred by the technician to reach the site.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the applicable warranty for the equipment expires.']
  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service is covered under an active warranty.']
  work_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the tasks to be performed during the service visit.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity representing a dispatched field service work order for on-site technical service, installation, commissioning, preventive maintenance, or repair of customer-installed automation systems and equipment. Captures work order type, assigned field service engineer, scheduled and actual visit dates, site address, work performed description, parts consumed, labor hours, travel time, customer sign-off status, and completion outcome. Linked to originating service request.'
}

Table service.installed_base {
  installed_base_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for installed_base']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Required for maintenance teams to identify which component version is installed on each equipment for service actions and spare parts planning.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Needed for Installed Base inventory report to attribute each equipment record to its customer account for warranty and service planning.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Installed base records are linked to IoT device entries to enable real‑time monitoring and predictive maintenance, a standard practice in smart factories.']
  installed_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Installation liability and warranty claims depend on the employee who performed the installation.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.material_master. Business justification: Installed Base records need the material master for product specifications, supporting the Installed Base Specification report.']
  node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_node. Business justification: Installed equipment is located at a logistics node; linking enables routing of maintenance crews and inventory planning.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Installed Base Asset Allocation to Projects tracks which assets were delivered under each project for warranty and maintenance.']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Installed base assets are delivered under a sales contract; linking allows the Asset Coverage compliance report to verify that each asset is covered by its sales contract.']
  service_warranty_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_warranty. Business justification: Installed base is covered by a warranty; FK provides authoritative warranty data and removes duplicate fields.']
  capacity_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Rated capacity of the equipment in kilowatts.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the site address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the installation site.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the installed base record was first created.']
  current decimal(10,2) [note: 'Operating current of the equipment in amperes.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version of the firmware loaded on the installed device.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the equipment was installed at the customer site.']
  installation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to install the equipment.']
  last_service_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent service activity performed on the asset.']
  maintenance_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Planned interval between preventive maintenance activities, in days.']
  maintenance_type varchar [note: 'Category of maintenance performed on the asset.']
  mean_time_between_failures_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average elapsed time between successive failures, expressed in hours.']
  mean_time_to_repair_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to repair the equipment after a failure, in hours.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model identifier for the product.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next preventive maintenance event.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational condition of the installed equipment.']
  overall_equipment_effectiveness decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated OEE percentage for the asset.']
  power_rating_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum power rating of the equipment in kilowatts.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the installed product.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the installed product model.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned unique serial number of the installed equipment.']
  site_address varchar [note: 'Physical street address of the installation site.']
  site_location varchar [note: 'Internal code identifying the customer site or plant where the asset resides.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version of the application software running on the device.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the site address.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the installed base record.']
  voltage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Operating voltage of the equipment in volts.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing the registry of all customer-installed automation systems, electrification solutions, and smart infrastructure components actively managed through after-sales service. Captures serial number, product model, firmware/software version, installation date, site location, operational status (running, degraded, stopped, decommissioned), last service date, next scheduled maintenance date, warranty linkage, and service contract coverage reference. Serves as the SSOT for the installed asset population and is the central reference point for all service transactions including requests, field orders, PM schedules, and remote diagnostics.'
}

Table service.service_entitlement {
  service_entitlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the service entitlement record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Entitlement usage incurs costs that must be charged to a cost center for budget control.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who holds the entitlement.']
  installed_base_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.installed_base. Business justification: Entitlement applies to a specific installed base; FK creates the needed inbound relationship.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_header. Business justification: Entitlement eligibility is derived from the original sales order; linking supports entitlement reporting and audit of service levels.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Entitlement Allocation to Projects ties service entitlements to the project that generated the entitlement.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent service contract governing this entitlement.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product or asset to which the entitlement applies.']
  acknowledgment_actual_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the request was acknowledged.']
  acknowledgment_breach_flag boolean [note: 'True if acknowledgment missed the SLA target.']
  acknowledgment_breach_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for acknowledgment SLA breach, if any.']
  acknowledgment_elapsed_minutes varchar [note: 'Minutes elapsed between request creation and acknowledgment.']
  acknowledgment_target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Target timestamp for acknowledgment of the service request.']
  business_hours_coverage boolean [note: 'True if SLA applies only during standard business hours; false if 24/7.']
  coverage_level varchar [note: 'Level of service coverage associated with the entitlement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the entitlement record was first created.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the entitlement expires or is terminated; null for open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the entitlement becomes effective.']
  entitlement_code varchar [note: 'Business code or number used externally to reference the entitlement.']
  entitlement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the entitlement offering.']
  entitlement_type varchar [note: 'Category of entitlement such as warranty, contract, complimentary, or service plan.']
  first_response_actual_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the first response was delivered.']
  first_response_breach_flag boolean [note: 'True if the first response missed the SLA target.']
  first_response_breach_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the first response SLA was breached, if applicable.']
  first_response_elapsed_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual minutes elapsed between request creation and first response.']
  first_response_target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Target timestamp by which the first response must be provided.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the entitlement for handling service requests.']
  resolution_actual_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when the service request was resolved.']
  resolution_breach_flag boolean [note: 'True if the resolution missed the SLA target.']
  resolution_breach_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the resolution SLA was breached, if applicable.']
  resolution_elapsed_minutes varchar [note: 'Minutes elapsed between request creation and resolution.']
  resolution_target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Target timestamp by which the service request must be resolved.']
  service_channel varchar [note: 'Delivery channel for the service (e.g., phone, email, on‑site, remote).']
  service_entitlement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the entitlement, including any special conditions.']
  service_entitlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the entitlement.']
  sla_resolution_time_target varchar [note: 'Target time in minutes to fully resolve a service request.']
  sla_response_time_target varchar [note: 'Target time in minutes for initial response to a service request.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the entitlement record.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining specific service entitlement rules, coverage levels, and SLA milestone tracking associated with a service contract or warranty. Specifies which service types, response/resolution time SLAs, and support channels a customer is entitled to for a given installed asset or product family. Captures entitlement name, type (warranty, contract, complimentary), SLA response time target, SLA resolution time target, business hours coverage, and active status. Includes milestone compliance tracking: each SLA milestone event (first response, acknowledgment, resolution) with target timestamp, actual completion timestamp, breach flag, breach reason, and elapsed time. Enables automated SLA enforcement during service request intake, SLA performance monitoring, and breach root-cause analysis. Maps to Salesforce Service Cloud Entitlement and Milestone objects.'
}

Table service.sla_milestone {
  sla_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the SLA milestone record.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.control_system. Business justification: SLA milestones are measured against specific control systems; linking enables SLA reporting per system for regulatory compliance.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: SLA Milestone Monitoring within Projects links SLA milestones to the owning project for compliance dashboards.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SLA contract governing the service request.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service request to which this SLA milestone belongs.']
  actual_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the milestone was completed.']
  assigned_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the support team responsible for meeting the milestone.']
  breach_flag boolean [note: 'True if the milestone was not met within the target time, otherwise False.']
  breach_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why the SLA milestone was breached.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SLA milestone record was first created in the system.']
  elapsed_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Total minutes elapsed between the target timestamp and the actual completion timestamp.']
  entitlement_tier varchar [note: 'Tier of service entitlement that defines SLA targets for the request.']
  escalated_flag boolean [note: 'True if the milestone required escalation to a higher support tier.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for escalating the SLA milestone.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Name of the SLA milestone (e.g., first response, acknowledgment, resolution, closure).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by the support team regarding the milestone.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the SLA milestone for handling urgency.']
  resolution_code varchar [note: 'Standard code indicating how the milestone was resolved (e.g., completed, workaround, escalated).']
  sla_milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the milestone (e.g., pending, met, breached, cancelled).']
  target_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The contractual target date and time by which the milestone must be achieved.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the SLA milestone record.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity tracking SLA milestone compliance events for individual service requests, capturing each SLA target (first response, acknowledgment, resolution), target timestamp, actual completion timestamp, breach flag, breach reason, and elapsed time. Enables SLA performance monitoring and breach root-cause analysis across service contracts and entitlement tiers. Maps to Salesforce Service Cloud Milestone.'
}

Table service.engineer {
  engineer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for engineer']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for payroll and performance review; each service engineer is an employee tracked in HR.']
  active_assignment_count varchar [note: 'Number of service assignments currently active for the engineer.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the engineer\'s mailing address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the engineer\'s mailing address.']
  certification_drive_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the drive‑systems certification.']
  certification_electrification_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the electrification certification.']
  certification_hmi_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the HMI certification.']
  certification_plc_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the PLC certification.']
  certification_robotics_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the robotics certification.']
  certification_scada_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the SCADA certification.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the engineer\'s mailing address.']
  classification_or_type varchar [note: 'Category of the engineer (e.g., internal employee, contractor, vendor).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the engineer\'s address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the engineer record was first created.']
  dispatch_zone varchar [note: 'Specific dispatch zone or area assigned to the engineer.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the engineer.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Type of employment relationship.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the engineer.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the engineer was hired.']
  labor_classification varchar [note: 'Labor classification for payroll and cost allocation.']
  labor_rate_hourly decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard hourly labor rate for the engineer.']
  last_dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent dispatch of the engineer.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the engineer.']
  max_travel_distance_km varchar [note: 'Maximum distance (in km) the engineer is willing to travel.']
  next_available_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Estimated timestamp when the engineer will be next available for dispatch.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the engineer is eligible for overtime work.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Most recent performance rating for the engineer.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the engineer.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the engineer\'s mailing address.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for contacting the engineer.']
  primary_language varchar [note: 'Primary language spoken by the engineer.']
  product_family_competency varchar [note: 'Product families or equipment lines the engineer is qualified to service.']
  secondary_languages varchar [note: 'Additional languages spoken by the engineer, comma‑separated.']
  security_clearance_level varchar [note: 'Security clearance level required for assignments.']
  service_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the engineer provides service.']
  shift_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred work shift for the engineer.']
  skill_drive_systems_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates if the engineer holds a drive‑systems certification.']
  skill_electrification_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates if the engineer holds an electrification certification.']
  skill_hmi_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates if the engineer holds an HMI certification.']
  skill_plc_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates if the engineer holds a PLC certification.']
  skill_robotics_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates if the engineer holds a robotics certification.']
  skill_scada_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates if the engineer holds a SCADA certification.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the engineer\'s mailing address.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the engineer\'s employment ended, if applicable.']
  travel_eligibility boolean [note: 'Indicates if the engineer is eligible for travel assignments.']
  union_member_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the engineer is a union member.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the engineer record.']
  years_of_experience varchar [note: 'Total years of relevant field service experience.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing field service engineers and technical support specialists assigned to service operations. Captures employee reference, skill certifications (PLC, SCADA, HMI, drive systems, electrification, robotics), product family competencies, service region assignment, current availability status (available, dispatched, on-leave, training), dispatch zone, language capabilities, active assignment count, and certification expiry dates. Serves as the SSOT for field service resource profiles and dispatch eligibility within the service domain.'
}

Table service.zone {
  zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the service zone.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Operational expenses of a service zone are allocated to a cost center for regional financial planning.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee responsible for managing the service zone.']
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the calendar that defines public holidays for the zone.']
  service_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service center or depot responsible for the zone.']
  city_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of major cities included in the zone.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the zone resides.']
  coverage_area_sqkm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Geographic size of the zone in square kilometres.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the zone record was first created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the zone becomes active for dispatch planning.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the zone is retired or re‑defined (null if open‑ended).']
  is_holiday_observed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether holidays from the linked calendar affect service commitments.']
  latitude_center decimal(9,6) [note: 'Latitude of the geographic centroid of the zone.']
  longitude_center decimal(9,6) [note: 'Longitude of the geographic centroid of the zone.']
  max_travel_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum expected travel time from the service center to any point in the zone.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard daily operating window for field service (e.g., 08:00-17:00).']
  postal_code_range varchar [note: 'Range of postal codes (e.g., 10001‑10292) defining the zone boundaries.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric rank used to prioritize dispatches across zones (1 = highest).']
  region varchar [note: 'High‑level region (e.g., North America, EMEA) for reporting and routing.']
  sla_coverage_level varchar [note: 'Level of service commitment promised for the zone.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province within the country covered by the zone.']
  supported_product_family varchar [note: 'Product families that can be serviced within this zone.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier for the zone (e.g., America/New_York).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the zone record.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used in external systems and contracts.']
  zone_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the zone’s characteristics and purpose.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the service territory.']
  zone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the zone.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining geographic service territories used for field service dispatch routing and SLA coverage planning. Captures territory name, geographic boundaries (region, country, state/province, postal code ranges), assigned service center or depot, operating hours, holiday calendar reference, territory manager, and supported product families. Enables optimized field engineer dispatch, travel time estimation, and ensures SLA commitments are achievable based on geographic coverage density.'
}

Table service.service_capa_record {
  service_capa_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the CAPA record.']
  asset_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Link to the maintenance work order associated with the CAPA.']
  compliance_capa_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.capa_record. Business justification: Service CAPA actions are reported to compliance CAPA records to ensure regulatory closure and audit traceability.']
  design_review_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.design_review. Business justification: CAPA records often stem from findings in a design review; linking provides the design context for corrective actions.']
  primary_service_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person accountable for implementing the corrective action.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: CAPA actions linked to Project enable quality improvement tracking for specific capital projects.']
  quaternary_service_preventive_action_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee responsible for implementing the preventive action.']
  related_service_case_service_request_id varchar [note: 'Link to the service case that originated the CAPA.']
  tertiary_service_corrective_action_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee responsible for executing the corrective action.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective/preventive actions were actually finished.']
  affected_units_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units impacted by the issue.']
  capa_number varchar [note: 'External CAPA number assigned by the quality system.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAPA record was closed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the CAPA with internal quality standards.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action to eliminate the root cause.']
  cost_actual decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred after actions are completed.']
  cost_estimate decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of implementing the corrective/preventive actions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAPA record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for cost fields.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to supporting documents or drawings related to the CAPA.']
  is_closed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CAPA has been formally closed.']
  issue_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the problem that triggered the CAPA.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by users.']
  preventive_action_description varchar [note: 'Plan to prevent recurrence of the issue.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the CAPA.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'Family or line of products affected by the CAPA.']
  product_model varchar [note: 'Specific model identifier of the affected product.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating associated with the non‑conformance.']
  root_cause_analysis_date date [note: 'Date when the root cause analysis was completed.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category of the root cause (e.g., Design, Process, Material).']
  root_cause_subcategory varchar [note: 'More detailed classification within the root cause category.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number of the equipment or component.']
  service_capa_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CAPA record.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the issue prompting the CAPA.']
  source varchar [note: 'Origin of the CAPA record.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the corrective/preventive actions should be completed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the CAPA record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the verification activity was performed.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the corrective action is effective.']
  verification_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the verification activity.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity representing Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) records initiated from service cases, field failures, or recurring defect patterns identified through after-sales support. Captures CAPA number, root cause category, affected product family, corrective action description, preventive action plan, responsible owner, target completion date, verification method, and closure status. Links service domain quality feedback to engineering and quality domain improvement processes.'
}

Table service.satisfaction_survey {
  satisfaction_survey_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for satisfaction_survey']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer who received the service and completed the survey.']
  engineer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the field service engineer or technician who performed the service.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Post‑Project Customer Satisfaction Survey ties survey results to the project for performance evaluation.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service request or field service work order that triggered the survey.']
  communication_rating varchar [note: 'Rating (1‑5) of the clarity and effectiveness of communication during the service.']
  engineer_competence_rating varchar [note: 'Rating (1‑5) of the engineer\'s technical competence and professionalism.']
  issue_resolution_rating varchar [note: 'Rating (1‑5) of how effectively the issue was resolved.']
  language varchar [note: 'Language in which the survey was presented to the customer.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the survey record.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Numeric NPS value ranging from -100 to 100, applicable when survey_type = NPS.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated satisfaction rating (e.g., 1‑5 or 0‑10) representing the customer\'s overall impression.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the survey record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the survey record.']
  response_time_rating varchar [note: 'Rating (1‑5) of how quickly the service request was responded to.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the customer submitted the survey response.']
  survey_channel varchar [note: 'Medium through which the survey was delivered to the customer.']
  survey_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier or code assigned to the survey instance.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the methodology of the survey, e.g., Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) or Net Promoter Score (NPS).']
  survey_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the survey template used, supporting template evolution tracking.']
  verbatim_feedback varchar [note: 'Open‑ended text field capturing free‑form comments from the customer.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity capturing post-service customer satisfaction survey responses collected after service request closure or field service visit completion. Captures survey type (CSAT, NPS), overall satisfaction score, individual dimension ratings (response time, engineer competence, issue resolution, communication), verbatim feedback, survey channel, and response timestamp. Provides voice-of-customer data for service quality improvement and SLA effectiveness measurement.'
}

Table service.knowledge_article {
  knowledge_article_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the knowledge article.']
  author_engineer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.engineer. Business justification: Knowledge article author is a service engineer; FK replaces free‑text author field.']
  applicable_models varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of product model identifiers covered by the article.']
  article_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the article (e.g., troubleshooting, bulletin, safety recall, mandatory retrofit, advisory, firmware patch).']
  attachment_url varchar [note: 'Link to any supporting documents or media files.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Regulatory or contractual deadline for completing the required action.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the article.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the article record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the article\'s guidance is applicable.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the article\'s guidance is no longer applicable (nullable).']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated effort in hours to execute the resolution.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Firmware version(s) to which the article applies.']
  is_faq boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the article is a Frequently Asked Question (true) or not (false).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'True if the article must be applied to comply with a regulation or contract.']
  knowledge_article_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the article.']
  language varchar [note: 'Language of the article content.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the article was last reviewed for accuracy.']
  parts_required varchar [note: 'List of part numbers or SKUs needed for the repair.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'Product family or line to which the article applies.']
  publication_date date [note: 'Date the article was first made publicly available.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the article satisfies applicable regulatory requirements.']
  related_article_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of knowledge_article_id values that are related.']
  required_action varchar [note: 'Action that field engineers must perform (e.g., replace part, update firmware).']
  resolution_procedure varchar [note: 'Step‑by‑step instructions to resolve the issue.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the article must be reviewed.']
  revision_history varchar [note: 'Chronological log of changes made to the article.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative of the underlying cause of the issue.']
  serial_number_range varchar [note: 'Range of equipment serial numbers the article applies to (e.g., 1000‑1999).']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Software version(s) to which the article applies.']
  summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary or abstract of the article content.']
  symptom_keywords varchar [note: 'Keywords describing observed symptoms that trigger the article.']
  tags varchar [note: 'User‑defined tags or keywords for searchability.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the knowledge article.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the article.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the article has been accessed by service personnel.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the article (e.g., v1.2).']

  Note: 'Master entity representing the technical knowledge base including troubleshooting articles, official service bulletins, field service notices, safety recalls, FAQs, and technical notes used by service engineers and support agents. Captures article type/classification (troubleshooting, bulletin, safety recall, mandatory retrofit, advisory update, firmware patch, FAQ), title, product family applicability, affected product models and serial number ranges, symptom keywords, root cause description, resolution procedure, applicable firmware/software versions, required action, compliance deadline, estimated labor hours, parts required, publication status, and usage count. Supports first-call resolution improvement, field engineer self-service, proactive service campaign execution, and regulatory recall compliance tracking.'
}

Table service.bulletin {
  bulletin_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for bulletin']
  author_engineer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.engineer. Business justification: Bulletin author is a service engineer; FK replaces free‑text author field.']
  action_required varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the service action(s) that must be performed.']
  affected_product_models varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of product model identifiers impacted by the bulletin.']
  bulletin_number varchar [note: 'Official alphanumeric identifier assigned to the bulletin by engineering.']
  bulletin_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the bulletin.']
  bulletin_type varchar [note: 'Category of the bulletin indicating its purpose and urgency.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Latest date by which the required actions must be completed to remain compliant.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bulletin record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency for estimated cost.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Method used to disseminate the bulletin to field personnel.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the bulletin\'s actions become mandatory or recommended.']
  engineering_department varchar [note: 'Department within engineering responsible for the bulletin.']
  estimated_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected total cost (parts + labor) for the bulletin actions.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected labor effort in hours to complete the required actions.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the bulletin is no longer applicable.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Specific firmware version that the bulletin addresses or updates.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the bulletin was formally issued to the field.']
  labor_rate_per_hour decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard labor rate used for cost estimation, expressed in the selected currency.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with the bulletin is mandatory (true) or advisory (false).']
  parts_required varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of part numbers and quantities needed to comply.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of any external regulation or standard that mandates the bulletin.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for the bulletin document.']
  safety_notice_flag boolean [note: 'True if the bulletin contains a safety‑critical notice.']
  serial_number_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending serial number in the inclusive range of units affected.']
  serial_number_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting serial number in the inclusive range of units affected.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Risk severity associated with the bulletin, used for prioritization.']
  software_patch_version varchar [note: 'Version of the software patch associated with the bulletin.']
  target_market varchar [note: 'Geographic market(s) to which the bulletin applies.']
  title varchar [note: 'Short descriptive title of the bulletin.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the bulletin record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version string (e.g., v1.2) of the bulletin.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing official technical service bulletins and field service notices issued by engineering or product management to communicate mandatory or advisory service actions for installed automation systems and equipment. Captures bulletin number, affected product models and serial number ranges, bulletin type (safety recall, mandatory retrofit, advisory update, firmware patch), action required, estimated labor hours, parts required, and compliance deadline. Drives proactive field service campaigns.'
}

Table service.service_pm_schedule {
  service_pm_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the preventive maintenance schedule.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory audit of preventive maintenance creation requires linking schedule to creator employee.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer‑installed equipment or system covered by the schedule.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical site or plant where the equipment resides.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Preventive Maintenance Scheduling for Project Assets ensures PM plans are associated with the correct capital project.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service contract under which the maintenance is provided.']
  task_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the checklist of tasks to be performed during the maintenance visit.']
  classification_or_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the maintenance approach.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the schedule complies with contractual or regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the schedule record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the schedule expires; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the schedule becomes effective and eligible for execution.']
  estimated_labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Projected labor effort for the maintenance activity, expressed in hours.']
  interval_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the maintenance interval (e.g., days, hours, cycles, meter reading).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the maintenance activity is mandatory under the contract (true) or optional (false).']
  last_execution_date date [note: 'Date when the maintenance task was last performed.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the schedule.']
  maintenance_interval decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric value of the interval between maintenance actions.']
  next_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next maintenance execution.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or remarks entered by planners or technicians.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the schedule for dispatch planning.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used externally to reference the schedule.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the maintenance schedule.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the maintenance schedule.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the schedule is time‑based, usage‑based, or condition‑based.']
  service_pm_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing additional context about the maintenance schedule.']
  spare_parts_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of part numbers required for the scheduled maintenance.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the schedule record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the schedule record.']

  Note: 'Master entity defining scheduled preventive maintenance plans for customer-installed automation systems and equipment under service contracts. Captures maintenance plan type (time-based, usage-based, condition-based), maintenance interval, task checklist reference, required spare parts list, estimated labor hours, last execution date, next due date, and compliance status. Drives proactive field service dispatch and contract fulfillment tracking. Abbreviated from preventive_maintenance_schedule to comply with 30-character product name limit.'
}

Table service.service_contract_line {
  service_contract_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the service_contract_line association']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the asset or product covered by this line.']
  primary_service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent service contract header.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Each contract line item is governed by a regulatory requirement (e.g., safety standard), needed for compliance verification per line.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Each contract line is fulfilled by a particular supplier; this link supports supplier performance metrics and pricing analysis per line.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when regulatory approval was granted.']
  approval_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document for regulatory approval.']
  contract_line_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the line item.']
  coverage_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact moment coverage ends.']
  coverage_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or functional scope of the coverage for this equipment']
  coverage_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact moment coverage begins.']
  coverage_type varchar [note: 'Type of coverage applied to the equipment (e.g., preventive, full‑service)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was created.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to the line.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the discount.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to supporting documentation for the line.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when coverage ends; null for open‑ended contracts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when coverage for this equipment begins']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates if the coverage is exclusive to the asset.']
  is_renewable boolean [note: 'Indicates if the line can be renewed after expiry.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle stage of the line item.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the contract.']
  metric_target_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target value for the service metric.']
  metric_unit varchar [note: 'Unit for the service metric (e.g., hours, percent).']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount after applying discount and tax.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes related to the contract line.']
  price_amount decimal [note: 'Monetary amount charged for this equipment under the contract line']
  price_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code for the price amount.']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'Billing unit associated with the price.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Category of the covered product.']
  product_family varchar [note: 'Family classification of the covered product.']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units covered by this line.']
