  regulatory_approval_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether regulatory approval has been obtained.']
  renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the line is set for automatic renewal.']
  renewal_terms varchar [note: 'Textual description of renewal conditions.']
  service_contract_line_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the contract line.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Description of the service level (e.g., response time, uptime).']
  service_metric varchar [note: 'Metric used to measure service performance.']
  sla_target_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target number of service hours promised in the SLA.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Service level agreement tier defining response and resolution targets']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax applied to the line item.']
  tax_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tax is already included in the price.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update.']
  warranty_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a warranty is included.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Length of warranty coverage in months.']

  Note: 'Master entity representing individual line items within a service contract, each covering a specific installed asset, product family, or service scope element. Captures covered asset or product reference, coverage type, SLA tier, line-level pricing, coverage start/end dates, and renewal flag. Enables granular entitlement checking at the asset level and supports contract profitability analysis by line.'
}

Table service.remote_diagnostic_session {
  remote_diagnostic_session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each remote diagnostic session.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer whose equipment was serviced.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service engineer who performed the remote session.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or system that was accessed remotely.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical site or plant where the asset resides.']
  bandwidth_mbps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average data transfer bandwidth measured during the session.']
  connection_method varchar [note: 'Technical method used to establish the remote link for the session.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the session record was first created in the system.']
  data_volume_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total amount of diagnostic data transferred during the session.']
  diagnostic_tool varchar [note: 'Software or utility employed by the technician during the session.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time of the session in seconds, calculated from start and end timestamps.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the remote diagnostic session ended.']
  error_message varchar [note: 'Any error text returned by the remote system or diagnostic tool.']
  fault_code varchar [note: 'Error or alarm code retrieved from the equipment during diagnosis.']
  field_dispatch_needed boolean [note: 'True if a subsequent on‑site service visit is required after the remote session.']
  findings_summary varchar [note: 'Concise narrative of the diagnostic observations and root cause analysis.']
  latency_ms decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average round‑trip latency observed during the session.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments captured by the technician.']
  parameters_captured varchar [note: 'Key parameter values (e.g., setpoints, sensor readings) recorded during the session.']
  plc_program_state varchar [note: 'Snapshot of the PLC program state captured at session start.']
  recommended_action varchar [note: 'Suggested remediation steps or configuration changes for the customer.']
  remote_diagnostic_session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the session.']
  resolved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the remote session fully resolved the reported issue.']
  session_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the session, used for tracking and reference in service records.']
  session_type varchar [note: 'Purpose category of the remote session.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the remote diagnostic session began.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the session record.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity capturing remote diagnostic and telemetry-assisted troubleshooting sessions conducted by technical support engineers for customer-installed automation systems via IIoT connectivity, SCADA remote access, VPN tunnels, or HMI remote desktop. Captures session start/end timestamps, connection method, diagnostic tool used, fault codes retrieved, PLC program state, parameters captured, findings summary, recommended action, and whether the session resolved the issue without field dispatch. Supports remote-first service resolution strategy and field dispatch avoidance metrics.'
}

Table service.part_consumption {
  part_consumption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for part_consumption']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Parts Consumption Accounting to Project Costs tracks part usage against the project budget for cost control.']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Parts used in service are billed to the originating sales contract; linking enables accurate cost allocation in the Service Parts Cost analysis.']
  service_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service request (transaction header) to which this part consumption line belongs.']
  spare_part_id varchar [note: 'Internal system identifier for the spare part consumed.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier of the external supplier providing the part.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the part was actually received.']
  consumption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the part was recorded as consumed on site.']
  contract_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'True if the part usage is reimbursable under a service contract separate from warranty.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center code charged for the part consumption.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the monetary amounts.']
  delivery_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier‑provided tracking identifier for the part shipment.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for part arrival at the service location.']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the part order fulfillment process.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the service part order, used for ordering and reference.']
  line_total_amount decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total monetary value for the line (quantity × unit price) before any discounts or taxes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments captured by the technician.']
  order_date timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the part order was created in the service system.']
  order_urgency varchar [note: 'Business‑defined urgency level for the part order, used for prioritization.']
  part_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the spare part, including model or variant details.']
  part_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or internal SKU that uniquely identifies the spare part.']
  quantity_consumed varchar [note: 'Number of units of the part used during the service activity.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the part consumption record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the part consumption record.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Origin of the part supplied to the field technician.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the part quantity is measured (e.g., each, set, box).']
  unit_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard price per unit of the part at the time of consumption, in the transaction currency.']
  warranty_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the part consumption is covered under a warranty or service contract.']

  Note: 'Transactional entity representing spare parts consumption and parts orders associated with field service activities, including emergency parts procurement for critical equipment downtime. Captures part number (SKU), quantity consumed, source (warehouse stock, van stock, supplier direct ship), cost, warranty/contract coverage flag, order urgency classification, fulfillment status, and delivery tracking. Provides traceability of parts usage per service event for cost recovery, warranty claims, inventory replenishment triggers, and bridges service domain parts demand with inventory and procurement domain supply.'
}

Table service.contract_line {
  contract_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for contract_line']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_contract. Business justification: Contract line must reference its parent service contract; adding service_contract_id creates the required parent‑child relationship within the service domain.']

  Note: 'Represents the association between equipment_register and service_contract, capturing coverage details, SLA tier, pricing, and effective dates for each equipment covered by a contract.. Existence Justification: An equipment asset can be covered by multiple service contracts over its lifecycle, and a single service contract can cover many equipment assets. Each equipment‑contract pairing is managed as a contract line item that stores coverage type, SLA tier, pricing, and effective dates. The relationship is actively created, updated, and deleted by contract administrators.'
}

Table service.engineer_assignment {
  engineer_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the engineer_assignment association']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the target control system']
  service_engineer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the assigned engineer']
  assignment_end_date date [note: 'Date when the engineer\'s assignment to the control system ends (null if ongoing)']
  assignment_role varchar [note: 'Role of the engineer on the control system (e.g., Lead, Support, Specialist)']
  assignment_start_date date [note: 'Date when the engineer\'s assignment to the control system begins']

  Note: 'Represents the assignment of a field service engineer to a control system. Each record captures the engineer\'s role on the system and the period of the assignment, enabling tracking of maintenance responsibilities and compliance.. Existence Justification: Field service engineers are actively assigned to one or more control systems for maintenance, commissioning, and support. Each control system can have multiple engineers assigned, with each assignment capturing the engineer\'s role and the start/end dates of the assignment. The assignment is a managed business entity that is created, updated, and retired as operational needs change.'
}

Table service.task_checklist {
  task_checklist_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for task_checklist']
  parent_task_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on task_checklist (parent_task_checklist_id)']
  applicable_equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of automation equipment or system that the checklist is designed for.']
  code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies the checklist across enterprise systems.']
  compliance_required boolean [note: 'True when the checklist must satisfy industry or safety regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the checklist record was initially loaded into the data platform.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explaining the purpose, scope, and usage guidelines for the checklist.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Calendar date from which the checklist is considered active and applicable.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Calendar date after which the checklist should no longer be used (nullable for open‑ended).']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'True if the checklist must be completed for compliance or safety reasons.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when the checklist was last examined for relevance and compliance.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the checklist used by service technicians and planners.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary information, comments, or special instructions related to the checklist.']
  owner_role varchar [note: 'Organizational role (e.g., Field Engineer, Service Manager) that maintains the checklist.']
  status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the checklist is currently in use, retired, or under development.']
  task_count varchar [note: 'Total count of tasks that belong to this checklist.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the checklist based on service activity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the checklist metadata.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version identifier used to track revisions of the checklist.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for task_checklist. Referenced by task_checklist_id.'
}

Table service.service_center {
  service_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for service_center']
  parent_service_center_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on service_center (parent_service_center_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the service center.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (e.g., suite or floor).']
  average_resolution_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Mean time from request receipt to case closure.']
  average_response_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Mean time from service request receipt to first response.']
  center_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the service center.']
  center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service center based on its operational scope.']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the service center is located.']
  closing_date date [note: 'Date when the service center ceased operations (if applicable).']
  compliance_certifications varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of certifications held by the center.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary service contract governing the center.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the service center location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service center record was first created.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the service center.']
  external_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the service center in an external ERP or CRM system.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest compliance inspection.']
  is_primary_center boolean [note: 'True if this center is designated as the primary hub for its region.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Most recent date on which the center passed a compliance inspection.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the service center.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the service center.']
  manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the service center manager.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person responsible for the service center.']
  manager_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the service center manager.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the service center.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form remarks or operational notes about the service center.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date when the service center began operations.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard weekly operating hours (e.g., Mon‑Fri 08:00‑17:00).']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the service center.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the service center address.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region where the center operates.']
  rma_processing_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if Return‑Material Authorization processing is supported.']
  service_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of service bays or workstations available at the center.']
  service_level_agreement varchar [note: 'Code referencing the SLA governing response and resolution times.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the service center location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service center.']
  supported_equipment varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of equipment categories the center can service.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time‑zone identifier for the center (e.g., America/New_York).']
  total_rma_cases_last_year varchar [note: 'Number of Return‑Material Authorization cases processed last year.']
  total_service_requests_last_year varchar [note: 'Count of all service requests handled in the previous calendar year.']
  total_warranty_cases_last_year varchar [note: 'Number of warranty‑covered service cases processed last year.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the service center record.']
  warranty_service_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the center provides warranty‑covered service.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for service_center. Referenced by service_center_id.'
}

Table service.holiday_calendar {
  holiday_calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for holiday_calendar']
  base_holiday_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on holiday_calendar (base_holiday_calendar_id)']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit year component of the holiday_date, stored for partitioning and reporting efficiency.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code indicating the nation where the holiday is observed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the holiday record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text providing additional details about the holiday.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the holiday entry becomes valid (useful for future‑planned holidays).']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the holiday entry is no longer valid (for temporary or retired holidays).']
  holiday_category varchar [note: 'Broad category describing the origin or purpose of the holiday.']
  holiday_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the holiday occurs.']
  holiday_name varchar [note: 'Official name or title of the holiday (e.g., "Independence Day").']
  holiday_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the holiday indicating its nature (public, bank, observed, company‑specific, or regional).']
  is_company_holiday boolean [note: 'True if the holiday is specific to the manufacturing company (e.g., plant shutdown).']
  is_national_holiday boolean [note: 'True if the holiday is recognized at the national level.']
  is_observed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the holiday is officially observed (true) or not (false).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑form notes or comments about the holiday.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region to which the holiday applies.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the holiday record.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that last updated the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the holiday record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or process that created the record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for holiday_calendar. Referenced by holiday_calendar_id.'
}

Table supplier.supplier {
  supplier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for supplier']
  account_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: PROCUREMENT: Assigns an internal account manager to each supplier for relationship management; required for supplier performance dashboards and contract negotiations.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for allocating supplier spend to internal cost centers (Procurement Cost Allocation Report).']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Default GL account for posting supplier invoices (AP posting rules).']
  business_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the supplier based on the type of goods or services provided. OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) supplies finished components, distributor resells products, raw_material provides base materials, MRO (Maintenance Repair Operations) supplies consumables, subcontractor provides manufacturing services, service_provider delivers non-manufacturing services.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supplier master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and master data governance.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit unique identifier assigned by Dun & Bradstreet to identify business entities globally. Used for supplier credit checks, risk assessment, and compliance verification.']
  headquarters_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the supplier\'s corporate headquarters. Used for legal correspondence, audit notices, and official communications.']
  headquarters_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or floor information at the supplier\'s headquarters location.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the supplier\'s headquarters is located.']
  headquarters_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the supplier\'s headquarters location. May differ from incorporation country for multinational organizations.']
  headquarters_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the supplier\'s headquarters location.']
  headquarters_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region for the supplier\'s headquarters address.']
  incorporation_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the country where the supplier is legally incorporated and registered. Used for trade compliance, tax jurisdiction, and regulatory reporting.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Standard three-letter Incoterms code defining the division of costs, risks, and responsibilities between buyer and seller in international trade (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP). Published by International Chamber of Commerce. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  iso14001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds valid ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification. True if certified, False if not certified or certification expired.']
  iso45001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds valid ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System certification. True if certified, False if not certified or certification expired.']
  iso9001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier holds valid ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. True if certified, False if not certified or certification expired.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent supplier audit conducted by Manufacturing\'s quality or procurement team. Used to track audit frequency and schedule next audit per supplier management policy.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this supplier master record. Used for change accountability, audit trails, and data governance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this supplier master record was last updated. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.']
  last_scorecard_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent supplier scorecard evaluation. Scorecards typically assess quality, delivery, cost, and responsiveness on a quarterly or annual basis.']
  minority_owned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is certified as a minority-owned business enterprise. Used for diversity supplier programs and regulatory reporting.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next supplier audit based on risk rating, performance, and audit frequency policy. Used for audit planning and compliance tracking.']
  on_time_delivery_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of purchase orders delivered on or before the requested delivery date over the evaluation period. Key performance indicator (KPI) for supplier scorecard and performance management. Expressed as percentage (0.00 to 100.00).']
  overall_scorecard_rating decimal(3,1) [note: 'Composite supplier performance score from the most recent scorecard evaluation, typically on a scale of 0.0 to 10.0 or 0.0 to 100.0. Aggregates quality, delivery, cost, and service metrics into a single performance indicator.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated with the supplier (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Defines the payment due date and any early payment discounts available.']
  preferred_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the supplier\'s preferred currency for invoicing and payment transactions (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary supplier contact for business communications, purchase orders, and quality notifications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the supplier organization for procurement, quality, and supply chain coordination.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Business phone number for the primary supplier contact including country code and extension if applicable.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Quality and compliance qualification status indicating whether the supplier has passed required audits, certifications, and capability assessments. Qualified suppliers meet all requirements, conditional suppliers have minor gaps, not_qualified suppliers failed assessment, under_review suppliers are being evaluated.']
  quality_acceptance_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of received materials that passed incoming quality inspection without rejection or rework over the evaluation period. Key performance indicator (KPI) for supplier quality performance. Expressed as percentage (0.00 to 100.00).']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship with this supplier was terminated or the vendor record was deactivated. Null for active suppliers. Used for supplier lifecycle tracking and historical analysis.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship with this supplier was established and the vendor record was first activated. Used for relationship tenure analysis and supplier lifecycle reporting.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment for the supplier based on financial stability, geopolitical factors, quality performance, delivery reliability, and compliance history. Used for supply chain risk management and contingency planning.']
  sap_vendor_number varchar [note: 'Vendor master number from SAP S/4HANA MM (Materials Management) or SAP Ariba. This is the authoritative supplier identifier in the ERP system used for procurement transactions.']
  small_business boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier qualifies as a small business based on revenue, employee count, or regulatory definitions. Used for small business procurement programs and compliance reporting.']
  supplier_category varchar [note: 'Strategic classification indicating the supplier\'s relationship tier and procurement priority. Strategic suppliers are critical long-term partners, preferred suppliers have favorable terms, approved suppliers meet minimum standards, conditional suppliers are under review, blocked suppliers are prohibited from new business.']
  supplier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supplier relationship. Active suppliers are approved for procurement, inactive suppliers are no longer used, suspended suppliers are temporarily restricted, pending_approval suppliers are under qualification review, blocked suppliers are prohibited from new transactions.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number for the supplier (e.g., EIN in USA, VAT number in EU, GST number in India). Used for tax reporting, invoice validation, and compliance.']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'Internal alphanumeric code assigned to the supplier for identification across procurement, quality, and supply chain systems. Often synchronized with SAP vendor master number.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the supplier organization as registered with incorporation authorities. Used for contracts, purchase orders, and legal documentation.']
  woman_owned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is certified as a woman-owned business enterprise. Used for diversity supplier programs and regulatory reporting.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this supplier master record. Used for accountability, audit trails, and data governance.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing the authoritative supplier identity record (SSOT) for all vendors, sub-contractors, and raw material suppliers engaged by Manufacturing. Captures legal entity name, DUNS number, tax ID, SAP vendor master number (from SAP Ariba/S4HANA MM), business type (OEM, distributor, raw material, MRO), incorporation country, headquarters address, primary contact, preferred currency, payment terms, incoterms, and active status. This is the golden record used across procurement, quality, supply chain, and logistics domains.'
}

Table supplier.site {
  site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for site']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Site‑specific cost allocation for production capacity spend (Site Cost Allocation process).']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: SITE PERFORMANCE: Links a responsible procurement employee to each supplier site; used in site audit scheduling and capacity monitoring reports.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent vendor legal entity that owns this site. Links to the vendor master record.']
  active_from_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor site became active and eligible for procurement transactions.']
  active_to_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor site was deactivated or disqualified. Null for currently active sites.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the vendor site facility. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as building, suite, or floor number. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  approved_for_new_business boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor site is approved to receive new purchase orders and sourcing awards.']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the production capacity metric such as units, tons, liters, or square meters.']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the primary quality management certification expires. Used to trigger recertification workflows.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the vendor site is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the vendor site location. Used for trade compliance, tariff calculation, and regional risk assessment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor site record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for transactions with this vendor site.']
  delivery_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'On-time delivery performance score for the vendor site. Scale 0-100.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Dun and Bradstreet Universal Numbering System identifier for the vendor site. Used for credit assessment and supplier verification.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Macro-geographic region classification for supply chain planning and regional sourcing strategy.']
  iatf_16949_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor site holds IATF 16949 automotive quality management certification. Required for automotive supply chain participation.']
  iso_14001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor site holds ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification.']
  iso_45001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor site holds ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System certification.']
  iso_9001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor site holds ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. Critical for supplier qualification.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent quality or compliance audit conducted at the vendor site.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the vendor site record was last updated.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the vendor site. Used for logistics optimization and distance calculations.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard procurement lead time in days from purchase order placement to delivery for this vendor site.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the vendor site. Used for logistics optimization and distance calculations.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum order quantity required by the vendor site for purchase orders.']
  moq_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum order quantity such as pieces, kilograms, or pallets.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next quality or compliance audit at the vendor site.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or site-specific considerations relevant to procurement and quality teams.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms applicable to this vendor site such as Net 30, Net 60, or 2/10 Net 30.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the vendor site address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  preferred_supplier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor site has preferred supplier status for strategic sourcing decisions.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary site contact. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the vendor site for procurement and quality coordination.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the vendor site contact. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  production_capacity_annual decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual production capacity of the site in units relevant to the site type. Used for capacity planning and sourcing allocation decisions.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite quality performance score for the vendor site based on defect rates, audit results, and corrective action responsiveness. Scale 0-100.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification of the vendor site based on quality performance, financial stability, geopolitical factors, and compliance history.']
  site_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the vendor site. Used for procurement routing and supplier qualification tracking.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Business name or designation of the vendor site facility.']
  site_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor site. Controls whether the site is eligible for new purchase orders and sourcing decisions.']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor site by primary function. Determines sourcing eligibility and capability matching.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the vendor site. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  tax_registration_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification or VAT registration number for the vendor site. Used for tax compliance and invoice validation.']

  Note: 'Represents a specific physical manufacturing, warehouse, or service site belonging to a vendor. A single vendor legal entity may operate multiple sites with distinct capabilities, certifications, and risk profiles. Captures site address, site type (manufacturing, distribution, R&D), production capacity, geographic region, ISO certification scope, and site-level contact. Used by procurement and quality to route sourcing decisions to the correct facility.'
}

Table supplier.supplier_contact {
  supplier_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for contact']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Supplier contacts serve as project points of contact; linking allows communication and escalation tracking per project.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent vendor organization this contact belongs to. Links to the supplier master record.']
  certification_held varchar [note: 'Professional certifications or qualifications held by the contact relevant to their role (e.g., Six Sigma Black Belt, PMP, ISO Lead Auditor, APICS CPIM). Multiple certifications separated by semicolon.']
  communication_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred method of communication for reaching this contact for routine supplier relationship activities.']
  contact_role varchar [note: 'Primary functional role of the contact in the supplier relationship. Determines routing of communications and escalations.']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor contact. Inactive or terminated contacts should not receive communications.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact based on their priority and purpose in the supplier relationship hierarchy.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person who created this vendor contact record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor contact record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  department varchar [note: 'Department or functional area within the vendor organization where the contact works (e.g., Sales, Quality Assurance, Logistics, Engineering, Customer Service).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this contact ceased to be active or left the vendor organization. Null for currently active contacts.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this contact became active and available for supplier relationship management activities.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the vendor contact. Used for procurement communications, quality notifications, and supply chain coordination.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Numeric escalation tier for this contact in the supplier relationship hierarchy. Level 1 is first point of contact, higher numbers represent management escalation paths.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'First name or given name of the vendor contact person.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Complete name of the vendor contact person, typically concatenation of first and last name.']
  interaction_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Target frequency in days for regular business interactions with this contact. Used for relationship management planning and alerts.']
  is_decision_maker boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this contact has decision-making authority for commercial negotiations, contract approvals, or strategic supplier relationship matters.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this contact is the primary point of contact for the vendor organization. Only one primary contact should exist per vendor.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title or position of the vendor contact within their organization (e.g., Account Manager, Quality Engineer, Logistics Coordinator, Sales Director).']
  last_interaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent business interaction or communication with this contact. Used for relationship health monitoring.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person who most recently modified this vendor contact record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor contact record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Last name or family name of the vendor contact person.']
  linkedin_profile_url varchar [note: 'URL to the contacts LinkedIn professional profile. Used for relationship intelligence and network mapping.']
  mobile_number varchar [note: 'Mobile phone number for the vendor contact. Used for urgent supply chain escalations and on-call support.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and observations about the contact, including communication preferences, relationship history, special considerations, or other relevant context for supplier relationship management.']
  office_location varchar [note: 'Physical office location or site where the contact is based (e.g., headquarters, regional office, manufacturing plant).']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the vendor contact. Includes country code and extension where applicable.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'ISO 639-2 three-letter language code representing the contacts preferred language for business communications (e.g., ENG, DEU, FRA, SPA, CHI, JPN).']
  relationship_strength_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score (0-100) representing the strength and quality of the business relationship with this contact. Based on interaction frequency, responsiveness, collaboration quality, and business outcomes.']
  specialization_area varchar [note: 'Specific area of expertise or specialization for the contact (e.g., electrification products, automation systems, quality management, supply chain logistics, contract negotiation).']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the contacts primary work location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/Berlin, Asia/Shanghai). Used for scheduling meetings and coordinating real-time communications.']
  years_of_experience varchar [note: 'Total years of professional experience the contact has in their field or industry. Used for relationship quality assessment.']

  Note: 'Tracks individual contacts associated with a vendor organization, including account managers, quality engineers, logistics coordinators, and executive sponsors. Captures full name, job title, department, email, phone, preferred language, contact role (commercial, technical, quality, logistics), and active status. Supports multi-threaded supplier relationship management across procurement, quality, and after-sales functions.'
}

Table supplier.qualification {
  qualification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier qualification record. Primary key.']
  qualification_approving_engineer_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  qualification_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality or sourcing engineer who authorized the qualification decision.']
  qualification_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the qualification record, captured for audit trail purposes.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier being qualified. Links to the supplier master data entity.']
  affected_commodities varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of commodity categories affected by a disqualification event. Null if not disqualified or if disqualification is global.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier was formally approved for the specified scope. Null if not yet approved or if disqualified.']
  audit_date date [note: 'Date when the on-site or remote supplier audit was conducted as part of the qualification process. Null if no audit was performed.']
  audit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score from the supplier qualification audit, typically expressed as a percentage (0.00 to 100.00). Null if no audit was conducted.']
  basis varchar [note: 'Primary method or standard used to qualify the supplier: PPAP (Production Part Approval Process), APQP (Advanced Product Quality Planning), on-site audit, sample approval, document review, third-party certification (ISO, UL, etc.), or legacy performance history. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ppap|apqp|on_site_audit|sample_approval|document_review|third_party_certification|legacy_performance — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  certification_required varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of certifications or standards the supplier must hold to maintain qualification (e.g., ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, IATF 16949, UL, CE). Null if no specific certifications are required.']
  commodity_category varchar [note: 'Primary commodity or part family for which the supplier is being qualified (e.g., electrical components, machined parts, castings, electronics, packaging materials). May reference a commodity taxonomy.']
  conditional_approval_expiry_date date [note: 'Date by which the supplier must satisfy conditional approval requirements to achieve full approval status. Null if not conditionally approved.']
  conditional_approval_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the supplier was granted conditional approval rather than full approval, including specific restrictions or pending actions. Null if not conditionally approved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualification record was first created in the system.']
  disqualification_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier was formally disqualified or approval was revoked. Null if not disqualified.']
  disqualification_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the supplier was disqualified or rejected, including specific quality, delivery, compliance, or financial issues. Null if not disqualified.']
  document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or link to the formal qualification documentation package, including audit reports, PPAP submissions, certificates, and approval memos.']
  evaluation_end_date date [note: 'Date when the qualification evaluation was completed and a decision was rendered.']
  evaluation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing additional observations, findings, or context from the qualification evaluation process.']
  evaluation_start_date date [note: 'Date when the formal supplier qualification evaluation process was initiated.']
  evaluation_team varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list or description of the cross-functional team members who participated in the supplier qualification evaluation (e.g., quality, engineering, procurement, operations).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the qualification approval expires and re-qualification is required. Null for indefinite approvals or disqualified suppliers.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the qualification record was last updated, reflecting the most recent change to status, scope, or other attributes.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review or re-qualification assessment of the supplier. Null if no review is scheduled or if disqualified.']
  part_family varchar [note: 'Specific part family or product line within the commodity category that the qualification covers. Allows for granular scope definition.']
  ppap_level varchar [note: 'PPAP submission level required or achieved (Level 1 through Level 5), or not applicable if PPAP was not the qualification basis. Higher levels require more comprehensive documentation.']
  qualification_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the qualification record, typically system-generated and used in external communications and audit trails.']
  qualification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supplier qualification: prospective (initial inquiry), under evaluation (assessment in progress), approved (fully qualified), conditional (approved with restrictions or pending actions), disqualified (rejected or revoked), expired (approval period lapsed).']
  qualification_type varchar [note: 'Type of qualification event: new supplier onboarding, periodic re-qualification, extension to new commodity categories, disqualification action, or conditional approval pending corrective action.']
  re_qualification_conditions varchar [note: 'Specific conditions or corrective actions the supplier must complete before being eligible for re-qualification. Null if not applicable or if permanently disqualified.']
  re_qualification_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a disqualified supplier is eligible to apply for re-qualification in the future. True if eligible, False if permanently disqualified.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment of the supplier at the time of qualification: low (minimal risk), medium (manageable risk), high (significant risk requiring mitigation), critical (unacceptable risk without immediate action).']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the qualification scope, including specific materials, processes, or services the supplier is approved to provide.']
  transition_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document number for the supplier transition or phase-out plan in the event of disqualification. Null if not applicable.']

  Note: 'Records the formal supplier qualification lifecycle for each vendor, tracking whether a vendor has been approved or disqualified for specific commodity categories or part families. Captures qualification status (prospective, under evaluation, approved, conditional, disqualified), qualification type (new supplier, re-qualification, commodity extension, disqualification), evaluation start/end dates, approving engineer, qualification basis (PPAP, APQP, audit, sample approval), expiry date, and for disqualification events: disqualification reason, affected commodities, transition plan reference, and re-qualification eligibility. Managed in SAP Ariba and aligned with PPAP/APQP standards.'
}

Table supplier.scorecard {
  scorecard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier performance scorecard record.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manager or director who approved and authorized the publication of this scorecard.']
  scorecard_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the buyer, procurement specialist, or quality engineer who conducted the scorecard evaluation.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier being evaluated in this scorecard.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scorecard was officially approved by the designated approver.']
  capa_count varchar [note: 'Total number of corrective and preventive action requests issued to this supplier during the evaluation period.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional comments, notes, or contextual information about the scorecard evaluation and results.']
  cost_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Performance score for the cost pillar, measuring price competitiveness, cost variance, and total cost of ownership, expressed as a percentage (0-100).']
  cost_variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between actual costs and target or budgeted costs during the evaluation period, where negative values indicate cost savings.']
  cost_weight_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight assigned to the cost pillar in the overall score calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scorecard record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values in this scorecard.']
  delivery_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Performance score for the delivery pillar, measuring on-time delivery performance and schedule adherence, expressed as a percentage (0-100).']
  delivery_weight_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight assigned to the delivery pillar in the overall score calculation.']
  evaluation_methodology varchar [note: 'Description of the scoring methodology, weighting scheme, and calculation approach used for this scorecard evaluation.']
  evaluation_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the performance evaluation period covered by this scorecard.']
  evaluation_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the performance evaluation period covered by this scorecard.']
  improvement_actions_required varchar [note: 'Summary of required improvement actions or corrective measures the supplier must implement based on scorecard results.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scorecard record was last modified or updated.']
  ncr_count varchar [note: 'Total number of non-conformance reports issued against this supplier during the evaluation period.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next performance review or scorecard evaluation for this supplier.']
  otd_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of deliveries received on or before the scheduled delivery date during the evaluation period, a key delivery performance indicator.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite overall performance score for the supplier, typically calculated as a weighted average of individual pillar scores, expressed as a percentage (0-100).']
  period_type varchar [note: 'The frequency or type of evaluation period for this scorecard (monthly, quarterly, annual, or ad-hoc).']
  ppm_defect_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of defective parts per million parts received during the evaluation period, a key quality performance indicator.']
  previous_rating_tier varchar [note: 'The rating tier assigned to the supplier in the previous evaluation period, used for trend analysis and tier movement tracking.']
  published_date date [note: 'The date when the scorecard was officially published and communicated to the supplier.']
  quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Performance score for the quality pillar, measuring defect rates, non-conformances, and quality compliance, expressed as a percentage (0-100).']
  quality_weight_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight assigned to the quality pillar in the overall score calculation.']
  rating_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic classification tier assigned to the supplier based on scorecard performance, driving sourcing decisions and supplier development programs.']
  responsiveness_index decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite index measuring supplier responsiveness across communication, issue resolution, and collaboration dimensions, expressed as a percentage (0-100).']
  responsiveness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Performance score for the responsiveness pillar, measuring communication effectiveness, issue resolution speed, and collaboration quality, expressed as a percentage (0-100).']
  responsiveness_weight_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight assigned to the responsiveness pillar in the overall score calculation.']
  rma_count varchar [note: 'Total number of return material authorizations processed for defective or non-conforming materials from this supplier during the evaluation period.']
  scorecard_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the scorecard, typically formatted as a human-readable code used in reports and communications.']
  scorecard_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scorecard indicating its approval and publication state.']
  strengths_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the supplier\'s key strengths and positive performance areas identified during the evaluation period.']
  total_line_items_evaluated varchar [note: 'Total number of purchase order line items included in the evaluation period for this scorecard.']
  total_orders_evaluated varchar [note: 'Total number of purchase orders included in the evaluation period for this scorecard.']
  total_purchase_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of purchases from this supplier during the evaluation period, used for spend-weighted performance analysis.']
  total_receipts_evaluated varchar [note: 'Total number of goods receipts or deliveries included in the evaluation period for this scorecard.']
  weaknesses_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the supplier\'s key weaknesses and performance gaps identified during the evaluation period.']

  Note: 'Periodic supplier performance scorecard capturing aggregated KPI results across quality, delivery, cost, and responsiveness dimensions for a defined evaluation period (monthly, quarterly, annual). Stores overall score, individual pillar scores (OTD — On-Time Delivery, PPM — Parts Per Million defect rate, cost variance, responsiveness index), scorecard period, rating tier (preferred, approved, conditional, development), and issuing buyer. Drives strategic sourcing decisions and supplier development programs.'
}

Table supplier.supplier_audit {
  supplier_audit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier audit record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or auditor record for the lead auditor conducting the audit.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Supplier audits are often scoped to a particular project; linking audit findings to the project ensures traceability and corrective‑action alignment.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Audits are performed to verify supplier adherence to specific regulations; linking enables audit‑to‑regulation traceability.']
  report_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or document management system link to the formal audit report document.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier being audited. Links to the supplier master data.']
  vendor_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_site. Business justification: Site‑specific audit information; each audit occurs at a particular supplier site, enabling site‑level risk tracking and reporting.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the quality manager or procurement director who approved the final audit report and findings.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the audit report was formally approved by the quality or procurement management.']
  audit_date date [note: 'The date when the supplier audit was conducted at the vendor site. This is the principal business event timestamp for the audit.']
  audit_method varchar [note: 'Method used to conduct the audit: on-site visit, remote audit via video conference, hybrid approach, or document review only.']
  audit_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique audit reference number assigned by the quality or procurement team for tracking and documentation purposes.']
  audit_result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the supplier audit: pass (no critical or major findings), conditional pass (findings require corrective action within timeframe), fail (critical findings or inability to meet standards), or pending (awaiting final determination).']
  audit_scope varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the audit scope including specific processes, departments, product lines, or standards being evaluated during the audit.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit: scheduled (planned but not started), in progress (fieldwork underway), completed (audit finished and report issued), cancelled (audit did not proceed), or report pending (fieldwork complete, awaiting final report).']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit based on its focus area: quality system audits (ISO 9001, IATF 16949), process audits, environmental audits (ISO 14001), cybersecurity audits (IEC 62443), social compliance audits, financial audits, or product audits. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: quality_system|process|environmental|cybersecurity|social_compliance|financial|product — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  auditor_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments and observations from the lead auditor summarizing key findings, strengths, and areas for improvement.']
  capa_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when all required corrective and preventive actions were verified as complete and closed.']
  capa_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the supplier must complete and submit corrective and preventive actions to address audit findings.']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether corrective and preventive action (CAPA) is required from the supplier to address audit findings.']
  certification_standard varchar [note: 'The specific certification standard or framework being audited against, such as ISO 9001, IATF 16949, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, IEC 62443, or other industry-specific standards.']
  cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for conducting the audit, including auditor fees, travel expenses, and administrative costs.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the audit cost (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the supplier audit record was first created in the system.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical non-conformances or findings identified during the audit that pose immediate risk to quality, safety, or compliance and require urgent corrective action.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the audit fieldwork was completed, applicable for multi-day audits.']
  follow_up_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled or actual date of the follow-up audit conducted to verify corrective action effectiveness.']
  follow_up_audit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether a follow-up audit is required to verify implementation and effectiveness of corrective actions.']
  frequency_months varchar [note: 'Standard audit frequency interval in months for this supplier based on risk classification and business requirements (e.g., 12, 24, 36 months).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the supplier audit record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  lead_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the lead auditor responsible for conducting and overseeing the supplier audit.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical location or site where the audit was conducted, typically the supplier facility address or site name.']
  major_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of major non-conformances or findings identified during the audit that represent significant deviations from standards or requirements.']
  minor_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of minor non-conformances or findings identified during the audit that represent isolated or low-impact deviations from standards.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next supplier audit based on audit frequency policy, risk rating, and previous audit results.']
  observation_count varchar [note: 'Number of observations or opportunities for improvement noted during the audit that do not constitute non-conformances but warrant attention.']
  report_date date [note: 'Date when the formal audit report was issued to the supplier and internal stakeholders.']
  risk_rating_post_audit varchar [note: 'Updated supplier risk rating assigned after the audit based on audit findings and overall performance evaluation.']
  risk_rating_pre_audit varchar [note: 'Supplier risk rating assigned before the audit based on historical performance, criticality, and risk assessment criteria.']
  score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score assigned to the audit based on evaluation criteria, typically expressed as a percentage or weighted score reflecting overall supplier performance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this audit record originated (e.g., SAP QM, Ariba Supplier Management, or dedicated audit management system).']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the audit fieldwork commenced, applicable for multi-day audits.']
  supplier_response varchar [note: 'Free-text response or feedback from the supplier regarding the audit findings and proposed corrective actions.']
  team_members varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of additional auditor names who participated in the audit as team members.']
  total_findings_count varchar [note: 'Total number of all findings (critical, major, minor) identified during the audit, excluding observations.']

  Note: 'Records formal supplier audits conducted at vendor sites, including quality system audits (ISO 9001, IATF 16949), process audits, environmental audits (ISO 14001), cybersecurity audits (IEC 62443), and social compliance audits. Captures audit type, audit date, lead auditor, audit scope, findings count by severity (critical, major, minor), overall audit result (pass, conditional pass, fail), and next scheduled audit date. Linked to CAPA records for finding remediation tracking.'
}

Table supplier.supplier_audit_finding {
  supplier_audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier audit finding record. Primary key.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the formal CAPA record created to address this finding, if applicable.']
  previous_finding_supplier_audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous finding record if this is identified as a repeat finding.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Audit findings often cite the exact regulatory clause violated; FK provides direct reference for corrective‑action tracking.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: FINDING REMEDIATION: Assigns a responsible employee for each audit finding; needed for corrective‑action tracking and closure reporting.']
  supplier_audit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent supplier audit event during which this finding was identified.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier against whom this finding was raised.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the lead auditor who identified and documented this finding during the supplier audit.']
  clause_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific standard clause, requirement, or specification section that was not met (e.g., ISO 9001:2015 Clause 8.5.1, customer specification section 4.2).']
  closure_date date [note: 'Actual date when the finding was formally closed after successful verification of corrective action effectiveness.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, context, or clarifications related to the finding, its resolution, or verification.']
  containment_action varchar [note: 'Immediate containment or interim action taken to prevent further impact while root cause analysis and permanent corrective action are developed.']
  corrective_action_required varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action(s) required to address the finding and prevent recurrence. Links to Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this finding record was first created in the system.']
  due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the corrective action must be completed and evidence submitted for verification.']
  escalation_date date [note: 'Date when the finding was escalated to higher management levels.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding has been escalated to senior management or supplier development team due to severity, repeat occurrence, or lack of timely response.']
  evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting evidence, documentation, photographs, or records that substantiate the finding (e.g., document IDs, photo file names, sample numbers).']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the non-conformance or observation, including objective evidence and specific details of what was found during the audit.']
  finding_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique reference number assigned to this finding for tracking and communication purposes.']
  finding_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the finding in its resolution lifecycle from identification through closure.']
  finding_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the finding based on its nature and severity impact. Non-conformance indicates a deviation from requirements; observation is a potential issue; opportunity for improvement suggests enhancement areas; positive finding highlights best practices.']
  identified_date date [note: 'Date when the finding was first identified during the audit activity.']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the potential or actual impact of this finding on product quality, delivery, cost, safety, or compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this finding record was last updated or modified.']
  process_area varchar [note: 'The business process or functional area where the finding was identified (e.g., Incoming Inspection, Production Control, Document Management, Calibration).']
  repeat_finding_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding is a repeat of a previously identified issue from a prior audit, suggesting ineffective corrective action or systemic problem.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the supplier personnel or department responsible for implementing the corrective action.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to this finding based on likelihood and impact assessment, used for prioritization and escalation decisions.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Detailed analysis of the underlying root cause(s) of the finding, typically using structured problem-solving methods such as 5 Whys, Fishbone Diagram, or Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA).']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the root cause (e.g., Training Gap, Process Inadequacy, Resource Constraint, Documentation Error, Equipment Failure, Communication Breakdown). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: training_gap|process_inadequacy|resource_constraint|documentation_error|equipment_failure|communication_breakdown|supplier_error|design_flaw — promote to reference product]']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the finding. Critical indicates immediate risk to product safety or compliance; major indicates significant system breakdown; minor indicates isolated or low-impact deviation.']
  supplier_response varchar [note: 'Formal response provided by the supplier acknowledging the finding and outlining their proposed corrective action plan.']
  supplier_response_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier submitted their formal response to the finding.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action effectiveness was verified.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify corrective action effectiveness (e.g., Follow-up Audit, Document Review, On-site Inspection, Performance Data Analysis).']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of the verification activity to confirm that the corrective action has been implemented and is effective in preventing recurrence.']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the auditor or quality personnel who verified the corrective action effectiveness.']

  Note: 'Individual non-conformance or observation identified during a supplier audit. Each finding has its own lifecycle from identification through closure. Captures finding reference number, finding type (non-conformance, observation, opportunity for improvement), severity (critical, major, minor), process area affected, finding description, root cause category, required corrective action, due date, closure date, and verification status. Supports CAPA tracking and repeat-finding analysis.'
}

Table supplier.supplier_certification {
  supplier_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier certification record. Primary key for the supplier certification entity.']
  certifying_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: CERTIFICATION OVERSIGHT: Tracks which internal employee manages each supplier certification; required for compliance audit traceability.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Projects require specific supplier certifications; linking enables per‑project certification compliance reporting and risk assessment.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Required for tracking which regulatory requirement each supplier certification satisfies; essential for compliance reporting and audit readiness.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier who holds this certification. Links to the supplier master data entity.']
  vendor_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific supplier site or facility where this certification applies. Nullable if certification applies to the entire supplier organization rather than a specific site.']
  accreditation_body varchar [note: 'The name of the accreditation body that accredits the issuing certification body. Examples include ANAB (ANSI National Accreditation Board), UKAS (United Kingdom Accreditation Service), DAkkS (German Accreditation Body), or other national accreditation authorities.']
  alert_threshold_days varchar [note: 'The number of days before expiry at which automated alerts should be triggered to notify stakeholders of the upcoming certification expiration. Typical values range from 30 to 180 days.']
  audit_report_url varchar [note: 'The URL or file path to the most recent audit report document stored in the document management system or cloud storage.']
  audit_result varchar [note: 'The outcome of the most recent audit. Passed indicates full compliance. Passed with observations indicates minor non-conformances that do not affect certification. Failed indicates major non-conformances. Conditional indicates certification granted pending corrective actions. Deferred indicates the audit decision is postponed.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'The type of audit that resulted in this certification or its most recent validation. Initial audit is the first certification audit. Surveillance audits are periodic checks. Recertification audits occur at the end of the certification cycle. Special audits address specific issues. Transfer audits occur when changing certification bodies.']
  auto_renewal_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether automatic renewal workflows and alerts are enabled for this certification. True if the system should automatically trigger renewal processes and notifications. False if renewal is managed manually.']
  business_criticality varchar [note: 'The importance of this certification to the business relationship with the supplier. Critical certifications are mandatory for continued business. High criticality certifications are strongly preferred. Medium and low criticality certifications are informational or nice-to-have.']
  certificate_document_url varchar [note: 'The URL or file path to the digital copy of the certification certificate document stored in the document management system or cloud storage.']
  certification_level varchar [note: 'The tier, grade, or level of certification achieved, if the standard defines multiple levels. Examples include Level 1, Level 2, Level 3 for IEC 62443, or Gold, Silver, Bronze for tiered certification programs.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'The unique certificate number or registration number issued by the certifying body. This is the externally-known identifier for the certification.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of the certification. Active indicates the certification is valid and in good standing. Expired indicates the certification has passed its expiry date. Suspended indicates temporary suspension by the issuing body. Revoked indicates permanent withdrawal. Pending renewal indicates the certification is in the renewal process. Under review indicates the certification is being audited or reassessed.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'The category or domain of the certification. Classifies certifications into quality management, environmental management, health and safety, energy management, cybersecurity, product safety, industry-specific standards, or regulatory compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: quality|environmental|health_safety|energy|cybersecurity|product_safety|industry_specific|regulatory_compliance — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  certified_processes varchar [note: 'A comma-separated or structured list of specific manufacturing processes, production lines, or operational activities that are covered under this certification. Examples include welding, machining, assembly, testing, or packaging.']
  certified_products varchar [note: 'A comma-separated or structured list of specific product categories, product families, or SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) that are covered under this certification. Used to gate procurement decisions for regulated commodity categories.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or context about this certification. May include information about special conditions, limitations, or historical context.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which all required corrective actions must be completed and verified. Failure to meet this date may result in certification suspension or revocation.']
  corrective_actions_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether corrective actions are required to address audit findings. True if corrective actions are pending or in progress. False if no corrective actions are needed or all have been completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this certification record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and compliance tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the certification becomes effective and valid for business use. May differ from issue date if there is a grace period or transition period.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the certification expires and is no longer valid. Critical for triggering renewal workflows and procurement gating decisions.']
  gated_commodity_categories varchar [note: 'A comma-separated list of commodity categories or material groups for which this certification is mandatory. Procurement of these commodities from this supplier is blocked if the certification is not active and valid.']
