  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project or capital investment program to which this purchase order is allocated.']
  requester_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who originated the purchase requisition leading to this purchase order.']
  requisition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating purchase requisition that triggered this purchase order.']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the RFQ or RFP that preceded this purchase order in the sourcing process.']
  sales_order_intake_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: Each purchase order fulfills a specific sales order intake; linking enables end‑to‑end order‑to‑procurement tracking.']
  service_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_contract. Business justification: Purchase orders for service work are issued under a specific service contract, enabling contract‑based billing and compliance reporting.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Needed for \'Order‑Driven Procurement\' process where purchase orders are tied to the specific product SKU being manufactured, supporting make‑to‑order execution.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific delivery address or warehouse location for goods receipt.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor to whom this purchase order is issued.']
  supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: Supply plans dictate purchase order creation; linking PO to supply_plan enables the \'Supply Plan Execution\' report.']
  vendor_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_site. Business justification: Required for Logistics Planning Report: PO must reference the exact supplier site delivering goods, enabling site‑specific lead‑time and cost calculations.']
  acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date when the supplier acknowledged the purchase order.']
  acknowledgement_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the supplier has acknowledged receipt and acceptance of the purchase order.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order received final approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status for the purchase order.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was administratively closed after all goods were received and invoices processed.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Financial accounting organizational unit representing the legal entity for this purchase order.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the purchase order meets all applicable procurement compliance policies and regulatory requirements.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'Date confirmed by the supplier for delivery of goods or completion of services.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the purchase order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this purchase order.']
  goods_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating the extent to which ordered goods have been received against this purchase order.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for delivery, insurance, and risk transfer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'Named place or port specified in the Incoterms agreement where risk and cost transfer occurs.']
  invoice_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating the extent to which supplier invoices have been received and matched against this purchase order.']
  material_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the type of materials or services being procured. Direct materials are used in production, indirect materials support operations, MRO is maintenance/repair/operations supplies.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the purchase order record was last modified.']
  net_po_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net total value of the purchase order after taxes and all adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional instructions, special requirements, or comments related to the purchase order.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Code representing the agreed payment terms with the supplier (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30).']
  po_date date [note: 'Date when the purchase order was created and issued to the supplier. Principal business event timestamp for the transaction.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the purchase order. Used in supplier communications and invoice matching.']
  po_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase order in the procure-to-pay workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|issued|acknowledged|in_progress|partially_received|fully_received|closed|cancelled — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  po_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchase order type. Standard for one-time orders, blanket for recurring orders with release schedules, framework for long-term agreements, subcontracting for external processing, consignment for supplier-owned inventory.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the purchase order affecting processing and delivery urgency.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Code identifying the buyer or procurement team responsible for this purchase order.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Code identifying the organizational unit responsible for procurement activities. Represents the legal entity negotiating with suppliers.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the buyer requests delivery of goods or completion of services.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation specified for delivery of goods.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to this purchase order.']
  total_po_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross value of the purchase order including all line items before taxes and charges.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'Work breakdown structure element for project-based purchase orders, enabling cost tracking at the project task level.']

  Note: 'Core transactional document representing a legally binding purchase order (PO) issued to a supplier. Captures PO number, supplier, plant/delivery location, payment terms, incoterms, total PO value, currency, PO type (standard, blanket, framework, subcontracting), approval status, and confirmation status. Central document in the procure-to-pay process linking requisitions to goods receipts and supplier invoices.'
}

Table procurement.po_line_item {
  po_line_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the purchase order line item. Primary key for the po_line_item product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Line‑item expense allocation to cost center enables detailed cost reporting and variance analysis.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: EQUIPMENT LINE ITEM: Each PO line for a device (PLC, sensor) maps to a specific device registry entry for configuration management.']
  engineering_bom_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.bom_line. Business justification: Order line items are derived from BOM lines during material planning; linking enables line‑level reconciliation.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Line‑item posting to GL account is required for accurate expense classification in the general ledger.']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.order_line. Business justification: Ensures traceability of each PO line to the specific sales order line it supplies, essential for the Order‑to‑Purchase line reconciliation process.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record being procured. Links to the specific product, component, or service being ordered.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent purchase order header. Links this line item to its containing purchase order document.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: REQUIRED: Line item defines where received material will be stored; required for put‑away execution and inventory valuation per location.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: Line‑item costs must be allocated to a WBS element for earned‑value and cost‑control reporting; the existing wbs_element string is replaced by a FK.']
  account_assignment_category varchar [note: 'Classification determining how procurement costs are allocated in financial accounting (e.g., to cost center, asset, WBS element, or sales order).']
  buyer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the procurement professional responsible for sourcing and managing this purchase order line item.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this purchase order line item record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the net price and line item value (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  deletion_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating this line item has been marked for deletion or cancellation. Prevents further goods receipt and invoice posting.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Requested or committed delivery date for this line item. Used for MRP planning, production scheduling, and supplier performance tracking.']
  final_invoice_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating the final invoice has been received and no further invoices are expected for this line item.']
  goods_receipt_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether goods receipt is required for this line item. Determines three-way match requirements for invoice verification.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Standardized trade terms defining responsibilities for shipping, insurance, and risk transfer (e.g., EXW, FOB, CIF, DDP).']
  incoterms_location varchar [note: 'Named place or port specified in the Incoterms clause where risk and cost transfer occurs.']
  invoice_receipt_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether invoice verification is required for this line item. Controls accounts payable processing workflow.']
  item_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the procurement item type. Determines procurement processing rules, inventory treatment, and account assignment requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this purchase order line item record was last updated or modified.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase order line item. Tracks progression from open through receipt to closure.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'Original equipment manufacturer part number for the material. Used for quality assurance and technical specification verification.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the material being procured. The externally-known SKU or part number used in procurement documents and supplier communication.']
  net_order_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of this line item calculated as (quantity_ordered / price_unit) * net_price. Excludes taxes and freight charges.']
  net_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The negotiated price per unit of measure before taxes and additional charges. Used to calculate line item total value.']
  open_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Outstanding quantity still to be delivered, calculated as quantity_ordered minus quantity_received. Used for supplier follow-up and expediting.']
  over_delivery_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable percentage by which the supplier may over-deliver beyond the ordered quantity without requiring approval.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The manufacturing plant or facility where the material will be received and consumed. Determines receiving location and inventory posting.']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'The quantity for which the net price is valid (e.g., price per 1, per 100, per 1000 units). Used in unit price calculation.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether incoming quality inspection is mandatory before goods receipt posting for this line item.']
  quantity_invoiced decimal(18,3) [note: 'Cumulative quantity invoiced by the supplier against this line item to date. Used for invoice verification and payment processing.']
  quantity_ordered decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material or service units being procured on this line item. Used for goods receipt matching and invoice verification.']
  quantity_received decimal(18,3) [note: 'Cumulative quantity of goods received against this line item to date. Used for partial delivery tracking and open quantity calculation.']
  requisitioner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the employee or department that originated the purchase requisition leading to this line item.']
  shipping_instruction varchar [note: 'Special instructions for packaging, labeling, or delivery requirements specific to this line item.']
  short_text varchar [note: 'Brief description of the material or service being procured on this line. Provides human-readable context for the line item.']
  source_of_supply varchar [note: 'Classification of the procurement source type. Determines procurement processing logic and inventory valuation method.']
  supplier_material_number varchar [note: 'The suppliers own part number or SKU for the material being procured. Used for supplier communication and catalog matching.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount calculated for this line item based on tax code and net order value.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code determining applicable tax rates and tax jurisdiction for this procurement line item.']
  under_delivery_tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable percentage by which the supplier may under-deliver below the ordered quantity without penalty or rejection.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the ordered quantity is expressed (e.g., EA, KG, M, L, HR). Must align with material master and supplier agreement.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a purchase order representing a specific material, service, or SKU being procured. Captures line number, material number, short text, quantity ordered, unit of measure, net price, delivery date, storage location, account assignment (cost center, WBS element, asset), and goods receipt indicator. Enables granular spend tracking, partial delivery management, and three-way match at the item level.'
}

Table procurement.rfq {
  rfq_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the request for quotation record. Primary key.']
  buyer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: RFQ process assigns a buyer employee responsible for the event; tracking this employee is required for RFQ ownership reports and compliance.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal business unit, plant, or legal entity issuing the RFQ. Links to organizational hierarchy for spend analysis and compliance reporting.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: RFQs are often issued for specific projects; linking to project_header enables RFQ‑to‑project reporting and compliance checks.']
  requisition_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the originating purchase requisition that triggered this RFQ. Links sourcing activity back to internal demand.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the RFQ was approved for issuance by the authorized approver. Part of procurement governance audit trail.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of internal approval workflow for issuing this RFQ. Ensures procurement governance and authorization controls are met before publication.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager or procurement authority who approved the issuance of this RFQ. Supports audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  award_date date [note: 'Date when the RFQ was awarded to one or more suppliers and purchase orders or contracts were issued. Null if not yet awarded.']
  bid_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the bid bond or financial guarantee required from suppliers, if applicable. Null if no bid bond is required.']
  bid_bond_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether suppliers are required to submit a bid bond or financial guarantee as part of their quotation. Common for high-value capital equipment or construction projects.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason code for why the RFQ was cancelled, if applicable. Supports root cause analysis and process improvement.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Standardized commodity or material group code identifying the category of goods or services being sourced. May align with UNSPSC or internal taxonomy.']
  commodity_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the commodity or material group associated with this RFQ.']
  confidentiality_agreement_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether suppliers must sign a non-disclosure agreement (NDA) or confidentiality agreement before accessing RFQ details.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this RFQ record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated value and supplier quotations. Ensures consistent financial reporting.']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'Destination site, plant, warehouse, or facility where materials or equipment are to be delivered. May reference a specific location code or address.']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) specifying delivery responsibilities, risk transfer, and cost allocation between buyer and supplier.']
  estimated_total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total monetary value of the procurement covered by this RFQ. Used for spend analysis and sourcing strategy planning.']
  evaluation_criteria varchar [note: 'Documented criteria and weighting factors used to evaluate and score supplier responses. May include price, quality, delivery, technical capability, and sustainability factors.']
  invited_supplier_count varchar [note: 'Number of suppliers invited to participate in this RFQ. Supports competitive sourcing analysis and supplier engagement metrics.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the RFQ was officially issued and published to invited suppliers. Marks the start of the supplier response period.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this RFQ record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or internal notes related to this RFQ. Not typically shared with suppliers.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms communicated to suppliers in the RFQ, such as Net 30, Net 60, or early payment discount terms.']
  procurement_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the procurement spend category. Aligns with organizational spend taxonomy and sourcing strategy.']
  purchasing_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the purchasing group or procurement team responsible for managing this RFQ. Aligns with SAP MM purchasing organization structure.']
  quality_certification_required varchar [note: 'List of quality certifications or standards that suppliers must hold or materials must meet, such as ISO 9001, ISO 14001, UL, CE marking, or industry-specific certifications.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Target date by which the buyer requires delivery of the materials or completion of services. Used to assess supplier lead time capability.']
  response_opening_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when submitted supplier responses will be opened and made available for evaluation. Used in sealed-bid scenarios.']
  response_received_count varchar [note: 'Number of valid quotations received from suppliers by the submission deadline. Used to assess competitive response rate.']
  rfq_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the materials, services, or capital equipment being sourced, including specifications, scope of work, and any special requirements.']
  rfq_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the RFQ document, externally visible to suppliers and internal stakeholders. Typically follows organizational numbering convention.']
  rfq_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RFQ. Tracks progression from creation through supplier response collection to award or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|published|open|closed|awarded|cancelled|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system from which this RFQ record originated. Supports data lineage and integration traceability.']
  sourcing_event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sourcing mechanism used for this RFQ. Determines bidding rules, evaluation process, and supplier interaction model.']
  submission_deadline timestamp [note: 'Date and time by which suppliers must submit their quotations. No late submissions are typically accepted after this timestamp.']
  sustainability_criteria varchar [note: 'Environmental, social, and governance (ESG) criteria or sustainability requirements that suppliers must address in their quotations, such as carbon footprint, ethical sourcing, or circular economy practices.']
  technical_specification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether suppliers must provide detailed technical specifications, certifications, or compliance documentation as part of their response.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title or subject line of the RFQ, summarizing the procurement need or sourcing event.']

  Note: 'Request for Quotation document issued to one or more suppliers to solicit competitive pricing for materials, services, or capital equipment. Captures RFQ number, sourcing event type, commodity category, submission deadline, evaluation criteria, invited supplier list, bid bond requirements, and status. Supports competitive sourcing and strategic procurement decisions for direct and indirect categories.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_quotation {
  supplier_quotation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier quotation record. Primary key.']
  compliance_product_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.product_certification. Business justification: Quotation evaluation includes verifying that quoted items have required product certifications per regulatory standards.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the procurement buyer or sourcing specialist responsible for evaluating and managing this quotation.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or service being quoted. Links to the material master record.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order created as a result of awarding this quotation.']
  sourcing_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating sourcing event (RFQ or RFP) for which this quotation was submitted.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier who submitted this quotation.']
  award_date date [note: 'Date when the quotation was officially awarded and the supplier was notified of the purchase order.']
  award_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this quotation was selected and awarded a purchase order (True) or not (False).']
  bid_rank varchar [note: 'Numerical ranking of this quotation relative to other quotations received for the same sourcing event, based on evaluation criteria (1 = best).']
  commercial_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quotation meets all commercial terms and conditions defined in the sourcing event (True) or has deviations (False).']
  commercial_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes explaining any commercial deviations, exceptions, or clarifications regarding payment terms, delivery terms, or other commercial conditions.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166 country code indicating where the material or product will be manufactured or sourced.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quotation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the quoted price (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'Destination location or plant code where the supplier will deliver the goods.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount offered by the supplier off the list price or baseline price.']
  environmental_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quotation meets environmental and sustainability requirements (e.g., RoHS, REACH, conflict minerals).']
  evaluation_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weighted evaluation score assigned to the quotation based on predefined criteria (price, quality, delivery, service). Scale typically 0-100.']
  freight_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Shipping and freight charges quoted by the supplier for delivery to the specified location.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for delivery, insurance, and risk transfer (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quotation record was last updated or modified.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from purchase order placement to delivery, as committed by the supplier.']
  material_group varchar [note: 'Classification code grouping similar materials for procurement and reporting purposes.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered for the supplier to honor the quoted price and terms.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms offered by the supplier (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Defines when payment is due and any early payment discounts.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility code where the material will be delivered and consumed.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Buyer group or commodity team responsible for the specific material category being quoted.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for procurement activities and supplier negotiations for this quotation.']
  quality_certification varchar [note: 'Quality certifications or standards compliance declared by the supplier (e.g., ISO 9001, AS9100, IATF 16949).']
  quotation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the supplier quotation, typically assigned by the supplier or sourcing system. Externally visible reference number used in communications and documentation.']
  quotation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the supplier quotation in the sourcing workflow.']
  quoted_unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit of measure quoted by the supplier for the material or service.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the quotation was rejected, if applicable. Used for supplier feedback and continuous improvement.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Additional comments, clarifications, or special conditions provided by the supplier or procurement team regarding the quotation.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier submitted the quotation to the sourcing event.']
  supplier_reference_number varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s internal reference or quotation number for tracking and correspondence purposes.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount included in or added to the quoted price, based on applicable tax jurisdiction.']
  technical_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the quotation meets all technical specifications and requirements defined in the sourcing event (True) or has deviations (False).']
  technical_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes explaining any technical deviations, exceptions, or clarifications regarding compliance with specifications.']
  total_cost_of_ownership decimal(18,2) [note: 'Comprehensive cost evaluation including quoted price, freight, taxes, quality costs, and other factors used for supplier comparison.']
  total_quoted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the quotation, calculated as quoted unit price multiplied by requested quantity.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quoted quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter).']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date of the quotation validity period. The date from which the quoted prices and terms become effective.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'End date of the quotation validity period. The date after which the quoted prices and terms expire.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration of warranty coverage in months offered by the supplier for the quoted material or service.']

  Note: 'Formal price quotation submitted by a supplier in response to an RFQ or RFP sourcing event. Captures quotation number, supplier reference, validity period, quoted unit price, lead time, minimum order quantity, payment terms, incoterms, technical compliance notes, and bid ranking. Enables side-by-side supplier comparison and award decision documentation. Links to the originating sourcing event and the awarded purchase order.'
}

Table procurement.sourcing_event {
  sourcing_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sourcing event. Primary key.']
  event_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Sourcing events are owned by a specific employee; the owner ID is needed for event‑ownership dashboards and audit trails.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Sourcing events must align with regulatory requirements governing commodity categories, ensuring sourced materials meet compliance.']
  actual_savings_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual cost savings amount achieved through the sourcing event, calculated as the difference between baseline and awarded pricing.']
  actual_savings_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual cost savings percentage achieved through the sourcing event, calculated as the percentage reduction from baseline to awarded pricing.']
  award_date date [note: 'Date when the sourcing event was awarded to selected suppliers.']
  award_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy used to determine award allocation. Lowest price awards to the supplier with the lowest bid, best value considers multiple factors beyond price, weighted score uses a scoring model with multiple criteria, multi-supplier splits award across multiple suppliers, single supplier awards entire volume to one supplier.']
  awarded_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total spend amount awarded to suppliers as a result of this sourcing event, representing the contracted value.']
  awarded_supplier_count varchar [note: 'Number of suppliers who received awards or contracts as a result of this sourcing event.']
  baseline_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Baseline or incumbent spend amount used as the reference point for calculating savings. Typically represents current or historical pricing.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'Business unit or division that initiated and owns this sourcing event.']
  commodity_category varchar [note: 'Commodity or category being sourced in this event, such as raw materials, components, MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Operations) supplies, indirect materials, or capital equipment.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Standardized classification code for the commodity or category, typically aligned with UNSPSC (United Nations Standard Products and Services Code) or internal taxonomy.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory, quality, or certification requirements that suppliers must meet to participate in this sourcing event, such as ISO certifications, environmental standards, or industry-specific compliance.']
  contract_duration_months varchar [note: 'Planned duration of the resulting contract in months.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Type of contract that will result from this sourcing event, such as blanket purchase agreement, framework agreement, spot buy, or long-term contract.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sourcing event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this sourcing event.']
  delivery_weight_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight assigned to delivery and lead time criteria in the overall evaluation scoring model.']
  estimated_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated spend value for the sourcing event, representing the anticipated contract value or purchase volume across all awarded suppliers.']
  evaluation_criteria varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the criteria used to evaluate supplier bids, such as price, quality, delivery time, technical capability, sustainability, and past performance.']
  evaluation_end_date date [note: 'Date when the evaluation of supplier bids was completed.']
  evaluation_start_date date [note: 'Date when the evaluation of supplier bids commenced.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the sourcing event, typically including commodity or category reference for easy identification.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the sourcing event, typically system-generated or manually assigned for external reference and tracking.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sourcing event. Draft indicates event is being prepared, published means event is visible to suppliers but not yet accepting bids, open indicates active bidding period, closed means bidding has ended and evaluation is underway, awarded indicates suppliers have been selected, cancelled means event was terminated without award.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of competitive sourcing event. RFQ (Request for Quotation) for price-focused procurement, RFP (Request for Proposal) for complex requirements, reverse auction for dynamic bidding, e-auction for electronic competitive bidding, multi-round negotiation for iterative supplier engagement, sealed bid for confidential single-round submissions.']
  invited_supplier_count varchar [note: 'Number of suppliers invited to participate in the sourcing event.']
  is_multi_round boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sourcing event includes multiple rounds of bidding or negotiation. True for multi-round events, false for single-round events.']
  is_sealed_bid boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supplier bids are sealed and confidential until the submission deadline. True for sealed bid events, false for transparent or visible bidding.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified the sourcing event record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the sourcing event record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the sourcing event.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility code for which materials or services are being sourced in this event.']
  price_weight_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight assigned to price in the overall evaluation scoring model. Typically ranges from 0 to 100.']
  publish_date date [note: 'Date when the sourcing event was published and made visible to invited suppliers.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Purchasing group or buyer team responsible for this sourcing event, typically specialized by commodity or category.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Purchasing organization responsible for executing this sourcing event, typically aligned with SAP organizational structure.']
  quality_weight_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight assigned to quality criteria in the overall evaluation scoring model.']
  responding_supplier_count varchar [note: 'Number of suppliers who submitted bids or responses to the sourcing event.']
  round_count varchar [note: 'Number of bidding or negotiation rounds conducted in this sourcing event.']
  savings_target_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target cost savings amount that the sourcing event aims to achieve compared to baseline or incumbent pricing.']
  savings_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target cost savings percentage that the sourcing event aims to achieve compared to baseline or incumbent pricing.']
  submission_deadline timestamp [note: 'Date and time by which suppliers must submit their bids or proposals. No submissions are accepted after this timestamp.']
  technical_weight_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage weight assigned to technical capability and compliance criteria in the overall evaluation scoring model.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created the sourcing event record.']

  Note: 'Strategic sourcing event record representing any competitive procurement event including RFQs, RFPs, reverse auctions, e-auctions, and multi-round negotiations for a commodity or category. Captures event name, event type, commodity category, total estimated spend, invited supplier count, submission deadline, evaluation criteria and weightings, award strategy (lowest price, best value, weighted score), savings target, actual savings achieved, event owner, supplier bid responses, bid rankings, and final award status. Serves as the SSOT for all competitive sourcing activity including quotation management. Supports category management governance, sourcing pipeline tracking, and procurement value reporting.'
}

Table procurement.procurement_contract {
  procurement_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the procurement contract record. Primary key.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Contracts are tied to specific compliance obligations they fulfill, linking contract management to obligation tracking.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.control_system. Business justification: CONTRACT MANAGEMENT: Contracts for control system supply/maintenance must reference the specific control system asset.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: CONTRACT COVERAGE: Device‑level contracts (service, warranty) require linking contract to each device in the registry.']
  primary_procurement_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or procurement manager responsible for managing and administering this contract.']
  product_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Supports \'Family‑Level Supply Contract Management\' allowing contracts to be associated with product families for volume‑discount negotiations.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Contracts are awarded for specific projects; linking contracts to project headers supports contract‑to‑project compliance and financial reporting.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Contract compliance review requires linking each contract to the regulatory requirement it must satisfy, ensuring legal and safety adherence.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor party with whom this procurement contract is established.']
  tertiary_procurement_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or employee who last modified this contract record.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of amendments or change orders issued against this contract since its original approval.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was formally approved by authorized signatories and became legally binding.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews upon expiration if not explicitly terminated. True for auto-renewing contracts, False otherwise.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the contract with internal procurement policies, regulatory requirements, and governance standards. Compliant indicates full adherence, non-compliant flags violations, under review indicates active audit, waived indicates approved exception.']
  confidentiality_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes confidentiality or non-disclosure provisions protecting proprietary information. True if confidentiality terms are present, False otherwise.']
  contract_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the scope, purpose, and key terms of the procurement contract.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the procurement contract for easy identification and reference.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the procurement contract, typically assigned by SAP Ariba or ERP system.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the procurement contract. Draft indicates initial creation, pending approval awaits authorization, active is in force, suspended is temporarily halted, expired has passed end date, terminated is cancelled before expiration, closed is completed and archived. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|expired|terminated|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the procurement contract structure. Blanket PO for recurring purchases with predefined terms, scheduling agreement for delivery schedules, value contract for spend-based limits, quantity contract for volume-based commitments, framework agreement for multi-supplier arrangements, or master agreement for overarching terms.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this procurement contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this contract (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY).']
  delivery_location varchar [note: 'Primary delivery destination or plant location for materials or services procured under this contract.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the procurement contract becomes legally binding and operational.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the procurement contract term ends and is no longer in force. Nullable for open-ended contracts.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Standardized trade terms defining responsibilities for shipping, insurance, and risk transfer between buyer and supplier per ICC Incoterms 2020. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent amendment or modification to the contract terms. Nullable if no amendments have been made.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this procurement contract record was last updated or modified.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard procurement lead time in days from purchase order release to delivery, as committed by the supplier in the contract.']
  material_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of materials or services covered by this contract. Direct materials are production inputs, indirect materials are non-production consumables, MRO supplies are maintenance/repair/operations items, capital equipment are fixed assets, services are labor or professional services.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity per release or purchase order required by the supplier under this contract. Nullable if no MOQ is specified.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated in the contract (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30 for early payment discounts).']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Description of financial penalties or liquidated damages applicable for supplier non-compliance with contract terms, SLA breaches, or quality failures.']
  price_deescalation_mechanism varchar [note: 'Formula or methodology for reducing contract prices based on volume commitments, market conditions, or continuous improvement targets.']
  price_escalation_mechanism varchar [note: 'Formula or methodology for adjusting contract prices over time based on indices (e.g., CPI, commodity indices), exchange rates, or negotiated percentage increases.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Buyer group or category team within the purchasing organization responsible for this contract, typically aligned to commodity or material category.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit or division responsible for negotiating and executing this procurement contract within the ERP system.']
  quality_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific quality standards, certifications, inspection criteria, and acceptance testing requirements mandated in the contract (e.g., ISO 9001, PPAP, APQP, specific Cpk targets).']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for target quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, M for meters, HR for hours).']
  release_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Cumulative quantity already released or called off against this contract through purchase orders or delivery schedules.']
  release_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value already released or spent against this contract through purchase orders.']
  remaining_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity still available for release under this contract, calculated as target quantity minus release quantity.']
  remaining_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value still available for release under this contract, calculated as total contract value minus release value.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months for each automatic renewal period if auto renewal is enabled. Nullable if auto renewal is not applicable.']
  sla_terms varchar [note: 'Service level commitments and performance targets defined in the contract, such as lead time, on-time delivery rate, quality standards, and response times.']
  target_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Planned or committed quantity of materials or services to be procured under this contract for quantity-based agreements. Nullable for value-based contracts.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the contract before expiration.']
  total_contract_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value committed under this procurement contract for value-based contracts, or estimated total spend for quantity-based contracts.']
  warranty_terms varchar [note: 'Warranty coverage and duration provided by the supplier for materials or services under this contract, including defect remediation and replacement terms.']

  Note: 'Master procurement contract or framework agreement established with a supplier for recurring supply of materials or services over a defined term. Captures contract number, contract type (blanket PO, scheduling agreement, value contract, quantity contract), effective/expiration dates, total contract value, target/release quantities, auto-renewal flag, SLA terms, penalty clauses, price escalation/de-escalation mechanisms, compliance status, and contract owner. Distinct from sales contracts owned by the sales domain. Supports contract coverage KPI tracking and maverick spend identification.'
}

Table procurement.contract_release_order {
  contract_release_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the contract release order record. Primary key for this entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center to which the release order value will be charged for financial accounting.']
  delivery_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the delivery address for this release, may differ from the default plant address for direct shipments.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the general ledger account for posting the release order expense.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or service being released under this order.']
  plant_data_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where the released materials will be delivered.']
  primary_contract_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or user who requested this release order.']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the scheduling agreement if this release is drawn against a scheduling agreement rather than a blanket PO.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent blanket purchase order or framework agreement against which this release is issued.']
  service_pm_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to service.service_pm_schedule. Business justification: Preventive Maintenance Scheduling: contract release orders include a link to the PM schedule they fulfill.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific storage location or warehouse within the plant where materials will be received.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor fulfilling this release order, inherited from the parent contract.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the WBS element if this release is charged to a specific project.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date when the materials or services were delivered and received, used for supplier performance evaluation.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who approved this release order for submission to the supplier.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this release order was approved.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this release order was closed after successful delivery and receipt.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'Date confirmed by the supplier for delivery of the released materials or services, may differ from requested date.']
  contract_remaining_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Remaining quantity available under the parent contract after this release is issued, used for tracking contract consumption.']
  contract_remaining_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining monetary value available under the parent contract after this release is issued.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this release order record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the release value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  goods_receipt_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the goods receipt document created when materials from this release are received.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Incoterms code defining the delivery terms and transfer of risk (e.g., EXW, FOB, CIF, DDP) as per ICC standards.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'Quality inspection lot number assigned when materials from this release undergo quality inspection.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Supplier invoice number associated with this release order for payment processing.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this release order record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this release order record was last modified.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms applicable to this release order, inherited from the parent contract (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30).']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether incoming materials from this release require quality inspection before acceptance (True) or can be received directly (False).']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the release order was created and issued to the supplier.']
  release_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions related to this release order, such as delivery instructions, quality requirements, or packaging specifications.']
  release_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the release order, typically a sequential number or alphanumeric code assigned by the procurement system.']
  release_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material or service units being called off in this release order.']
  release_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering of this release within the parent contract or scheduling agreement, used for tracking call-off progression.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the release order: draft (being prepared), submitted (sent to supplier), approved (confirmed by supplier), in_transit (goods shipped), received (goods delivered), closed (completed), or cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|in_transit|received|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  release_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the release order indicating the nature of the call-off: standard scheduled release, urgent expedited release, partial delivery, final release closing the contract, blanket call-off, or scheduled delivery.']
  release_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of this release order, calculated as release quantity multiplied by unit price.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the released materials or services are requested to be delivered to the receiving location.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation for delivering the released materials: air freight, sea freight, road transport, rail, courier service, or customer pickup.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier tracking number for monitoring the shipment status of this release order.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the release quantity, such as pieces (EA), kilograms (KG), liters (L), hours (HR), or other standard units.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit for the material or service as defined in the parent contract, applied to this release.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this release order record in the system.']

  Note: 'Release order or call-off issued against a blanket purchase order or scheduling agreement to draw down committed contract quantities. Captures release number, parent contract reference, release quantity, release value, delivery schedule date, plant, and release status. Enables tracking of contract consumption, remaining balance, and delivery schedule adherence against framework agreements.'
}

Table procurement.procurement_goods_receipt {
  procurement_goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the goods receipt document. Primary key for the procurement goods receipt entity.']
  delivery_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.delivery. Business justification: Supports inbound‑to‑outbound logistics matching; links supplier goods receipt to the outbound delivery for the cross‑dock reconciliation report.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: GOODS RECEIPT: Received automation equipment is recorded against the device registry to confirm installation and start warranty.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: GOODS RECEIPT: Receiving equipment updates the equipment register for inventory, commissioning, and warranty start dates.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material or service received. Links to the material master for product specifications and inventory management.']
  po_line_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific line item on the purchase order for which goods are being received. Enables line-level three-way matching.']
  primary_procurement_employee_id varchar [note: 'The employee or user identifier of the person who received the goods. Links to human resources or user management systems.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order against which this goods receipt is recorded. Links the GR to the originating procurement document.']
  sales_order_intake_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order_intake. Business justification: Goods receipt must be tied to the originating sales order intake to close the fulfillment loop and recognize revenue.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific storage location within the warehouse where the received goods are placed. Enables precise inventory tracking.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier from whom the goods or services were received. Key counterparty in the procurement transaction.']
  tertiary_procurement_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'The user identifier of the person who last modified this goods receipt record. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  warehouse_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or receiving facility where the goods were physically received.']
  accounting_document_number varchar [note: 'The financial accounting document number generated when the goods receipt value is posted to the General Ledger (GL). Links the GR to financial transactions.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number assigned to the received material. Critical for traceability, quality control, and recall management in regulated industries.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this goods receipt record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the goods receipt value is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  damage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received goods were damaged during transit or delivery. True if damage was observed; false otherwise. Triggers quality hold and supplier notification.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the goods were physically delivered to the receiving location. Used for supplier performance evaluation and lead time analysis.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'The delivery note or packing slip number provided by the supplier. Used to match physical delivery documentation with the goods receipt record.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date printed on the goods receipt document. May differ from the posting date for backdated or forward-dated transactions.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique document number assigned to this goods receipt transaction. Used for tracking, auditing, and cross-system reconciliation.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the received material expires or becomes unusable. Critical for perishable goods, chemicals, and materials with shelf-life constraints.']
  goods_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the goods receipt document. Indicates whether the GR is in draft, posted to inventory, blocked for quality issues, cancelled, or reversed.']
  goods_receipt_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary value of the goods received, calculated as received quantity multiplied by the purchase order unit price. Posted to inventory and General Ledger (GL) accounts.']
  gr_ir_clearing_status varchar [note: 'Indicates the clearing status of the GR/IR account in accounts payable. Open means no invoice received; partially cleared means partial invoice match; fully cleared means invoice fully matched and cleared.']
  invoice_verification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an invoice has been received and verified against this goods receipt as part of the three-way match process (PO-GR-Invoice). True if invoice verified; false otherwise.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this goods receipt record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail purposes.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'The date on which the received material was manufactured by the supplier. Used for shelf-life calculation and quality tracking.']
  material_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP material document number generated when the goods receipt is posted. Links the GR to inventory movements and financial postings in the ERP system.']
  material_document_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the material document was created. Used in combination with material document number for unique identification in SAP.']
  movement_type varchar [note: 'The SAP movement type code that classifies the type of goods receipt transaction (e.g., 101 for GR against PO, 501 for GR without PO). Drives inventory and financial posting logic.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments recorded by the receiving personnel regarding the condition of the goods, packaging issues, or any discrepancies observed during receipt.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity originally ordered on the purchase order line. Used to calculate over-delivery or under-delivery variances.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the goods receipt was posted to the inventory and financial systems. This is the accounting date for inventory valuation and General Ledger (GL) posting.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received goods must undergo quality inspection before being released to unrestricted inventory. True if inspection is required; false otherwise.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the quality inspection process for the received goods. Determines whether goods can be moved to unrestricted stock or must remain in quality hold.']
  quantity_variance decimal(18,3) [note: 'The difference between the received quantity and the ordered quantity. Positive values indicate over-delivery; negative values indicate under-delivery.']
  received_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material or service units physically received and recorded in this goods receipt. Used for inventory update and three-way match validation.']
  receiving_person_name varchar [note: 'The name of the individual who physically received and inspected the goods. Used for accountability and audit trail purposes.']
  receiving_plant_code varchar [note: 'The code of the manufacturing plant or facility that received the goods. Used for multi-plant inventory and cost center allocation.']
  return_authorization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Return Material Authorization (RMA) has been initiated for the received goods due to quality issues, damage, or incorrect delivery. True if RMA initiated; false otherwise.']
  reversal_date date [note: 'The date on which this goods receipt was reversed. Used for audit trail and financial period reconciliation.']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number of the reversal transaction if this goods receipt has been reversed. Links to the cancelling document for audit trail.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this goods receipt has been reversed or cancelled. True if reversed; false otherwise. Reversed GRs do not contribute to inventory or financial balances.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'The unique serial number of the received item, applicable for serialized inventory such as equipment, tools, or high-value components.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'The inventory stock type to which the received goods are posted. Unrestricted stock is available for use; quality inspection and blocked stock are not.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure in which the received quantity is expressed (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter). Must align with the purchase order and material master UOM.']
  vendor_batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number assigned by the supplier to the delivered goods. Used for supplier traceability and quality issue root cause analysis.']

  Note: 'Goods receipt (GR) document recorded when materials or services are physically received from a supplier against a purchase order. Captures GR document number, posting date, delivery note number, received quantity, unit of measure, storage location, batch number, quality inspection flag, and GR/IR clearing status. Triggers inventory update and initiates the three-way match process (PO–GR–Invoice) for accounts payable.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_invoice {
  supplier_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier invoice record. Primary key.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Invoice approval workflow must record which employee approved the supplier invoice to satisfy internal controls and audit requirements.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Invoice posting requires cost center to allocate vendor expenses to the correct internal unit.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: INVOICE TRACKING: Supplier invoice for a device links to device registry for cost allocation and compliance auditing.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Vendor invoices are posted to GL accounts for expense recognition per accounting policy.']
  procurement_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the goods receipt document used in three-way match verification.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order against which this invoice is matched in the three-way match process (PO–GR–Invoice).']
  sales_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.sales_contract. Business justification: Supplier invoice is linked to the sales contract for accurate contract‑based invoicing and financial reconciliation.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier (vendor) who issued this invoice.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was approved for payment.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'The reference date from which payment terms are calculated, typically the invoice date or goods receipt date.']
  blocking_reason varchar [note: 'The reason why the invoice is blocked for payment, such as price variance, quantity variance, or quality issues.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The company code in the ERP system to which this invoice is assigned.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the invoice is denominated.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any discount amount applied to the invoice, such as early payment discounts or volume discounts.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date recorded on the invoice document, which may differ from the invoice date or posting date.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The exchange rate applied for currency conversion if the invoice currency differs from the company currency.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which the invoice is posted.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the invoice is posted for accounting purposes.']
  freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The freight or shipping charges included in the invoice.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total invoice amount before taxes and adjustments.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was issued by the supplier. This is the principal business event timestamp for the invoice.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'The unique invoice number assigned by the supplier. This is the externally-known business identifier for the invoice.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the procure-to-pay workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: parked|posted|blocked|cleared|cancelled|rejected|pending_approval — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'The type or category of the invoice document.']
  material_category varchar [note: 'The category of materials or services covered by this invoice.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the invoice record was last modified.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total invoice amount after all taxes, discounts, and adjustments. This is the amount payable to the supplier.']
  payment_block_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the invoice is blocked for payment.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual date the payment was made to the supplier.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment must be made to the supplier according to the payment terms.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The method by which payment will be made to the supplier.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'The reference number assigned to the payment transaction when the invoice is paid.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status indicating whether the invoice has been paid.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The agreed payment terms between the buyer and supplier, such as Net 30, Net 60, or 2/10 Net 30.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The plant or facility code where the goods or services were received.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was posted to the financial accounting system.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'The purchasing group or buyer responsible for the procurement transaction.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'The organizational unit responsible for procurement activities related to this invoice.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Additional reference number or code provided by the supplier on the invoice document for their internal tracking.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount applied to the invoice, including VAT, sales tax, or other applicable taxes.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax code applied to the invoice for tax calculation purposes.']
  tax_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The tax jurisdiction or authority under which the invoice tax is calculated.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Status of the three-way match verification process comparing purchase order, goods receipt, and invoice.']
  tolerance_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the tolerance check comparing invoice amounts against purchase order and goods receipt amounts.']
  tolerance_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount by which the invoice differs from the expected amount based on the purchase order and goods receipt.']
  tolerance_variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage variance between the invoice amount and the expected amount.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'The WBS element for project-related invoices, linking the invoice to a specific project phase or deliverable.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of withholding tax deducted from the invoice payment.']

  Note: 'Supplier invoice document received from a vendor and processed for three-way match verification (PO–GR–Invoice). Captures invoice number, supplier invoice reference, invoice date, posting date, gross amount, tax amount, currency, payment due date, payment terms, invoice status (parked, posted, blocked, cleared), and tolerance check results. Serves as the SSOT for procurement-side payables within the procure-to-pay cycle.'
}

Table procurement.invoice_line_item {
  invoice_line_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the invoice line item. Primary key for this entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Line‑level cost allocation to cost center supports detailed profitability and variance reporting.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: INVOICE ALLOCATION: Invoice line items for equipment need a FK to the equipment register for accurate asset cost tracking.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Each invoice line must map to a GL account for correct posting in the general ledger.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent supplier invoice header. Links this line item to the overall invoice document.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for the product or material being invoiced. Links to the material catalog for direct materials, indirect materials, or MRO supplies.']
  po_line_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order line item that this invoice line corresponds to. Used for three-way matching between PO, goods receipt, and invoice.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Profitability analysis requires linking invoice lines to profit centers for revenue/expense attribution.']
  service_entry_sheet_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service entry sheet if this line item corresponds to a service rather than a material. Used for service procurement verification.']
  verified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Line‑item verification is performed by an employee; capturing the verifier supports three‑way match audit and compliance reporting.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'The baseline date from which payment terms are calculated for this line item. Typically the invoice date or goods receipt date depending on payment term configuration.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or lot number for the material being invoiced. Critical for traceability, quality management, and recall management in regulated industries.']
  blocking_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code or description explaining why this invoice line is blocked from payment. Examples include price variance exceeds tolerance, quantity mismatch, missing goods receipt, or quality hold.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the line amount and unit price (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CNY). Must match the invoice header currency.']
  delivery_note_number varchar [note: 'The supplier delivery note or packing slip number referenced on this invoice line. Used for traceability and shipment reconciliation.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any discount amount applied at the line level. Reduces the line amount before tax calculation. May represent early payment discounts, volume discounts, or promotional discounts.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The discount percentage applied to this line item. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5% discount).']
  goods_receipt_number varchar [note: 'The goods receipt document number that this invoice line is matched against. Critical for three-way matching in logistics invoice verification.']
  goods_receipt_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity recorded on the goods receipt document. Used to compare against invoiced quantity for quantity variance detection.']
  invoiced_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material or service units being invoiced on this line. Used for three-way match verification against PO quantity and goods receipt quantity.']
  line_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total amount for this invoice line before taxes. Typically calculated as invoiced quantity multiplied by unit price, adjusted for any line-level discounts or surcharges.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the invoice. Determines the ordering and position of this line item on the invoice document.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice line item type. Distinguishes between material purchases, services, freight charges, handling fees, taxes, discounts, surcharges, and other cost elements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: material|service|freight|handling|tax|discount|surcharge|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  match_status varchar [note: 'The three-way match status of this invoice line. Indicates whether the line passed matching validation or has variances requiring resolution. Blocked status prevents payment until resolved.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the material, service, or item being invoiced. Provides human-readable detail about what was purchased.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'The material number or SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) as specified on the invoice line. May differ from internal material master number in case of supplier-specific identifiers.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net amount for this invoice line after applying discounts and adding taxes. Represents the final cost impact of this line item.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this invoice line. May contain variance explanations, special handling instructions, or resolution details.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The payment terms applicable to this invoice line if different from header-level terms. May specify early payment discounts or extended payment periods.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The manufacturing plant or facility code where the material or service is consumed or delivered. Used for multi-site cost allocation.']
  po_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity specified on the original purchase order line. Used as the baseline for three-way match verification.']
  po_unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The unit price specified on the original purchase order line. Used as the baseline for price variance detection during invoice verification.']
  price_variance decimal(18,2) [note: 'The difference between invoiced unit price and PO unit price, multiplied by invoiced quantity. Indicates pricing discrepancies requiring review.']
  quantity_variance decimal(18,3) [note: 'The difference between invoiced quantity and goods receipt quantity. Positive values indicate over-invoicing, negative values indicate under-invoicing.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'The serial number for serialized materials or equipment. Enables individual unit tracking for warranty, maintenance, and asset management.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'The storage location within the plant where the material is received or stored. Used for inventory management and warehouse assignment.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated tax amount for this invoice line based on the tax code and line amount. Added to line amount to determine total line cost.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax code applied to this invoice line. Determines the tax rate and tax jurisdiction for calculating line-level tax amounts.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The tax rate percentage applied to this line item. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 7.50 for 7.5% tax rate).']
