  tolerance_group varchar [note: 'The tolerance group that defines acceptable variance thresholds for this line item. Different tolerance groups may apply to different material categories or supplier relationships.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the invoiced quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, M for meters, HR for hours). Must align with PO and material master UOM for proper matching.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The price per unit of measure as stated on the invoice line. Used to calculate line amount and compared against PO price for variance detection.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'The valuation type for split valuation scenarios where the same material is valued differently based on procurement source, quality grade, or other criteria.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date when this invoice line was verified and approved for payment. Critical for tracking invoice processing cycle time and SLA compliance.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'The current verification status of this invoice line in the approval workflow. Tracks progress through invoice verification and approval process.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'The WBS element for project-related procurement. Links invoice line to specific project phases or deliverables for project cost tracking.']

  Note: 'Individual line item on a supplier invoice corresponding to a specific PO line, service entry sheet, or unplanned delivery cost. Captures line number, material or service description, invoiced quantity, unit price, line amount, tax code, account assignment, and three-way match status (matched, quantity variance, price variance, blocked). Enables granular invoice verification and exception management in logistics invoice verification.'
}

Table procurement.spend_record {
  spend_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the spend transaction record. Primary key for the spend_record data product.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the purchase order. Used for approval audit trail and delegation of authority compliance.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Spend records are aggregated by cost center for budgeting and spend analysis reports.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Associating spend records with GL accounts supports financial statement reconciliation and audit.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the material or service procured. Links to material master data in SAP S/4HANA MM or Siemens Teamcenter PLM.']
  primary_spend_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who initiated the purchase requisition. Used for spend accountability and approval workflow tracking.']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the procurement contract under which this spend was executed. Links to contract master data for compliance tracking and savings validation.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Linking spend to profit centers enables profitability tracking of procurement spend.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order that authorized this spend transaction. Links to the purchase order master record in SAP Ariba or SAP S/4HANA MM module.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Spend analysis reports track expenditures against regulatory requirements for spend compliance and audit trails.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Provides product‑level spend analysis, required for the \'Product Spend Reporting\' dashboard used by finance and supply chain.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier who provided the goods or services. Links to supplier master data in SAP Ariba or SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  commodity_code_l1 varchar [note: 'Top-level commodity classification code from the United Nations Standard Products and Services Code (UNSPSC) taxonomy. Enables high-level category spend aggregation.']
  commodity_code_l2 varchar [note: 'Second-level commodity classification code from UNSPSC taxonomy. Provides family-level categorization for spend cube analytics.']
  commodity_code_l3 varchar [note: 'Third-level commodity classification code from UNSPSC taxonomy. Provides class-level categorization for detailed spend analysis.']
  commodity_code_l4 varchar [note: 'Fourth-level commodity classification code from UNSPSC taxonomy. Provides commodity-level categorization for granular spend analysis and category management.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the spend transaction. Used for multi-currency spend analysis and currency conversion.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Date the goods or services were delivered. Used for supplier on-time delivery performance measurement and lead time analysis.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Foreign exchange rate used to convert transaction currency to USD. Captured for audit trail and variance analysis.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the spend was posted. Supports monthly spend tracking and period-over-period analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the spend transaction was posted. Enables year-over-year spend trend analysis and budget variance reporting.']
  gl_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which the spend is posted. Used for financial reporting and chart of accounts reconciliation.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms defining the responsibilities of buyer and seller for delivery, insurance, and risk transfer (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP).']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the supplier invoice was issued. Used for payment terms calculation and aging analysis.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Supplier invoice number associated with this spend transaction. Used for payment reconciliation and audit trail.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Short text description of the material or service procured. Denormalized for reporting and spend analysis.']
  maverick_spend_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this spend transaction was executed outside of established contracts or preferred suppliers. True indicates non-compliant spend requiring corrective action.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date the payment was made to the supplier. Used for cash flow analysis and Days Payable Outstanding (DPO) calculation.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to pay the supplier. Used for payment channel analysis and transaction cost optimization.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms agreed with the supplier (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Used for cash flow forecasting and early payment discount analysis.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility code where the material or service is consumed. Used for site-level spend analysis and supply chain optimization.']
  po_line_item_number varchar [note: 'Line item number within the purchase order that corresponds to this spend transaction. Enables drill-down to specific material or service line.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date the spend transaction was posted to the financial ledger. Determines the fiscal period for spend recognition and accrual accounting.']
  procurement_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the procurement was executed. Used for maverick spend detection and procurement compliance monitoring.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Buyer or procurement team responsible for this purchase. Used for buyer performance evaluation and workload distribution analysis.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for procurement activities. Used for procurement performance tracking and organizational spend analysis.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material or service units procured in this transaction. Used for unit price calculation and volume-based spend analysis.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this spend record was first created in the data lakehouse. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this spend record was last updated in the data lakehouse. Used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  savings_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Documented cost savings achieved on this transaction compared to baseline or previous pricing. Used for procurement performance measurement and ROI calculation.']
  spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the spend transaction in the transaction currency. Represents the actual expenditure at line-item level before currency conversion.']
  spend_amount_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Spend amount converted to US Dollars using the exchange rate at posting date. Enables consolidated spend reporting across global operations.']
  spend_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of spend type: direct materials (production inputs), indirect materials (non-production goods), MRO (Maintenance, Repair, Operations supplies), or CapEx (Capital Expenditure for fixed assets).']
  supplier_segment varchar [note: 'Strategic classification of the supplier based on spend volume, criticality, and performance. Used for supplier relationship management and sourcing strategy.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount included in the spend transaction. Used for tax reporting and compliance with local tax regulations.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction code or tax type applied to this transaction. Used for tax compliance reporting and audit trail.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity field (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, M for meter). Follows ISO 80000 or industry-specific UOM standards.']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Price per unit of measure in the transaction currency. Calculated as spend_amount divided by quantity. Used for price variance analysis and benchmarking.']

  Note: 'Normalized spend transaction record representing actual procurement expenditure at the line-item level, enriched with commodity classification (UNSPSC L1-L4), supplier segmentation, and cost object allocation. Captures spend amount, currency, spend category (direct, indirect, MRO, CapEx), cost center, profit center, plant, fiscal period, payment date, and PO/invoice reference. Serves as the foundational fact table for spend cube analytics, category management dashboards, maverick spend detection, and savings validation across all procurement channels including P-card, PO-based, and non-PO spend.'
}

Table procurement.approval_workflow {
  approval_workflow_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for approval_workflow']
  approval_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the document being approved (purchase requisition, purchase order, or sourcing event). Links to the source document requiring approval.']
  primary_approval_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the individual who performed the approval action. Links to employee or user master data.']
  requester_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who initiated the procurement request. Links to employee or user master data.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier or vendor associated with the procurement document being approved. Links to supplier master data.']
  approval_action varchar [note: 'Action taken by the approver on the document. Approved indicates authorization granted, rejected indicates denial, escalated indicates forwarding to higher authority, delegated indicates reassignment to another approver, returned indicates sent back for revision.']
  approval_comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes entered by the approver explaining the approval decision, conditions, or reasons for rejection. Supports audit trail and communication.']
  approval_document_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier of the document being approved. Human-readable reference number such as PR number, PO number, or RFQ number.']
  approval_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of procurement document being approved. Indicates whether the approval is for a purchase requisition, purchase order, RFQ (Request for Quotation), RFP (Request for Proposal), contract, or other sourcing event.']
  approval_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the approval action should be completed. Used for SLA (Service Level Agreement) tracking and escalation triggers.']
  approval_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time elapsed in hours between approval request and approval action completion. Used for cycle time analysis and process optimization.']
  approval_level varchar [note: 'Sequential level in the multi-tier approval hierarchy. Level 1 represents first-line approval, with higher numbers indicating escalation through management layers.']
  approval_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approval request notification was successfully sent to the approver. True indicates notification sent, false indicates pending or failed.']
  approval_reminder_count varchar [note: 'Number of reminder notifications sent to the approver for this pending approval. Used to track follow-up communications and escalation triggers.']
  approval_request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the approval request was submitted to this approver. Marks the start of the approval cycle for this level.']
  approval_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order number of this approval step within the overall workflow for the document. Used to track the progression through the approval chain.']
  approval_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or application from which the approval workflow originated. Examples include SAP Ariba, SAP S/4HANA, custom procurement portal.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this approval step. Pending indicates awaiting action, in progress indicates under review, approved indicates completed successfully, rejected indicates denied, cancelled indicates workflow terminated, expired indicates approval deadline passed.']
  approval_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary threshold amount that defines the approval authority limit for this approver role at this level. Part of Delegation of Authority (DoA) matrix.']
  approval_threshold_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the approval threshold amount. Defines the currency in which the approval authority is denominated.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the approval action was performed by the approver. Represents the business event time of the authorization decision.']
  approver_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the approver. Used for notification and audit trail purposes.']
  approver_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who performed the approval action. Captured for audit trail and reporting purposes.']
  approver_role varchar [note: 'Functional role or job title of the approver responsible for this approval level. Examples include Purchasing Manager, Plant Manager, Finance Director, VP Procurement, CFO.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity or company code within the enterprise structure. Represents the financial accounting entity for which the procurement is being executed.']
  compliance_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of automated compliance checks performed during the approval process. Indicates whether the document passed policy validation, supplier screening, budget checks, and regulatory compliance rules.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center to which the procurement expense will be charged. Used for internal cost allocation and management accounting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this approval workflow record was first created in the system. Represents the audit trail start point for this approval step.']
  delegation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for delegating the approval to another approver. Examples include out of office, conflict of interest, workload balancing, temporary reassignment.']
  document_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the document total amount. Indicates the currency in which the procurement document is denominated.']
  document_total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the document being approved. Used to determine routing through approval hierarchy based on DoA (Delegation of Authority) thresholds.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for escalating the approval to a higher authority level. Examples include exceeds_authority, requires_executive_review, policy_exception, risk_assessment.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this approval workflow record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking purposes.']
  mandatory_approval_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this approval step is mandatory or optional in the workflow. True indicates required approval, false indicates advisory or optional review.']
  material_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the materials or services being procured. Examples include direct materials, indirect materials, MRO (Maintenance, Repair, and Operations) supplies, capital equipment, services.']
  parallel_approval_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this approval step is part of a parallel approval process where multiple approvers at the same level must approve simultaneously. True indicates parallel, false indicates sequential.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility code where the procured materials or services will be delivered or consumed. Represents the physical location in the supply chain.']
  policy_violation_description varchar [note: 'Description of the specific procurement policy or business rule that was violated. Populated when policy violation flag is true.']
  policy_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the procurement document violates any procurement policies or business rules. True indicates policy violation detected, false indicates compliant.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Buyer group or team responsible for specific categories of materials or suppliers. Represents the operational procurement team handling the transaction.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for procurement activities. Represents the procurement entity within the enterprise structure (e.g., regional purchasing office, plant purchasing group).']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for rejection if the approval action was rejected. Examples include budget_exceeded, supplier_not_approved, specification_incomplete, pricing_issue.']

  Note: 'Approval workflow record tracking the multi-level authorization process for purchase requisitions, purchase orders, and sourcing events. Captures approval document reference, approval level, approver role, approver identity, approval action (approved, rejected, escalated, delegated), approval timestamp, approval threshold amount, and comments. Supports procurement governance, delegation of authority (DoA) compliance, and audit trail requirements.'
}

Table procurement.service_entry_sheet {
  service_entry_sheet_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the service entry sheet record. Primary key.']
  approval_workflow_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the approval workflow instance used to review and approve this service entry sheet.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or user who accepted and confirmed the services in this service entry sheet.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to asset.equipment_register. Business justification: SERVICE ENTRY: Service records must link to the equipment register to track maintenance history and warranty compliance.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service purchase order against which services were rendered and accepted.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier who rendered the services being accepted in this service entry sheet.']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date on which the services were formally accepted by the receiving organization.']
  acceptance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments entered by the acceptor regarding the service acceptance, quality observations, or deviations.']
  accepted_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of service units accepted and confirmed through this service entry sheet.']
  acceptor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who accepted the services for business reference and audit trail.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the service entry sheet received final approval through the workflow process.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Company code of the legal entity receiving and accepting the services.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center to which the accepted service costs are allocated for financial accounting and controlling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service entry sheet record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the service entry sheet monetary values.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period within the fiscal year in which the service entry sheet was posted.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the service entry sheet was posted for financial reporting and period closing.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which the service entry sheet value is posted for financial reporting.']
  gross_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross monetary value of the accepted services including taxes and all adjustments.']
  invoice_verification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator whether this service entry sheet has been used for invoice verification and payment processing.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the individual who last modified the service entry sheet record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the service entry sheet record was last modified or updated.']
  net_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total net monetary value of the accepted services before taxes and adjustments.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Original quantity of service units ordered on the purchase order line item for comparison with accepted quantity.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Plant or facility code where the services were rendered or consumed.']
  po_line_number varchar [note: 'Line item number on the purchase order to which this service entry sheet applies.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the service entry sheet was posted to financial accounting for cost recognition.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Buyer group or team within the purchasing organization responsible for managing this service procurement.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for the procurement of the services covered by this service entry sheet.']
  quantity_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the accepted service quantity such as hours, days, pieces, or service-specific units.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why the service entry sheet was rejected, if applicable.']
  service_category varchar [note: 'Classification category of the service type for spend analysis and procurement reporting.']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the services rendered by the supplier and accepted through this service entry sheet.']
  service_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the supplier completed rendering the services covered by this service entry sheet.']
  service_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the supplier began rendering the services covered by this service entry sheet.']
  ses_number varchar [note: 'Business document number assigned to the service entry sheet. Externally visible identifier used for tracking and reference in procurement workflows.']
  ses_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service entry sheet indicating its approval and posting state.']
  ses_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service entry sheet based on the procurement scenario and service delivery model.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to the accepted services based on tax jurisdiction and service category.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Status of the three-way match process comparing service entry sheet, purchase order, and supplier invoice for payment approval.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'Project work breakdown structure element for project-related service procurement cost allocation.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the individual who created the service entry sheet record.']

  Note: 'Service entry sheet (SES) document created to confirm the acceptance of services rendered by a supplier against a service purchase order. Captures SES number, service PO reference, service line descriptions, accepted service quantity, acceptance date, acceptor identity, and SES status (created, accepted, rejected). Triggers invoice verification for service-based procurement and is mandatory for service PO three-way match.'
}

Table procurement.commodity_category {
  commodity_category_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for commodity_category']
  parent_category_commodity_category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent category in the hierarchy. Null for top-level (L1) categories.']
  primary_commodity_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or procurement specialist responsible for managing this commodity category and its sourcing strategy.']
  tertiary_commodity_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified this commodity category record.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the commodity category. May align with UNSPSC (United Nations Standard Products and Services Code) or internal taxonomy.']
  category_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the commodity category scope, including typical materials, services, or products classified under this category.']
  category_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the category within the taxonomy structure. L1 represents top-level, L2 sub-category, L3 detailed category, etc.']
  category_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the category manager responsible for strategic sourcing and supplier relationships within this category.']
  category_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the commodity category (e.g., Electrical Components, Raw Steel, MRO Supplies).']
  category_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the commodity category within the procurement taxonomy.']
  compliance_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category has specific regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., RoHS, REACH, conflict minerals, ISO certifications).']
  contract_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category is covered by active procurement contracts or framework agreements.']
  cost_reduction_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual cost reduction target percentage for this category as part of strategic sourcing initiatives.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity category record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this commodity category classification became effective in the procurement taxonomy.']
  environmental_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category requires environmental compliance certifications such as ISO 14001, RoHS, REACH, or conflict minerals reporting.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this commodity category classification expires or is scheduled for review. Null for active categories without planned expiration.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this commodity category record was last updated or modified.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this category was last reviewed for accuracy, relevance, and alignment with business needs.']
  lead_time_days_avg varchar [note: 'Average procurement lead time in days for materials and services within this category, from requisition to delivery.']
  moq_applicable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether suppliers in this category typically enforce minimum order quantity requirements.']
  negotiation_frequency varchar [note: 'Typical frequency of contract negotiations and RFx events for this commodity category.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this commodity category classification and sourcing strategy.']
  preferred_supplier_count varchar [note: 'Number of preferred suppliers currently approved and active for this commodity category.']
  preferred_supplier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this category has designated preferred suppliers with negotiated contracts or framework agreements.']
  price_volatility_index varchar [note: 'Classification of price volatility for commodities in this category based on historical price fluctuations and market dynamics.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Code identifying the purchasing group (buyer team) responsible for operational procurement within this category.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Code identifying the purchasing organization responsible for procurement activities within this category. Aligns with SAP organizational structure.']
  quality_certification_required varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of required quality certifications for suppliers in this category (e.g., ISO 9001, IATF 16949, AS9100).']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this commodity category.']
  risk_classification varchar [note: 'Risk classification based on supply chain vulnerability, supplier concentration, geopolitical factors, and material criticality.']
  sourcing_strategy varchar [note: 'Defined sourcing strategy for this category: single-source, dual-source, multi-source, global sourcing, or local sourcing approach.']
  spend_type varchar [note: 'Classification of spend type: direct materials (production inputs), indirect materials (non-production), MRO (Maintenance, Repair, Operations), CapEx (Capital Expenditure), or services.']
  strategic_sourcing_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this category for strategic sourcing initiatives based on spend volume, supply risk, and business impact.']
  unspsc_class varchar [note: 'Third-level UNSPSC class name within the family (e.g., Cutting and Forming Machines).']
  unspsc_code varchar [note: 'Eight-digit UNSPSC classification code for global commodity standardization and cross-industry spend analysis.']
  unspsc_commodity varchar [note: 'Fourth-level UNSPSC commodity name, the most granular classification level (e.g., CNC Milling Machines).']
  unspsc_family varchar [note: 'Second-level UNSPSC family name within the segment (e.g., Metalworking Machinery and Equipment).']
  unspsc_segment varchar [note: 'Top-level UNSPSC segment name (e.g., Industrial Manufacturing and Processing Machinery and Accessories).']

  Note: 'Commodity and spend category taxonomy used to classify all purchased materials and services into a hierarchical structure aligned with UNSPSC or internal category codes. Captures category code, category name, category level (L1/L2/L3), parent category, spend type (direct/indirect/MRO/CapEx), category owner, preferred supplier flag, and strategic sourcing priority. Enables category management, spend cube construction, and sourcing strategy alignment.'
}

Table procurement.sourcing_strategy {
  sourcing_strategy_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sourcing strategy record.']
  commodity_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.commodity_category. Business justification: Sourcing strategy must be linked to the commodity category it governs; adding procurement_category_id creates this essential relationship and eliminates the silo.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the strategy was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the strategy.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the strategy with internal policies and external regulations.']
  contract_coverage_target_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount representing the desired contract coverage for the category.']
  contract_coverage_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Desired percentage of spend covered by contracts under this strategy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sourcing strategy record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary fields.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the sourcing strategy becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the sourcing strategy expires or is superseded (nullable for open‑ended).']
  expected_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Anticipated lead time for sourcing items under this strategy.']
  is_global_strategy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the strategy applies globally across all sites.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent strategic review.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who performed the most recent review.']
  make_vs_buy varchar [note: 'Decision whether the organization will make the item internally or buy it from external suppliers.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the strategy.']
  preferred_supplier_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of internal supplier identifiers preferred for this strategy.']
  review_cycle_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between formal reviews of the sourcing strategy.']
  risk_assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score quantifying the risk level of the strategy (0‑100 scale).']
  risk_mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Brief description of actions to mitigate identified risks.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the sourcing strategy.']
  savings_target_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of cost savings the strategy aims to achieve.']
  savings_target_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the savings target amount.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record (e.g., SAP Ariba).']
  sourcing_strategy_category varchar [note: 'Spend category or commodity to which the strategy applies.']
  sourcing_strategy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sourcing strategy.']
  strategic_owner varchar [note: 'Internal stakeholder responsible for the strategy\'s execution.']
  strategy_code varchar [note: 'External code used to reference the sourcing strategy in procurement systems.']
  strategy_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the sourcing strategy.']
  strategy_type varchar [note: 'Approach used for sourcing the category (e.g., single source, dual source, multi‑source, global, local).']
  target_savings_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target savings expressed as a percentage of total spend.']
  total_spend_last_year decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate spend for the category in the most recent fiscal year.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the sourcing strategy record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the sourcing strategy document.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the sourcing strategy record.']

  Note: 'Category-level sourcing strategy document defining the procurement approach for a commodity or spend category over a planning horizon. Captures strategy name, target category, strategy type (single source, dual source, multi-source, global sourcing, local sourcing), make-vs-buy decision, risk rating, savings target, preferred supplier list, contract coverage target, and review cycle. Supports strategic procurement planning and category management governance.'
}

Table procurement.purchase_info_record {
  purchase_info_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the purchase info record.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Creating a purchase info record is an employee action; storing the creator ID enables accountability and change‑log reporting.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the material or service for which this info record is maintained.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier linked to this info record.']
  approved_source_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is an approved source for the material (true = approved).']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to an outline agreement or contract that governs this price.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the info record was initially created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency in which the net price is expressed.']
  fixed_source_flag boolean [note: 'True if procurement must always source from this supplier for the material.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International commercial terms defining delivery responsibilities; limited to six most common values.']
  info_record_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the purchase info record, used for reference in procurement processes.']
  info_record_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the info record indicating the sourcing strategy (e.g., standard, subcontracting, pipeline, consignment).']
  last_price_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the price information was last updated in the system.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered from the approved source.']
  mrp_relevant_flag boolean [note: 'True if the info record should be considered during MRP runs.']
  net_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Negotiated net price for the material/service from the approved supplier.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional comments or special instructions.']
  order_quantity_multiple decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity increment that must be respected when ordering (e.g., multiples of 10).']
  planned_delivery_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of days from order creation to delivery for this source.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code of the plant where the approved source is valid.']
  price_change_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent price change became effective.']
  price_change_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the price has increased, decreased, or remained unchanged since the previous version.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification for the latest price adjustment.']
  price_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the net price (e.g., per piece, per kilogram).']
  purchase_info_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the info record.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Group of buyers assigned to handle transactions for this info record.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Organizational unit responsible for procurement activities for this info record.']
  reminder_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days before the planned delivery date when a reminder is generated.']
  source_of_supply_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the source (e.g., internal, external, third‑party).']
  source_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric rank indicating preference order among multiple approved sources for the same material.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification applicable to the purchase price.']
  tolerance_limit_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowed deviation (percentage) from the negotiated price before a tolerance check is triggered.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the info record.']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the info record is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended).']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the info record becomes effective.']
  vendor_material_number varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s own material identifier for the supplied item.']
  vendor_price_list varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier\'s price list or catalogue containing this price.']

  Note: 'Approved source master record linking a material or service to a qualified supplier, storing the negotiated price, standard order quantity, planned delivery time, tolerance limits, validity period, and plant-level sourcing assignments. Captures info record type (standard, subcontracting, pipeline, consignment), net price, price unit, minimum order quantity, reminder lead times, approved source indicators, MRP relevance flags, fixed source flags, valid-from/to dates, and outline agreement references. Drives automatic price proposals during PO creation and ensures MRP-driven orders are directed to approved suppliers at the correct plant.'
}

Table procurement.source_list {
  source_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the source list record.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Maintaining a source list is performed by a responsible employee; the creator ID is required for governance and audit of sourcing data.']
  material_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material for which this source list applies.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant/location associated with the source list.']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the underlying procurement contract or agreement governing the source.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Enables the \'Component Sourcing per Product\' view, linking source‑list entries directly to the product SKU that uses the material.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the approved supplier for the material at the plant.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the source with internal or regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the source list record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price fields.']
  fixed_source_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is a fixed (mandatory) source for the material.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the source list record.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard lead time in calendar days for the supplier to deliver the material.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered from the supplier for this material.']
  mrp_source_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicator used by MRP planning to select this source (standard, alternative, or special).']
  price_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'Unit price agreed with the supplier for the material.']
  price_valid_from date [note: 'Date from which the quoted unit price is effective.']
  price_valid_to date [note: 'Date until which the quoted unit price remains valid.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used to rank multiple approved sources for the same material.']
  procurement_type varchar [note: 'Category of procurement for the source (e.g., direct, indirect, MRO, capital equipment).']
  source_list_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes about the source list entry.']
  source_list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the source list record.']
  source_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label for the source list entry.']
  source_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rating (e.g., 0‑5) reflecting supplier performance for this material.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the source list entry indicating its sourcing strategy.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which the source list entry becomes effective.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date on which the source list entry expires (null if open‑ended).']

  Note: 'Approved source record defining qualified suppliers for a material at a specific plant, including validity periods, fixed source indicators, and MRP relevance flags. Captures material, plant, supplier, agreement reference, valid-from/to dates, fixed source flag, MRP source indicator, and procurement type. Ensures purchase orders are automatically directed to approved suppliers.'
}

Table workforce.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the employee record. Primary key sourced from Workday HCM. Serves as the single source of truth for employee identity across all manufacturing systems including SAP S/4HANA, Siemens Opcenter MES, and Maximo Asset Management.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Payroll tax reporting requires linking each employee to their legal entity (company code) for statutory compliance.']
  supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the direct supervisor or manager. Establishes reporting hierarchy for organizational structure, approval workflows, and performance management.']
  annual_salary decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual base salary for salaried employees. Used for budgeting, compensation analysis, and financial reporting. Null for hourly employees.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code for labor cost allocation and budgeting. Used in SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling) module for tracking labor expenses against departmental budgets and calculating manufacturing overhead.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this employee record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with data governance policies.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for compensation. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP, CNY. Used for multi-currency payroll processing and global workforce reporting.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the employee. Used for age verification, benefits eligibility, retirement planning, and compliance with labor laws.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the organizational department to which the employee is assigned. Used for labor cost allocation, reporting hierarchy, and organizational analytics. Aligns with SAP S/4HANA organizational structure.']
  department_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the department. Examples: Assembly Line 1, Quality Control Lab, Maintenance Shop, Production Planning, Procurement. Provides human-readable context for organizational assignment.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary corporate email address for the employee. Used for system notifications, training communications, and business correspondence.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee\'s designated emergency contact person. Used for workplace incidents, medical emergencies, and ISO 45001 safety compliance.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the employee\'s emergency contact. Used for urgent notifications in case of workplace accidents or medical emergencies.']
  emergency_contact_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the emergency contact to the employee. Examples: Spouse, Parent, Sibling, Friend. Used for emergency response protocols.']
  employee_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable employee number assigned by HR system. Used for payroll, timekeeping, and shop floor identification. Displayed on employee badges and MES (Manufacturing Execution System) terminals.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current employment lifecycle status of the employee. Determines access rights, payroll processing, and system permissions. Active employees are eligible for work assignments and compensation.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment arrangement. Determines benefits eligibility, labor cost allocation, and compliance with labor regulations. Full-time and part-time are permanent positions; contract and temporary are fixed-term.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name of the employee as recorded in HR system. Used for official documentation, payroll processing, and compliance reporting.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee was originally hired by the organization. Used for seniority calculations, benefits eligibility, service awards, and tenure analytics.']
  hourly_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hourly wage rate for hourly employees. Used for payroll calculation, labor cost allocation, and OpEx (Operational Expenditure) tracking. Null for salaried employees.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this employee record is currently active in the system. Used for filtering active workforce, system access control, and data retention policies.']
  job_code varchar [note: 'Standardized job classification code used for compensation planning, labor reporting, and workforce analytics. Maps to job families and pay grades in Workday HCM.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad occupational category grouping similar roles. Examples: Production Operations, Engineering, Quality Assurance, Maintenance, Supply Chain, Finance. Used for workforce planning and talent management.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title assigned to the employee. Reflects role, seniority, and functional responsibility. Examples: Production Operator, Quality Engineer, Maintenance Technician, Plant Manager.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name (surname) of the employee as recorded in HR system. Used for official documentation, payroll processing, and compliance reporting.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal performance review. Used for tracking review cycles and ensuring timely performance evaluations.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the employee. Optional field used for complete legal identification.']
  national_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued national identification number (e.g., Social Security Number in USA, National Insurance Number in UK). Used for tax reporting, payroll processing, and legal compliance.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation grade level assigned to the employee. Determines salary range, bonus eligibility, and compensation structure. Used for pay equity analysis and compensation planning.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Most recent performance evaluation rating. Used for talent management, promotion decisions, compensation adjustments, and workforce development planning.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the employee. Used for emergency contact, shift scheduling notifications, and business communication.']
  safety_certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s safety certification expires. Used for recertification scheduling and compliance tracking. Null if no certification required or status is not_required.']
  safety_certification_status varchar [note: 'Status of required safety certifications for the employee\'s role. Used for ISO 45001 compliance, OSHA safety requirements, and work authorization on shop floor and hazardous areas.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary shift assignment. Examples: 1ST, 2ND, 3RD, NIGHT, DAY. Used for production scheduling, labor tracking in MES (Manufacturing Execution System), and shift differential pay calculation.']
  skill_level varchar [note: 'Overall skill proficiency level of the employee in their primary job function. Used for work assignment, training needs assessment, and workforce capability planning.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment ended. Null for active employees. Used for final payroll processing, benefits termination, system access revocation, and turnover analytics.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for employment termination. Used for turnover analysis, exit interview tracking, and workforce planning. Null for active employees. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: voluntary_resignation|retirement|involuntary_termination|layoff|end_of_contract|deceased|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  training_hours_ytd decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total training hours completed by the employee in the current calendar year. Used for compliance with training requirements, skills development tracking, and ISO 9001 competency management.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the labor union to which the employee belongs. Null for non-union employees. Used for contract administration and union reporting.']
  union_member_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is a member of a labor union. Used for collective bargaining compliance, union dues processing, and labor relations reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this employee record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.']
  work_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary physical work location (plant, facility, office). Used for shift scheduling, safety compliance, and geographic labor reporting.']
  work_location_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the work location. Examples: Springfield Manufacturing Plant, Chicago Distribution Center, Detroit Assembly Facility. Provides geographic context for workforce distribution.']
  work_permit_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s work permit or visa expires. Used for renewal tracking and compliance with immigration regulations. Null if no work permit required.']
  work_permit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee requires a work permit or visa to work in the current location. Used for immigration compliance and work authorization tracking.']

  Note: 'Core master record for all employees in the manufacturing organization. Serves as the SSOT for employee identity, personal details, employment status, job classification, cost center assignment, and HR administrative data sourced from Workday HCM. Covers full-time, part-time, contract, and temporary workers on the shop floor and in corporate functions.'
}

Table workforce.org_unit {
  org_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the organizational unit. Primary key for the organizational hierarchy master.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Org‑unit budgeting and cost allocation reports need a direct FK to the cost center, replacing the denormalized cost_center_code.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who manages this organizational unit. Used for organizational reporting lines and approval workflows.']
  parent_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent organizational unit in the hierarchy. Null for top-level corporate entity. Enables multi-level hierarchical structures.']
  budget_amount_annual decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual operating budget allocated to this organizational unit in base currency. Used for financial planning and variance analysis.']
  business_function varchar [note: 'Primary business function or operational area of this organizational unit. Used for functional reporting and cross-functional analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: production|quality|maintenance|engineering|supply_chain|sales|finance|hr|it|r_and_d — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  capacity_utilization_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target capacity utilization percentage for production organizational units. Used for Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) and Key Performance Indicator (KPI) tracking.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity or company code in SAP FI for financial consolidation and statutory reporting. Links organizational unit to legal entity structure.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the organizational unit is located. Used for regulatory compliance and geographic analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizational unit record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for financial amounts associated with this organizational unit.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this organizational unit ceased or will cease operations. Null for currently active units. Supports historical organizational structure analysis.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this organizational unit became or will become operational. Used for time-based organizational hierarchy reporting.']
  environmental_certification_iso14001 boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this organizational unit holds ISO 14001 environmental management system certification. Used for environmental compliance reporting.']
  external_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used in external systems or legacy systems for data integration and cross-system reconciliation.']
  headcount_actual varchar [note: 'Current actual number of employees assigned to this organizational unit. Updated from employee assignment transactions.']
  headcount_planned varchar [note: 'Planned or budgeted number of employees for this organizational unit. Used for workforce planning and budget variance analysis.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the organizational hierarchy (1=corporate, 2=division, 3=business unit, etc.). Used for hierarchical rollup reporting.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Full hierarchical path from corporate root to this organizational unit (e.g., \'/CORP/DIV01/PLANT05/DEPT20\'). Enables efficient hierarchy traversal queries.']
  is_production_unit boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this organizational unit is directly involved in manufacturing production activities. Used for production reporting and Manufacturing Execution System (MES) integration.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Physical location or site code where this organizational unit operates. Links to facility master data for geographic reporting.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that last modified this organizational unit record. Used for audit trail and data stewardship.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizational unit record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  org_unit_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the organizational unit used across enterprise systems. Typically alphanumeric code assigned by ERP system.']
  org_unit_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the organizational units purpose, responsibilities, and scope of operations.']
  org_unit_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the organizational unit (e.g., \'Assembly Line 3\', \'Quality Control Department\', \'North American Division\').']
  org_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the organizational unit. Active units are operational and can have employees assigned. Planned units are future entities not yet operational.']
  org_unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the organizational unit within the enterprise hierarchy. Defines the level and function of the unit.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant code if this organizational unit is or belongs to a production facility. Links to SAP PP (Production Planning) plant master.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center code for business units that are evaluated as independent profit-generating entities. Used for profitability analysis.']
  quality_certification_iso9001 boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this organizational unit holds ISO 9001 quality management system certification. Required for quality compliance reporting.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region or territory code for regional reporting and analysis (e.g., \'EMEA\', \'APAC\', \'AMER\').']
  safety_certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees in this organizational unit require specific safety certifications per ISO 45001 occupational health and safety standards.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Standard shift operation pattern for this organizational unit. Used for production scheduling and labor planning.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated name or acronym for the organizational unit used in reports and displays.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for this organizational units primary location (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/Berlin\'). Used for shift scheduling and time-based reporting.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the labor union representing employees in this organizational unit, if applicable.']
  union_representation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this organizational unit has union representation. Used for labor relations and collective bargaining agreement compliance.']
  work_center_code varchar [note: 'Work center identifier for shop floor production units. Used in Manufacturing Execution System (MES) for production scheduling and capacity planning.']

  Note: 'Organizational hierarchy master representing departments, cost centers, business units, plants, and work centers within the manufacturing enterprise. Supports multi-level hierarchical structures (corporate → division → plant → department → work center) used for labor cost allocation, headcount reporting, and production scheduling.'
}

Table workforce.job_profile {
  job_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the job profile record. Primary key.']
  responsible_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Job profiles are defined at the organizational unit level; adding responsible_org_unit_id links each job_profile to its owning org_unit, eliminating the need for duplicate org‑unit descriptive columns']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status for this job profile: Pending (awaiting review), Approved (authorized for use), Rejected (not approved).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the HR or business leader who approved this job profile.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this job profile was approved for use.']
  base_pay_type varchar [note: 'Primary compensation structure for this job profile: Hourly (paid per hour worked), Salary (fixed annual amount), or Piece Rate (paid per unit produced).']
  bonus_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target annual bonus as a percentage of base salary for this job profile (e.g., 10.00 represents 10% target bonus).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this job profile record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for salary range values (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this job profile is retired or superseded. Null for open-ended profiles.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this job profile becomes active and available for use in hiring, workforce planning, and organizational design.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'FLSA overtime eligibility classification: Exempt (salaried, not eligible for overtime) or Non-Exempt (hourly, eligible for overtime).']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad occupational category grouping similar roles (e.g., Production, Engineering, Quality, Maintenance, Supply Chain, Sales, Finance, HR, IT, Management). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: production|engineering|quality|maintenance|supply_chain|sales|finance|hr|it|management — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the position within the organization (e.g., Entry, Intermediate, Senior, Lead, Manager, Director, Executive). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entry|intermediate|senior|lead|manager|director|executive — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  job_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job profile: Active (in use), Inactive (temporarily suspended), Draft (under development), Obsolete (retired).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this job profile record was last updated.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this job profile was last reviewed and validated by HR and business leadership.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review and update of this job profile.']
  occupational_classification_code varchar [note: 'Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) code aligning this job profile to national occupational taxonomy (format: XX-XXXX).']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions under this job profile are eligible for overtime compensation under FLSA and local labor laws.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation grade band assigned to this job profile, determining salary range and benefits eligibility.']
  physical_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of physical demands and working conditions for this job profile (e.g., \'Ability to lift 50 lbs, stand for extended periods, work in noisy environment, exposure to chemicals\').']
  proficiency_level_required varchar [note: 'Overall skill proficiency level expected for this job profile (e.g., Basic, Intermediate, Advanced, Expert).']
  profile_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the job profile across the enterprise. Used in HR systems, requisitions, and organizational planning.']
  profile_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose, scope, and key responsibilities of the job profile.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the job profile (e.g., \'Senior CNC Machinist\', \'Production Supervisor\', \'Quality Engineer\').']
  remote_work_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job profile is eligible for remote or hybrid work arrangements.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of mandatory professional certifications, licenses, or credentials required for this job profile (e.g., \'CNC Operator Certification, OSHA 30-Hour, Forklift License\').']
  required_education_level varchar [note: 'Minimum educational attainment required for this job profile (e.g., High School, Associate, Bachelor, Master, Doctorate, Vocational, None). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: high_school|associate|bachelor|master|doctorate|vocational|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  safety_sensitive_position boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job profile is classified as safety-sensitive, requiring additional screening, training, and compliance monitoring under OSHA and ISO 45001.']
  salary_range_maximum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum annual base salary for this job profile in the enterprise default currency.']
  salary_range_midpoint decimal(15,2) [note: 'Midpoint annual base salary for this job profile, representing the target compensation for fully competent performance.']
  salary_range_minimum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum annual base salary for this job profile in the enterprise default currency.']
  shift_premium_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions under this job profile are eligible for shift differential pay (e.g., night shift, weekend premium).']
  soft_skills_required varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of interpersonal and behavioral competencies required for this job profile (e.g., \'Leadership, Communication, Problem Solving, Teamwork, Attention to Detail\').']
  succession_planning_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job profile is designated as critical for succession planning and talent pipeline development.']
  technical_skills_required varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of technical skills and competencies required for this job profile (e.g., \'CNC Programming, PLC Troubleshooting, Welding, CAD, SAP MM, Statistical Process Control\').']
  travel_requirement_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of work time requiring business travel for this job profile (e.g., 10.00 represents 10% travel).']
  union_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions under this job profile are eligible for union membership and collective bargaining representation.']
  years_experience_minimum varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant work experience required for this job profile.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of standardized job profiles and position classifications used across the manufacturing enterprise. Defines job family, job level, pay grade band, salary range, FLSA classification, required certifications, standard competency and skill requirements, and compensation structure parameters (base pay type, shift premium eligibility, bonus target). Also serves as the reference for skill/competency taxonomy — each profile specifies the technical skills (CNC, PLC, welding, electrical), soft skills, and proficiency levels required. Sourced from Workday HCM Job Profile and aligned to ANSI/ISO occupational classification standards.'
}

Table workforce.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the authorized headcount position within the organizational structure.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.control_system. Business justification: Position definitions include responsibility for a control system, used in competency mapping and safety certification.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Position‑level headcount and labor‑cost planning rely on linking each position to its cost center.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee currently filling this position. Null if the position is vacant.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the standardized job profile that defines the role, responsibilities, competencies, and requirements for this position.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary work location (plant, office, facility) where this position is based.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational unit (department, cost center, division) to which this position is assigned.']
  supervisor_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the position that supervises this position in the organizational hierarchy.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when this position was approved by management for inclusion in the authorized headcount.']
  budgeted_headcount decimal(5,2) [note: 'Approved headcount allocation for this position in the annual budget, used for workforce planning and gap analysis.']
  business_unit varchar [note: 'High-level business unit or division to which this position belongs for strategic workforce planning.']
  created_date date [note: 'Date when this position record was originally created in the system.']
  critical_position_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position is classified as business-critical for succession planning and talent management purposes.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this position was closed or is scheduled to be closed. Null for open-ended positions.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this position became active and available for assignment within the organizational structure.']
  employment_category varchar [note: 'Employment category indicating the work schedule pattern: full-time, part-time, casual, or on-call.']
  exempt_status varchar [note: 'FLSA classification indicating whether the position is exempt or non-exempt from overtime pay requirements.']
  fte_allocation decimal(5,2) [note: 'Full-time equivalent allocation for this position, representing the proportion of a full-time workload (e.g., 1.00 for full-time, 0.50 for half-time).']
  is_safety_sensitive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position is classified as safety-sensitive, requiring additional health and safety compliance per ISO 45001 and OSHA regulations.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad job family classification grouping similar positions (e.g., Engineering, Operations, Finance, Human Resources).']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the position within the organization (e.g., entry, mid, senior, executive).']
  justification varchar [note: 'Business justification for creating or maintaining this position, used for budget approval and workforce planning.']
  last_filled_date date [note: 'Date when this position was most recently filled by an employee. Null if never filled.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this position record was last updated in the system.']
  minimum_education_level varchar [note: 'Minimum educational qualification required for this position (e.g., high school, associate degree, bachelor degree, master degree).']
  minimum_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant work experience required for this position.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments about this position for internal HR and management reference.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation grade or band assigned to this position, defining the salary range and compensation structure.']
  position_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique code for the position, used for external identification and reporting.']
  position_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the position responsibilities, duties, and key accountabilities.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the position indicating whether it is filled, vacant, frozen for budget reasons, pending approval, closed, or suspended.']
  position_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the position based on employment type: regular (permanent), temporary, contract, seasonal, or intern.']
  remote_work_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position is eligible for remote or hybrid work arrangements.']
  replacement_position_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position is a replacement for a previously closed position or a net-new headcount addition.']
  requires_certification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position requires specific professional certifications, licenses, or qualifications to perform the role.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Standard shift pattern assigned to this position for manufacturing operations and scheduling.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official job title for the position as defined in the organizational structure.']
  travel_requirement_percentage varchar [note: 'Percentage of time this position is expected to travel for business purposes (0-100).']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the labor union or collective bargaining unit to which this position belongs, if applicable.']
  vacancy_start_date date [note: 'Date when this position became vacant, used for time-to-fill metrics and workforce gap analysis.']
  work_schedule_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard number of hours per week assigned to this position for scheduling and labor tracking purposes.']

  Note: 'Authorized headcount positions within the organizational structure, representing approved roles that may be filled or vacant. Tracks position title, org unit assignment, FTE allocation, budgeted headcount, position status (filled/vacant/frozen), and effective dates. Enables workforce planning and headcount gap analysis against production staffing requirements.'
}

Table workforce.assignment {
  assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for assignment']
  assignment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee master record. Links this assignment to the worker in the workforce domain.']
  assignment_supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee record of the direct supervisor or manager for this assignment. Establishes reporting relationships.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the business unit for financial and operational reporting. Links to the enterprise business unit hierarchy.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary cost center for labor cost allocation. Used for financial reporting and production cost tracking.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Equipment assignment records which employee is assigned to maintain or operate a device, supporting maintenance planning.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job profile defining the role, responsibilities, and competencies for this assignment.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational unit to which the employee is assigned. Links to the organizational hierarchy.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the position master record. Identifies the specific position to which the employee is assigned.']
  work_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary physical work location for this assignment. Links to facility or site master data.']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the assignment. Used for external reference and reporting.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assignment. Indicates whether the assignment is currently active, inactive, suspended, terminated, or pending activation.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assignment nature. Distinguishes between permanent roles, temporary assignments, acting positions, secondments, contract work, and internships.']
  base_pay_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base compensation rate for the assignment. Amount is in the currency specified by pay_currency_code.']
  bonus_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target bonus as a percentage of base pay. Represents the expected variable compensation opportunity for this assignment.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Legal entity or company code for financial reporting and compliance. Aligns with ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) financial structure.']
  cost_center_split_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of labor cost allocated to the primary cost center. Used when an employee\'s time is split across multiple cost centers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the assignment ends or is scheduled to end. Nullable for open-ended assignments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the assignment becomes effective. Marks the beginning of the assignment period.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the employment arrangement. Distinguishes between full-time, part-time, casual, and seasonal employment.']
  fte_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of full-time equivalent hours for this assignment. 100.00 represents full-time, values below 100 represent part-time.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad categorization of the job role. Groups similar positions for workforce planning and talent management.']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the position within the organization. Used for career progression tracking and compensation benchmarking.']
