  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified the assignment record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes or comments about the assignment. Used for documenting special circumstances or conditions.']
  overtime_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assignment is eligible for overtime compensation. True if eligible for overtime pay under labor regulations.']
  pay_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for compensation amounts. Specifies the currency in which base pay and other compensation are denominated.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the base pay rate is applied. Indicates whether compensation is calculated hourly, daily, weekly, biweekly, monthly, or annually.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation grade or band assigned to this position. Used for pay equity analysis and compensation benchmarking.']
  primary_assignment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the employees primary assignment. True for primary assignment, false for secondary or concurrent assignments.']
  probation_end_date date [note: 'Date when the probationary period for this assignment ends. Null if no probation period applies.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or trigger for creating or modifying this assignment. Examples include new hire, promotion, transfer, reorganization, or contract renewal.']
  rehire_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for rehire after assignment termination. True if eligible, false if not eligible. Null for active assignments.']
  scheduled_weekly_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard number of hours the employee is scheduled to work per week under this assignment.']
  shift_premium_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assignment is eligible for shift premium pay. True if eligible for additional compensation for non-standard shifts.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this assignment record originated. Used for data lineage and integration tracking.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the assignment was terminated. Null for active assignments. Used for historical tracking and compliance reporting.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for assignment termination. Examples include resignation, retirement, layoff, dismissal, end of contract, or transfer. Null for active assignments.']
  time_type varchar [note: 'Classification of time tracking method for this assignment. Examples include salaried exempt, salaried non-exempt, hourly, or piece-rate.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the labor union or collective bargaining unit covering this assignment. Null if not unionized.']
  union_membership_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assignment is covered by a collective bargaining agreement. True if the position is unionized.']
  work_shift_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the standard work shift pattern for this assignment. Examples include day shift, night shift, rotating shift, or flexible schedule.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created the assignment record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of an employee\'s assignment to a specific position, org unit, job profile, compensation terms, and work location over a defined effective period. Captures primary and secondary assignments, supervisory relationships, cost center splits, assignment type (permanent, temporary, acting), base pay rate, pay frequency, shift premium eligibility, and bonus target percentage. Serves as the central employment terms record supporting labor cost allocation to production cost centers, pay equity analysis, workforce planning, and compensation history tracking.'
}

Table workforce.workforce_certification {
  workforce_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the workforce certification record. Primary key for the workforce certification entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who holds or is pursuing this certification. Links to the employee master record in Workday HCM.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Required for tracking which regulatory requirement each certification satisfies, essential for compliance audit reports.']
  type_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the certification type from the master catalog. Defines the regulatory, safety, or professional certification standard (e.g., ISO 45001, OSHA 10/30, forklift operator, electrical safety, AWS/ASME welding qualifications, quality auditor).']
  attachment_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the digital copy of the certification document, certificate, or credential stored in the document management system. Null if no digital copy is available.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate or credential number issued by the certifying authority. Used for verification and audit purposes.']
  certification_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the certification domain: safety (OSHA, ISO 45001), quality (ISO 9001, Six Sigma), technical (welding, electrical), regulatory (EPA, FDA), professional (PMP, CPA), or operational (forklift, crane operator).']
  certification_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code or abbreviation for the certification type used for internal classification and reporting (e.g., \'OSHA-30\', \'AWS-CWI\', \'ISO9001-LA\').']
  certification_level varchar [note: 'Proficiency or qualification level of the certification (e.g., \'Basic\', \'Intermediate\', \'Advanced\', \'Lead Auditor\', \'Master Craftsman\'). Null if the certification does not have levels.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the certification as recognized by the issuing authority (e.g., \'OSHA 30-Hour General Industry\', \'AWS Certified Welder\', \'ISO 9001 Lead Auditor\').']
  compliance_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification is mandated by regulatory or company policy for the employees role (True) or is optional/voluntary (False). Used to enforce ISO 45001 occupational health compliance.']
  cost_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to obtain or renew this certification, including training fees, examination fees, and certification fees. Used for labor cost allocation and training budget tracking.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'SAP FI/CO cost center code to which the certification cost is allocated. Used for departmental budget tracking and labor cost allocation.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the certification cost amount (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'CNY\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the certification is valid and the employee is authorized to perform certified tasks. May differ from issue date if there is a waiting period.']
  equipment_authorization varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of equipment types or asset classes the employee is authorized to operate or work on with this certification (e.g., \'Overhead Crane, Gantry Crane\', \'MIG Welder, TIG Welder\', \'High Voltage Switchgear\').']
  examination_date date [note: 'Date on which the employee took the certification examination or assessment. Null if no examination is required.']
  examination_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score or percentage achieved by the employee on the certification examination. Null if no examination is required or score is not recorded.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification expires and is no longer valid unless renewed. Null for certifications with no expiration (lifetime certifications).']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification was originally issued to the employee by the certifying authority.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or regulatory body that issued the certification (e.g., \'OSHA\', \'American Welding Society\', \'International Register of Certificated Auditors\').']
  issuing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction where the certification was issued (e.g., \'USA\', \'DEU\', \'CHN\').']
  job_role_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of job roles or job families for which this certification is required or recommended (e.g., \'Welder, Fabricator\', \'Quality Inspector, Auditor\', \'Forklift Operator, Material Handler\').']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this certification record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_renewal_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent renewal or recertification event. Null if the certification has never been renewed.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the HR administrator or system user who last modified this certification record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special conditions, or administrative notes related to this certification record (e.g., \'Renewal pending manager approval\', \'Certification suspended due to incident investigation\').']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Result of the certification examination or assessment: pass (met requirements), fail (did not meet requirements), pending (awaiting results), or not_applicable (no examination required).']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory standard or framework that mandates this certification (e.g., \'ISO 45001\', \'OSHA 1910.147\', \'IEC 61131\', \'EPA 40 CFR 112\'). Null for non-regulatory certifications.']
  renewal_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this certification has been renewed for this employee. Zero for initial certifications that have not yet been renewed.']
  renewal_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the certification must be renewed to maintain continuous validity. Typically set before the expiry date to allow processing time. Null if renewal is not required.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certification requires periodic renewal (True) or is a one-time/lifetime certification (False).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this certification record originated (e.g., \'Workday HCM\', \'Manual Entry\', \'Third-Party Verification Service\'). Used for data lineage and integration tracking.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this certification record in the source operational system. Used for reconciliation and data lineage.']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the employee completed the required training or coursework for this certification. Null if training is not required or not yet completed.']
  training_hours_completed decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of training or continuing education hours the employee has completed toward this certification. Used to track progress for in-progress certifications.']
  training_hours_required decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total number of training or continuing education hours required to obtain or maintain this certification. Null if not applicable.']
  training_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or institution that provided the training or preparation for this certification (e.g., \'Internal Training Center\', \'American Welding Society\', \'TUV Rheinland\'). May differ from the issuing authority.']
  validity_period_months varchar [note: 'Standard validity period of the certification in months from the issue date (e.g., 12, 24, 36). Null for lifetime certifications.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification was last verified with the issuing authority. Null if never verified.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of third-party verification of the certification with the issuing authority: verified (confirmed authentic), pending_verification (awaiting confirmation), failed_verification (could not be confirmed), or not_verified (not yet checked).']
  verified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the HR administrator or system that performed the most recent verification. Null if never verified.']
  work_location_restriction varchar [note: 'Geographic or facility-level restrictions on where this certification is valid (e.g., \'USA only\', \'EU member states\', \'Plant 101 only\'). Null if valid globally.']
  workforce_certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification: active (valid and in effect), expired (past expiry date), suspended (temporarily invalid), revoked (permanently invalidated), pending (awaiting approval), or in_progress (training/testing underway).']

  Note: 'Master catalog of regulatory, safety, and professional certification types relevant to industrial manufacturing, combined with transactional attainment records for individual employees. Defines certification types (ISO 45001, OSHA 10/30, forklift operator, electrical safety, AWS/ASME welding qualifications, quality auditor) with issuing body, validity period, and renewal requirements. Tracks individual employee attainment including issue date, expiry date, certificate number, issuing authority, current status (active/expired/suspended), and automated renewal workflow triggers. Serves as the SSOT for all certification compliance — ensuring only certified workers are assigned to regulated tasks per ISO 45001 occupational health requirements.'
}

Table workforce.shift_schedule {
  shift_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shift schedule record. Primary key for the shift schedule entity.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Operator shift schedules assign specific machines to employees, enabling production planning and labor tracking.']
  primary_shift_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee assigned to this shift. Links to the employee master record in Workday HCM.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the production line to which the employee is assigned during this shift. Links to production line master data.']
  shift_pattern_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the shift pattern template applied to this schedule (e.g., 3-shift rotation, 4-on-4-off). Links to shift pattern reference data.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the work center where the employee is scheduled to work. Links to the work center master in SAP S/4HANA PP.']
  break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total planned break time in minutes during the shift (e.g., lunch break, rest breaks). Used to calculate net productive hours and ensure compliance with labor regulations.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for shift cancellation (e.g., production order cancelled, employee absence, equipment failure). Populated only when schedule_status is cancelled.']
  cancelled_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shift schedule was cancelled. Populated only when schedule_status is cancelled. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  confirmed_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the employee acknowledged or confirmed the shift assignment. Used for tracking employee acceptance and attendance planning. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who created this shift schedule record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  created_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shift schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  exception_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for any schedule exception (e.g., production surge, equipment breakdown, employee request). Populated only when schedule_exception_flag is true.']
  is_holiday boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the shift occurs on a recognized company or statutory holiday. Used for premium pay calculation and compliance reporting.']
  is_night_shift boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the shift occurs during night hours (typically 10 PM to 6 AM). Used for shift differential pay and occupational health compliance.']
  is_overtime boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this shift qualifies as overtime under labor regulations. True if the shift exceeds standard working hours or occurs outside regular schedule.']
  is_weekend boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the shift occurs on a weekend (Saturday or Sunday). Used for premium pay calculation and workforce analytics.']
  labor_cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center code to which labor hours and costs for this shift are allocated. Used for financial reporting and cost accounting in SAP S/4HANA CO.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this shift schedule record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  modified_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shift schedule record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  net_productive_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Net available labor hours after deducting break time from scheduled duration. Used for capacity planning and Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) calculations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the shift schedule. Used for special instructions, safety reminders, or coordination information.']
  published_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shift schedule was published and communicated to the employee. Used for compliance with advance notice requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  schedule_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the shift occurs. Used for day-level aggregation and reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  schedule_exception_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this shift has any exceptions or deviations from the standard shift pattern (e.g., early start, late end, split shift).']
  schedule_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) of the scheduled shift. Used for monthly labor cost allocation and workforce planning.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the shift schedule, typically generated by Opcenter MES or Workday HCM. Format: SCH-YYYYMMDD.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shift schedule. Draft schedules are under planning; published schedules are communicated to employees; confirmed schedules are acknowledged; cancelled schedules are voided; completed schedules have ended.']
  schedule_week varchar [note: 'ISO week number of the year for the scheduled shift. Used for weekly labor planning and reporting.']
  schedule_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year of the scheduled shift. Used for annual labor reporting and compliance.']
  scheduled_duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total planned duration of the shift in hours, calculated as the difference between scheduled end and start datetime. Used for labor capacity planning and payroll calculation.']
  scheduled_end_datetime timestamp [note: 'Planned end date and time of the shift. Represents the clock-out time for the employee at the assigned work center. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  scheduled_start_datetime timestamp [note: 'Planned start date and time of the shift. Represents the clock-in time for the employee at the assigned work center. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  scheduling_horizon_weeks varchar [note: 'Number of weeks in advance that this shift was scheduled. Used for workforce planning analytics and compliance with advance notice regulations.']
  shift_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the shift (e.g., Morning Shift, Night Shift, Swing Shift, Weekend Crew). Used for human-readable identification and communication.']
  shift_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level of the shift assignment. Critical shifts support high-priority production orders; high-priority shifts support key customer deliveries; normal shifts are standard operations; low-priority shifts are for non-urgent tasks.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift assignment. Regular shifts are standard production hours; overtime shifts exceed standard hours; on-call and standby shifts are for emergency coverage; training shifts are for skill development; maintenance shifts are for equipment servicing.']
  skill_requirement varchar [note: 'Required skill or certification for the employee to perform this shift (e.g., CNC Operator, Forklift Certified, Quality Inspector). Used for skill-based scheduling and compliance.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that created or last updated this shift schedule. Opcenter MES for shop floor scheduling; Workday HCM for HR-driven scheduling; SAP S/4HANA for integrated planning; manual for ad-hoc entries.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this shift schedule record in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Operational schedule assigning employees to specific shifts, work centers, and production lines over a defined scheduling horizon. Captures scheduled start/end datetime, assigned work center, production line, shift pattern, schedule status (draft, published, confirmed), and any schedule exceptions. Integrates with Opcenter MES for shop floor labor availability and capacity planning.'
}

Table workforce.time_entry {
  time_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the time entry record. Primary key for the time entry transaction.']
  asset_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the production work order or maintenance job that this labor time is charged to. Used for labor cost allocation to manufacturing operations.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Time‑entry records must allocate labor hours to a cost center for accurate financial statements.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the machine, equipment, or asset operated or maintained during this time entry. Used for equipment utilization and maintenance labor tracking.']
  payroll_period_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the payroll period to which this time entry is assigned for payment processing.']
  primary_time_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who recorded this time entry. Links to the employee master record in Workday HCM.']
  tertiary_time_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the user or employee who last modified this time entry record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  absence_type varchar [note: 'Type of paid or unpaid absence if this time entry represents non-working time. Set to \'none\' for regular working time entries. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|sick_leave|vacation|personal|bereavement|jury_duty|training|other — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  activity_code varchar [note: 'The specific work activity or operation code performed during this time entry (e.g., ASSEMBLY, WELDING, INSPECTION, SETUP, MAINTENANCE). Used for detailed labor tracking and OEE analysis.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the time entry in the workflow. Time entries must be approved before being released to payroll processing.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this time entry was approved by the supervisor. Null if not yet approved.']
  break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of unpaid breaks taken during this time entry, expressed in minutes. Deducted from total hours for payroll calculation.']
  clock_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the employee clocked in or started work for this time entry. Captured from MES shop floor terminals or Workday HCM time tracking.']
  clock_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the employee clocked out or ended work for this time entry. Used to calculate total hours worked.']
  compliance_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this time entry has been verified for compliance with labor regulations, union agreements, and ISO 45001 occupational health and safety requirements (e.g., maximum consecutive hours, mandatory rest periods).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this time entry record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for labor rate, premium, and cost amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any time entry exception or anomaly requiring review (e.g., LATE_CLOCK_IN, MISSING_CLOCK_OUT, OVERTIME_THRESHOLD). Empty if no exception.']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments explaining any time entry exception, correction, or special circumstance. Used for audit trail and dispute resolution.']
  hours_worked decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total number of hours worked during this time entry, calculated from clock-in and clock-out timestamps. Expressed in decimal hours (e.g., 8.50 for 8 hours 30 minutes).']
  job_code varchar [note: 'The job classification or position code of the employee at the time this work was performed. Used for labor mix analysis and skill-based costing.']
  labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total labor cost for this time entry, calculated as hours worked multiplied by labor rate plus any shift premiums. Used for work order costing and variance analysis.']
  labor_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The hourly labor rate applied to this time entry for cost calculation purposes. May differ from actual employee pay rate for standard costing purposes.']
  labor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the labor time for payroll and costing purposes. Determines the pay rate multiplier and labor cost calculation method.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'The plant, facility, or work center location code where this time was worked. Used for multi-site labor reporting and compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this time entry record was last modified or updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  oee_productive_time boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this time entry represents productive manufacturing time that should be included in OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) labor efficiency calculations. False for indirect labor, breaks, or non-productive activities.']
  operation_sequence varchar [note: 'The sequence number of the manufacturing operation or routing step being performed during this time entry. Used to track labor against specific production process steps.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for overtime pay for this time entry based on employment classification and labor regulations.']
  payroll_processed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this time entry has been processed and included in a payroll run. Once true, the time entry is locked from further edits.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'The project or capital expenditure (CapEx) project code to which this labor time is charged. Used for project accounting and capital asset construction tracking.']
  quantity_produced decimal(12,3) [note: 'The number of units or pieces produced or processed during this time entry. Used for productivity analysis and labor efficiency calculations (units per hour).']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'The shift identifier or code during which this time was worked (e.g., DAY, NIGHT, SWING). Used for shift premium calculation and production scheduling analysis.']
  shift_premium_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The additional premium amount paid for this time entry due to shift differential, expressed in the payroll currency. Null if no premium applies.']
  shift_premium_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this time entry qualifies for shift differential or premium pay based on the shift worked and labor agreement terms.']
  time_entry_source varchar [note: 'The system or method through which this time entry was captured (e.g., MES shop floor terminal, Workday mobile app, manual supervisor entry).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the quantity produced (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, M for meters). Aligns with material master UOM definitions.']
  work_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the work was performed. Used for payroll period assignment and labor reporting.']

  Note: 'Granular transactional record of employee time worked, capturing clock-in/clock-out events, work order or cost center charged, labor type (regular, overtime, double-time), shift premium eligibility, and approval status. Sourced from Workday HCM Time Tracking and MES shop floor terminals. Feeds payroll processing, labor cost allocation to production work orders, and OEE labor efficiency calculations.'
}

Table workforce.absence_record {
  absence_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the absence record. Primary key.']
  asset_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work order or production order that was impacted by this absence. Used to track production capacity gaps and schedule adjustments.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Absence cost impact is charged to the employee\'s cost center for variance analysis.']
  payroll_period_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payroll period in which this absence is processed for payroll deduction or accrual adjustment.']
  primary_absence_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who is absent. Links to the employee master record in Workday HCM.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the scheduled shift from which the employee was absent. Supports shift-level labor availability tracking.']
  tertiary_absence_replacement_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the replacement worker assigned to cover the absent employee\'s shift or responsibilities.']
  absence_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the absence, including special circumstances, accommodation requests, or manager observations.']
  absence_reason_code varchar [note: 'Detailed reason code for the absence. May include specific illness codes, family emergency types, or other granular classifications as defined by HR policy.']
  absence_type_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the category of absence: VACATION (planned vacation leave), SICK (unplanned sick leave), PERSONAL (personal time off), PARENTAL (maternity/paternity leave), FMLA (Family and Medical Leave Act protected leave), BEREAVEMENT (compassionate leave), JURY_DUTY (civic duty leave), MILITARY (military service leave), UNPAID (unpaid leave of absence). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VACATION|SICK|PERSONAL|PARENTAL|FMLA|BEREAVEMENT|JURY_DUTY|MILITARY|UNPAID — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  accrual_balance_deducted decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of hours deducted from the employee\'s leave accrual balance (vacation bank, sick leave bank, etc.) for this absence.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of the absence request: PENDING (awaiting manager review), APPROVED (authorized by manager), REJECTED (denied by manager), CANCELLED (approved but subsequently cancelled), WITHDRAWN (employee withdrew request before approval).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the absence request was approved or rejected by the manager.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this absence record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  duration_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the absence measured in calendar days. Supports fractional days for partial-day absences.']
  duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total duration of the absence measured in hours. Used for hourly payroll deduction and accrual calculations.']
  end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the absence period. Nullable for open-ended absences pending return-to-work confirmation.']
  is_fmla_protected boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this absence is protected under the Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) regulations, providing job protection and continuation of benefits.']
  is_intermittent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this absence is part of an intermittent leave pattern (true) such as recurring FMLA leave, or a continuous block of absence (false).']
  is_paid boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the absence is paid (true) or unpaid (false). Determines whether payroll deduction or accrual balance adjustment is required.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this absence record was last updated in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  medical_certification_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required medical certification documentation has been received and validated by HR.']
  medical_certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether medical certification documentation is required for this absence per company policy or regulatory requirement.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the employee originally submitted the absence request in Workday HCM.']
  return_to_work_certification_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a medical clearance or return-to-work certification was received before the employee resumed duties, as required by occupational health policy per ISO 45001.']
  return_to_work_date date [note: 'Actual date the employee returned to work following the absence. May differ from the planned end_date if the absence was extended or shortened.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this absence record originated (e.g., WORKDAY_HCM, SAP_HR, MANUAL_ENTRY).']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this absence record in the source system (e.g., Workday HCM absence event ID). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the absence period. Used for production capacity planning and labor availability forecasting.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of employee absences including planned leave (vacation, personal, parental), unplanned absences (sick leave, FMLA), and approved time-off requests. Captures absence type, start/end dates, duration in hours/days, approval status, and reason code. Supports production capacity planning by surfacing labor availability gaps and feeds payroll deduction or accrual calculations.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_result {
  payroll_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payroll calculation result record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Payroll expenses are allocated to cost centers to support financial reporting and budgeting.']
  payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payroll processing batch or run that generated this result. Used for traceability and audit purposes.']
  primary_payroll_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee for whom this payroll result was calculated. Links to employee master data in Workday HCM.']
  allowance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total allowances paid to the employee (housing, transportation, meal allowances, etc.).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payroll result was approved for payment.']
  bank_account_last_four varchar [note: 'Last four digits of the bank account number used for direct deposit payment. Stored for verification purposes only.']
  base_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The base salary or hourly wage earnings for the pay period before any additional earnings or deductions.']
  bonus_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'One-time or periodic bonus payments included in this payroll result (performance bonus, retention bonus, etc.).']
  capex_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total labor cost allocated to capital expenditure projects for this pay period. Used for financial capitalization of labor costs.']
  commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sales commission or incentive earnings included in this payroll result.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this payroll result (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  department_code varchar [note: 'The department code of the employee at the time of payroll calculation.']
  employer_benefits_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total employer-paid benefits costs (employer contribution to health insurance, retirement matching, etc.) for this payroll result. Used for total labor cost calculation.']
  employer_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total employer-paid payroll taxes (employer portion of Social Security, Medicare, unemployment insurance, etc.) for this payroll result. Used for CapEx/OpEx labor cost allocation.']
  federal_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Federal income tax withheld from the employee\'s gross pay for this pay period.']
  garnishment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Court-ordered wage garnishments deducted from gross pay (child support, tax levies, creditor garnishments).']
  gross_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total earnings before any deductions. Sum of base pay, overtime, bonuses, commissions, allowances, and other earnings.']
  health_insurance_deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee contribution to health insurance premiums deducted from gross pay.']
  local_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Local or municipal tax withheld from the employee\'s gross pay for this pay period.']
  medicare_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Medicare tax withheld from the employee\'s gross pay for this pay period.']
  net_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final take-home pay amount after all deductions. This is the amount paid to the employee.']
  opex_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total labor cost allocated to operational expenditure for this pay period. Used for financial expense reporting.']
  other_benefits_deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total deductions for other employee benefits (dental, vision, life insurance, disability insurance, etc.).']
  other_deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total of all other miscellaneous deductions not categorized above (loan repayments, charitable contributions, etc.).']
  overtime_hours_worked decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total overtime hours worked by the employee during the pay period.']
  overtime_pay_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total overtime earnings for the pay period, calculated based on hours worked beyond standard schedule.']
  paid_time_off_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total paid time off hours (vacation, sick leave, personal days) used during the pay period.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the employee is paid (weekly, biweekly, semimonthly, monthly).']
  pay_group varchar [note: 'The pay group to which the employee belongs, determining pay schedule and processing rules.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the pay period covered by this payroll result.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the pay period covered by this payroll result.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the employee will receive or received payment for this payroll result.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The method by which the employee receives payment (direct deposit, physical check, cash, wire transfer).']
  payroll_calculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this payroll result was calculated by the payroll system.']
  payroll_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payroll result indicating its processing stage.']
  regular_hours_worked decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total regular hours worked by the employee during the pay period, excluding overtime.']
  retirement_contribution_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Employee contribution to retirement savings plan (401k, pension) deducted from gross pay.']
  shift_premium_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional compensation for working non-standard shifts (night shift, weekend shift) as per labor agreements.']
  social_security_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Social Security (FICA) tax withheld from the employee\'s gross pay for this pay period.']
  state_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'State income tax withheld from the employee\'s gross pay for this pay period.']
  total_deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all deductions (taxes, benefits, garnishments, etc.) subtracted from gross pay.']
  total_labor_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total fully-loaded labor cost including gross pay, employer taxes, and employer benefits. Used for CapEx/OpEx allocation and financial reporting.']
  union_dues_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Union membership dues deducted from gross pay as per collective bargaining agreement.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of payroll calculation outcomes per employee per pay period. Captures gross pay, net pay, earnings components (base, overtime, shift premium, bonus), deduction components (tax withholding, benefits, garnishments), pay period dates, payroll run identifier, and payment method. Sourced from Workday HCM Payroll. Supports labor cost allocation to cost centers and CapEx/OpEx reporting.'
}

Table workforce.benefit_plan {
  benefit_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the benefit plan. Primary key.']
  offering_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Benefit plans are offered by a specific organizational unit; adding offering_org_unit_id provides clear ownership and enables joins to org_unit for cost center and location context.']
  aca_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this health benefit plan meets ACA minimum essential coverage and minimum value standards. Required for employer mandate reporting.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance carrier or third-party administrator providing the benefit plan (e.g., \'Blue Cross Blue Shield\', \'Aetna\', \'Fidelity\').']
  carrier_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy or contract number assigned by the insurance carrier to this benefit plan. Used for claims processing and carrier communications.']
  cobra_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit plan is subject to COBRA continuation coverage requirements, allowing terminated employees to continue coverage at their own expense.']
  coinsurance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of covered service costs the employee pays after meeting the deductible (e.g., 20% coinsurance means employee pays 20%, plan pays 80%).']
  contribution_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which employee and employer contributions are collected (e.g., biweekly aligns with payroll cycles).']
  copay_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount the employee pays for a covered service (e.g., $25 for a doctor visit). Applies to health and dental plans.']
  coverage_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum benefit amount or coverage limit provided by the plan (e.g., $500,000 life insurance coverage, $50,000 annual disability benefit). Null for unlimited coverage plans.']
  coverage_tier varchar [note: 'Level of coverage offered by the plan, determining who is covered (employee only, employee plus dependents, family coverage). Affects premium rates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this benefit plan record was first created in the HR system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for contribution amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multinational benefit administration.']
  deductible_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual deductible amount the employee must pay out-of-pocket before insurance coverage begins. Applies to health, dental, and vision plans.']
  dependent_coverage_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit plan allows employees to enroll eligible dependents (spouse, children, domestic partner).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this benefit plan is no longer available for new enrollments or when coverage terminates. Null for open-ended plans.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this benefit plan becomes available for enrollment and coverage begins. Typically aligns with plan year start or new hire eligibility.']
  eligibility_rule varchar [note: 'Business rule defining which employee classes or employment types are eligible for this benefit plan (e.g., \'Full-time employees with 90 days of service\', \'Salaried employees only\').']
  employee_contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount the employee pays per pay period for this benefit plan coverage tier. Deducted from payroll pre-tax or post-tax depending on plan type.']
  employer_contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount the employer pays per pay period toward this benefit plan coverage tier. Represents labor cost allocation for financial reporting.']
  enrollment_close_date date [note: 'Last date employees can enroll in or make changes to this benefit plan during the annual open enrollment period.']
  enrollment_open_date date [note: 'First date employees can enroll in or make changes to this benefit plan during the annual open enrollment period.']
  erisa_plan_number varchar [note: 'Three-digit plan number assigned to this benefit plan for ERISA Form 5500 reporting. Required for all ERISA-covered plans.']
  hipaa_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit plan complies with HIPAA privacy and security requirements for protected health information (PHI).']
  max_dependents varchar [note: 'Maximum number of dependents an employee can enroll under this benefit plan. Null if no limit applies.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the HR administrator who last modified this benefit plan record. Supports audit and compliance requirements.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this benefit plan record was last updated. Tracks changes to plan configuration, rates, or terms.']
  out_of_pocket_max decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum amount an employee will pay out-of-pocket for covered services in a plan year. After this limit, the plan pays 100% of covered expenses.']
  plan_administrator_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the plan administrator. Used for employee benefit inquiries and regulatory communications.']
  plan_administrator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or entity responsible for administering the benefit plan and handling employee inquiries, claims, and appeals.']
  plan_administrator_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the plan administrator. Provided to employees for benefit support and claims assistance.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the benefit plan in HR systems and employee communications. Used on enrollment forms and benefit statements.']
  plan_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the official Summary Plan Description (SPD) document required under ERISA. Provides employees with detailed plan terms and conditions.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the benefit plan as presented to employees (e.g., \'Premium Health PPO Plan\', \'Basic Dental Coverage\', \'Employee Stock Purchase Plan\').']
  plan_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or administrative comments about this benefit plan. Used for internal HR reference.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the benefit plan. Active plans are available for enrollment; inactive plans are closed to new enrollments but may have existing participants.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of benefit plan offered. Determines regulatory compliance requirements and enrollment rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: health|dental|vision|life|disability|retirement|hsa|fsa|eap|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  plan_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year for which this benefit plan configuration is effective. Benefit plans are typically versioned annually with new rates and terms.']
  section_125_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit plan is eligible for pre-tax payroll deductions under an IRC Section 125 cafeteria plan.']
  tax_treatment varchar [note: 'Tax treatment of employee contributions to this benefit plan. Pre-tax contributions reduce taxable income; post-tax contributions do not.']
  waiting_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days a new hire must wait before becoming eligible to enroll in this benefit plan. Regulatory limits apply under ACA.']

  Note: 'Reference master of employee benefit plans offered by the manufacturing enterprise — including health, dental, vision, life, disability, 401(k)/pension, HSA/FSA, and EAP — combined with transactional enrollment records per employee per plan year. Plan definitions capture plan type, carrier/provider, coverage tiers, enrollment periods, contribution rates, and eligibility rules by employee class. Enrollment records track individual elections: coverage level selected, effective dates, dependent coverage, and life event changes. Sourced from Workday HCM Benefits. Serves as the SSOT for both benefit plan configuration and employee enrollment status.'
}

Table workforce.training_course {
  training_course_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the training course. Primary key for the training course master catalog.']
  instructor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Training management tracks internal instructors delivering courses to ensure qualification and cost allocation.']
  owner_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Each training course is owned by a department; linking to org_unit provides hierarchy and removes redundant owner_department string.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Ensures each training course is linked to the specific regulation it fulfills, used in compliance training matrices.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the external training vendor in the procurement system. Null for internal training.']
  accreditation_number varchar [note: 'Official accreditation or approval number issued by the regulatory body or industry association for the training course. Used for compliance audit trails.']
  assessment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal assessment (test, exam, practical demonstration) is required to complete the course. True if assessment is mandatory; false if completion is based on attendance only.']
  certification_awarded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether successful completion of the course results in a formal certification or credential being awarded to the employee.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Name of the certification or credential awarded upon successful course completion. Null if no certification is awarded.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard that the training course addresses. Examples include ISO 45001 (occupational health and safety), OSHA (workplace safety), ISO 9001 (quality management), IEC 62443 (industrial cybersecurity), EPA (environmental), or internal compliance policies. Multiple frameworks may be listed separated by semicolons.']
  cost_per_participant decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard cost per employee for delivering the training course, including instructor fees, materials, facility costs, and external vendor charges. Used for training budget planning and labor cost allocation.']
  course_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the training course by subject area. Safety includes ISO 45001 and OSHA training; technical covers CNC, PLC, SCADA, welding; quality includes SPC, FMEA, PPAP; leadership covers management development; compliance covers regulatory requirements; operational covers MES, ERP, and process training.']
  course_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the training course in the learning management system. Used for course catalog reference and enrollment processing.']
  course_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the training course content, learning objectives, target audience, and prerequisites.']
  course_materials_url varchar [note: 'Web address or document management system link to access course materials, handouts, presentations, and reference documents.']
  course_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training course. Active courses are available for enrollment; inactive courses are temporarily unavailable; draft courses are under development; retired courses are no longer offered; under review courses are being updated or audited.']
  course_title varchar [note: 'Full descriptive title of the training course as displayed in the learning catalog and on certificates.']
  course_type varchar [note: 'Classification indicating whether the course is mandatory for specific roles, elective for employee development, or recommended for career progression.']
  course_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the training course content. Incremented when course materials, objectives, or assessments are updated. Used for version control and compliance tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the training course record was first created in the learning management system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost per participant. Standard currency for training cost reporting.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the training course is delivered. Classroom is in-person instructor-led; e-learning is online self-paced; OJT (On-the-Job Training) is hands-on workplace training; blended combines multiple methods; virtual instructor-led is live online; self-paced is asynchronous learning.']
  duration_days decimal(4,1) [note: 'Standard duration of the training course in business days. Used for multi-day courses and scheduling planning.']
  duration_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard duration of the training course in hours. Used for scheduling, capacity planning, and labor cost allocation.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the training course is retired or no longer available for new enrollments. Null for active courses with no planned retirement date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the training course becomes available for enrollment and delivery. Used for course catalog management and scheduling.']
  instructor_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the course requires a qualified instructor to deliver the training. True for classroom, OJT, and virtual instructor-led courses; false for self-paced e-learning.']
  language varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code indicating the primary language in which the course is delivered. Used for multi-language workforce training planning.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the training course content was last reviewed for accuracy, relevance, and compliance. Used to track course maintenance and ensure content currency.']
  max_participants varchar [note: 'Maximum number of employees that can be enrolled in a single course session. Used for capacity planning and session scheduling. Null for unlimited enrollment courses such as self-paced e-learning.']
  min_participants varchar [note: 'Minimum number of enrolled employees required to run a scheduled course session. Sessions below this threshold may be cancelled or rescheduled. Null for self-paced courses.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the person who last modified the training course record. Used for change tracking and accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the training course record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the training course content. Used for course maintenance planning and compliance with ISO 9001 training program requirements.']
  passing_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum score required to successfully complete the course, expressed as a percentage (0-100). Used to determine pass/fail status and certification eligibility.']
  prerequisite_courses varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of course codes that must be completed before enrolling in this course. Null if no prerequisites exist.']
  recurrence_interval_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required course repetitions for recurrent training. Null if recurrence is not required. Common values include 12 (annual), 24 (biennial), or 36 (triennial) for safety and compliance training.']
  recurrence_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the training course must be repeated periodically to maintain competence or compliance. True for courses with expiration requirements such as safety training, regulatory compliance, or equipment certifications.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory or governing body that mandates or recognizes the training. Examples include OSHA, EPA, ISO, IEC, ANSI, UL, NIST. Null for non-regulatory training.']
  subject_matter_expert varchar [note: 'Name of the subject matter expert responsible for course content accuracy and technical review. Used for course maintenance and update coordination.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the intended audience for the training course, including job roles, departments, or skill levels. Used for course recommendation and enrollment planning.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the person who created the training course record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of training courses and learning programs combined with transactional enrollment and completion records for manufacturing employees. Course definitions include code, title, delivery method (classroom/e-learning/OJT), duration, passing criteria, mandatory/elective classification, and recurrence requirements. Enrollment records capture individual employee participation: enrollment date, scheduled session, completion date, assessment score, pass/fail status, instructor, and any linked certification earned upon completion. Covers safety training (ISO 45001, OSHA), technical skills (CNC, PLC, SCADA, welding), quality methods (SPC, FMEA, PPAP), and leadership development. Serves as the SSOT for ISO 45001 competence management and mandatory training compliance tracking.'
}

Table workforce.performance_review {
  performance_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the performance review record. Primary key.']
  primary_performance_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee being reviewed. Links to employee master data.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the manager or supervisor conducting the performance review.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the performance rating was calibrated through organizational review and alignment process.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the performance rating has been through organizational calibration process to ensure consistency (not calibrated, pending calibration, calibrated, calibration waived).']
  competency_rating varchar [note: 'Rating reflecting the employees demonstration of core competencies and behavioral skills (expert, proficient, developing, basic, not demonstrated).']
  competency_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing competency assessment, typically on a scale (e.g., 1.0 to 5.0).']
  compliance_training_completed boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the employee completed all required compliance training during the review period, relevant for ISO 45001 occupational health and safety compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the performance review record was first created in the system.']
  department_at_review varchar [note: 'Department or organizational unit of the employee at the time the review was conducted.']
  development_plan varchar [note: 'Documented plan outlining specific development actions, training, and growth opportunities for the employee going forward.']
  employee_acknowledgement_date date [note: 'Date when the employee acknowledged receipt and review of their performance evaluation.']
  employee_acknowledgement_status varchar [note: 'Status indicating whether the employee has acknowledged the review (pending, acknowledged, disputed).']
  employee_rebuttal_comments varchar [note: 'Optional comments provided by the employee if they disagree with or wish to provide additional context to the performance review.']
  employee_self_assessment varchar [note: 'Employees self-evaluation narrative describing their own performance, accomplishments, and development needs during the review period.']
  goal_achievement_rating varchar [note: 'Rating reflecting the employees achievement of individual goals and objectives set for the review period.']
  goal_achievement_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing goal achievement performance, typically on a scale (e.g., 1.0 to 5.0).']
  job_profile_at_review varchar [note: 'Job profile or position title of the employee at the time the review was conducted.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the performance review record was last updated or modified.']
  location_at_review varchar [note: 'Work location or site of the employee at the time the review was conducted.']
  manager_comments varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative comments and feedback provided by the reviewing manager regarding employee performance, strengths, and development areas.']
  merit_increase_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the employee is eligible for merit-based salary increase based on this performance review outcome.']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'Overall performance rating assigned to the employee for the review period (exceeds expectations, meets expectations, needs improvement, unsatisfactory, outstanding).']
  overall_rating_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score representing the overall performance rating, typically on a scale (e.g., 1.0 to 5.0).']
  performance_improvement_plan_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a formal Performance Improvement Plan is required due to unsatisfactory performance.']
  promotion_recommended boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the reviewing manager recommends the employee for promotion based on performance.']
  review_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the performance review was finalized and marked as complete.']
  review_cycle_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year in which the performance review cycle occurred.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Target or deadline date by which the performance review should be completed.']
  review_language varchar [note: 'Language in which the performance review was conducted and documented, using ISO 639-2 three-letter codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ENG|SPA|FRA|DEU|ZHO|JPN|POR — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  review_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the performance evaluation period being assessed.']
  review_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the performance evaluation period being assessed.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the performance review (draft, in progress, submitted, manager review, calibration, approved, completed, cancelled). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|in_progress|submitted|manager_review|calibration|approved|completed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  review_template_name varchar [note: 'Name of the performance review template or form used for this evaluation (e.g., standard annual review, leadership review).']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Type or frequency of the performance review cycle (annual, mid-year, probationary, project-based, ad-hoc, quarterly).']
  reviewer_signature_date date [note: 'Date when the reviewing manager signed or approved the performance review.']
  safety_incident_count varchar [note: 'Number of safety incidents or violations recorded for the employee during the review period, relevant for ISO 45001 compliance and manufacturing safety standards.']
  second_level_reviewer_comments varchar [note: 'Comments provided by the second-level reviewer regarding the performance evaluation.']
  succession_plan_candidate boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the employee has been identified as a candidate for succession planning based on performance and potential.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of formal employee performance evaluations conducted on a periodic basis (annual, mid-year, probationary). Captures review period, overall rating, individual goal ratings, competency ratings, manager comments, employee self-assessment, calibration status, and review completion date. Sourced from Workday HCM Performance. Feeds merit increase decisions and talent development planning.'
}

Table workforce.requisition {
  requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the job requisition record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center that will bear the labor cost for this position.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who created the requisition record.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job profile defining role responsibilities, qualifications, and competencies.']
  last_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who last modified the requisition record.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work location or facility where the position will be based.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational department or business unit for this position.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational position this requisition is intended to fill.']
  requisition_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who approved the requisition for opening.']
  requisition_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who is the hiring manager responsible for this requisition.']
  requisition_recruiter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the HR recruiter assigned to manage the candidate pipeline for this requisition.']
  applicant_count varchar [note: 'Total number of candidates who have applied to this requisition.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of the requisition approval workflow indicating whether it has been authorized for recruiting.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the requisition was approved by the authorizing manager or HR.']
  background_check_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a background check is mandatory for candidates selected for this position.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the requisition was closed, either due to being filled or cancelled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the requisition record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for salary and compensation amounts.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Type of employment arrangement for the position being filled.']
  internal_posting_only boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requisition is restricted to internal candidates only.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requisition is currently active and accepting applications.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the requisition record was last updated.']
  number_of_openings varchar [note: 'Total number of positions to be filled under this requisition.']
  opened_date date [note: 'Date when the requisition was officially opened and approved for recruiting.']
  positions_filled varchar [note: 'Number of positions that have been successfully filled under this requisition.']
  posting_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the role, responsibilities, and qualifications for external job postings.']
  posting_end_date date [note: 'Date when the job posting is scheduled to be removed from public visibility.']
  posting_start_date date [note: 'Date when the job posting becomes publicly visible on job boards and career sites.']
  posting_title varchar [note: 'Public-facing job title used in external job postings and advertisements.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this requisition indicating urgency of filling the position.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or reason for opening this requisition, such as growth, attrition, or reorganization.']
  remote_work_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is eligible for remote or hybrid work arrangements.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the requisition, externally visible and used for tracking and communication.']
  requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the requisition indicating its progress through the recruiting workflow.']
  requisition_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the requisition indicating whether it is for a new position, replacement, or other hiring scenario.']
  salary_range_max decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum annual salary or hourly rate for the position in local currency.']
  salary_range_min decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum annual salary or hourly rate for the position in local currency.']
  screening_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether formal candidate screening or assessment is required for this requisition.']
  security_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position requires a security clearance or special authorization.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Type of work shift schedule for the position.']
  sourcing_channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel or method used to source candidates for this requisition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: internal|external|referral|agency|campus|job_board|social_media — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_start_date date [note: 'Desired date for the selected candidate to begin employment.']
  time_to_fill_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from requisition opening to position being filled, used for recruiting KPI tracking.']
  travel_percentage varchar [note: 'Expected percentage of time the position requires business travel.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of approved requests to fill positions through internal transfer or external hiring. Captures requisition number, linked position, job profile, target start date, hiring manager, recruiter assignment, requisition status (open/in-progress/on-hold/filled/cancelled), sourcing channel, and time-to-fill metrics. Includes candidate pipeline tracking: applicant records with source, pipeline stage (applied/screened/interviewed/offered/hired/rejected), assessment results, background check status, and offer details. Sourced from Workday HCM Recruiting. Serves as the SSOT for the full recruiting lifecycle from requisition opening through candidate hire.'
}

Table workforce.labor_agreement {
  labor_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for labor_agreement']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.org_unit. Business justification: Labor agreements are tied to a specific org unit; linking provides context and removes redundant business_unit string.']
  agreement_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the full legal text of the labor agreement. Links to document management system or shared repository.']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or title of the labor agreement. Typically includes union name and location or business unit covered.']
  agreement_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special provisions, or context about the agreement not captured in structured fields.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier for the collective bargaining agreement or labor contract. Used for reference in HR systems, union communications, and legal documentation.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the labor agreement. Indicates whether the agreement is in draft, pending ratification, active, expired, or under renegotiation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_ratification|active|expired|terminated|suspended|under_negotiation — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the labor agreement. Distinguishes between collective bargaining agreements (CBAs), individual contracts, memoranda of understanding, and other labor relations instruments.']
  annual_wage_increase_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Negotiated annual percentage increase in base wages. May be fixed or tied to cost-of-living adjustments (COLA).']
  arbitration_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes binding arbitration as the final step in the grievance process.']
  bargaining_unit_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the bargaining unit or employee group covered by this agreement. Used for payroll, benefits administration, and labor cost allocation.']
  base_wage_scale_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the wage scale or pay table defined in the agreement. Links to detailed wage schedules by job classification and seniority.']
  cola_provision_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes a cost-of-living adjustment provision that ties wage increases to inflation indices.']
  covered_employee_group varchar [note: 'Description of the employee population covered by this agreement. May reference job families, locations, business units, or other grouping criteria.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this labor agreement record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the labor agreement expires or terminates. Null for open-ended agreements or those without a defined end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the labor agreement becomes binding and enforceable. Marks the beginning of the agreement term.']
  grievance_procedure_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of the multi-step grievance and arbitration process defined in the agreement for resolving disputes between management and union.']
  health_benefits_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of health insurance, medical, dental, and vision benefits provided under the agreement, including employer contribution levels.']
  job_security_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes job security provisions such as no-layoff guarantees, advance notice requirements, or severance terms.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this labor agreement record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  lead_negotiator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary management representative who led negotiations for this agreement. Used for accountability and historical reference.']
  management_rights_clause varchar [note: 'Text or summary of the management rights clause, which reserves certain decision-making authority to the employer outside the scope of collective bargaining.']
  negotiation_completion_date date [note: 'Date when negotiations concluded and a tentative agreement was reached, prior to ratification.']
  negotiation_start_date date [note: 'Date when formal negotiations for this agreement began. Used for tracking negotiation timelines and labor relations history.']
  no_strike_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes a no-strike provision prohibiting work stoppages during the term of the contract.']
  overtime_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base wage for overtime hours. Common values include 1.5 for time-and-a-half and 2.0 for double-time.']
  paid_holidays_count varchar [note: 'Number of paid holidays per year guaranteed under the agreement. Supports benefits administration and labor cost planning.']
  pension_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the pension or retirement plan covered by this agreement. May link to defined benefit or defined contribution plan documents.']
  plant_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing plant or facility where this agreement applies. Supports multi-site labor relations management.']
  ratification_approval_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of votes in favor of ratifying the agreement. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 87.50 for 87.5%).']
  ratification_date date [note: 'Date when the agreement was formally ratified by union membership vote or other approval process. Critical for legal enforceability.']
  ratification_vote_count varchar [note: 'Total number of votes cast in the ratification vote. Used for transparency and compliance documentation.']
  reopener_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes a reopener clause allowing renegotiation of specific terms (e.g., wages) before the contract expiration date.']
  safety_provisions_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of occupational health and safety provisions, including personal protective equipment (PPE) requirements, safety committee structure, and hazard reporting procedures.']
  seniority_rule_description varchar [note: 'Description of seniority provisions governing layoffs, recalls, promotions, shift bidding, and vacation scheduling as defined in the agreement.']
  shift_differential_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional compensation per hour for non-standard shifts (e.g., night shift, weekend shift) as defined in the agreement.']
  subcontracting_restrictions varchar [note: 'Description of any restrictions on the employers ability to subcontract work covered by the bargaining unit, protecting union jobs.']
  successor_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the agreement includes a successor clause requiring a new owner to honor the existing labor agreement in the event of a business sale or merger.']
  union_dues_deduction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employer is required to deduct union dues from employee paychecks and remit them to the union (checkoff provision).']
  union_local_number varchar [note: 'Local chapter or branch number of the union. Used to identify the specific union local that negotiated and is covered by this agreement.']
  union_name varchar [note: 'Name of the labor union or bargaining unit that is party to this agreement. Null for individual labor agreements not involving a union.']
  union_security_clause_type varchar [note: 'Type of union security provision in the agreement. Defines whether union membership or dues payment is required as a condition of employment.']
  vacation_accrual_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rate at which vacation time accrues per pay period or year of service, as defined in the agreement. Expressed in hours per year or similar unit.']
