  Note: 'Master record of collective bargaining agreements (CBAs), union contracts, and individual labor agreements governing employment terms for specific worker groups in manufacturing plants. Captures agreement name, union/bargaining unit, covered employee group, effective date range, key terms (wage scales, overtime rules, seniority provisions, grievance procedures), and ratification status. Distinct from procurement contracts — this is the HR-owned labor relations record.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_period {
  payroll_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payroll_period']
  previous_payroll_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payroll_period (previous_payroll_period_id)']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payroll period record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for payroll amounts in this period.']
  description varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text description of the payroll period.']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in the payroll period.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date covered by the payroll period.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter (1‑4) for the payroll period.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the payroll period belongs.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is the active payroll period.']
  lock_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payroll period was locked for changes.']
  payroll_cycle_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal number of the payroll cycle within the year (e.g., 1 for first weekly cycle).']
  payroll_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which payroll is processed for this period.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'External code representing the payroll period, typically in YYYYMM format.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the payroll period (e.g., "January 2024 Payroll").']
  period_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the period within the fiscal year.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Category of the payroll period indicating its purpose.']
  processed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when payroll calculations were completed for the period.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar date covered by the payroll period.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payroll period record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payroll_period. Referenced by payroll_period_id.'
}

Table workforce.shift_pattern {
  shift_pattern_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for shift_pattern']
  rotation_shift_pattern_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on shift_pattern (rotation_shift_pattern_id)']
  break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total allotted break time within the shift, expressed in minutes.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the shift pattern, used in scheduling and reporting.']
  compliance_iso45001 boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shift pattern complies with ISO 45001 occupational health and safety standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shift pattern record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description providing details about the shift pattern.']
  duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total length of the shift pattern expressed in hours.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the shift pattern becomes valid for scheduling.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the shift pattern expires; null if open‑ended.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this pattern is the default for new employees or locations.']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Category of labor to which the shift pattern applies.']
  max_consecutive_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days an employee may work consecutively under this pattern.']
  min_rest_hours decimal(4,2) [note: 'Minimum required rest period between the end of one shift and the start of the next, expressed in hours.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the shift pattern.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks about the shift pattern.']
  overtime_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates if overtime work is permitted under this shift pattern.']
  pattern_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift pattern based on its scheduling logic.']
  shift_end_time varchar [note: 'Scheduled end time of the shift within a day (24‑hour format).']
  shift_start_time varchar [note: 'Scheduled start time of the shift within a day (24‑hour format).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shift pattern.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shift pattern record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for shift_pattern. Referenced by shift_pattern_id.'
}

Table workforce.certification_type {
  certification_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for certification_type']
  parent_certification_type_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on certification_type (parent_certification_type_id)']
  applicable_job_roles varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of job role identifiers to which the certification applies.']
  category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the certification (e.g., safety, quality, environmental, skill, regulatory).']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to reference the certification type in systems and reports.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Text describing regulatory or internal compliance obligations tied to the certification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certification type record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the certification purpose, scope, and applicability.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the certification type becomes effective for use.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Optional date after which the certification type is no longer valid for new assignments.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used in external systems (e.g., ISO catalog) to reference the certification.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the certification is mandatory for the associated job roles.']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'Organization or authority that issues and governs the certification.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the certification type definition was last reviewed for relevance or updates.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the certification type (e.g., "ISO 45001 Safety Certification").']
  renewal_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between required renewals when renewal_required is true.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certification must be renewed periodically.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification type.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification type record.']
  validity_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months the certification remains valid from the effective date.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for changes to the certification type definition.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for certification_type. Referenced by type_id.'
}

Table finance.cost_center {
  cost_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the cost center.']
  parent_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent cost center in the hierarchy.']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual costs incurred by the cost center.']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate costs from this center to other entities.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned budget allocated to the cost center for the fiscal period.']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'Code of the controlling area to which the cost center belongs.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Business key used to identify the cost center in financial transactions.']
  cost_center_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the cost center\'s purpose and scope.']
  cost_center_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of cost centers for reporting or allocation purposes.']
  cost_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the cost center.']
  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center.']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost center by functional area.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost center record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency in which the cost center\'s financials are recorded.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier used to map the cost center to external systems or legacy codes.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Depth of the cost center within the organizational hierarchy.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Slash‑delimited path representing the cost center\'s position in the hierarchy (e.g., "/100/200/300").']
  is_overhead boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost center is used for overhead cost allocation.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Code of the physical location or plant associated with the cost center.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department that owns the cost center.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cost center record.']
  variance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Difference between budgeted and actual costs.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date when the cost center becomes effective.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date when the cost center is retired or becomes inactive.']

  Note: 'Master record for all SAP CO cost centers representing organizational units responsible for incurring costs. Captures cost center hierarchy, responsible manager, controlling area assignment, profit center assignment, currency, validity period, cost center category (production, administration, R&D, sales), and standard hierarchy position. Serves as the SSOT for internal cost allocation, overhead absorption, and management accounting structures. The primary controlling object for budget assignment, actual cost collection, and variance analysis in manufacturing operations.'
}

Table finance.profit_center {
  profit_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the profit center.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Link to the primary cost center that supplies cost data for this profit center.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  parent_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent profit center in the hierarchy, if any.']
  actual_profit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Realized profit for the profit center in the reporting period.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Approved budget amount for the profit center for the fiscal period.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Code of the controlling area to which the profit center belongs for internal cost accounting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the profit center record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code in which the profit center records its functional amounts.']
  financial_reporting_line varchar [note: 'Line of financial reporting hierarchy (e.g., segment, division, business unit) used for IFRS segment reporting.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level indicating the profit center\'s depth in the organizational hierarchy.']
  is_reportable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the profit center is included in statutory and management reporting.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent strategic review of the profit center.']
  legal_entity_code varchar [note: 'Code of the legal entity that owns the profit center, aligning with statutory reporting.']
  oee_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness percentage assigned to the profit center for performance benchmarking.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Business unit or individual accountable for the profit center\'s performance.']
  planned_profit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Profit amount planned for the profit center at the start of the reporting period.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the profit center, often used in financial postings.']
  profit_center_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context about the profit center\'s purpose and scope.']
  profit_center_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping name used for consolidated reporting across multiple profit centers.']
  profit_center_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the profit center used in reports and dashboards.']
  profit_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the profit center.']
  profit_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification indicating whether the profit center is internal, external, or a joint‑venture entity.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the profit center primarily operates.']
  reporting_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency code used for external financial reporting of the profit center.']
  segment varchar [note: 'High‑level business segment classification for the profit center.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction applicable to the profit center.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the profit center record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the profit center record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the profit center record.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date on which the profit center becomes effective for reporting.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date on which the profit center ceases to be effective; null if still active.']

  Note: 'Master record for profit centers representing autonomous business segments within the manufacturing enterprise used for internal profitability reporting. Captures profit center hierarchy, controlling area, responsible manager, segment classification, currency, and validity dates. Enables P&L reporting at sub-entity level per SAP EC-PCA (Profit Center Accounting) aligned with IFRS segment reporting.'
}

Table finance.gl_account {
  gl_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the GL account record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: GL account may be assigned to a cost center for reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: GL account may be assigned to a profit center for reporting.']
  account_category varchar [note: 'Business‑level categorisation used for reporting and analysis.']
  account_group varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping code that aggregates multiple GL accounts for reporting.']
  account_long_description varchar [note: 'Extended description providing additional context for reporting and audit.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the purpose of the GL account.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the GL account, used in postings and reporting.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the account by financial statement section.']
  balance_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account is a natural debit or credit account.']
  consolidation_group varchar [note: 'Identifier of the consolidation group for legal entity roll‑up.']
  cost_element_category varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account is linked to a primary or secondary cost element for CO integration.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the GL account record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter code of the currency in which the account is denominated.']
  current_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Running balance reflecting all posted transactions to date.']
  external_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source ERP or external system that created the account.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'Organisational functional area to which the account belongs (e.g., Manufacturing, R&D, Sales).']
  gl_account_description varchar [note: 'Long textual description of the account purpose and usage.']
  gl_account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the account.']
  is_bank_account boolean [note: 'True if the account is linked to a bank account for cash management.']
  is_budget_enabled boolean [note: 'True if the account participates in budgeting processes.']
  is_cash_account boolean [note: 'True if the account represents cash or cash equivalents.']
  is_deprecated boolean [note: 'True if the account is scheduled for retirement and should no longer be used for new postings.']
  is_expense_account boolean [note: 'True if the account is used for operating expense postings.']
  is_fixed_asset_account boolean [note: 'True if the account is used for fixed‑asset accounting.']
  is_forecast_enabled boolean [note: 'True if the account is included in financial forecasting.']
  is_income_account boolean [note: 'True if the account records income other than sales.']
  is_intercompany boolean [note: 'True if the account is used for intercompany transactions.']
  is_inventory_account boolean [note: 'True if the account tracks inventory valuation.']
  is_purchase_account boolean [note: 'True if the account records purchase expenses.']
  is_reconciliation_account boolean [note: 'True if the account is used for automatic reconciliation of sub‑ledger postings.']
  is_sales_account boolean [note: 'True if the account records sales revenue.']
  is_tax_relevant boolean [note: 'True if the account is subject to tax reporting requirements.']
  opening_balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Balance of the account at the start of the fiscal year.']
  reporting_currency varchar [note: 'Currency used for consolidated financial reporting.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Business segment code (e.g., Industrial, Energy, Services) for segment reporting.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification applied to postings against this account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the GL account record.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which the account becomes effective for posting.']
  valid_to date [note: 'Date after which the account is no longer valid for posting (nullable).']

  Note: 'General ledger account master record serving as the SSOT for the chart of accounts used across all financial postings. Captures account number, account type (balance sheet, P&L), account group, reconciliation account flag, currency, tax category, field status group, cost element category (primary/secondary for CO integration), functional area, and IFRS/GAAP classification. Bridges financial accounting and management accounting by carrying cost element attributes that enable controlling postings. Governs all financial postings and is the backbone of the enterprise chart of accounts.'
}

Table finance.company_code {
  company_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate key for company code record.']
  business_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Business Partner master record representing the legal entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Company code is associated with a cost center for controlling.']
  annual_revenue_local_currency decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual revenue reported in the entity\'s local currency.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to audit trail records for changes to this company code.']
  chart_of_accounts varchar [note: 'Chart of accounts key linked to the company code.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier for the legal entity as defined in SAP.']

  company_code_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the company code.']
  consolidation_group varchar [note: 'Group identifier for financial consolidation.']
  consolidation_method varchar [note: 'Method used for consolidation (e.g., full, proportional).']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Controlling area assigned for cost accounting.']
  country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the legal entity is registered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the company code record was created in the source system.']
  currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for the legal entity\'s financial reporting.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the company code ceases to be effective, if applicable.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the company code becomes effective.']
  elimination_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of intercompany eliminations for the company code.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'Fiscal year variant assigned to the company code for period accounting.']
  headquarter_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the legal entity\'s headquarters address.']
  headquarter_city varchar [note: 'City of the legal entity\'s headquarters.']
  headquarter_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code of the headquarters address.']
  headquarter_state varchar [note: 'State or province of the headquarters.']
  industry_classification_code varchar [note: 'Industry classification code (e.g., NAICS) for the entity.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the company code is included in group consolidation.']
  legal_form varchar [note: 'Legal form of the entity (e.g., corporation, LLC).']
  number_of_employees varchar [note: 'Total number of employees for the legal entity.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for official communications with the legal entity.']
  public_company_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the entity is publicly listed.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration number of the entity with the corporate registry.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Business segment classification for reporting.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the entity is tax-exempt.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (e.g., VAT, EIN) for the legal entity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the company code record.']

  Note: 'Master record for SAP FI company codes representing legally independent entities within the manufacturing group. Captures company code identifier, company name, country, currency, fiscal year variant, chart of accounts assignment, controlling area assignment, consolidation group, consolidation method, and elimination scope. Serves as the organizational anchor for all financial postings, statutory reporting under IFRS/GAAP, and the consolidation unit for group financial statement preparation.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the journal entry record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Journal entry references a cost center; replace code with FK.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Journal entry can be linked to an internal order for cost tracking.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ledger (e.g., primary, secondary) where the entry is posted.']
  posted_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Audit requirement to trace each journal entry to the employee who posted it.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Journal entry references a profit center; replace code with FK.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit log entry for compliance tracking.']
  batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the processing batch for mass postings.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'Higher‑level business area used in financial reporting.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the journal entry record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the transaction currency.']
  document_date date [note: 'Date on the original source document (e.g., invoice date).']
  document_header_text varchar [note: 'Header description of the journal document.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the journal entry, typically the SAP document number.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Category of the journal entry indicating its purpose (e.g., invoice, credit note).']
  entry_date date [note: 'Date when the journal entry was entered into the system.']
  exchange_rate decimal(15,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert transaction currency to local company currency.']
  exchange_rate_type varchar [note: 'Indicator of the exchange rate calculation method (e.g., average, spot).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Numeric period (e.g., month) within the fiscal year for the posting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit fiscal year to which the posting belongs.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'Variant of the fiscal year used for special reporting calendars.']
  gaap_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the posting complies with local GAAP reporting standards.']
  ifrs_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the posting complies with IFRS reporting standards.']
  is_adjusted boolean [note: 'True if the entry represents an adjustment rather than a primary posting.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of the posting converted to the company’s local currency.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount (debits minus credits) of the journal entry.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the journal entry was posted to the general ledger.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'SAP posting key indicating debit or credit nature of the line.']
  posting_period varchar [note: 'Period identifier used for period‑end closing.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the journal entry.']
  posting_text varchar [note: 'Free‑form text entered by the user describing the posting.']
  posting_time timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the entry was posted to the ledger.']
  reference_document varchar [note: 'External document number referenced by this journal entry (e.g., purchase order).']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the original entry that this entry reverses.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this entry reverses a previous posting.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Segment classification for reporting (e.g., Industrial, Services).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source ERP/system that generated the journal entry (e.g., SAP FI).']
  tax_amount_total decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate tax amount calculated for the posting.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applicable to the posting.']
  total_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of credit line amounts for the journal entry in local currency.']
  total_debit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of debit line amounts for the journal entry in local currency.']
  transaction_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of the posting in the transaction currency.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the journal entry.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'WBS element reference for project accounting.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record capturing all general ledger postings at both document header and line item level, serving as the single source of truth for the complete financial audit trail. Header attributes include document number, document type, posting date, fiscal year, period, company code, currency, exchange rate, reference document, reversal indicator, and IFRS/GAAP compliance flags. Line item attributes include line number, GL account, debit/credit indicator, amount in transaction and local currencies, cost center, profit center, WBS element reference, internal order, tax code, assignment field, and posting text. Covers manual journal entries, automated system postings, accruals, reversals, reclassifications, and period-end closing entries. Foundation for trial balance, financial statements, reconciliation, and audit evidence.'
}

Table finance.finance_budget {
  finance_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the budget record.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.obligation. Business justification: Budgets are planned for specific compliance obligations; linking enables obligation‑level budget tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Budget is planned for a specific cost center; replace code with FK.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager responsible for the budget.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Budget can be linked to a GL account for accounting entries.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Budget may be tied to an internal order for project cost tracking.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Budget is planned for a specific profit center; replace code with FK.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project associated with the budget, if any.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the budget was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the budget.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the budget.']
  budget_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the budget, used for reference and reporting.']
  budget_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the budget.']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget version: original, revised, or supplemental.']
  cost_element varchar [note: 'Cost element classification for budgeting purposes.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for budget amounts.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code of the department owning the budget.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the budget expires or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the budget becomes effective.']
  finance_budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit fiscal year to which the budget applies.']
  forecast_method varchar [note: 'Method used to generate forecasted amounts within the budget.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the budget is currently active.']
  is_capex boolean [note: 'Indicates if the budget pertains to capital expenditures.']
  is_opex boolean [note: 'Indicates if the budget pertains to operational expenditures.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent budget review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the budget.']
  period varchar [note: 'Reporting period covered by the budget (quarterly or annual).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget record was first captured in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the budget record.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code for the budget.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency of budget review cycles.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of the budget data (e.g., SAP S/4HANA).']
  total_committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate amount that has been committed (e.g., purchase orders).']
  total_planned_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate planned amount for the budget across all periods and categories.']
  total_revised_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate revised amount after adjustments.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last updated the budget record.']
  variance_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable variance percentage between planned and actual amounts.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the budget.']
  version_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this budget version was created or released.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the budget record.']

  Note: 'Budget planning and tracking records capturing both header-level governance and period-level line item detail for all controlling objects (cost centers, profit centers, internal orders). Header captures budget version, fiscal year, budget type (original, revised, supplemental), approval workflow status, responsible manager, and total planned amounts. Line items capture controlling object assignment, GL account, cost element, fiscal period, planned amount, revised amount, committed amount, currency, and budget category (CapEx, OpEx, headcount, materials). Supports FP&A variance analysis (plan vs actual vs forecast), budget-to-actual reporting, rolling forecast integration, budget transfer workflows, and CapEx/OpEx governance across the manufacturing enterprise.'
}

Table finance.internal_order {
  internal_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the internal order record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who is accountable for the internal order execution.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which the internal order is linked, if applicable.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total costs posted to the internal order to date.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the internal order.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned budget allocated to the internal order.']
  capex_flag boolean [note: 'True if the internal order is classified as capital expenditure.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Costs that have been committed (e.g., purchase orders) but not yet posted.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Controlling area that governs the internal order\'s cost accounting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center responsible for the costs recorded against the internal order.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the internal order record was first captured.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for monetary values.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to an external system\'s identifier (e.g., ERP, project system).']
  final_closure_date date [note: 'Date when the internal order was finally closed and settled.']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'Business functional area responsible for the internal order.']
  internal_order_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the purpose, scope, or details of the internal order.']
  internal_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the internal order.']
  opex_flag boolean [note: 'True if the internal order is classified as operating expenditure.']
  order_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the internal order was initially created in the business process.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the internal order, used in finance and controlling processes.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the internal order indicating its purpose such as maintenance, capital project, research, or service.']
  planned_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted costs for the internal order based on planning.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the profit center to which the internal order\'s results are allocated.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the internal order has been released for posting.']
  responsible_cost_center varchar [note: 'Cost center assigned as the primary owner for budgeting and settlement of the internal order.']
  settlement_rule varchar [note: 'Rule that defines how costs of the internal order are settled to receivers (e.g., cost centers, projects).']
  technical_completion_date date [note: 'Date when technical completion of the order was confirmed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the internal order record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between budgeted and actual costs.']
  variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between budgeted and actual costs.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'Work Breakdown Structure element code for detailed cost tracking.']

  Note: 'SAP CO internal order master records used to track costs for specific manufacturing projects, capital investments, maintenance activities, or R&D initiatives. Captures order number, order type, description, responsible cost center, profit center, controlling area, settlement rule, budget, actual costs, commitment amounts, status (created, released, technically completed, closed), and CapEx/OpEx classification.'
}

Table finance.cost_allocation {
  cost_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each cost allocation record.']
  allocation_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the allocation run or cycle to which this record belongs.']
  allocation_rule_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the allocation rule applied.']
  cost_object_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost object associated with the allocation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created or last modified the allocation record.']
  primary_sender_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Cost center from which the cost is allocated.']
  receiver_internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Internal order receiving the allocated cost, if applicable.']
  receiver_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Profit center receiving the allocated cost, if applicable.']
  sender_cost_element_id varchar [note: 'Cost element associated with the sender cost center.']
  statistical_key_figure_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the statistical key figure used for allocation, if method is statistical.']
  allocation_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount allocated from sender to receiver.']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'Key figure or driver used as basis for variable or statistical allocation.']
  allocation_category varchar [note: 'Business category of the allocation (e.g., overhead, direct, indirect).']
  allocation_date date [note: 'Date on which the allocation was posted.']
  allocation_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description or notes about the allocation.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the allocation (fixed percentage, variable key, or statistical key figure).']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage used when allocation method is fixed percentage.']
  allocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or justification for the allocation.']
  allocation_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the allocation entry.']
  allocation_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the allocation record for change tracking.']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost center type.']
  cost_object_type varchar [note: 'Type of cost object linked to the allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the allocation amount.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date when the allocation becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date when the allocation ceases to be effective, if applicable.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal month period (01-12) of the allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 01|02|03|04|05|06|07|08|09|10|11|12 — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year of the allocation period.']
  internal_order_type varchar [note: 'Type of internal order receiving the allocation.']
  is_manual_allocation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allocation was entered manually (true) or generated automatically (false).']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number of the allocation line within the allocation cycle.']
  posted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allocation was posted to the ledger.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current posting status of the allocation line.']
  profit_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the profit center type.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this allocation line reverses a previous allocation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the allocation originated (e.g., SAP CO).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the allocation record.']
  variable_key_figure_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the variable key figure used for allocation, if method is variable.']

  Note: 'Transactional records capturing periodic cost allocation, distribution, and assessment cycles executed in SAP CO. Captures allocation cycle, sender cost center/cost element, receiver cost center/profit center/internal order, allocation method (fixed percentage, variable key, statistical key figure), allocation amount, fiscal period, and posting status. Supports overhead absorption, indirect cost distribution, and internal service recharging across manufacturing business units. Drives management accounting accuracy for product costing and profitability analysis.'
}

Table finance.capex_request {
  capex_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the capital expenditure request.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Capex request is associated with a cost center for cost allocation.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Capex request may be linked to an internal order representing the project.']
  primary_capex_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual who last approved or rejected the request.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Capital‑expenditure requests are tied to a specific project for tracking spend against project budgets.']
  requester_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who submitted the CapEx request.']
  approval_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the request was approved or rejected.']
  approval_stage varchar [note: 'Current approval tier the request is pending (e.g., manager, finance, executive).']
  asset_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the asset to be acquired (e.g., equipment, facility, automation system).']
  budget_line_item varchar [note: 'Specific budget line or account code to which the request will be charged.']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative explaining the strategic and financial rationale for the investment.']
  capitalized_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the asset will be capitalized on the balance sheet.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal compliance constraints applicable to the request.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CapEx request record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the estimated amount.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Accounting method used to depreciate the asset.']
  depreciation_years varchar [note: 'Number of years over which the asset will be depreciated.']
  estimated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected total cost of the capital investment before approval.']
  expected_roi_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected ROI expressed as a percentage of the estimated investment.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Origin of funds for the investment (e.g., internal budget, external financing, lease).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the latest update.']
  needed_by_date date [note: 'Target date by which the requested asset must be available.']
  payback_period_months varchar [note: 'Estimated number of months required to recoup the investment.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the request.']
  project_end_date date [note: 'Planned completion date for the capital project.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the project or investment initiative.']
  project_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for the capital project.']
  regulatory_approval_needed boolean [note: 'True if external regulatory sign‑off is required before proceeding.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date the CapEx request was initially submitted.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the CapEx request.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the CapEx request through its approval workflow.']
  requesting_department varchar [note: 'Department that originated the CapEx request.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of financial and operational risk.']
  tax_implications varchar [note: 'Notes on tax treatment or benefits associated with the investment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the request record.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'WBS element that groups the request within project accounting.']

  Note: 'Capital expenditure request and approval records governing CapEx investment decisions for manufacturing equipment, facilities, automation systems, and infrastructure. Captures request number, project description, asset category, requesting cost center, business justification, estimated investment amount, expected ROI, payback period, approval workflow status, approver hierarchy, approval dates, and linkage to internal order or WBS element.'
}

Table finance.ap_invoice {
  ap_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the accounts payable invoice record.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.company_code']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: AP invoice belongs to a cost center; replace string code with FK for referential integrity.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: AP invoice belongs to a profit center; replace string code with FK.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the vendor that issued the invoice.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the invoice approval workflow.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the invoice was approved.']
  approval_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the invoice.']
  bank_statement_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of matching the payment against bank statement entries.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'Date used as the starting point for payment terms calculation.']
  cash_discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied when payment is made within discount period.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the accounting document that clears the invoice.']
  currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the invoice amounts.']
  discount_taken decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of any cash discount applied.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'External invoice number assigned by the vendor or the organization.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which the invoice must be paid according to payment terms.']
  goods_receipt_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the goods receipt confirming receipt of materials.']
  gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes, discounts, and withholdings.']
  house_bank_account varchar [note: 'Bank account from which the payment will be issued.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the vendor issued the invoice.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice (e.g., standard invoice, credit memo).']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount payable after taxes, discounts, and withholdings.']
  payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount actually paid for the invoice.']
  payment_block_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why payment processing is blocked for this invoice.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment was actually made.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to settle the invoice.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number provided by the bank for the payment transaction.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment execution.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard terms defining when payment is due (e.g., Net 30, 2% 10 Net 30).']
  payment_value_date date [note: 'Date on which the payment amount is considered effective.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date the invoice was posted to the general ledger.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order that triggered the invoice.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the invoice record.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax calculated for the invoice.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code that determines the tax rate and calculation rules applied.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice is exempt from tax.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax percentage for the invoice.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Result of PO/GR/IR verification: matched, unmatched, or partially matched.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of withholding tax deducted at source.']
  withholding_tax_code varchar [note: 'Code defining the withholding tax rate and rules.']

  Note: 'Accounts payable full lifecycle records capturing vendor invoices and their associated payment execution processed as a unified payables document. Invoice attributes include document number, vendor, company code, invoice date, posting date, baseline date, payment terms, gross amount, tax amount, net amount, three-way match status (PO/GR/IR verification), payment block reason, and approval workflow status. Payment attributes include payment method (ACH, wire, check, direct debit), house bank account, value date, payment amount, discount taken, cash discount percentage, clearing document reference, payment run identifier, and bank statement reconciliation status. Covers the complete payables cycle from invoice receipt through three-way matching, approval, payment proposal, payment execution, and bank clearing. Serves as the SSOT for all vendor obligations, payment commitments, and outgoing cash flows.'
}

Table finance.ar_item {
  ar_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the AR line item record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: AR item is posted to a cost center; replace string code with FK.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the customer party to whom the invoice is issued.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal employee responsible for collections on this item.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: AR item is posted to a profit center; replace string code with FK.']
  aging_bucket varchar [note: 'Categorization of the outstanding amount based on days past due.']
  aging_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the invoice is overdue.']
  business_area varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of profit centers for consolidated reporting.']
  cleared_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the AR item has been fully settled.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the payment or credit memo that cleared this AR item.']
  collection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the collection effort for the AR item.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Four‑character code identifying the legal entity within the enterprise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the AR line item record was first created in the system.']
  credit_memo_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of credit memos applied to reduce the invoice balance.']
  credit_memo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a credit memo has been issued against this AR item.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the transaction currency.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cash discount taken on the invoice.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any dispute raised against the invoice.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'External document number assigned by SAP FI for the AR transaction.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is expected according to payment terms.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current dunning step indicating the severity of overdue collection.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert the invoice amount to the company code\'s local currency.']
  invoice_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gross amount of the invoice in the document currency before discounts, taxes, or adjustments.']
  invoice_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of goods or services billed.']
  last_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of the most recent payment applied to this AR item.']
  last_payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment method used for the most recent payment.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Invoice amount expressed in the company code\'s functional currency.']
  material_number varchar [note: 'SAP material identifier for the product sold.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Invoice amount after discounts and taxes, representing the amount due.']
  open_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining amount still outstanding after payments and adjustments.']
  original_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Original gross amount at invoice creation before any adjustments.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the invoice was fully or partially paid.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Means by which the customer remitted payment.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier supplied by the customer for the payment transaction.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition (e.g., NET30, 2% 10 NET30).']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date the AR document was posted to the general ledger.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the AR item indicating its processing stage.']
  sales_order_number varchar [note: 'Sales order that generated the invoice.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Business segment classification for reporting (e.g., Industrial, Services).']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax component of the invoice calculated using the applicable tax code.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code that determines tax rate and calculation rules for the invoice.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax percentage derived from the tax code.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the AR line item.']
  write_off_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount that has been written off for this AR item.']
  write_off_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the remaining balance has been written off as uncollectible.']

  Note: 'Accounts receivable open item and cleared item records from SAP FI-AR tracking customer receivables arising from product sales and service billings. Captures AR document number, customer account, company code, posting date, due date, invoice amount, currency, payment terms, dunning level, clearing document, aging bucket, and dispute status. Serves as the SSOT for customer receivables aging and cash collection tracking.'
}

Table finance.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each fixed asset record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fixed asset is assigned to a cost center for depreciation tracking.']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation expense recorded to date.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original cost incurred to acquire the asset, recorded in functional currency.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the asset was acquired or placed into service.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the asset for reporting and depreciation policy.']
  asset_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the asset, including purpose and key characteristics.']
  asset_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the asset.']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'External asset identifier used in accounting and operations, often the tag or barcode.']
  asset_origin varchar [note: 'Source of the asset acquisition.']
  capitalized_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost has been capitalized as a fixed asset.']
  condition_rating varchar [note: 'Current physical condition of the asset as assessed by maintenance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the asset record was first created in the system.']
  department_responsible varchar [note: 'Department that owns or manages the asset.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate periodic depreciation expense.']
  depreciation_start_date date [note: 'Date when depreciation calculations begin for the asset.']
  fixed_asset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the asset.']
  insurance_coverage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary amount the policy will pay for the asset.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the insurance coverage ends.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the insurance policy covering the asset.']
  insurance_provider varchar [note: 'Company that provides the insurance coverage.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance activity.']
  lease_contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the lease agreement.']
  lease_end_date date [note: 'Date when a leased asset\'s contract expires.']
  lease_provider varchar [note: 'Company that provides the leased asset.']
  maintenance_contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the maintenance service agreement.']
  maintenance_provider varchar [note: 'Company or internal unit responsible for scheduled maintenance.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that produced the asset.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer\'s model identifier for the asset.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Asset\'s carrying amount after subtracting accumulated depreciation.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled maintenance.']
  plant varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant or site where the asset is located.']
  replacement_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated cost to replace the asset with a similar new one.']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated residual value of the asset at the end of its useful life.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer.']
  tax_accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation expense recorded for tax purposes.']
  tax_depreciation_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Depreciation rate applied for tax reporting purposes, expressed as a percentage.']
  tax_net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Asset\'s tax book value after tax depreciation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the asset record.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated number of years the asset will generate economic benefits.']
  warranty_end_date date [note: 'Date when the asset warranty expires.']
  warranty_provider varchar [note: 'Entity that provides the warranty coverage.']
  warranty_start_date date [note: 'Date when the asset warranty becomes effective.']

  Note: 'Fixed asset register and complete transaction history serving as the lifecycle record for all capitalized assets of the manufacturing enterprise including production machinery, CNC equipment, robotics, automation systems, buildings, vehicles, tooling, and IT infrastructure. Master data captures asset number, asset class, description, serial number, acquisition date and value, useful life, depreciation method/key, salvage value, location/plant assignment, and cost center assignment. Transaction history captures all asset movements: acquisitions, retirements, transfers, revaluations, impairments, and periodic depreciation postings with transaction type, posting date, amount, document reference, and depreciation area (book/tax/IFRS). Provides accumulated depreciation, net book value, replacement value, and insurance value. The SSOT for asset valuation, depreciation scheduling, capital base reporting, and insurance declarations.'
}

Table finance.financial_plan {
  financial_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the financial plan record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Financial plan is built for a cost center; replace code with FK.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for the plan.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Financial plan is built for a profit center; replace code with FK.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the plan.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the plan.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the plan was approved.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'Category of budget (e.g., operational, capital) associated with the plan.']
  capex_plan_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned capital expenditures for the plan horizon.']
  classification varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the plan for reporting and governance purposes.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Internal code identifying the legal entity or company within the enterprise.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the plan complies with internal and external financial regulations.']
  consolidation_level varchar [note: 'Level at which the plan is consolidated (e.g., company, region, global).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the plan record was initially created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts in the plan.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system from which the plan data originates (e.g., SAP FI/CO).']
  ebitda_target_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization for the plan.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date on which the financial plan becomes effective and binding for the organization.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the financial plan expires; null for open‑ended plans.']
  external_audit_flag boolean [note: 'True if the plan has been subject to an external audit.']
  financial_year_end date [note: 'End date of the financial year for the plan.']
  financial_year_start date [note: 'Start date of the financial year for the plan.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the plan primarily relates.']
  headcount_plan varchar [note: 'Projected number of full‑time equivalents (FTEs) to be employed under the plan.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the plan is a consolidated view across multiple entities.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the plan record.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of the plan.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the plan, indicating whether it is in draft, active, suspended, closed, or archived.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next formal review of the plan.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for additional comments or explanations.']
  opex_plan_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned operating expenditures for the plan horizon.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the financial plan for easy identification by business users.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the financial plan, used for external reference and tracking.']
  plan_owner_department varchar [note: 'Department to which the plan owner belongs.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the plan indicating its purpose, such as annual operating plan, rolling forecast, or long‑range plan.']
  plan_version_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of changes introduced in this version of the plan.']
  planning_horizon_years varchar [note: 'Number of years covered by the plan horizon.']
  revenue_plan_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned revenue amount for the period covered by the plan.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of financial risk associated with the plan.']
  scenario_name varchar [note: 'Name of the planning scenario (e.g., best case, worst case).']
  sensitivity_analysis_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether sensitivity analysis has been performed for the plan.']
  total_plan_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary amount of all planned financial components.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the plan record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the plan record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the plan record.']

  Note: 'Financial planning and analysis (FP&A) plan records capturing multi-year financial plans, rolling forecasts, and scenario models for the manufacturing enterprise. Captures plan version, plan type (annual operating plan, rolling forecast, long-range plan), fiscal year, planning horizon, company code, profit center, revenue plan, EBITDA target, CapEx plan, OpEx plan, headcount plan, and approval status. Supports management reporting and board-level financial governance.'
}

Table finance.intercompany_transaction {
  intercompany_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the intercompany transaction record.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory and internal controls need the approving employee linked to each intercompany transaction.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Intercompany transaction is charged to a cost center; replace code with FK.']
  intercompany_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master agreement governing the transaction.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Invoice identifier that documents the intercompany billing.']
  order_header_id varchar [note: 'Sales or production order identifier associated with the transaction.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Intercompany transaction is charged to a profit center; replace code with FK.']
  related_project_project_header_id varchar [note: 'Project identifier linked to the transaction, if applicable.']
  reversal_transaction_intercompany_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original transaction that this record reverses.']
  amount_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the transaction amounts.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes, fees, and adjustments.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount after tax and adjustments; the amount to be settled.']
  amount_tax decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount applicable to the transaction.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow state of the transaction.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the transaction was approved.']
  consolidation_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (e.g., FY2024Q1) for which the transaction is consolidated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the transaction record was first created in the system.']
  elimination_date date [note: 'Date when the transaction was eliminated during consolidation.']
  elimination_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction should be eliminated in consolidation.']
  elimination_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the elimination process.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert foreign currency amounts to group reporting currency.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the transaction belongs.']
  intercompany_contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract number associated with the transaction.']
  intercompany_transaction_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description or notes about the transaction.']
  intercompany_transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the transaction.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transaction amount expressed in the reporting (group) currency.']
  markup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage markup applied over the base transfer price.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the transaction is posted to the general ledger.']
  posting_period varchar [note: 'Accounting period identifier (e.g., 2024M03) for the posting.']
  receiving_company_code varchar [note: 'Code of the legal entity receiving the intercompany amount.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External document number (e.g., invoice, purchase order) linked to the transaction.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if the transaction is a reversal of a prior transaction.']
  sending_company_code varchar [note: 'Code of the legal entity sending the intercompany amount.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating ERP or application system (e.g., SAP_FI, Oracle_EBS).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated tax amount based on the tax code.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applied to the transaction for tax determination.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the intercompany transaction.']
  transaction_subtype varchar [note: 'Additional categorisation of the transaction type when needed.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the intercompany transaction occurred in the business process.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transaction (e.g., goods transfer, service recharge).']
  transfer_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Unit price applied for the intercompany transfer.']
  transfer_pricing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to determine the intercompany price.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the transaction record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Intercompany transaction records capturing all financial postings between legal entities within the manufacturing group that require elimination during consolidated financial statement preparation. Captures sending/receiving company codes, transaction type (goods transfer, service recharge, management fee, loan, dividend, royalty), amounts, transfer pricing details, markup percentages, elimination status, and consolidation period. Supports IFRS 10 consolidation, transfer pricing documentation, and intercompany reconciliation processes.'
}

Table finance.bank_account {
  bank_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the bank account record.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Bank account is linked to a GL account for cash management.']
  pool_header_bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on bank_account (pool_header_bank_account_id)']
  account_label varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or label for the bank account used in reports and UI.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Primary bank account number used for payments and cash management.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bank account (checking, savings, operating, cash‑pool, loan).']
  balance decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current monetary balance of the account as of the latest reconciliation.']
  bank_account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bank account.']
  bank_address varchar [note: 'Physical address of the bank branch.']
  bank_branch varchar [note: 'Specific branch of the bank where the account is held.']
  bank_city varchar [note: 'City where the bank branch is located.']
  bank_contact_number varchar [note: 'Telephone number for the bank branch.']
  bank_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code where the bank is located.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the banking institution holding the account.']
  cash_pool_membership varchar [note: 'Indicates if the account participates in a cash‑pool (primary, secondary, or none).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the bank account record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the account (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  daily_transaction_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum aggregate amount that can be processed in a single day.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the bank account becomes active for posting.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the bank account is no longer active (nullable for open‑ended).']
  iban varchar [note: 'Standardized international account identifier for cross‑border transactions.']
  internal_reference varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for internal notes or reference codes.']
  last_reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent bank statement reconciliation.']
  payment_method_eligibility varchar [note: 'Supported payment methods for this account (e.g., wire, ACH).']
  swift_bic varchar [note: 'Bank Identifier Code used for international wire transfers.']
  treasury_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region of the treasury unit responsible for the account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the bank account record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all house bank accounts used by the manufacturing enterprise for payment processing, cash pooling, and liquidity management. Captures bank key, account number, IBAN, SWIFT/BIC, bank name, country, currency, GL account assignment, payment method eligibility, daily transaction limits, and cash pool membership. Serves as the SSOT for treasury operations and bank statement reconciliation in SAP FI.'
}

  swift_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC code for international transfers to the partner\'s bank.']

  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the partner record.']
  vat_number varchar [note: 'Value Added Tax registration number, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for business_partner. Referenced by business_partner_id.'

  Note: 'Master reference table for cost_element. Referenced by sender_cost_element_id.'

  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the allocation rule.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Category of the rule defining how allocation is calculated.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the allocation rule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the allocation rule record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the rule for change management and audit.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for allocation_rule. Referenced by allocation_rule_id.'
}

Table finance.cost_object {
  cost_object_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cost_object']

  parent_cost_object_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent cost object in the hierarchy.']
  actual_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual incurred cost recorded against the cost object.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate costs to this object.']
  amortization_end_date date [note: 'Date when amortization of the cost object ends.']
  amortization_start_date date [note: 'Date when amortization of the cost object begins.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget amount allocated to the cost object for the fiscal period.']
  category varchar [note: 'High‑level business category for the cost object (e.g., Manufacturing, R&D, Sales).']
  cost_nature varchar [note: 'Indicates whether costs are capitalized, expensed, or classified as investment/operating.']
  cost_object_code varchar [note: 'External code or number assigned to the cost object (e.g., cost‑center code, internal order number).']
  cost_object_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of the cost object within the cost hierarchy (0 = top level).']

  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost object record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts associated with the cost object.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to depreciate assets linked to this cost object.']
  depreciation_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual depreciation rate expressed as a percentage.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the purpose and scope of the cost object.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the cost object is retired or no longer used for allocation.']
  external_system_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost object in an external ERP or legacy system.']
  financial_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the cost object is primarily associated.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter or period for reporting purposes.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Delimited path representing the full ancestry of the cost object (e.g., "/100/200/300").']
  is_reportable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the cost object should be included in financial reporting.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the cost object used in reports and analyses.']
  planned_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned cost amount for the cost object before actuals are recorded.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the cost object becomes effective for cost allocation.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the cost object.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'More detailed sub‑category within the main category.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category of the cost object indicating its nature for accounting and reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the cost object record.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Estimated useful life of assets associated with the cost object, in years.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between planned and actual cost amounts (planned – actual).']
  variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between planned and actual costs.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for cost_object. Referenced by cost_object_id.'
}

Table finance.cost_estimate {
  cost_estimate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cost_estimate']
