  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cost center responsible for the estimate.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who initially created the estimate record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the estimate.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project or initiative the estimate supports.']
  reference_cost_estimate_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on cost_estimate (reference_cost_estimate_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the estimate was approved.']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'Confidence in the accuracy of the estimate.']
  cost_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost type covered by the estimate.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the estimate.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the scope and assumptions of the cost estimate.']
  estimate_amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total estimated cost before taxes, discounts, or adjustments.']
  estimate_amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net estimated cost after tax and adjustments.']
  estimate_date date [note: 'Date on which the cost estimate was generated.']
  estimate_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the cost estimate, used in finance and project communications.']
  estimate_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated tax component associated with the gross amount.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or comments captured by the estimator.']
  quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Number of units or hours estimated for the cost item.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the estimate record was first captured in the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the estimate record.']
  risk_factor varchar [note: 'Qualitative risk rating associated with the estimate assumptions.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the cost estimate.']
  total_estimated_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregated cost (quantity × unit price) stored for reporting convenience.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit applied to the quantity field.']
  unit_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Estimated price per unit of measure.']
  valid_until date [note: 'Date after which the estimate expires; null if open‑ended.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version of the estimate record.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which the estimate is considered valid.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for cost_estimate. Referenced by cost_estimate_id.'
}

Table finance.chart_of_accounts {
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for chart_of_accounts']
  parent_chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on chart_of_accounts (parent_chart_of_accounts_id)']
  account_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification providing finer granularity of the account.']
  account_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code identifying the GL account.']
  account_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the account purpose and usage.']
  account_group varchar [note: 'Higher-level grouping of accounts for consolidated reporting.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the GL account.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the account by financial statement category.']
  balance_nature varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the account normally carries a debit or credit balance.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center associated with the account for internal cost allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the account record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code in which the account is denominated.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the account becomes effective for posting.']
  effective_to date [note: 'Date when the account ceases to be effective; null if open-ended.']
  financial_reporting_level varchar [note: 'Level in the financial reporting hierarchy (e.g., 1‑5).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account is currently active for posting.']
  is_actual boolean [note: 'True if the account records actual transactions.']
  is_budgeted boolean [note: 'True if the account is included in budgeting processes.']
  is_consolidation_account boolean [note: 'True if the account is used in corporate consolidation reporting.']
  is_forecasted boolean [note: 'True if the account is used in forecasting.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity that owns the account.']
  parent_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent GL account for hierarchical structuring.']
  posting_restriction_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if posting to this account is restricted due to policy or control.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center linked to the account for profitability reporting.']
  tax_reporting_code varchar [note: 'Code used for tax reporting purposes associated with the account.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the account record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for chart_of_accounts. Referenced by chart_of_accounts_id.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_requirement {
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each regulatory requirement record.']
  adoption_status varchar [note: 'Current internal adoption state of the requirement.']
  applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of applicability (e.g., plant, product line, business unit, global).']
  audit_frequency_months varchar [note: 'How often internal audits must be performed for this requirement.']
  authority_code varchar [note: 'Standard abbreviation or code for the issuing authority (e.g., ISO, OSHA).']
  authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or body that issued the requirement (e.g., ISO, OSHA, EPA).']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High‑level domain category of the requirement.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Latest date by which the organization must achieve compliance.']
  compliance_level varchar [note: 'Degree to which compliance is required by law or policy.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the organization with respect to this requirement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the regulatory requirement record was first created in the data lake.']
  document_last_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent amendment to the requirement document.']
  document_release_date date [note: 'Date the current version of the requirement document was released.']
  document_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the official requirement document.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the requirement becomes effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the requirement expires or is superseded, if applicable.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic jurisdiction to which the requirement applies, expressed as a three‑letter ISO country code.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when the requirement was last reviewed for relevance or changes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty associated with non‑compliance, if defined.']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the penalty amount.']
  regulatory_requirement_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the requirement, including scope and key obligations.']
  regulatory_requirement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the requirement within the organization.']
  reporting_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific reporting obligations (e.g., quarterly emissions report).']
  requirement_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code assigned by the issuing authority (e.g., ISO‑9001, OSHA‑1910).']
  requirement_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the regulation, standard, or directive.']
  requirement_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the requirement.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Number of months between mandatory compliance reviews.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with non‑compliance.']
  source_url varchar [note: 'Web link to the official source or full text of the requirement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the regulatory requirement record.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all applicable laws, regulations, standards, and compliance obligations governing Manufacturing operations across all jurisdictions. Encompasses ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, ISO 50001, IEC 62443, OSHA, EPA, CE Marking, UL, ANSI, REACH, RoHS, and jurisdiction-specific requirements. Each record defines the regulation source, issuing authority, jurisdiction (federal, state, local, EU, international), applicability scope (plant, product line, geography), effective date, mandatory compliance deadline, review frequency, and current adoption status. Serves as the single authoritative legal register and compliance universe from which all specific obligations, permits, and filings are derived.'
}

Table compliance.obligation {
  obligation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for obligation']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for budgeting: each compliance obligation is charged to a cost center for cost tracking and reporting.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Internal orders are used to fund corrective actions for obligations; linking provides order‑level cost control.']
  obligation_control_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Person responsible for the control that satisfies the obligation.']
  obligation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Individual (e.g., compliance officer) who owns the obligation.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Internal department accountable for fulfilling the obligation.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Obligations affect profit performance; linking to profit center enables profit‑center based compliance cost analysis.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master regulatory requirement that drives this obligation.']
  related_facility_id varchar [note: 'Facility or plant to which the obligation is tied.']
  related_product_line_family_id varchar [note: 'Product line or family associated with the obligation.']
  audit_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings recorded for this obligation.']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any audit linked to the obligation.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the obligation for reporting and analytics.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent compliance assessment.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completing the corrective action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the obligation record was first created in the system.']
  due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which compliance must be demonstrated.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the obligation becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the obligation expires, if applicable.']
  evidence_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to electronic evidence supporting compliance (e.g., certificates, test reports).']
  evidence_type varchar [note: 'Category of the supporting evidence attached to the obligation.']
  external_reference_number varchar [note: 'Identifier used in external regulatory filings or partner systems.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the obligation is mandatory (true) or optional (false).']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'Geographic or legal jurisdiction (e.g., country code) to which the obligation applies.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance assessment.']
  next_assessment_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next compliance assessment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or additional information about the obligation.']
  obligation_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code assigned to the obligation for reference in reports and audits.']
  obligation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the obligation.']
  obligation_type varchar [note: 'Category of the regulatory requirement the obligation satisfies.']
  recurrence_schedule varchar [note: 'Frequency with which the obligation must be reassessed or renewed.']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Planned corrective action to address a non‑compliant status.']
  risk_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score (e.g., 0.00‑10.00) derived from severity and likelihood.']
  risk_severity varchar [note: 'Qualitative severity of the risk if the obligation is not met.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the obligation record (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Teamcenter).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the obligation record.']

  Note: 'Operational compliance obligation instance record assigning a specific regulatory requirement to a designated organizational unit, facility, product line, or process within Manufacturing. Each obligation represents a concrete compliance task or demonstration requirement derived from the regulatory_requirement master catalog. Tracks obligation owner, responsible department, due date, recurrence schedule, current compliance status (compliant, partially compliant, non-compliant, not yet assessed), evidence of compliance (linked documents, test results, certifications), last assessment date, next assessment due, and linkage to the governing regulatory requirement. Enables compliance gap analysis, obligation coverage reporting, and proactive deadline management across all jurisdictions and standards.'
}

Table compliance.audit_plan {
  audit_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the audit plan record.']
  lead_auditor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Links audit plan to the employee acting as lead auditor, needed for audit scheduling and responsibility tracking.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Audit plans are created for projects to assess compliance with standards and regulations.']
  applicable_standards varchar [note: 'Standards, regulations, or certifications the audit addresses (e.g., ISO 14001, ISO 45001, IEC 62443).']
  audit_frequency varchar [note: 'Planned recurrence of the audit (e.g., annual, semi‑annual, quarterly, monthly, ad‑hoc).']
  audit_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodological approach used for the audit (e.g., checklist, interview, sampling).']
  audit_objectives varchar [note: 'Key objectives and goals the audit intends to achieve.']
  audit_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the audit plan.']
  audit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall audit performance score (e.g., 0‑100 scale).']
  audit_team varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of auditors assigned to execute the audit.']
  audit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the audit (internal, external, supplier, regulatory, certification).']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated compliance rating derived from audit findings.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Deadline by which identified corrective actions must be completed.']
  creation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit plan record was first created in the system.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the audit plan becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the audit plan expires or is no longer active (nullable for open‑ended plans).']
  findings_count varchar [note: 'Total count of audit findings recorded for the plan.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit plan record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier or code used to reference the audit plan.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the audit based on potential impact and likelihood.']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date when the audit is expected to conclude.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date when the audit is to commence.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the audit scope, including processes, facilities, and products covered.']
  target_facilities varchar [note: 'Facilities, plants, or operational units that are subject to the audit.']

  Note: 'Planned internal or external audit program record defining the audit scope, type (ISO surveillance, OSHA inspection, IEC 62443 cybersecurity, supplier audit, internal quality audit, management system review), scheduled dates, lead auditor, audit team, applicable standards, and target facilities or processes. Serves as the master schedule and compliance calendar for all audit and periodic evaluation activities across Manufacturing.'
}

Table compliance.audit_event {
  audit_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the audit event record.']
  audit_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit plan that scheduled this event.']
  compliance_audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Link to the collection of findings generated from this audit.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Audit Event records need to reference the specific automation device audited for regulatory compliance reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the auditor (links to party master data).']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Audit events are logged against the project they audit, supporting traceability and reporting.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to conduct the audit.']
  assessment_type varchar [note: 'Category of the audit activity (e.g., certification_audit, regulatory_inspection, internal_audit, periodic_evaluation, safety_inspection, cybersecurity_assessment, management_review). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: certification_audit|regulatory_inspection|internal_audit|periodic_evaluation|safety_inspection|cybersecurity_assessment|management_review — promote to reference product]']
  audit_event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the audit event record.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who performed the audit.']
  certification_body varchar [note: 'External organization that issued a certification (e.g., ISO, UL, CE).']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completion of required corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a corrective action plan is mandated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the audit event record was first created in the system.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the audited area.']
  documentation_link varchar [note: 'URL or path to supporting audit documentation and evidence.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the audit or inspection was performed.']
  evidence_attached boolean [note: 'True if supporting evidence files are attached to the audit record.']
  follow_up_action varchar [note: 'Planned follow‑up activity after the audit closure.']
  hazards_identified varchar [note: 'List of safety or health hazards discovered during the audit.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical location or facility where the audit took place.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the audit after evaluation.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Government or industry agency responsible for the inspection (e.g., OSHA, EPA).']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical risk rating derived from severity and likelihood (0.00‑10.00).']
  scope varchar [note: 'Defined boundaries of the audit (facility, zone, process, system, etc.).']
  severity_rating varchar [note: 'Risk severity assigned to any non‑conformance or observation.']
  summary varchar [note: 'Free‑text summary of the audit findings and observations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the audit event record.']
  vulnerabilities_identified varchar [note: 'Security or process vulnerabilities uncovered, especially for cybersecurity assessments.']

  Note: 'Unified execution record of any compliance assessment, verification, or inspection activity conducted at Manufacturing. Encompasses formal certification audits (ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 45001, IEC 62443), regulatory inspections (OSHA, EPA), internal quality audits, periodic compliance evaluations (ISO 14001 Clause 9.1.2, ISO 45001 Clause 9.1.2), workplace safety inspections and walkthroughs (routine, pre-startup, LOTO verification, fire safety, ergonomic), cybersecurity risk assessments (IEC 62443-3-2 zone/conduit analysis, NIST CSF), and management system reviews. Captures assessment type (certification_audit, regulatory_inspection, internal_audit, periodic_evaluation, safety_inspection, cybersecurity_assessment, management_review), actual dates, assessor/auditor names, assessment method (on-site, remote, document review, observation, testing, checklist), scope (facility, zone, process, system, area), overall outcome (conforming, nonconformance, observation, pass/fail), severity/risk rating, summary narrative, hazards or vulnerabilities identified, and certification body or regulatory agency involved. Links to the governing audit_plan and generates audit_findings. Serves as the single authoritative transactional record for ALL compliance verification activities at Manufacturing.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_audit_finding {
  compliance_audit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the audit finding record.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.control_system. Business justification: Audit findings often target a specific control system; linking enables traceability for corrective actions.']
  controlled_document_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supporting evidence document linked to the finding.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Audit findings are tied to the audited customer account; this link supports the \'Audit Findings per Account\' compliance report.']
  prior_finding_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior related finding, if any.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Findings are linked to the project they affect to drive corrective actions within that project.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Assigns a responsible employee for corrective action on each finding, essential for CAPA tracking.']
  actual_resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the finding was actually resolved.']
  affected_process varchar [note: 'Business process impacted by the finding.']
  audit_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the audit event that generated this finding.']
  audit_finding_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier for the finding used in reports.']
  clause_violated varchar [note: 'Specific clause or requirement that was violated.']
  compliance_audit_finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the audit finding.']
  compliance_audit_finding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the finding.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action to be taken.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a corrective action is mandated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the finding record was created in the system.']
  discovery_date date [note: 'Date when the finding was identified.']
  finding_type varchar [note: 'Category of the audit finding indicating its nature.']
  impact_area varchar [note: 'Primary business area impacted by the finding.']
  is_repeat_finding boolean [note: 'Indicates if this finding repeats a previously recorded issue.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes related to the finding.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory standard or clause associated with the finding.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who reported the finding.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating associated with the finding.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Analysis of the underlying cause of the finding.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the finding based on impact.']
  target_resolution_date date [note: 'Planned date for corrective action completion.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the finding record.']

  Note: 'Individual finding record raised during a compliance audit, capturing finding type (major nonconformance, minor nonconformance, observation, opportunity for improvement), the specific clause or requirement violated, affected process or area, finding description, severity, and required response timeline. Each finding is linked to an audit event and may trigger a CAPA record. Serves as the authoritative record of audit outcomes requiring corrective action.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_capa_record {
  compliance_capa_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the CAPA record.']
  compliance_audit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the audit finding that triggered this CAPA.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: CAPA costs are allocated to cost centers for overhead accounting and compliance cost visibility.']
  customer_complaint_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer complaint that led to this CAPA.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for the CAPA.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: CAPA corrective actions are funded through internal orders; the link tracks spend per corrective action.']
  ncr_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the NCR linked to this CAPA.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: CAPA records address issues identified in project audits and must be tied to the originating project.']
  related_regulatory_citation_regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the regulatory citation associated with this CAPA.']
  abatement_deadline date [note: 'Date by which the penalty must be paid or remedial action completed.']
  capa_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the CAPA record.']
  citation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the regulatory citation.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date when the CAPA record was formally closed.']
  closure_status varchar [note: 'Final status indicating how the CAPA was concluded.']
  compliance_capa_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CAPA.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Compliance framework(s) relevant to the CAPA.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action to address the root cause.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAPA record was created in the system.']
  department_responsible varchar [note: 'Department accountable for executing the CAPA.']
  documentation_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting documentation for the CAPA.']
  effectiveness_review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the effectiveness review after verification.']
  effectiveness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric rating (0‑100) indicating the effectiveness of the corrective action after verification.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAPA was initiated.']
  is_external_citation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the citation originates from an external regulator.']
  issuing_agency varchar [note: 'Agency that issued the regulatory citation prompting the CAPA.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent effectiveness review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any additional information or comments.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty associated with the regulatory citation.']
  penalty_currency varchar [note: 'Currency code of the penalty amount, following ISO 4217.']
  preventive_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of the preventive action to avoid recurrence.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the CAPA.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the CAPA effectiveness is reviewed.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk level associated with the CAPA.']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the identified root cause.']
  root_cause_method varchar [note: 'Method used to determine the root cause of the issue.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity of the issue addressed by the CAPA.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the corrective and preventive actions should be completed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the CAPA record.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the corrective action was effective.']

  Note: 'Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) record managing the full lifecycle of corrective actions arising from audit findings, safety incidents, enforcement actions, NCRs, customer complaints, or regulatory citations. Captures root cause analysis method (5-Why, Fishbone, 8D, FMEA), root cause description, corrective action plan, preventive action plan, responsible owner, target completion date, verification method, effectiveness review outcome, and closure status. Also accommodates enforcement action details (issuing agency, citation reference, penalty amount, abatement deadline) when the CAPA originates from a regulatory citation. Aligns with ISO 9001 Clause 10.2 and APQP CAPA requirements.'
}

Table compliance.safety_incident {
  safety_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for safety_incident']
  account_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Incident management requires associating each safety incident with the specific site for root cause analysis and regulatory reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee (worker) involved or affected by the incident.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or asset involved in the incident.']
  previous_incident_safety_incident_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the prior incident if this is a repeat.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Safety incidents occurring on a project site are linked to that project for reporting and trend analysis.']
  area_code varchar [note: 'Code for the specific area or zone within the plant.']
  body_part_affected varchar [note: 'Body part(s) impacted by the injury.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completion of corrective actions.']
  corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the longer‑term corrective action plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident record was first created in the system.']
  days_away_from_work varchar [note: 'Number of workdays the employee was unable to work due to the incident.']
  equipment_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the equipment involved.']
  immediate_corrective_action varchar [note: 'Actions taken immediately after the incident to mitigate impact.']
  incident_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of what happened.']
  incident_location varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the physical location where the incident occurred.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the incident, used for tracking and reporting.']
  incident_report_number varchar [note: 'External report number assigned by regulatory bodies.']
  incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the incident.']
  incident_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident occurred.']
  incident_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the incident according to regulatory definitions.']
  injury_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of injury or illness sustained.']
  investigation_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the investigation was formally closed.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the incident investigation.']
  is_repeat_incident boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this incident is a repeat of a prior incident.']
  lost_time_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident resulted in lost work time.']
  medical_treatment_required boolean [note: 'True if medical treatment beyond first aid was required.']
  osha_300_log_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the incident for OSHA 300 reporting.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing plant where the incident happened.']
  regulatory_report_submission_date date [note: 'Date the regulatory report was filed.']
  regulatory_report_submitted_flag boolean [note: 'True if the incident has been submitted to required regulators.']
  reportable_to_iso_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the incident must be reported under ISO 45001.']
  reportable_to_osha_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the incident must be reported to OSHA.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who reported the incident.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was reported.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned during incident assessment.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the underlying cause(s) of the incident.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Overall severity rating of the incident.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the incident occurred.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System where the incident was originally recorded.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the incident record.']
  witness_count varchar [note: 'Number of witnesses to the incident.']
  witness_names varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of witness names.']

  Note: 'ISO 45001 and OSHA occupational health and safety incident record capturing workplace injuries, illnesses, near-misses, dangerous occurrences, and first-aid events. Records incident date/time, location, affected worker(s), incident type (OSHA recordable, lost-time, near-miss, property damage), injury/illness description, body part affected, days away from work, OSHA 300 log classification, root cause, and immediate corrective actions taken. Primary source for OSHA 300/300A/301 regulatory reporting.'
}

Table compliance.safety_inspection {
  safety_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each safety inspection record.']
  account_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Safety inspections are recorded per manufacturing site; linking enables the \'Safety Inspection Summary by Site\' report used by operations.']
  checklist_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the checklist template used for this inspection.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Safety inspections must record which automation device was inspected to satisfy OSHA safety compliance.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee or contractor who performed the inspection.']
  node_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the plant, factory, or site where the inspection took place.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Safety inspections are performed for each construction/upgrade project; required for safety compliance reports.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Safety inspections are performed to satisfy regulatory requirements; linking to regulatory_requirement provides context and eliminates need for separate regulatory reference fields.']
  area varchar [note: 'Specific area, zone, or work cell within the facility that was inspected.']
  average_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weighted average score derived from checklist item results (0‑100 scale).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the inspection against regulatory and internal safety standards.']
  corrective_action_deadline date [note: 'Final date by which all corrective actions must be closed.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any immediate corrective actions are mandated.']
  corrective_action_summary varchar [note: 'Brief description of the corrective actions to be taken.']
  documents_attached boolean [note: 'True if supporting photos, reports, or certificates are attached to the record.']
  emergency_exit_accessible boolean [note: 'True if all emergency exits were clear and unobstructed.']
  fire_extinguisher_checked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fire extinguishers were inspected and found serviceable.']
  follow_up_date date [note: 'Planned date by which corrective actions must be completed.']
  hazards_identified varchar [note: 'Number of distinct safety hazards observed during the inspection.']
  inspection_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total time spent on the inspection, measured in minutes.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the inspection, often used in reports and work orders.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the inspection was actually performed on the facility/area.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection purpose or trigger.']
  is_loto_verified boolean [note: 'True if lock‑out/tag‑out procedures were verified during the inspection.']
  items_failed varchar [note: 'Count of checklist items that were non‑compliant or required corrective action.']
  items_passed varchar [note: 'Count of checklist items that met the required safety criteria.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form observations, comments, or remarks recorded by the inspector.']
  ppe_compliance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether required PPE was observed being used by personnel.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the inspection record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the inspection record.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who reported the safety issue or initiated the inspection.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the inspection request or incident was logged.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned based on identified hazards and severity.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the underlying cause(s) of identified hazards.']
  safety_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the inspection record.']
  safety_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite safety performance metric for the inspected area.']
  total_items_checked varchar [note: 'Number of individual checklist items evaluated during the inspection.']
  training_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which required safety training must be completed.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'True if the inspection identified a need for additional safety training.']

  Note: 'Workplace safety inspection record capturing scheduled and ad-hoc safety walkthroughs, hazard assessments, and safety audits conducted under ISO 45001 and OSHA requirements. Records inspection date, inspector, facility/area inspected, inspection type (routine, pre-startup, LOTO verification, fire safety, ergonomic), checklist items evaluated, hazards identified, risk rating, and immediate corrective actions. Distinct from audit_event which covers formal certification audits.'
}

Table compliance.environmental_aspect {
  environmental_aspect_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each environmental aspect record.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.control_system. Business justification: Environmental aspect monitoring is managed by a control system; linking provides traceability for regulatory reporting.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Environmental aspects are assessed per project for regulatory reporting and impact tracking.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Environmental aspects are tracked against specific regulatory requirements; FK replaces duplicated legal requirement reference fields.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Assigns an employee responsible for each environmental aspect, required for environmental compliance reporting.']
  actual_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Most recent measured value for the aspect.']
  aspect_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the aspect (e.g., air emission, waste).']
  aspect_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the aspect in reports and systems.']
  aspect_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name describing the environmental aspect (e.g., "CO2 Emissions from Furnace").']
  baseline_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Historical or reference value against which current performance is compared.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the aspect against legal requirements.']
  control_measure_description varchar [note: 'Description of the control or mitigation measure applied to the aspect.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective action to address non‑compliance.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completion of the corrective action.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if a corrective action is needed.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Aveva SCADA).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the aspect record became effective.']
  impact_type varchar [note: 'Primary environmental medium affected by the aspect.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the aspect is deemed critical to environmental compliance.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the aspect record.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the aspect’s quantitative values.']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the aspect is measured or reviewed.']
  monitoring_method varchar [note: 'Technique or instrument used to monitor the aspect (e.g., continuous emission monitoring system).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the aspect.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the aspect record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the aspect record.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department accountable for managing the aspect.']
  review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal review of the aspect.']
  significance_rating varchar [note: 'Business‑defined importance of the aspect to the organization’s environmental impact.']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Regulatory or internal target for the aspect.']
  variance decimal(18,4) [note: 'Difference between actual and target values (actual – target).']

  Note: 'ISO 14001 environmental aspect and impact register record identifying the environmental aspects of Manufacturing operations (emissions, effluents, waste, energy use, noise, land contamination) and their associated environmental impacts. Captures aspect description, activity or process generating the aspect, significance rating, applicable legal requirements, control measures in place, and monitoring obligations. Serves as the foundation for the ISO 14001 Environmental Management System (EMS).'
}

Table compliance.emissions_record {
  emissions_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the emissions record.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device that captured the measurement.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who recorded or verified the measurement.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Emissions measurements are tied to specific projects to monitor environmental impact and meet permits.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Emissions records are governed by regulatory limits; linking to the governing requirement removes the duplicated limit column.']
  source_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the emission source (e.g., plant, stack, equipment).']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the measurement device was last calibrated.']
  calibration_status varchar [note: 'Current calibration status of the measurement device.']
  carbon_intensity decimal(12,5) [note: 'Amount of CO₂e emitted per unit of production output.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the emissions record was first created in the system.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) indicating confidence in the measurement data.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that provided the emission measurement (e.g., SAP, SCADA).']
  emission_factor decimal(12,6) [note: 'Factor used to convert activity data to emissions (e.g., kg CO2 per unit).']
  exceedance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the measured value exceeds the regulatory limit.']
  facility_address varchar [note: 'Physical address of the facility.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Name of the facility where the emission source is located.']
  greenhouse_gas_equivalent decimal(15,5) [note: 'CO₂‑equivalent value of the measured pollutant.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the facility.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the facility.']
  measured_value decimal(15,5) [note: 'Numeric value of the measured emission.']
  measurement_device_type varchar [note: 'Type or model of the measurement device.']
  measurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to obtain the emission measurement.']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Quality status of the measurement.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the emission measurement was taken.']
  operational_shift varchar [note: 'Shift during which the measurement was taken.']
  pollutant_type varchar [note: 'Type of pollutant measured.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the regulatory report.']
  report_submission_date date [note: 'Date the emission report was submitted to the agency.']
  reported_to_agency varchar [note: 'Regulatory agency to which this emission data is reported.']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'End date of the reporting period for this emission record.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the reporting period for this emission record.']
  reporting_quarter varchar [note: 'Quarter of the reporting period.']
  reporting_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year of the reporting period.']
  source_category varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the emission source.']
  source_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the emission source.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the measured value is expressed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the emissions record.']

  Note: 'EPA and ISO 14001 emissions monitoring record capturing measured air emissions, greenhouse gas (GHG) data, wastewater discharge, and hazardous waste generation at Manufacturing facilities. Records emission source, pollutant type (CO2, NOx, SOx, VOCs, particulates), measurement method (continuous monitoring, periodic sampling, calculated), measured value, unit of measure, regulatory limit, exceedance flag, and reporting period. Primary source for EPA regulatory filings and GHG inventory reporting.'
}

Table compliance.compliance_product_certification {
  compliance_product_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each product certification record.']
  compliance_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Designates the employee overseeing product certification, required for certification management and audit trails.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Product certifications (e.g., UL) are granted to specific automation devices; linking tracks certification status.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Certification costs are posted to a GL account; linking enables financial reporting of certification expenses.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Product certifications are required for components used in a specific project to meet regulatory standards.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Product certifications reference the regulatory requirement they satisfy; FK consolidates this relationship.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.sku_master']
  attached_document_path varchar [note: 'File system or storage reference to the digital copy of the certificate.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Unique number assigned by the certifying body to this certification.']
  certification_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost incurred to obtain the certification.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the certification (e.g., CE Marking for Model X).']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Category of certification indicating the standard or scheme applied.']
  certifying_body varchar [note: 'Organization that issued the certification. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EU|US|UL|IEC|ISO|CE|Other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_audit_date date [note: 'Date the latest compliance audit was performed.']
  compliance_audit_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent compliance audit.']
  compliance_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the certification purpose.']
  compliance_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region for which the certification is valid.']
  compliance_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with the certification status or upcoming expiry.']
  compliance_status_date date [note: 'Date when the certification status was last updated.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for certification cost.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the certification is considered effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the certification remains effective (may be null for open‑ended).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the certification becomes invalid unless renewed.']
  export_control_category varchar [note: 'Classification of export control regime applicable to the product.']
  is_export_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the product is subject to export control regulations.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certification is mandatory for market entry or regulatory compliance.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the certification was officially issued.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inspection that validated the certification.']
  next_inspection_due date [note: 'Planned date for the next compliance inspection.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the certification.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certification record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification record.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Planned date for renewal activities.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certification must be renewed before expiry.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the product scope covered by the certification.']
  test_standard varchar [note: 'Standard or test method used to achieve the certification.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the certification record (e.g., v1, v2).']

  Note: 'Product safety and regulatory certification master record tracking CE Marking, UL certification, IEC compliance, RoHS, REACH, and other product-level certifications for manufactured automation systems, electrification solutions, and smart infrastructure components. Captures certification type, certifying body, certificate number, issue date, expiry date, applicable product scope, test standard, and certification status. Distinct from asset.asset_certification which covers equipment/facility certifications.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_filing {
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each regulatory filing record.']
  automation_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.automation_project. Business justification: Regulatory filings are submitted for specific automation projects; linking ties filing to project for audit.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Regulatory filings are filed by a legal entity; company_code links filing to the responsible entity for audit trails.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal party responsible for the document.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Regulatory filings are made against specific regulatory requirements; FK replaces the agency reference field.']
  related_product_sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product to which the filing pertains.']
  related_project_project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project associated with the filing, if any.']
  acknowledgment_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or code returned by the agency confirming receipt.']
  applicable_standard varchar [note: 'Standard or regulation that the document satisfies (e.g., ISO 14001).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the document received final approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the formal approval workflow.']
  attached_file_path varchar [note: 'File system or storage location of the attached document.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the filing.']
  compliance_area varchar [note: 'Domain of compliance addressed by the filing.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the document\'s sensitivity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the filing record was first created in the system.']
  distribution_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of distribution for the document.']
  document_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the document\'s purpose and scope.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the document (e.g., ISO‑9001‑DOC‑001).']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the document indicating its purpose within compliance.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date the document becomes effective for the organization.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the document expires or is superseded, if applicable.']
  file_checksum varchar [note: 'Checksum (e.g., SHA‑256) for integrity verification of the attached file.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the attached file in bytes.']
  filing_period varchar [note: 'Reporting period the filing covers.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the external filing.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the filing is required by law or regulation.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review activity.']
  next_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp for the upcoming review.']
  regulatory_filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the document.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the document must be retained according to policy.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next mandatory review of the document.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version label for the document (e.g., A, B, 1.0, 2.1).']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with non‑compliance.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the filing was submitted to the external regulatory agency.']
  submission_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to submit the filing.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable title of the filing or compliance document.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the filing record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential numeric version of the document.']

  Note: 'Unified compliance document and regulatory submission master record serving as the single source of truth for: (1) all mandatory regulatory filings, disclosures, and notifications submitted to external bodies including EPA, OSHA, ISO certification bodies, CE notified bodies, and government agencies; and (2) all controlled internal compliance documents including ISO management system procedures, work instructions, environmental management plans, safety programs, audit reports, and certification records. Captures document/filing type (external_submission, internal_procedure, work_instruction, plan, program, record), document number, revision level, applicable standard or regulation, document owner, approval workflow status, effective date, review due date, retention period, distribution scope, and for external filings: submission date, regulatory agency, filing period, submission method, acknowledgment reference, and filing status. Supports ISO 9001 Clause 7.5 document control requirements and serves as the compliance submission calendar and document audit trail.'
}

Table compliance.permit {
  permit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the permit record.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (person or organization) that holds the permit.']
  compliance_officer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Links permit oversight to a compliance officer employee, needed for permit compliance monitoring.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Regulatory permits are often issued per automation device; linking ensures permit compliance tracking.']
  node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing facility to which the permit applies.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Permits are issued for individual projects (construction, operation) and must be linked for compliance tracking.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Permits are issued to satisfy regulatory requirements; linking provides direct association.']
  activity_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of the activity or operation authorized by the permit.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date when the latest amendment was filed.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the most recent amendment to the permit.']
  attached_documents varchar [note: 'Reference to supporting documents (e.g., PDFs) stored in the document management system.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance determination for the permit.']
  condition varchar [note: 'Specific condition, limitation, or requirement imposed by the authority.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the permit record was first created in the system.']
  expiration_notice_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a renewal/expiration notice has been sent to the responsible party.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date the permit expires or becomes invalid unless renewed.']
  fee_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary fee associated with obtaining or renewing the permit.']
  fee_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the permit fee.']
  fee_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the permit fee must be paid.']
  inspection_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the last inspection.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the permit was officially issued by the authority.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Regulatory body or agency that issued the permit.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory inspection related to the permit.']
  limit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the limit value.']
  limit_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Numeric limit associated with the permit (e.g., emission limit).']
  monitoring_frequency varchar [note: 'How often compliance monitoring must be performed.']
  next_monitoring_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required monitoring activity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the permit.']
  number varchar [note: 'Official permit number or code assigned by the issuing authority.']
  permit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the permit.']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Category of the permit (e.g., air emissions, wastewater discharge, hazardous waste, building, operating license).']
  renewal_deadline date [note: 'Last date to submit renewal application before expiration.']
  renewal_status varchar [note: 'Renewal state indicating if renewal is due, completed, not required, or pending.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the permit record.']

  Note: 'Environmental and operational permit master record tracking all permits required for Manufacturing facility operations, including EPA air permits, wastewater discharge permits (NPDES), hazardous waste permits (RCRA), building permits, and operating licenses. Captures permit type, issuing authority, permit number, facility, permitted activity, conditions and limits, issue date, expiry date, renewal status, and associated monitoring obligations. Enables proactive permit renewal management.'
}

Table compliance.cybersecurity_control {
  cybersecurity_control_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the cybersecurity control record.']
  control_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Assigns a responsible employee to each control, required for control ownership accountability reports.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.control_system. Business justification: Cybersecurity controls are implemented on specific control systems; linking supports compliance reporting.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Security controls are applied to project assets and need linkage for audit and compliance.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Cybersecurity controls are defined to meet regulatory cybersecurity standards; FK adds the governing requirement.']
  audit_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of audit findings associated with this control.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance state of the control against its target level.']
  control_applicability_scope varchar [note: 'Industrial automation domains the control applies to (e.g., PLC, SCADA, DCS, HMI, IIoT).']
  control_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the control. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: access_control|network_segmentation|patch_management|incident_response|monitoring|authentication|authorization|encryption|audit|physical_security — promote to reference product]']
  control_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code or identifier for the control as defined in IEC 62443 or related standards.']
  control_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual cost associated with implementing the control.']
  control_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of what the control does and its scope.']
  control_evidence_reference varchar [note: 'Link or reference to documentation/evidence proving control implementation.']
  control_mechanism varchar [note: 'Technical or procedural mechanism used to enforce the control.']
  control_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the security control.']
  control_objective varchar [note: 'Business or security objective that the control is intended to achieve.']
  control_priority varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned priority (1 = highest) for implementing or maintaining the control.']
  control_source varchar [note: 'Standard or framework from which the control originates (e.g., IEC 62443, NIST SP 800‑53).']
  control_subcategory varchar [note: 'More detailed classification within the main control category.']
  control_type varchar [note: 'Strategic type of the control: preventive, detective, or corrective.']
  control_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the control definition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the control record was first created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the control became effective.']
  implementation_status varchar [note: 'Current implementation phase of the control.']
  last_assessment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent security assessment for the control.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the control was last maintained or verified.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle state of the control.']
  maintenance_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between required maintenance activities for the control.']
  next_maintenance_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next maintenance activity.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next formal review of the control.']
  owner_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department that the control owner belongs to.']
  remediation_deadline date [note: 'Latest date by which identified deficiencies must be remediated.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date when the control was retired or decommissioned, if applicable.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating associated with the control based on its effectiveness and exposure.']
  security_level_achieved varchar [note: 'Current IEC 62443 security level actually achieved by the control.']
  security_level_target varchar [note: 'Targeted IEC 62443 security level the control aims to achieve.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the control record.']

  Note: 'IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity control master record defining the security controls, countermeasures, and safeguards implemented across Manufacturing\'s industrial automation and control systems (IACS), including PLCs, SCADA, DCS, HMI, and IIoT infrastructure. Captures control identifier, IEC 62443 security level target (SL-T), security level achieved (SL-A), control category (access control, network segmentation, patch management, incident response), implementation status, and responsible owner.'
}

Table compliance.cybersecurity_assessment {
  cybersecurity_assessment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cybersecurity assessment record.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Assessment evaluates security posture of a particular automation device; linking enables tracking of findings.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the business owner responsible for the assessed system.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the asset (e.g., PLC, SCADA server) assessed.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location where the assessed system resides.']
  mitigation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the mitigation plan record linked to this assessment.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Cybersecurity assessments are performed for project systems to ensure security compliance.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Cybersecurity assessments evaluate compliance with specific regulatory requirements; FK replaces the reference field.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assessment was approved by authority.']
  assessed_zone varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the industrial control system zone or asset assessed.']
  assessment_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the assessment was performed.']
  assessment_methodology varchar [note: 'Methodology used for the cybersecurity assessment.']
  assessment_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable identifier for the assessment, often used in reports.']
  assessment_scope varchar [note: 'Scope description covering assets, processes, and boundaries evaluated.']
  assessment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assessment.']
  assessment_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the assessment methodology or report.']
  assessor_contact varchar [note: 'Email address of the assessor for follow-up.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or team conducting the assessment.']
  audit_trail_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing audit trail comments and changes.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards framework applied.']
  control_gap_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of gaps between required and existing security controls.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assessment record was first created in the system.']
  document_link varchar [note: 'URL or path to the detailed assessment report document.']
  evidence_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting evidence files are attached.']
  external_auditor_contact varchar [note: 'Contact email for the external auditor.']
  external_auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external auditor organization or individual.']
  is_critical_asset boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assessed asset is classified as critical to operations.']
  is_external_assessment boolean [note: 'Indicates if the assessment was performed by an external third party.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when the assessment was last reviewed for updates.']
  overall_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric risk score calculated from assessment (0-100).']
  recommended_remediation_actions varchar [note: 'Suggested actions to mitigate identified vulnerabilities.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which recommended remediation actions should be completed.']
  remediation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of remediation implementation.']
  risk_justification varchar [note: 'Narrative justification for the assigned risk rating.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned based on identified vulnerabilities.']
  security_level_gap varchar [note: 'Difference between required and actual security level per IEC 62443.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assessment record.']
  vulnerabilities_identified varchar [note: 'List or description of vulnerabilities discovered during the assessment.']

  Note: 'IEC 62443 cybersecurity risk assessment and vulnerability assessment record for Manufacturing\'s industrial control systems and OT networks. Captures assessment date, assessed system or zone, assessment methodology (IEC 62443-3-2 zone and conduit analysis, NIST CSF), identified vulnerabilities, risk rating, security level gap analysis, recommended remediation actions, and assessment outcome. Drives the cybersecurity CAPA and control improvement roadmap.'
}

Table compliance.hazardous_substance {
  hazardous_substance_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the hazardous substance record.']
  primary_hazardous_vendor_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Enterprise identifier for the supplier of the substance.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Hazardous substances are regulated; linking to the applicable requirement removes duplicated regulatory list/status columns.']
  cas_number varchar [note: 'Unique numeric identifier assigned by the CAS registry for the chemical.']
  chemical_family varchar [note: 'Broad chemical family or class (e.g., halogenated, aromatic).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the substance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the hazardous substance record was created.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Approved method for disposing of the substance (e.g., incineration, landfill).']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person or team to contact in case of an incident.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for emergency response related to the substance.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the substance is considered expired or unsuitable for use.']
  handling_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific handling instructions (e.g., temperature control, ventilation).']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'Primary GHS hazard class for the substance.']
  hazard_statements varchar [note: 'Standardized statements describing the nature of the hazards.']
  hazardous_substance_name varchar [note: 'Common or trade name of the hazardous chemical.']
  hazardous_substance_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the record (e.g., active, inactive, retired).']
  internal_code varchar [note: 'Enterprise‑specific code used to reference the substance in internal systems.']
  is_controlled_substance boolean [note: 'True if the substance is subject to additional controls (e.g., dual‑use, export restrictions).']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the substance is classified as hazardous under GHS.']
  is_reportable boolean [note: 'True if the substance quantity exceeds reporting thresholds for EPA or OSHA.']
  last_inventory_date date [note: 'Date when the substance quantity was last physically verified.']
  molecular_formula varchar [note: 'Standard chemical formula representing the composition of the substance.']
  molecular_weight decimal(10,4) [note: 'Molecular weight of the substance expressed in grams per mole.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next regulatory or safety review.']
  personal_protective_equipment varchar [note: 'Recommended PPE for safe handling of the substance.']
  precautionary_statements varchar [note: 'Recommended safety measures and handling instructions.']
  procurement_date date [note: 'Date the substance was purchased or received.']
  quantity_on_hand decimal(18,4) [note: 'Current inventory amount of the substance at the facility.']
  reporting_threshold_exceeded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether current quantity exceeds the regulatory threshold quantity.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk rating derived from hazard class, quantity, and exposure potential.']
  sds_url varchar [note: 'Link to the electronic Safety Data Sheet for the substance.']
  signal_word varchar [note: 'GHS signal word indicating the level of hazard (Danger or Warning).']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Physical location (e.g., warehouse, cabinet) where the substance is stored.']
  threshold_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity that triggers regulatory reporting (e.g., TPQ, SARA 313 threshold).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit used for quantity_on_hand and threshold_quantity (e.g., kg, L).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the record.']
  waste_code varchar [note: 'Regulatory waste code (e.g., D001) associated with the substance.']

  Note: 'Hazardous substance and chemical master record tracking all hazardous materials used, stored, or generated at Manufacturing facilities, supporting OSHA HazCom (GHS), EPA EPCRA Tier II, REACH, and RoHS compliance. Captures substance name, CAS number, chemical family, physical/chemical properties, GHS hazard classification, SDS reference, storage quantity, threshold planning quantity (TPQ), and applicable regulatory lists (SARA 313, SVHC). Serves as the chemical inventory for regulatory reporting.'
}

Table compliance.waste_record {
  waste_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the waste record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the reporting employee.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Waste generation is recorded per project to manage disposal compliance and reporting.']
  regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Waste records are subject to regulatory reporting; FK provides direct link to the governing requirement.']
  related_product_line_product_family_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product line associated with the waste.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the facility or area where waste is stored.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'System identifier for the disposal contractor.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the employee who approved the waste record for reporting.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the waste record was approved.']
  chain_of_custody_document varchar [note: 'Reference (URL or file name) to the chain‑of‑custody record.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the waste record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the waste record was first created in the system.']
  disposal_contractor varchar [note: 'Licensed contractor responsible for waste disposal.']
  disposal_date date [note: 'Date the waste was disposed or transferred to a licensed contractor.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispose of the waste.']
  disposal_site varchar [note: 'Name of the off‑site location where waste was disposed.']
  disposal_site_address varchar [note: 'Street address of the disposal site.']
