  disposal_site_city varchar [note: 'City of the disposal site.']
  disposal_site_country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the disposal site.']
  disposal_site_state varchar [note: 'State or province of the disposal site.']
  disposal_site_zip varchar [note: 'Postal code of the disposal site.']
  epa_waste_code varchar [note: 'Four‑character EPA hazardous waste code (RCRA).']
  generation_date date [note: 'Date the waste was generated on site.']
  hazard_classification varchar [note: 'Specific hazard class of the waste material.']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'True if the waste is classified as hazardous under EPA/ISO standards.']
  manifest_number varchar [note: 'EPA manifest number linking the waste to transportation documentation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the waste event.']
  quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Amount of waste generated, expressed in the selected unit of measure.']
  related_facility_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility where the waste was generated.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name of the employee who recorded the waste event.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Name or description of the on‑site storage area for the waste.']
  transport_distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Distance traveled for waste transport, expressed in kilometers.']
  transport_emission_kg_co2 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Estimated CO₂ emissions from waste transport, in kilograms.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used to move waste to the disposal site.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity field.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the waste record.']
  waste_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category describing the waste material.']
  waste_identifier varchar [note: 'Business identifier such as a manifest or tracking number assigned to the waste event.']
  waste_origin varchar [note: 'Business process that generated the waste.']
  waste_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the waste record.']
  waste_type varchar [note: 'Classification of waste by regulatory definition.']

  Note: 'Hazardous and non-hazardous waste generation, storage, and disposal record supporting EPA RCRA compliance and ISO 14001 waste management objectives. Captures waste generation date, waste type (hazardous, universal, non-hazardous), EPA waste code, quantity generated, storage location, disposal method, licensed disposal contractor, manifest number, disposal date, and chain-of-custody documentation. Primary source for EPA biennial hazardous waste report and waste minimization tracking.'
}

Table compliance.periodic_evaluation {
  periodic_evaluation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for periodic_evaluation']
  assessed_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific compliance obligation evaluated.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee or external auditor who performed the assessment.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical site or facility where the assessment was performed.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department accountable for the assessed obligation.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Periodic compliance evaluations are conducted per project to verify ongoing conformance.']
  assessment_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code assigned to the evaluation for tracking and reference.']
  assessment_date date [note: 'Date on which the compliance assessment was performed.']
  assessment_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to conduct the evaluation.']
  assessment_scope varchar [note: 'Narrative describing the functional or regulatory scope covered by the assessment.']
  assessor_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the person conducting the assessment.']
  conformance_rating decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric rating (0‑100) reflecting the degree of compliance.']
  conformance_status varchar [note: 'Overall result of the assessment against the regulation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the evaluation record was first created in the system.']
  evidence_documentation varchar [note: 'Link or reference to the primary evidence supporting the assessment.']
  gaps_identified varchar [note: 'Summary of compliance gaps discovered during the evaluation.']
  next_assessment_due date [note: 'Planned date for the subsequent periodic evaluation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations captured by the assessor.']
  periodic_evaluation_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the evaluation record.']
  recommended_actions varchar [note: 'Suggested remediation steps to address identified gaps.']
  regulation_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code of the regulation or legal requirement (e.g., ISO14001‑9.1.2).']
  regulation_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the regulation or standard being assessed.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative risk score derived from severity and likelihood.']
  risk_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the risk associated with the identified gaps.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the evaluation record.']

  Note: 'Periodic compliance evaluation record assessing Manufacturing\'s conformance with specific legal register entries or regulatory requirements. Captures assessment date, assessor, assessed obligation or regulation, assessment method (document review, interview, observation, testing), conformance status (compliant, partially compliant, non-compliant), evidence reviewed, gaps identified, and recommended actions. Supports ISO 14001 Clause 9.1.2 and ISO 45001 Clause 9.1.2 compliance evaluation requirements.'
}

Table compliance.controlled_document {
  controlled_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for controlled_document']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual who approved the document.']

  related_regulation_regulatory_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory requirement linked to this document.']
  applicable_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard to which the document applies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ISO 9001|ISO 14001|ISO 45001|IEC 62443|OSHA|EPA|UL|CE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the document was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the document.']
  archive_location varchar [note: 'Physical or digital location where the archived document is stored.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the most recent change or revision.']
  checksum varchar [note: 'Checksum (e.g., SHA-256) for file integrity verification.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the document with respect to its applicable standard.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level of the document.']
  controlled_document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the document record was created.']
  distribution_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of distribution for the document.']
  document_language varchar [note: 'Language in which the document is written.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to the document within the organization.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Category of the controlled document. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: procedure|work_instruction|policy|plan|form|specification|record — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the document becomes effective for use.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the document is no longer valid.']
  file_path varchar [note: 'Path to the electronic file of the document.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the document file in bytes.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document is currently active.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent review of the document.']
  retention_end_date date [note: 'Date when the document\'s retention period expires.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the document must be retained per policy.']
  review_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the document must be reviewed for continued relevance.']
  revision_date date [note: 'Date when the current revision was released.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric identifier of the document revision.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the controlled document.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the document record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version label combining revision number and date.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the record.']

  Note: 'Controlled compliance document master record managing the document control lifecycle for all compliance-critical documents including ISO management system procedures, work instructions, environmental plans, safety programs, regulatory submissions, and certification records. Captures document title, document number, revision level, document type, applicable standard or regulation, owner, approval status, effective date, review due date, and distribution scope. Supports ISO document control requirements (ISO 9001 Clause 7.5).'
}

Table compliance.process_hazard {
  process_hazard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for process_hazard']
  parent_process_hazard_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on process_hazard (parent_process_hazard_id)']
  associated_processes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of business processes impacted by the hazard.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the hazard with relevant regulations.']
  control_measures varchar [note: 'Mitigation actions or engineering controls applied to reduce the hazard.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hazard record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the nature, cause, and potential impact of the hazard.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the hazard record became effective.']
  emergency_response_procedure varchar [note: 'Reference to the emergency response procedure for the hazard.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the hazard record expires or is superseded, if applicable.']
  exposure_limit_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the exposure limit.']
  exposure_limit_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable exposure level for the hazard.']
  hazard_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the hazard type.']
  hazard_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the hazard for quick reference.']
  hazard_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the hazard as used in safety documentation.']
  hazard_owner varchar [note: 'Department or individual responsible for managing the hazard.']
  hazard_subcategory varchar [note: 'More specific classification within the main hazard category.']
  incident_history_flag boolean [note: 'True if historical incidents have been recorded for this hazard.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hazard is currently active and applicable.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of the hazard.']
  likelihood_level varchar [note: 'Estimated probability of occurrence for the hazard.']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Detailed plan outlining steps to mitigate or eliminate the hazard.']
  monitoring_method varchar [note: 'Method used to monitor the hazard.']
  monitoring_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether continuous monitoring is required for this hazard.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any additional information or comments.']
  regulatory_standard varchar [note: 'Applicable regulatory or industry standards governing the hazard (e.g., ISO 14001, OSHA, EPA, IEC 62443).']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Planned interval in months between hazard reviews.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Combined risk rating derived from severity and likelihood.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Potential impact severity if the hazard materializes.']
  training_program varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the training program associated with the hazard.']
  training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if specific training is required for personnel handling the hazard.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the hazard record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for process_hazard. Referenced by process_hazard_id.'
}

Table compliance.emission_source {
  emission_source_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for emission_source']

  parent_emission_source_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on emission_source (parent_emission_source_id)']

  category varchar [note: 'Broad environmental category of the emissions produced.']
  compliance_certification_code varchar [note: 'Code of any certification or permit associated with the source (e.g., EPA permit number).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the emission source.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date on which the emission source was permanently shut down.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the source, its function, and any relevant notes.']
  emission_factor decimal(12,4) [note: 'Quantitative factor representing emissions per unit of activity (e.g., kg CO₂e per MWh).']
  last_reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the most recent emission report for this source was submitted.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the emission source (decimal degrees).']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the emission source (decimal degrees).']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the emission source (e.g., Boiler A, Paint Shop).']
  next_report_due_date date [note: 'Calendar date by which the next emission report must be filed.']
  plant_location varchar [note: 'Physical address or site name of the plant housing the source.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the emission source record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the emission source record.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Primary agency overseeing the source\'s emissions.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'How often emissions from this source are reported.']
  source_code varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned code used to reference the emission source in reports and regulatory filings.']
  source_operating_company varchar [note: 'External or internal company that operates the emission source.']
  source_owner varchar [note: 'Business unit or department responsible for the emission source.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the emission source.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the source based on its physical or operational characteristics.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit used for the emission factor (e.g., kilograms CO₂e).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for emission_source. Referenced by source_id.'
}

Table compliance.facility {
  facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for facility']
  parent_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent facility in a hierarchical location structure, if applicable.']
  related_facility_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on facility (related_facility_id)']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the facility.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, building, etc.).']
  audit_status varchar [note: 'Current status of internal or external audits.']
  backup_generator_present boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a backup generator is installed on site.']
  capacity_units varchar [note: 'Units used to express the facility\'s production or storage capacity (e.g., units per day, pallets).']
  city varchar [note: 'City where the facility is located.']
  compliance_iec_62443_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility meets IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards for industrial automation.']
  compliance_iso_14001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is certified to ISO 14001 environmental management standards.']
  compliance_iso_45001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is certified to ISO 45001 occupational health and safety standards.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the facility resides. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|DEU|FRA|CHN|JPN|IND|BRA — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the facility record was first created in the system.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number to use for emergency communications.']
  emissions_co2_tons decimal(10,3) [note: 'Annual carbon dioxide emissions from the facility in metric tons.']
  energy_consumption_mwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total electricity consumption of the facility measured in megawatt‑hours for the reporting period.']
  epa_permit_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the EPA permit.']
  epa_permit_number varchar [note: 'Environmental Protection Agency permit identifier for emissions or waste handling.']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'External or legacy code used to identify the facility in enterprise systems.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Category of the facility indicating its primary function.']
  fire_suppression_type varchar [note: 'Type of fire suppression system installed (e.g., sprinkler, FM‑200).']
  hazardous_material_storage boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility stores hazardous materials.']
  hvac_type varchar [note: 'Heating, ventilation, and air‑conditioning system type used.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent inspection.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the current insurance policy expires.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the facility\'s insurance coverage.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory or safety inspection.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the facility (decimal degrees).']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the facility (decimal degrees).']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the facility.']
  next_inspection_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required inspection.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code for the facility address.']
  power_source varchar [note: 'Primary source of electrical power (e.g., grid, solar, generator).']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for the primary facility contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the main facility contact person.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for the primary facility contact.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the facility.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Security classification of the facility based on risk assessment.']
  size_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total usable floor area of the facility in square feet.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the facility location.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the facility.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the facility.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the facility record.']
  waste_generated_tons decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total waste generated on site measured in metric tons.']
  water_usage_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Volume of water consumed by the facility in cubic metres.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for facility. Referenced by related_facility_id.'
}

Table project.project_header {
  project_header_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the project header record.']
  account_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Project execution location is tied to a specific customer site for safety, compliance, and resource planning.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Multi‑entity manufacturing groups consolidate project results by company code for regulatory and internal reporting.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Project ownership & billing require linking each project to the customer account for financial reporting and contract management.']
  customer_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Project sponsor/primary contact is a customer contact; needed for stakeholder registers and communication plans.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee managing the project.']
  health_and_safety_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the health and safety plan document.']
  parent_project_header_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on project_header (parent_project_header_id)']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Project profitability reporting is done per profit center; linking enables profit‑center based P&L statements.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Project charter defines target product SKU for which the project delivers a new manufacturing line; essential for product‑specific reporting.']
  supply_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.plan. Business justification: Project Execution Dashboard requires the supply plan used to meet project material needs.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to date.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Date when the project actually completed.']
  actual_oee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured OEE achieved.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Date when the project actually started.']
  actual_work_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual labor hours recorded.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the project received formal approval.']
  automation_project_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.automation_project. Business justification: Automation Implementation Tracking report requires linking each overall project to its specific automation project for budgeting and schedule alignment.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget amount for the project.']
  change_order_count varchar [note: 'Total number of engineering change orders (ECO/ECN) associated.']
  closeout_date date [note: 'Date when the project was officially closed.']
  completed_milestone_count varchar [note: 'Number of milestones completed.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance status of the project.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Associated contract number for EPC or procurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the project record was created.']
  critical_path_duration_days varchar [note: 'Duration of the project\'s critical path.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for budget and cost. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|JPY|GBP|CNY|CHF — promote to reference product]']
  environmental_impact_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score assessing environmental impact per ISO 14001.']
  expected_oee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness for the project.']
  external_project_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used in external systems (e.g., ERP, PLM).']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Source of funding for the project.']
  governance_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of governance approval for the project.']
  is_template boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this project record is a reusable template.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average lead time for critical materials in days.']
  location varchar [note: 'Primary geographic location of the project (city, site).']
  milestone_count varchar [note: 'Number of milestones defined for the project.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date for the project.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date for the project.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the project.']
  procurement_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy used for procuring materials.']
  project_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the project.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code for the project.']
  project_header_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the project\'s scope and objectives.']
  project_header_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the project.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the project.']
  project_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the project lifecycle.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the project type.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal region code for reporting.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment of the project.']
  safety_incident_count varchar [note: 'Number of safety incidents recorded for the project.']
  sponsor_business_unit varchar [note: 'Business unit that sponsors the project.']
  total_work_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total labor hours planned for the project.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the project record.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'Hierarchical code representing the project\'s WBS element.']

  Note: 'Master record for all capital projects, engineering projects, plant construction initiatives, and project-based manufacturing engagements. Serves as the SSOT for project identity, classification, lifecycle stage, budget baseline, schedule baseline, sponsoring business unit, project manager assignment, priority, and governance attributes. Covers EPC (Engineering, Procurement, Construction), CapEx, NPI, and MTO project types.'
}

Table project.wbs_element {
  wbs_element_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the WBS element.']
  capacity_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.capacity_plan. Business justification: Enables Capacity Utilization Report per WBS element, linking work center capacity to project breakdown.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: WBS cost tracking requires cost‑center assignment for budgeting and reporting; cost_center_code is denormalized.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person accountable for delivering the WBS element.']
  parent_wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on wbs_element (parent_wbs_element_id)']
  parent_wbs_wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent WBS element; null for top‑level elements.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the overarching project to which this WBS element belongs.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: WBS elements track material requirements; linking to SKU enables accurate material planning and cost allocation per product.']
  account_assignment_category varchar [note: 'Category used for financial posting (e.g., cost, revenue, internal order, asset).']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Incurred cost amount recorded against the WBS element.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Date when work on the WBS element was completed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Date when work on the WBS element actually commenced.']
  billing_element_flag boolean [note: 'True if the WBS element is billable to a customer or external party.']
  cost_variance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between planned and actual cost (planned_cost - actual_cost).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the WBS element record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for cost amounts.']
  milestone_flag boolean [note: 'True if the element represents a project milestone.']
  percent_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Progress of the WBS element expressed as a percentage (0‑100).']
  planned_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budgeted cost amount allocated to the WBS element.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled finish date for the WBS element as per project plan.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date for the WBS element as per project plan.']
  responsible_department_code varchar [note: 'Organizational department that owns the WBS element.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current schedule performance relative to the baseline.']
  short_description varchar [note: 'Brief description (max 100 characters) for UI listings.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the WBS element record.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the WBS element, typically hierarchical (e.g., 1.2.3).']
  wbs_element_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the WBS element\'s scope and purpose.']
  wbs_element_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of the element within the WBS hierarchy (root = 1).']
  wbs_element_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the WBS element.']
  wbs_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the WBS element by business purpose.']

  Note: 'Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element master record representing a discrete node in the hierarchical project decomposition tree. Captures WBS code, description, level, parent WBS reference, responsible cost center, billing element flag, account assignment category, planned cost, actual cost, and status. Aligns with SAP PS WBS element (PRPS) and PMI WBS standards.'
}

Table project.milestone {
  milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the project milestone.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.control_system. Business justification: Control System Commissioning Milestone ties a project milestone to the specific control system being commissioned, used in status dashboards.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the party (person or organization) accountable for delivering the milestone.']
  predecessor_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on milestone (predecessor_milestone_id)']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent project containing this milestone.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Work Breakdown Structure element to which the milestone belongs.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred to complete the milestone.']
  actual_date date [note: 'Date when the milestone was actually completed.']
  actual_duration_days varchar [note: 'Actual number of calendar days taken to complete the milestone.']
  budgeted_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned cost budget allocated for the milestone.']
  completion_criteria varchar [note: 'Specific criteria that must be satisfied for the milestone to be considered complete.']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory or contractual requirement linked to the milestone.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the milestone against its requirement.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center to which the milestone costs are charged.']
  cost_variance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between budgeted and actual cost (budgeted_cost - actual_cost).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the milestone record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  forecast_date date [note: 'Updated forecast date based on latest project outlook.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone is classified as critical for project success.']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the milestone (e.g., M-001, M-002).']
  milestone_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the milestone scope and deliverables.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the milestone.']
  milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the milestone.']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Category of the milestone indicating its purpose (e.g., contractual, internal, payment trigger).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments captured by project team members.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount associated with the milestone payment trigger.']
  payment_trigger boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone serves as a trigger for invoicing or payment.']
  planned_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the milestone is expected to be achieved.']
  planned_duration_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of calendar days allocated to complete the milestone.']
  responsible_party_role varchar [note: 'Role of the responsible party with respect to the milestone.']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of risks associated with the milestone.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative risk rating assigned to the milestone.']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the milestone within the project schedule.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the milestone record.']

  Note: 'Project milestone master record defining key schedule gates, contractual delivery checkpoints, payment trigger events, and phase-completion criteria within a project or WBS element. Captures milestone name, type (contractual, internal, payment), planned date, forecast date, actual completion date, responsible party, completion criteria, and linked billing schedule reference.'
}

Table project.activity {
  activity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the project activity record.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Device Installation Activity logs which physical device is being installed, needed for work order execution and traceability.']
  material_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.material_requirement. Business justification: Supports Project Material Allocation process, tying each activity to its MRP‑generated material requirement.']
  predecessor_activity_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on activity (predecessor_activity_id)']
  product_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: Activities such as build or test reference a specific product configuration; needed for configuration‑level progress and quality tracking.']
  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.production_work_order. Business justification: EXECUTION TRACKING: Links a project activity to the actual production work order that executes it, required for activity status and cost roll‑up.']
  project_contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of an external contract or subcontract linked to the activity.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent project to which this activity belongs.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: CAPACITY PLANNING: Project activities need a specific work center assignment for scheduling and capacity load reports.']
  activity_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the activity, used in schedules and reports.']
  activity_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the work to be performed.']
  activity_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or title of the activity.']
  activity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the activity.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the activity (e.g., internal work, external subcontract, cost‑only, or milestone).']
  actual_finish_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when work on the activity actually finished.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when work on the activity actually began.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center responsible for the activity\'s expenses.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the activity record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned duration of the activity expressed in hours.']
  earned_value_acwp decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred for the work performed on the activity.']
  earned_value_bcwp decimal(15,2) [note: 'Value of work actually performed, expressed as budgeted cost.']
  earned_value_bcws decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned cost of work scheduled for the activity (Earned Value Management).']
  equipment_cost_actual decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred for equipment during the activity.']
  equipment_cost_estimated decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned cost of equipment usage for the activity.']
  float_days decimal(10,2) [note: 'Available scheduling float for the activity in days.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the activity lies on the project critical path.']
  labor_hours_actual decimal(10,2) [note: 'Labor hours actually recorded for the activity.']
  labor_hours_estimated decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned labor effort for the activity in hours.']
  material_cost_actual decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual cost of materials consumed by the activity.']
  material_cost_estimated decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned cost of materials required for the activity.']
  milestone_flag boolean [note: 'True if the activity represents a project milestone.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments entered by planners.']
  percent_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current progress of the activity expressed as a percentage.']
  planned_finish_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for activity completion.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for activity start.']
  predecessor_activity_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of activity IDs that must finish before this activity can start.']
  resource_requirements varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of labor, material, and equipment needed.']
  successor_activity_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of activity IDs that follow this activity in the schedule.']
  total_cost_estimated decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of estimated labor, material, and equipment costs for the activity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the activity record.']
  wbs_element varchar [note: 'Identifier of the WBS element to which the activity belongs.']

  Note: 'Granular project activity (network activity) record representing a discrete work package or task within the project schedule network. Captures activity ID, description, activity type (internal, external, cost), work center assignment, planned start/finish, actual start/finish, duration, float, predecessor/successor relationships, resource requirements, and earned value data (BCWS, BCWP, ACWP).'
}

Table project.project_budget {
  project_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the project budget record.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person responsible for the budget.']
  planning_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.planning_parameter. Business justification: Aligns budgeting assumptions with supply planning parameters for accurate project budgeting.']
  primary_project_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the budget record.']
  product_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Budget allocations are often planned per product family to align financial planning with product strategy.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which this budget belongs.']
  transferred_from_project_budget_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on project_budget (transferred_from_project_budget_id)']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the WBS element associated with this budget line.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate the budget (manual, automatic, roll‑up).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the budget was formally approved.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the budget (e.g., capital project, maintenance, R&D).']
  budget_number varchar [note: 'External budget number or code used for reference in financial systems.']
  budget_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the budget funds.']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget as Capital Expenditure, Operational Expenditure, or mixed.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or justification for the latest budget change.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of the budget that has been committed to purchase orders or contracts.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Code of the cost center responsible for the budget.']
  cost_element_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the cost element (e.g., labor, material, overhead).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the budget amounts.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the budget expires or is superseded.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the budget becomes effective for spending.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the budget applies.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the budget is locked from further changes.']
  last_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent budget approval action.']
  original_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved original budget amount at the time of creation.']
  owner_role varchar [note: 'Role or title of the budget owner (e.g., Project Manager, Finance Lead).']
  project_budget_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the budget line purpose or scope.']
  project_budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget.']
  remaining_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget amount still available after commitments and spend.']
  revised_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget amount after approved revisions or supplements.']
  spent_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual expenditures recorded against the budget.']
  transferred_in_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount transferred into this budget from other budgets.']
  transferred_out_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount transferred out of this budget to other budgets.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the budget record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the budget (e.g., initial, revised).']

  Note: 'Project budget record capturing the approved cost baseline for a project or WBS element by cost element, fiscal year, and budget version. Tracks original budget, approved supplements, transfers, returns, current budget, and commitment values. Supports CapEx/OpEx split, budget availability control, and multi-year capital planning. Aligns with SAP PS budget management (BPGE/BPJA).'
}

Table project.cost_actual {
  cost_actual_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each actual cost posting record.']
  activity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific project activity or task associated with the cost.']
  cost_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_allocation. Business justification: Applying cost‑allocation rules to actual project cost lines is required for internal cost distribution reporting.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key of the cost center entity.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Actual project cost lines must be posted to GL accounts for statutory financial statements and audit trails.']
  primary_cost_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who created the cost line record.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which this cost line belongs.']
  resource_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material, labor, or service resource charged.']
  reversal_cost_actual_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on cost_actual (reversal_cost_actual_id)']
  tertiary_cost_posting_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User who performed the cost posting operation.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the WBS element charged with this cost.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value posted in the transaction currency.']
  amount_controlling_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Posted amount converted to the company’s controlling currency.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the cost line.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the detailed audit trail for this cost line.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned amount for this cost line according to the budget.']
  cost_actual_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description or notes for the cost line.']
  cost_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the cost allocated to this line based on the rule.']
  cost_allocation_rule varchar [note: 'Name or code of the rule used to allocate overhead or indirect costs.']
  cost_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the cost (direct, indirect, etc.).']
  cost_element_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the type of cost (e.g., labor, material, overhead).']
  cost_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost (e.g., labor, material, service).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cost line record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the posting currency.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the source document (e.g., invoice, goods receipt).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Rate used to convert posting currency to controlling currency.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to an external document or system (e.g., vendor invoice).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter of the posting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the cost was posted.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'Internal order identifier linked to the cost posting.']
  is_budgeted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost line was part of the approved budget.']
  is_manual_entry boolean [note: 'True if the cost line was entered manually rather than via interface.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the posting document.']
  overhead_allocation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cost line includes overhead allocation.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the cost was posted.']
  posting_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the cost line.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the cost posting was recorded.']
  project_phase varchar [note: 'Lifecycle phase of the project when the cost was incurred.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of the resource consumed (e.g., hours, units, kilograms).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of the cost data.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the posted amount, if applicable.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity (e.g., EA, KG, HOUR).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between actual amount and budgeted amount.']
  variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance relative to the budgeted amount.']

  Note: 'Actual cost posting record capturing all cost transactions charged to a WBS element or project activity, including labor confirmations, material goods issues, purchase order commitments, service entry sheet postings, and overhead allocations. Captures cost element, cost center origin, posting date, document reference, quantity, and amount in project and controlling currency.'
}

Table project.commitment {
  commitment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the project commitment record.']
  activity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific project activity or task the commitment funds.']
  internal_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.internal_order. Business justification: Procurement commitments are booked against internal orders to control budget and cost allocation.']
  originating_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on commitment (originating_commitment_id)']
  procurement_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_contract. Business justification: Project commitments (fund reservations) are tied to procurement contracts; the FK enables commitment‑to‑contract reconciliation.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which the commitment belongs.']
  replenishment_proposal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.replenishment_proposal. Business justification: Allows Project Commitment Review to reference the supply proposal that generated the commitment.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or supplier associated with the commitment.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the WBS element linked to the commitment.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date when the goods or services were actually delivered.']
  actual_spent_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual monetary amount spent against the commitment.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the commitment.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the commitment was approved.']
  budgeted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned monetary amount allocated for the commitment.']
  commitment_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the commitment was recorded or became effective.']
  commitment_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the commitment purpose or details.']
  commitment_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the commitment by the source system.']
  commitment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the commitment.']
  commitment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the origin of the commitment such as purchase order, requisition, or fund reservation.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross monetary value of the commitment before taxes or adjustments.']
  committed_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of goods or services committed.']
  cost_element_code varchar [note: 'Accounting cost element code used for budgeting and expense tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the commitment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the monetary values.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for delivery of the committed goods or services.']
  is_funds_reserved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether funds have been reserved for this commitment.']
  net_committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net monetary value after tax and adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Supplementary remarks or comments related to the commitment.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the fund reservation record linked to the commitment.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the committed amount.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the committed quantity (e.g., EA, KG, L).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the commitment record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between budgeted and actual spent amounts.']

  Note: 'Open commitment record tracking purchase order commitments, purchase requisition commitments, and funds reservations against WBS elements and project activities. Captures commitment type, originating document, vendor, cost element, committed quantity, committed value, expected delivery date, and open/closed status. Critical for budget availability control and cash flow forecasting.'
}

Table project.plan_version {
  plan_version_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each version of a project plan.']
  baseline_plan_version_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on plan_version (baseline_plan_version_id)']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent project to which this plan version belongs.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the version received final approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the version after review by governance.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the version.']
  baseline_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this version is the active baseline for earned‑value management.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the engineering change order or change request that triggered this version.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the version complies with relevant regulatory and internal standards.']
  cost_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between total planned cost and actual cost incurred to date.']
  created_by_department varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for creating the version.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the version record was initially created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the cost fields.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this version ceases to be operative (null if open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this version becomes the operative baseline.']
  freeze_date date [note: 'Date on which the plan version was frozen and became immutable for reporting.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the version is currently active for reporting.']
  last_reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the reviewer who performed the latest review.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent formal review of the version.']
  milestone_count varchar [note: 'Number of defined milestones captured in this version of the plan.']
  plan_version_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual description of the purpose or scope of this version.']
  plan_version_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the version record.']
  planned_duration_days varchar [note: 'Planned overall duration of the project plan version expressed in calendar days.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification assigned to this version during planning.']
  schedule_variance_days varchar [note: 'Difference in days between planned schedule and actual schedule at the time of snapshot.']
  total_planned_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregated cost budget for the entire project plan version, expressed in the selected currency.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that performed the latest update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the version record.']
  version_comment varchar [note: 'Additional comments or notes entered by the planner during version creation.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential integer indicating the version order of the project plan.']
  version_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the version is the original baseline, a re-baseline, or a what‑if scenario.']
  wbs_code varchar [note: 'Hierarchical code representing the WBS element associated with this version.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the version record.']

  Note: 'Versioned project plan snapshot record capturing the full schedule and cost plan baseline at a specific point in time. Supports baseline management (original baseline, re-baseline, what-if scenarios), earned value management (EVM) reference, and schedule variance tracking. Captures version number, version type, freeze date, total planned cost, planned duration, and approval status.'
}

Table project.resource_assignment {
  resource_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each resource assignment record.']
  activity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the project activity or WBS element to which the resource is assigned.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the assigned resource (personnel, equipment, or contractor).']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the physical location where the resource performs the work.']
  replaced_resource_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on resource_assignment (replaced_resource_assignment_id)']
  actual_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of work hours actually logged for the assignment.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager who approved the assignment.']
  assignment_role varchar [note: 'Role performed by the resource on the activity (e.g., Engineer, Technician).']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the assignment.']
  assignment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the resource is internal staff or external contractor.']
  billing_rate_type varchar [note: 'Type of billing rate applied to the assignment.']
  billing_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the assignment is billable to a customer.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center to which the assignment cost is charged.']
  cost_rate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary rate (per hour or per unit) charged for the resource.']
  cost_rate_currency varchar [note: 'Currency of the cost rate applied to the resource.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment record was first created.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Code of the department responsible for the resource.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the assignment becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the assignment ends or expires (null if open‑ended).']
  is_overtime boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the assignment includes overtime work.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last updated the record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the assignment.']
  overtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of overtime hours logged for the assignment.']
  planned_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of work hours originally planned for the assignment.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the resource assignment.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Logical status of the record for soft‑delete handling.']
  resource_utilization_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the resource\'s employment type for utilization reporting.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the work shift for the assignment.']
  skill_level varchar [note: 'Declared skill proficiency of the resource for the assigned role.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the assignment record.']
  utilization_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the resource\'s available capacity consumed by this assignment.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for optimistic concurrency control.']

  Note: 'Project resource assignment record linking workforce personnel, work centers, equipment, or external contractors to specific project activities or WBS elements. Captures resource type, assigned resource identity, planned hours/quantity, actual hours/quantity, assignment period, role, skill requirement, utilization percentage, and cost rate applied.'
}

Table project.procurement_item {
  procurement_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the procurement line item within a project.']
  blanket_procurement_item_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on procurement_item (blanket_procurement_item_id)']
  planned_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.planned_order. Business justification: Needed for Project Procurement Tracking report, showing which planned order fulfills each procurement item.']
  procurement_header_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent procurement transaction header that groups line items for the same purchase request.']
  procurement_purchase_requisition_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_requisition. Business justification: Project procurement items originate from a purchase requisition; linking enables traceability from project spend back to the original requisition.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Procurement items belong to a specific project; linking enables aggregation, reporting and eliminates the need for any ad‑hoc project identifiers stored elsewhere.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Project procurement items are fulfilled by purchase orders; the FK allows project cost roll‑up and PO status monitoring.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Procurement lines must be tied to a definitive SKU for inventory, cost, and compliance tracking; replaces denormalized material_number.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the external supplier providing the item.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.wbs_element. Business justification: The string column wbs_element_code is a duplicate of the WBS element identifier; adding a proper foreign key to wbs_element provides full hierarchy access and removes the redundant code column.']
  actual_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Observed number of days between order and receipt.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow state of the procurement line.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the procurement complies with internal and external regulations.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to a master contract or agreement governing the purchase.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center responsible for the expense of this procurement item.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  delivery_date date [note: 'Date the supplier is expected to deliver the item.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Discounts granted on the line item.']
  expected_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Planned number of days between order and delivery.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the supplier issued the invoice.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Supplier invoice identifier for this line item.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the invoice.']
  is_invoiced boolean [note: 'True when an invoice has been recorded for the line.']
  is_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the goods have been received (true) or not (false).']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the material, service, or equipment being procured.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall state of the procurement line from creation to closure.']
  line_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Requested quantity of the item in the unit of measure.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line item within the procurement header.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Net monetary value after tax and discount (total_price + tax_amount - discount_amount).']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment must be made to the vendor.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Payment settlement status for the invoice.']
  procurement_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the spend type for reporting and compliance.']
  procurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to acquire the item, e.g., internal requisition or external purchase.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the item was actually received on site.']
  receipt_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of the item actually received.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments entered by users.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk assessment of the procurement item (e.g., supplier risk, safety risk).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system for the record (e.g., SAP Ariba, SAP S/4HANA).']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Tax applied to the line item.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction or rate code applied to the line.']
  total_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Line quantity multiplied by unit price (gross amount).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for the quantity (e.g., EA, KG, L).']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed price per unit of the item before taxes and discounts.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created the record.']

  Note: 'Project-specific procurement item record tracking purchase requisitions and purchase orders raised against WBS elements for capital equipment, construction services, and engineering materials. Captures item description, vendor, quantity, unit price, total value, delivery date, WBS assignment, goods receipt status, and invoice verification status. Bridges project management and procurement domains.'
}

Table project.project_status_event {
  project_status_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for each immutable project status event record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who triggered the status change.']
  preceding_project_status_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on project_status_event (preceding_project_status_event_id)']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which this status event belongs.']
  approval_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., document number or URL) to the approval document associated with the event, if applicable.']
  event_comment varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments captured at the time of the event.']
  event_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason explaining why the status change was made.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the status event occurred.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the event indicating the nature of the status transition or related action.']
  is_audit_trail boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this record is part of the compliance audit trail.']
  new_status varchar [note: 'Project status value after this event was applied.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'Project status value before this event was applied.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this event record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  record_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Siemens Opcenter) that generated the event.']
  source_system_event_code varchar [note: 'Original identifier of the event in the source system.']
  triggered_by_user_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the user who triggered the event.']

  Note: 'Immutable event log capturing every status transition and lifecycle milestone in the project lifecycle including project creation, approval, release, execution start, suspension, completion, settlement, and closure. Captures event type, previous status, new status, event timestamp, triggered by user, and associated approval document reference. Supports project governance audit trail.'
}

Table project.issue {
  issue_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the project issue record.']
  alarm_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.alarm_event. Business justification: Issue Management process records the originating alarm event to support root‑cause analysis and compliance reporting.']
  issue_employee_id varchar [note: 'Person or role accountable for resolving the issue.']
  issue_reporter_employee_id varchar [note: 'Person who originally logged the issue.']
  parent_issue_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on issue (parent_issue_id)']
  related_project_project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which this issue belongs.']
  risk_register_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.risk_register. Business justification: Lets Issue Log incorporate supply‑side risk assessments for material/vendor risks.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'WBS code linking the issue to a specific work package.']
  action_items varchar [note: 'List of concrete actions assigned to resolve the issue.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Real cost incurred after issue resolution.']
  actual_resolution_date date [note: 'Date the issue was actually closed or resolved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was initially created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for cost fields.']
  escalation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the issue has been escalated for higher‑level attention.']
  estimated_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected financial impact if the issue materializes.']
  identified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the issue was first recorded in the system.']
  impact varchar [note: 'Potential effect on schedule, cost, or scope if the issue materializes.']
  issue_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the problem, risk, or change request.']
  issue_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable unique code assigned to the issue for tracking across systems.']
  issue_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the issue within its lifecycle.']
  issue_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the record.']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Planned actions to reduce probability or impact.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Combined assessment of severity, probability, and impact used for triage.']
  probability_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Likelihood of the issue occurring, expressed as a percentage.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite numeric score derived from severity, probability, and impact.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified underlying cause of the issue.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Impact severity rating assigned by the project team.']
  target_resolution_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the issue should be closed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']

  Note: 'Project issue and risk register record capturing identified risks, open issues, blockers, and action items that threaten project schedule, cost, or scope. Captures issue type (risk, issue, change request, blocker), severity, probability, impact, owner, mitigation plan, target resolution date, actual resolution date, and escalation status. Supports PMO governance and project health reporting.'
}

Table project.project_change_request {
  project_change_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the project change request record.']
  primary_project_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who originated the change request.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent project to which the change request belongs.']
  quaternary_project_updated_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the latest update.']
  superseded_project_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on project_change_request (superseded_project_change_request_id)']
  tertiary_project_created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who initially created the change request record.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Work‑breakdown‑structure element that will be affected by the change.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval outcome of the change request.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the change request was approved or rejected.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Root cause or business driver prompting the change request.']
  change_request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the change request, used in communications and tracking.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Category of the change request indicating what aspect of the project is affected.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'List of regulatory or internal compliance items impacted by the change.']
  cost_delta_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net change in project budget resulting from the request (positive for increase, negative for decrease).']
  cost_delta_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost delta. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|JPY|CNY|CHF|CAD|AUD|NZD|SEK|NOK|DKK|ZAR|INR|BRL — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the change request record was first created in the system.']
  impact_assessment varchar [note: 'Qualitative and quantitative analysis of how the change will affect cost, schedule, scope, and risk.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the change request is deemed critical to project success.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business‑defined urgency level for processing the change request.']
  project_change_request_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the requested change, including rationale and scope.']
  project_change_request_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the change request.']
  regulatory_impact varchar [note: 'Description of how the change affects regulatory reporting or certifications.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the change request was formally submitted.']
  revised_baseline_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the new project baseline version that incorporates this change.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk impact of the change on project objectives.']
  schedule_delta_days varchar [note: 'Planned change in project duration expressed in calendar days (positive for extension, negative for compression).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the change request record.']

  Note: 'Project change request (PCR) record governing scope changes, schedule changes, and budget amendments to an approved project baseline. Captures change request number, description, change type (scope, schedule, cost, technical), impact assessment, requested by, approval workflow status, approved/rejected date, cost delta, schedule delta, and revised baseline reference.'
}

Table project.timesheet {
  timesheet_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each timesheet entry.']
  corrected_timesheet_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on timesheet (corrected_timesheet_id)']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant or site where work was performed.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which the labor is allocated.']
  timesheet_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or supervisor who approved the entry.']
  timesheet_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee or contractor who performed the work.']
  timesheet_entered_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who entered the timesheet data.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Work Breakdown Structure element the labor is charged to.']
  activity_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the work performed.']
  activity_type varchar [note: 'Category of work performed for the timesheet entry. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: design|assembly|testing|maintenance|inspection|setup|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the timesheet line.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the entry was approved.']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the audit log entry for this record.']
  batch_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the timesheet batch or header that groups related line entries.']
  billable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the labor entry is billable to the customer.']
  compliance_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the specific compliance requirement (e.g., ISO45001).']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the labor entry is subject to regulatory compliance tracking.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center code for financial allocation.']
  cost_rate decimal(12,4) [note: 'Hourly billing or cost rate applicable to the employee for this activity.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the timesheet line was initially created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the labor.']
  entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the line was entered into the system.']
  expense_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the expense component.']
  expense_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the expense amount.']
  expense_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the entry includes a reimbursable expense.']
  expense_type varchar [note: 'Category of the expense, if expense_flag is true.']
  external_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier from an external timesheet or payroll system.']
  hours_worked decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total regular labor hours recorded for the activity.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Soft‑delete flag indicating logical removal.']
  labor_category varchar [note: 'Classification of labor for costing and compliance purposes.']
  labor_grade varchar [note: 'Skill or pay grade of the labor resource.']
  labor_rate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rate applied to the labor.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the line within the timesheet batch.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Additional hours worked beyond regular schedule, if any.']
  overtime_multiplier decimal(4,2) [note: 'Factor applied to cost_rate for overtime hours.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the timesheet line.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the labor was performed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system (e.g., SAP, Workday).']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier of the record in the source system.']
  total_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Calculated cost = (hours_worked + overtime_hours) * cost_rate.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Optimistic lock version for concurrency control.']
  work_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the labor was performed.']

  Note: 'Project labor time recording document capturing hours worked by employees and contractors against specific WBS elements and project activities.
