Captures employee/contractor identity, work date, WBS element, activity type, hours worked, activity description, approval status, and cost rate. Feeds actual labor cost postings to project cost accounting.'
}

Table project.commissioning_checklist {
  commissioning_checklist_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the commissioning checklist record.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Commissioning Checklist must reference the exact device being commissioned to verify configuration and safety sign‑offs.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific equipment or asset being commissioned.']
  parent_commissioning_checklist_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on commissioning_checklist (parent_commissioning_checklist_id)']
  primary_commissioning_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer accountable for completing the checklist.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the capital or engineering project associated with the checklist.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external vendor, if applicable.']
  actual_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Measured effort duration from actual start to actual end.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when work on the checklist actually finished.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when work on the checklist actually began.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the checklist after review.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the checklist received formal approval.']
  change_order_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to any engineering change order that impacts the checklist.']
  checklist_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the checklist for tracking and reference.']
  checklist_type varchar [note: 'Category of the checklist reflecting the phase of plant commissioning.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the engineer or reviewers.']
  commissioning_checklist_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the checklist.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date on which the checklist was formally completed.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard governing the commissioning activity.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the cost estimate.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Financial estimate for the commissioning work.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the checklist record was first created in the system.']
  documentation_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or URL linking to detailed supporting documents (e.g., test reports).']
  estimated_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned effort duration for completing the checklist.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the checklist execution was initiated.']
  failed_item_count varchar [note: 'How many checklist items have been marked as failed.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'True if the checklist is on the project\'s critical path.']
  is_environmental_review_done boolean [note: 'True if an environmental compliance review was performed and closed.']
  is_external_vendor_involved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an external supplier contributed to the commissioning.']
  is_overtime_required boolean [note: 'True if overtime work is needed to meet the schedule.']
  is_training_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether specialized training is required for the commissioning task.']
  line_item_count varchar [note: 'Count of individual verification items defined in the checklist.']
  overall_pass boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entire checklist met the pass criteria (true = pass).']
  overtime_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of overtime hours planned or used.']
  passed_item_count varchar [note: 'How many checklist items have been marked as passed.']
  punch_list_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of open punch‑list items remaining after the checklist execution.']
  quality_sign_off boolean [note: 'Indicates whether quality sign‑off was obtained (true = yes).']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the checklist document, incremented on each change.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative risk rating associated with the commissioning activity.']
  safety_sign_off boolean [note: 'Indicates whether safety sign‑off was obtained (true = yes).']
  scheduled_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date for the checklist execution.']
  scheduled_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date for the checklist execution.']
  system_scope varchar [note: 'Name or code of the system/subsystem that the checklist covers (e.g., HVAC, Control System).']
  training_completion_date date [note: 'Date when required training was completed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the checklist record.']

  Note: 'Plant commissioning and startup checklist record tracking the structured verification activities required to bring a new production asset, automation system, or facility into operational service. Captures checklist type (mechanical completion, pre-commissioning, commissioning, startup), system/subsystem scope, line items with pass/fail status, responsible engineer, completion date, and punch list item count.'
}

Table project.punch_list_item {
  punch_list_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'System generated unique identifier for the punch list item.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Punch‑list items for equipment require linking to the specific device to track resolution and cost impact.']
  parent_punch_list_item_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on punch_list_item (parent_punch_list_item_id)']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the WBS element to which the punch item is linked.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date the punch item was actually closed.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes added by inspectors or contractors.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance state of the punch item.']
  cost_actual decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual monetary cost incurred to resolve the punch item.']
  cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary cost to resolve the punch item.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the punch list record was created in the source system.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date the deficiency was identified during inspection.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the punch item is deemed critical to project safety or schedule.']
  item_number varchar [note: 'Business visible identifier assigned to the punch list item, e.g., PLI-000123.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Plant or area code where the punch item is located.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level indicating urgency: A (critical), B (high), C (normal).']
  punch_list_item_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the punch item type.']
  punch_list_item_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the deficiency or corrective action required.']
  punch_list_item_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the punch item.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Method used to close the punch item.']
  responsible_contractor varchar [note: 'Name of the contractor responsible for addressing the punch item.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity of the issue impacting safety, quality, or schedule.']
  sign_off_by varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who signed off the punch item.']
  sign_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the punch item was formally signed off as completed.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the punch item should be resolved.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the punch list record.']

  Note: 'Individual punch list item record representing an outstanding deficiency, incomplete work item, or corrective action identified during construction inspection, pre-commissioning, or commissioning walkdowns. Captures item number, description, category (A/B/C priority), responsible contractor, target completion date, actual completion date, and sign-off status. Critical for mechanical completion and handover management.'
}

Table project.handover {
  handover_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each project handover record.']
  accepting_organization_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the organization that receives the handover.']
  handover_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the approver.']
  handover_employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the user who created the handover record.']
  handover_updated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the user who last updated the record.']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Identifies the physical network node (site) where handover occurs, supporting Handover Checklist.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the organization that performed the handover.']
  partial_handover_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on handover (partial_handover_id)']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the project whose deliverables are being transferred.']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date when the accepting organization formally accepted the handover.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the handover received formal approval.']
  approved_by_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who approved the handover.']
  as_built_document_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether as‑built drawings and documents are complete.']
  change_order_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of any Engineering Change Order linked to the handover.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Any supplemental remarks entered by users.']
  compliance_check_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance verification.']
  compliance_check_passed boolean [note: 'True if all regulatory and internal compliance checks were satisfied.']
  created_by_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who created the record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the handover record was initially entered into the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter currency code for monetary amounts.']
  documentation_package_status varchar [note: 'Indicates completeness of the documentation handed over.']
  final_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost captured at handover closure.']
  final_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the final cost amount.']
  handover_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the handover took place.']
  handover_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible handover number assigned by the project management system.']
  handover_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the handover record.']
  handover_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the handover is a partial or final transfer of deliverables.']
  location varchar [note: 'Site or plant identifier where the handover was executed.']
  maintenance_plan_details varchar [note: 'Summary of the maintenance schedule, scope, and responsibilities.']
  maintenance_plan_transfer_flag boolean [note: 'True if the ongoing maintenance plan is handed over.']
  outstanding_items_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of remaining issues or actions.']
  outstanding_items_flag boolean [note: 'True if any punch‑list items are still open at handover.']
  record_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source application (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Siemens Teamcenter).']
  review_meeting_attendees varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of participants in the review meeting.']
  review_meeting_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the post‑handover review meeting.']
  risk_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned during handover review.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of what is included in the handover.']
  signature_captured boolean [note: 'True when a signed handover acknowledgment is on file.']
  signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the handover signature was recorded.']
  signoff_approvals varchar [note: 'List of approvers and timestamps for handover sign‑off.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Native primary key from the originating system.']
  training_completion_status varchar [note: 'Status of required training for the receiving team.']
  updated_by_user_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the user who performed the most recent update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the handover record.']
  value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Financial value assigned to the handed‑over deliverables.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the transferred warranty period ends.']
  warranty_transfer_flag boolean [note: 'True when warranty obligations are passed to the receiving organization.']

  Note: 'Project handover record documenting the formal transfer of a completed project deliverable, facility, or system from the project team to the operations/maintenance organization. Captures handover type (partial, final), scope of handover, handover date, accepting organization, outstanding items, as-built documentation status, training completion status, and sign-off approvals.'
}

Table project.project_contract {
  project_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the project contract record.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Contracts must be associated with the customer account for legal, billing, and compliance purposes.']
  master_project_contract_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on project_contract (master_project_contract_id)']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal employee responsible for contract administration.']
  primary_project_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who created the contract record.']
  primary_project_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the external contractor or vendor.']
  project_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which this contract belongs.']
  sourcing_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.sourcing_rule. Business justification: Provides sourcing policy for contract items in Project Contract Management process.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Work Breakdown Structure element linked to the contract.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the contract has been amended.']
  contract_category varchar [note: 'Business domain of the contract (e.g., EPC, consulting).']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract reference number assigned by the contracting party.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contract pricing model.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Accounting cost center associated with the contract.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three‑letter code of the contract currency.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic contract document.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes binding.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or terminates (nullable for open‑ended).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the contract is scheduled to expire, if different from effective_until.']
  last_amendment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent contract amendment.']
  liquidated_damages_clause varchar [note: 'Text of the clause defining penalties for delayed performance.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Agreed payment schedule and conditions (e.g., Net 30, milestone based).']
  performance_bond_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the performance bond, if required.']
  performance_bond_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a performance bond is required for the contract.']
  project_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract.']
  renewal_option_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes a renewal option.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Length of the renewal period in months, if renewal_option_flag is true.']
  retention_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount held as retention based on the retention percentage.']
  retention_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of contract value retained until final acceptance.']
  scope_of_work varchar [note: 'Brief description of the work, deliverables, and responsibilities covered by the contract.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date the contract was formally signed by all parties.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the gross contract value.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  value_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contract amount before taxes, discounts, or retentions.']
  value_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Contract amount after taxes, discounts, and retentions.']

  Note: 'Project-specific contract record governing agreements with EPC contractors, construction subcontractors, engineering consultants, and equipment vendors for project execution. Captures contract number, contract type (lump sum, cost-plus, unit rate, T&M), contractor identity, scope of work, contract value, payment terms, retention percentage, performance bond, liquidated damages clause, and contract status.'
}

Table project.invoice_request {
  invoice_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the project invoice request record.']
  credited_invoice_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on invoice_request (credited_invoice_request_id)']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer to be billed.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Invoice requests must be linked to the actual invoice issued for audit, reconciliation, and compliance reporting.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project associated with the invoice request.']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the WBS element linked to the billing.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes and discounts.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount after tax and discounts.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the invoice request.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the invoice request.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice request was approved.']
  billing_basis varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate the billable amount.']
  billing_period_end date [note: 'End date of the billing period covered by the request.']
  billing_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the billing period covered by the request.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing contract or agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first captured in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the invoice amounts.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to the invoice.']
  discount_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for the discount granted.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is expected.']
  invoice_request_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description or notes for the invoice request.']
  invoice_request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice request.']
  is_advance boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is an advance (pre‑payment) invoice.']
  is_final boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this invoice request represents the final billing for the project.']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project milestone tied to this billing request.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual payment terms associated with the invoice.']
  percent_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of project completion used for percentage‑based billing.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the invoice request.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice request was generated.']
  revenue_recognition_method varchar [note: 'Method used for recognizing revenue from this invoice.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source operational system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Original identifier of the record in the source system.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component of the invoice.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the tax jurisdiction or rule applied.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate expressed as a percentage.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who initially created the record.']

  Note: 'Project billing and invoice request record capturing milestone-based or progress-based billing events triggered by project milestone completion, percentage completion, or contractual billing schedule. Captures billing basis (milestone, POC, time-and-material), billing period, billable amount, WBS billing element, customer reference, revenue recognition method, and billing document status.'
}

Table project.earned_value_record {
  earned_value_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the earned value performance record.']
  previous_earned_value_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on earned_value_record (previous_earned_value_record_id)']
  wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the WBS element to which this earned value data applies.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual cost incurred for work performed up to the reporting date.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the earned value record was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the earned value record.']
  baseline_version varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project baseline version against which the metrics are measured.']
  cost_performance_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Ratio of earned value to actual cost (EV/AC). Values >1 indicate cost efficiency.']
  cost_type varchar [note: 'Classification of costs represented in the record.']
  cost_variance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between earned value and actual cost (EV‑AC). Positive indicates under budget.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the earned value record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  earned_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of work actually performed by the reporting date.']
  earned_value_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the earned value record.']
  estimate_at_completion decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted total cost of the project at completion based on current performance.']
  estimate_to_complete decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected cost required to finish the remaining work.']
  forecast_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to generate forecasted earned value figures.']
  is_forecast boolean [note: 'True if the record represents a forecasted value rather than actual measured data.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations related to the earned value record.']
  percent_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the WBS element work that has been completed to date.']
  planned_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned monetary value of work scheduled to be performed by the reporting date.']
  reporting_date date [note: 'Date on which the earned value record is reported (typically the period end date).']
  reporting_period_end_date date [note: 'Last day of the reporting period for which the earned value metrics are calculated.']
  reporting_period_start_date date [note: 'First day of the reporting period for which the earned value metrics are calculated.']
  schedule_performance_index decimal(10,4) [note: 'Ratio of earned value to planned value (EV/PV). Values >1 indicate schedule efficiency.']
  schedule_variance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between earned value and planned value (EV‑PV). Positive indicates ahead of schedule.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that supplied the earned value data (e.g., SAP, Opcenter).']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the source record in the originating system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the earned value record.']
  variance_at_completion decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between the original budget and the estimate at completion (BAC‑EAC).']

  Note: 'Earned Value Management (EVM) performance record capturing periodic EVM metrics for a WBS element or project. Stores BCWS (Planned Value), BCWP (Earned Value), ACWP (Actual Cost), Schedule Variance (SV), Cost Variance (CV), SPI, CPI, EAC, ETC, and VAC per reporting period. Enables project performance trending and forecasting at WBS and project level.'
}

Table project.project_document {
  project_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the project document record.']
  related_project_project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which the document belongs.']

  approver varchar [note: 'Name of the person who approved the document.']
  archive_date date [note: 'Date the document was archived.']

  checksum varchar [note: 'Hash value (e.g., SHA‑256) used to verify file integrity.']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory or standard requirement the document satisfies.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the document.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Classification indicating the sensitivity of the document.']
  control_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier assigned to controlled documents for traceability.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 code indicating the country where the document originated.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the document record was first created in the system.']
  distribution_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of parties who receive the document.']
  document_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping of the document (e.g., Engineering, Legal, Safety).']
  document_description varchar [note: 'Brief free‑text description summarizing the document\'s purpose.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the document, often used for tracking and reference.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Category of the document indicating its purpose or content. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: drawing|specification|contract|permit|inspection_report|test_record|as_built|procedure|policy — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which the document\'s content becomes effective.']
  electronic_signature_present boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document includes an electronic signature.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the document is no longer valid.']
  file_path varchar [note: 'File system or repository path where the document is stored.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the stored file in bytes.']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the document has been moved to archive storage.']
  is_controlled boolean [note: 'True if the document is subject to formal control and change management.']
  is_electronic boolean [note: 'True if the document exists in electronic form.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the document was officially issued.']
  keywords varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of keywords for search and classification.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑2 language code of the document content.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date the document was last reviewed for relevance or compliance.']
  mime_type varchar [note: 'Standard MIME type describing the file format (e.g., application/pdf).']
  originator varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or department that originated the document.']
  project_document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external regulation or filing that references this document.']
  retention_period_years varchar [note: 'Number of years the document must be retained per policy.']
  review_cycle_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled reviews of the document.']
  revision varchar [note: 'Revision identifier (e.g., A, B, 1.0, 2.1) indicating the version of the document.']
  signed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who signed the document electronically or physically.']
  signed_date date [note: 'Date the document was signed.']
  storage_location varchar [note: 'Logical storage area or bucket name (e.g., Azure Blob container).']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'More specific classification within the main category.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the document as shown to users.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the document record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential numeric version of the document.']

  Note: 'Project document register record tracking all formal project documents including engineering drawings, specifications, contracts, permits, inspection reports, test records, and as-built documentation. Captures document number, title, document type, revision, originator, issue date, distribution list, approval status, and document management system reference. Supports project document control per ISO 9001.'
}

Table project.phase {
  phase_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the project phase record.']
  predecessor_phase_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on phase (predecessor_phase_id)']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent project to which this phase belongs.']
  actual_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for the phase to date.']
  actual_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the phase actually took to complete.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Date the phase was completed or closed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Date the phase actually commenced on the shop floor or in engineering.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the phase gate was approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the formal approval process for the phase gate.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person or role that approved the phase.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for any change control associated with the phase.']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory or internal compliance requirement applicable to the phase.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status for the phase.']
  cost_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Budgeted cost for the phase as originally planned.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the phase record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for cost fields.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the phase definition becomes effective (often same as actual_start_date).']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the phase definition is superseded or closed (often same as actual_end_date).']
  gate_review_criteria varchar [note: 'Key criteria that must be satisfied for the phase gate to be approved.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the phase is deemed critical to project success.']
  milestone_flag boolean [note: 'True if the phase includes a major project milestone.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the phase.']
  percent_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current percentage of work completed for the phase, expressed with two decimal precision.']
  phase_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the phase in project documentation and systems.']
  phase_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the phase scope, objectives, and deliverables.']
  phase_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the phase (e.g., Concept, FEED, Detailed Engineering).']
  phase_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the phase.']
  planned_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days originally planned for the phase.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled end date for the phase as defined in the project plan.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled start date for the phase as defined in the project plan.']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of identified risks for the phase.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the phase.']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric order of the phase within the overall project lifecycle.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the phase record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the phase definition, incremented on each redesign.']
  wbs_element_code varchar [note: 'Code of the associated Work Breakdown Structure element.']

  Note: 'Project phase master record defining the structured lifecycle phases of a capital or engineering project (e.g., Concept, FEED, Detailed Engineering, Procurement, Construction, Commissioning, Startup, Closeout). Captures phase name, phase code, sequence, planned start/end dates, actual start/end dates, gate review criteria, approval status, and phase completion percentage.'
}

Table project.team_member {
  team_member_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the project team member assignment record.']
  primary_team_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the person assigned to the project (links to master party/person record).']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the project to which the team member is assigned.']
  reports_to_team_member_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on team_member (reports_to_team_member_id)']
  access_level varchar [note: 'System access level assigned to the team member for project data and tools.']
  allocation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the person\'s effort allocated to the project (0-100).']
  authority_level varchar [note: 'Level of decision‑making authority granted to the team member for this project.']
  cost_rate_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Hourly cost rate for the team member\'s labor on the project, expressed in the specified currency.']
  cost_rate_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the cost rate associated with the team member\'s allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assignment record was created.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the team member\'s assignment to the project ends (null if ongoing).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes regarding the assignment, such as special conditions or comments.']
  role varchar [note: 'Role of the team member within the project organization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: project_manager|project_engineer|cost_controller|scheduler|site_manager|quality_engineer|procurement_lead|safety_officer — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the team member\'s assignment to the project begins.']
  team_member_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the team member\'s assignment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assignment record.']

  Note: 'Project team member assignment record defining the project organizational structure by linking personnel to specific project roles, responsibilities, and authority levels. Captures team member identity, project role (PM, project engineer, cost controller, scheduler, site manager), assignment start/end dates, allocation percentage, reporting line within project, and access authorization level.'
}

Table project.progress_report {
  progress_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each project progress report record.']
  previous_progress_report_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on progress_report (previous_progress_report_id)']
  primary_progress_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who authored the progress report.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which this progress report belongs.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred during the reporting period.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the report was approved.']
  approved_by_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who approved the report.']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the employee who authored the report.']
  budgeted_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost originally budgeted for the reporting period.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for any change control request associated with this report.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or notes added by the author.']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory or quality requirement applicable to the project.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the project complies with relevant standards and regulations.']
  cost_status varchar [note: 'Cost performance relative to budget (on budget, over budget, under budget).']
  cost_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary difference between budgeted and actual cost.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the progress report record was first created in the system.']
  forecast_completion_date date [note: 'Projected date when the project is expected to be fully completed.']
  forecast_percent_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected overall percent complete at forecast completion date.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the reporting period includes a critical milestone or decision point.']
  issues_and_risks varchar [note: 'Summary of current issues, risks, and mitigation actions.']
  key_accomplishments varchar [note: 'Narrative description of major achievements during the reporting period.']
  overall_status varchar [note: 'Overall health of the project using Red/Amber/Green (RAG) coding.']
  percent_complete decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall percentage of work completed to date.']
  report_date date [note: 'Date on which the progress report was submitted or finalized.']
  report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the report (draft, submitted, approved, rejected).']
  reporting_cadence varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the report is generated (e.g., weekly, monthly, quarterly).']
  reporting_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the reporting period covered by this progress report.']
  reporting_period_start date [note: 'First day of the reporting period covered by this progress report.']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of identified risks and their potential impact.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating for the project during the reporting period.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current schedule performance relative to the baseline (on track, behind, ahead).']
  schedule_variance_days varchar [note: 'Difference in days between planned and actual schedule milestones.']
  upcoming_activities varchar [note: 'Planned work and milestones for the next reporting period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the progress report record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the report for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Periodic project progress report record capturing the formal status update submitted by the project team at a defined reporting cadence (weekly, monthly). Captures reporting period, overall project health (RAG status), schedule performance summary, cost performance summary, key accomplishments, upcoming activities, issues and risks summary, and forecast completion date.'
}

Table project.settlement {
  settlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each project settlement record.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to project.project_header. Business justification: Settlement records are always associated with a project; linking to project_header provides context for financial reporting and eliminates the need for external project look‑ups.']
  reversal_of_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original settlement that is being reversed.']
  reversal_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on settlement (reversal_settlement_id)']
  sender_wbs_element_id varchar [note: 'WBS element from which costs are being settled.']
  settlement_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the settlement.']
  settlement_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who initiated the settlement.']
  amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before tax or adjustments.']
  amount_net decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount after tax and adjustments.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the settlement.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement was approved.']
  cost_element_code varchar [note: 'Cost element used for the settlement (e.g., L001 for labor).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the settlement record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code of the settlement amounts.']
  external_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to an external document (e.g., invoice, contract) linked to the settlement.']
  is_manual_settlement boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the settlement was entered manually (true) or generated automatically (false).']
  posting_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period identifier (e.g., 2023Q1) for the settlement posting.']
  receiver_object_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the receiving cost object (e.g., cost center, asset, order).']
  receiver_object_type varchar [note: 'Type of the cost object receiving the settled amount.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'True if this record represents a reversal of a prior settlement.']
  rule varchar [note: 'Name or code of the settlement rule used to allocate costs.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement was executed or posted.']
  settlement_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description or notes about the settlement.']
  settlement_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the settlement document by the source system.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the settlement.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating ERP or system that generated the settlement record.']
  source_system_settlement_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the settlement in the source system.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component associated with the settlement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the settlement record.']

  Note: 'Project cost settlement record capturing the periodic or final allocation of project costs from WBS elements to receiving cost objects (fixed assets, cost centers, orders, profitability segments). Captures settlement rule, sender WBS element, receiver object type and identity, settlement amount by cost element, settlement period, and settlement document reference. Aligns with SAP PS settlement (KO88/CJ88).'
}

Table project.gate_review {
  gate_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the gate review record.']
  deferred_gate_review_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on gate_review (deferred_gate_review_id)']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the project to which this gate review belongs.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the project meets relevant compliance requirements at this gate.']
  conditions_attached varchar [note: 'Specific conditions or actions required for the project to proceed to the next gate.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this gate review record was initially created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the reauthorized amount.']
  decision_outcome varchar [note: 'Formal outcome of the gate review governing the next project step.']
  decision_reason varchar [note: 'Narrative explanation for the decision outcome.']
  financial_status varchar [note: 'Current financial approval status of the gate.']
  gate_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the gate (e.g., Concept, Design, Production).']
  gate_number varchar [note: 'Numeric identifier indicating the gate sequence within the project lifecycle.']
  gate_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the gate within the overall project schedule.']
  gate_type varchar [note: 'Category of the gate based on the project methodology.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if this gate is deemed critical for project success.']
  next_gate_target_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next gate review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any additional comments or observations.']
  reauthorized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount re‑approved for the project at this gate.']
  review_board varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of individuals or groups that formed the review board.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the gate review decision was made.']
  risk_assessment_summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of risks identified at this gate.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned during the gate review.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this gate review record.']

  Note: 'Stage-gate review event record capturing the formal governance decision point at each project phase boundary. Captures gate number, gate name, review date, review board members, decision outcome (proceed, hold, kill, recycle), conditions attached to approval, risk assessment summary, financial re-authorization status, and next gate target date. Supports CapEx governance and investment committee oversight.'
}

Table automation.device_registry {
  device_registry_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the device registry record.']
  account_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Site‑level device inventory needed for maintenance scheduling and site‑specific service contracts.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Maintenance BOM links physical components to devices for spare‑part tracking; required in Asset Maintenance Bill of Materials report.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: CAPEX tracking: each device is assigned to a cost center for depreciation and maintenance budgeting, required for the Asset Capitalization Report.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Asset inventory report requires linking each device to its owning customer for warranty, support, and billing.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'FK to asset.equipment_register.equipment_register_id — Automation devices (PLCs, sensors) are physically installed on equipment assets. This link enables asset-to-device correlation for maintenance planning and OT/IT convergence queries.']
  maintenance_responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Maintenance Management System requires tracking which technician is responsible for each device for compliance and OEE reporting.']
  parent_device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on device_registry (parent_device_registry_id)']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant hosting the device.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production line or cell where the device operates.']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Production scheduling assigns each device to a primary SKU; OEE and performance reports require device‑to‑SKU mapping.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Asset Management requires each device\'s physical location for maintenance planning; linking device to stock_location enables location‑based work orders.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Procurement tracks which supplier provided each device for warranty, maintenance, and cost allocation; required for Asset Management reports.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or station associated with the device.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the device was first placed into service.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Primary industrial communication protocol used by the device.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the device registry record was first created.']
  current_rating_a decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum continuous current draw of the device in amperes.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the device was retired or removed from service.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or asset tag used to reference the device in operational systems.']
  device_registry_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form textual description of the device and its purpose.']
  device_registry_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the automation device.']
  device_registry_status varchar [note: 'Current operational condition of the device.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the device (e.g., PLC, HMI, RTU, DCS, Edge Gateway, Sensor).']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the device firmware currently installed.']
  hardware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the device hardware revision.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IPv4 address assigned to the device for network communication.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent preventive or corrective maintenance activity.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle stage of the device within asset management.']
  mac_address varchar [note: 'Media Access Control address of the device\'s network interface.']
  maintenance_status varchar [note: 'Current maintenance compliance status of the device.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Vendor‑assigned model number of the device.']
  network_address varchar [note: 'Hostname or DNS name used to reach the device on the network.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Any supplemental remarks, installation notes, or special handling instructions.']
  operating_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Approved ambient temperature range for device operation in degrees Celsius.']
  power_rating_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum power rating of the device in kilowatts.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number assigned by the manufacturer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the device registry record.']
  voltage_rating_v decimal(10,2) [note: 'Nominal operating voltage of the device in volts.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date the manufacturer warranty for the device expires.']

  Note: 'Master record for all industrial automation devices including PLCs, HMIs, SCADA servers, edge gateways, sensors, actuators, drives, and IIoT endpoints deployed across manufacturing facilities. Serves as the SSOT for device identity, firmware version, hardware model, communication protocol, network address, installation location, operational status, and lifecycle state. Each device is uniquely identified and linked to a plant, production line, or work center. Captures device type classification (PLC, HMI, RTU, DCS, edge node), vendor, model number, serial number, firmware version, IP address, MAC address, communication protocol (OPC-UA, Modbus, PROFINET, EtherNet/IP), commissioning date, decommission date, and current operational status.'
}

Table automation.control_system {
  control_system_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the control system record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: OPEX allocation: control systems incur operational expenses charged to a cost center, needed for the Monthly Operations Cost Report.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Control system ownership drives SLA terms, billing, and compliance reporting per customer.']
  network_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.network_segment. Business justification: Control system records a network segment; using FK to network_segment enables network topology management and removes the free‑text segment field.']
  parent_control_system_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on control_system (parent_control_system_id)']
  product_family_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Control system configuration is managed per product family; maintenance plans and safety standards reference the family.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: Control system contracts are managed per supplier; linking enables support contract tracking and compliance reporting.']
  system_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Control system ownership and maintenance responsibility are recorded per system for safety audits and change management.']
  alarm_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of concurrent alarms the system can handle.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date when the control system was commissioned and first put into service.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Primary industrial communication protocol used by the system.']
  control_program_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the main control logic program deployed on the system.']
  control_system_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the control system purpose and scope.']
  control_system_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the control system as used by engineering and operations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the control system record was first created in the data lake.']
  data_retention_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how long configuration and log data are retained.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the control system was retired or removed from service (nullable).']
  device_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual devices (e.g., PLCs, I/O modules) that belong to the control system.']
  engineering_workstation varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary engineering workstation used for configuration.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the control system was physically installed.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the control system is classified as critical to plant operation.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent compliance audit of the control system.']
  last_config_backup_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent configuration backup of the control system.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle stage of the control system.']
  mtbf_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average elapsed time between successive failures, expressed in hours.']
  mttr_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average time required to restore the system after a failure.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, special instructions, or historical notes.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Real‑time operational state of the control system.']
  plant_area varchar [note: 'Physical area or production line where the control system is deployed.']
  redundancy_configuration varchar [note: 'Describes the redundancy scheme applied to the control system.']
  safety_integrity_level varchar [note: 'Safety certification level of the system as defined by IEC 61508.']
  security_classification varchar [note: 'Data security classification of the control system record.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version string of the control system runtime software.']
  system_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or tag used to reference the control system in plant documentation and ERP.']
  system_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the control system by its functional architecture.']
  tag_count varchar [note: 'Total number of OPC‑UA or PLC tags defined in the system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the control system record.']
  uptime_hours varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of hours the system has been in an online state.']

  Note: 'Master record representing a discrete industrial control system instance — a named, configured automation system such as a DCS (Distributed Control System), SCADA system, PLC-based control panel, or safety instrumented system (SIS). Captures control system name, type (DCS, SCADA, PLC, SIS, ESD), vendor platform (Siemens S7, Allen-Bradley ControlLogix, ABB 800xA, Honeywell Experion), software version, redundancy configuration, safety integrity level (SIL), associated plant area, network segment, engineering workstation reference, last configuration backup date, and lifecycle status. Acts as the parent grouping entity for all devices, tag definitions, and control programs within a logical control boundary.'
}

Table automation.plc_program {
  plc_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the PLC program record.']
  engineering_revision_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.revision. Business justification: Traceability of PLC program releases to component revisions; needed for Change Management Release Report.']
  parent_plc_program_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on plc_program (parent_plc_program_id)']
  primary_plc_device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PLC or controller hardware that this program is intended for.']
  product_configuration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.configuration. Business justification: Configuration management links each PLC program version to the product configuration it implements, ensuring correct program deployment.']
  target_device_device_registry_id varchar [note: 'FK to automation.device_registry']
  author varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer or developer who authored the current version of the program.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Brief description of why the program was revised or updated.']
  checksum varchar [note: 'Hash value used to verify program file integrity (e.g., SHA‑256).']
  checksum_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to generate the checksum (MD5, SHA‑1, or SHA‑256).']
  compatible_hardware_model varchar [note: 'Model number of hardware that can run this program.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the program record was first created in the system.']
  language varchar [note: 'IEC 61131‑3 language used for the program: Ladder Diagram (LD), Function Block Diagram (FBD), Structured Text (ST), Sequential Function Chart (SFC), or Instruction List (IL).']
  plc_program_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the program\'s purpose, functionality, and scope.']
  plc_program_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the PLC program as used by engineers and operators.']
  plc_program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the program: draft, tested, released, or archived.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the PLC program for cataloguing and lookup.']
  program_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the compiled program binary in bytes.']
  release_notes varchar [note: 'Narrative of changes, fixes, and new features included in the release.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the program was officially released for production use.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential integer incremented each time the program is revised.']
  safety_critical boolean [note: 'True if the program controls safety‑related functions and must meet safety certification requirements.']
  source_control_branch varchar [note: 'Version‑control branch name where the program source resides.']
  source_control_commit_hash varchar [note: 'Commit hash identifying the exact source snapshot used for this program version.']
  supported_firmware_version varchar [note: 'Firmware version of the target device required for this program.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the program record.']
  validation_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the program passed all automated and manual validation tests.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the most recent validation was performed.']
  version varchar [note: 'Semantic version string (e.g., 1.0.3) indicating the released version of the program.']

  Note: 'Master record for PLC and controller programs (ladder logic, function block diagrams, structured text, sequential function charts) managed within the automation domain. Captures program name, controller target (device_registry reference), programming language (IEC 61131-3: LD, FBD, ST, SFC, IL), program version, revision number, author, last modified timestamp, checksum/hash for integrity verification, release status (draft, tested, released, archived), associated control system, and program description. Tracks the lifecycle of control logic from development through release and archival. Supports change management and version control for safety-critical control programs.'
}

Table automation.tag_definition {
  tag_definition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the tag definition record.']
  component_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.component. Business justification: Tag definitions map to physical sensor components; needed for Configuration Management and Safety Audits.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'FK to automation.control_system']
  parent_tag_definition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on tag_definition (parent_tag_definition_id)']
  product_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_specification. Business justification: Tag definitions are used to monitor compliance with product specifications (e.g., voltage, temperature).']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Permission level for reading and/or writing the tag.']
  alarm_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tag is configured to generate alarms.']
  alarm_priority varchar [note: 'Severity level for alarms generated by this tag.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tag definition was created in the system.']
  data_type varchar [note: 'Data type of the tag value.']
  deadband decimal(18,6) [note: 'Minimum change in value required to trigger an update.']
  device_name varchar [note: 'Name of the physical device or controller hosting the tag.']
  engineering_unit varchar [note: 'Physical unit of measurement for the tag (e.g., °C, %, psi, kW).']
  eu_range_high decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper bound of the engineering unit range.']
  eu_range_low decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower bound of the engineering unit range.']
  is_historian_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating if the tag value is captured in the historian.']
  process_area varchar [note: 'Logical area of the plant or process where the tag is located.']
  quality_code varchar [note: 'Quality indicator for the tag value (e.g., Good, Bad, Uncertain).']
  raw_range_high decimal(18,6) [note: 'Upper bound of the raw sensor value range.']
  raw_range_low decimal(18,6) [note: 'Lower bound of the raw sensor value range.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the tag\'s historical data is retained.']
  scaling_factor decimal(18,6) [note: 'Multiplicative factor applied to raw value to obtain engineering value.']
  scan_rate_ms varchar [note: 'Polling interval in milliseconds for the tag.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record for the tag (e.g., Aveva SCADA, Siemens Opcenter).']
  tag_definition_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the tag purpose and usage.']
  tag_definition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tag definition.']
  tag_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the tag as used in engineering documentation.']
  tag_path varchar [note: 'Fully qualified OPC‑UA node identifier locating the tag in the address space.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the tag definition.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the tag definition.']

  Note: 'Master record for OPC-UA, SCADA, and DCS process tag definitions — the authoritative catalog of all named data points (tags) within the automation infrastructure. Each tag represents a named variable mapped to a physical I/O point, calculated value, or communication register. Captures tag name, tag path (OPC-UA node ID), data type (Boolean, INT, FLOAT, STRING), engineering unit (EU), EU range (low/high), raw range (low/high), scan rate (ms), deadband, access level (read/write/read-write), associated device, associated control system, process area, tag description, alarm enabled flag, and tag status. Serves as the SSOT for all process variable definitions consumed by SCADA historians, MES, and IIoT platforms.'
}

Table automation.edge_gateway {
  edge_gateway_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the edge gateway device.']
  account_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.account_site. Business justification: Edge gateway placement is tracked per site for network topology and maintenance planning.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Remote‑monitoring service subscription is billed per customer; linking gateway to account enables usage reporting.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.device_registry. Business justification: Edge gateways are physical devices; linking to device_registry consolidates device master data and removes duplicate attributes.']
  upstream_edge_gateway_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on edge_gateway (upstream_edge_gateway_id)']
  connected_device_count varchar [note: 'Number of field devices currently registered to the gateway.']
  cpu_utilization_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average CPU utilization percentage over the last monitoring interval.']
  data_throughput_mbps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum data throughput capacity of the gateway expressed in megabits per second.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when the gateway is retired from service.']
  edge_gateway_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the gateway indicating whether it is actively processing data, under maintenance, or retired.']
  edge_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping of gateways for management or reporting purposes.']
  edge_last_config_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent configuration change applied to the gateway.']
  edge_location_latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude of the gateway installation point.']
  edge_location_longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude of the gateway installation point.']
  edge_os_license_key varchar [note: 'License key required for the gateway\'s operating system.']
  edge_os_vendor varchar [note: 'Vendor of the operating system installed on the gateway.']
  edge_os_version varchar [note: 'Version of the operating system running on the gateway.']
  edge_processing_capabilities varchar [note: 'Enumerated list of processing functions the gateway can perform locally.']
  edge_role varchar [note: 'Primary functional role of the gateway within the IIoT architecture.']
  edge_security_status varchar [note: 'Current security posture of the gateway.']
  edge_status_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation for the current status, e.g., cause of maintenance mode.']
  edge_zone varchar [note: 'Logical zone within the plant where the gateway resides.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the firmware currently installed on the gateway.']
  gateway_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the gateway based on its functional role within the IIoT architecture.']
  hardware_model varchar [note: 'Model designation of the gateway hardware as defined by the vendor.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date on which the gateway was installed at the deployment location.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IPv4 address assigned to the gateway on the IT network.']
  it_protocol varchar [note: 'Protocol used on the information‑technology side for cloud or enterprise integration.']
  last_firmware_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent firmware update applied to the gateway.']
  last_heartbeat_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent heartbeat signal received from the gateway.']
  mac_address varchar [note: 'Media Access Control address of the gateway\'s network interface.']
  maintenance_window varchar [note: 'Scheduled time window for routine maintenance activities.']
  memory_usage_mb varchar [note: 'Amount of RAM currently in use by the gateway, expressed in megabytes.']
  network_bandwidth_limit_mbps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum network bandwidth allocated to the gateway.']
  ot_protocol varchar [note: 'Industrial protocol used on the operational‑technology side to communicate with field devices.']
  power_supply_type varchar [note: 'Type of power supply used by the gateway.']
  processing_capacity_ops varchar [note: 'Number of processing operations the gateway can perform per second.']
  rack_position varchar [note: 'Physical rack location identifier within the data center or plant.']
  security_certification varchar [note: 'Security certifications held by the gateway hardware/software.']
  software_modules varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of software modules installed on the gateway.']
  storage_capacity_gb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total onboard storage capacity of the gateway in gigabytes.']
  supports_local_ml boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the gateway can execute local machine‑learning inference.']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current ambient temperature measured at the gateway enclosure in degrees Celsius.']
  uptime_seconds varchar [note: 'Total number of seconds the gateway has been continuously operational since last restart.']

  Note: 'Master record for industrial edge gateway devices that aggregate, pre-process, and forward IIoT data from field devices to cloud or on-premise platforms. Captures gateway name, hardware model, vendor, firmware version, deployment location (plant, line, zone), network interfaces (OT-side protocol, IT-side protocol), connected device count, data throughput capacity, edge processing capabilities (local ML inference, protocol translation, data buffering), VPN/security configuration reference, last heartbeat timestamp, operational status, and associated control system. Distinct from device_registry in that edge gateways are data aggregation and protocol translation nodes rather than direct control devices.'
}

Table automation.process_parameter {
  process_parameter_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the process parameter record.']
  derived_from_process_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on process_parameter (derived_from_process_parameter_id)']
  project_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering change request (ECR/ECO) that triggered the update.']
  address varchar [note: 'Network address or node identifier of the device exposing the tag (e.g., IP address or PLC slot).']
  alarm_high decimal(18,6) [note: 'Value at which a high‑level alarm is raised.']
  alarm_high_high decimal(18,6) [note: 'Value at which a critical high alarm is raised.']
  alarm_low decimal(18,6) [note: 'Value at which a low‑level alarm is raised.']
  alarm_low_low decimal(18,6) [note: 'Value at which a critical low alarm is raised.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the parameter was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer or manager who approved the parameter configuration.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason for the most recent change to the parameter configuration.']
  control_system varchar [note: 'Automation system that hosts the parameter (e.g., PLC, SCADA, DCS).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the parameter record was first created in the system.']
  data_type varchar [note: 'Underlying data type of the parameter value in the control system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the parameter configuration is retired (null if still active).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the parameter configuration becomes effective.']
  engineering_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the parameter (e.g., °C, bar, psi, %).']
  lower_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Statistical lower control limit used for process monitoring.']
  lower_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Minimum acceptable value defined by product or process specifications.']
  nominal_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'Target or default value for the parameter under normal operating conditions.']
  offset decimal(10,4) [note: 'Additive offset applied after scaling to align raw data with engineering units.']
  parameter_type varchar [note: 'Category of the parameter indicating its purpose (e.g., set‑point, limit, alarm threshold, PID tuning constant).']
  process_area varchar [note: 'Logical area of the plant or production line where the parameter is applied (e.g., furnace, mixing, packaging).']
  process_parameter_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code for the parameter, often used in engineering drawings and PLC programs.']
  process_parameter_description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the parameter purpose, usage, and any special notes.']
  process_parameter_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the control parameter as used by engineers and operators.']
  process_parameter_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the parameter record.']
  scaling_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Multiplicative factor applied to raw sensor data to obtain engineering units.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record (e.g., Siemens Opcenter MES, Aveva SCADA).']
  tag_definition varchar [note: 'Standardized OPC‑UA tag name that maps to the physical sensor or control point.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the parameter record.']
  upper_control_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Statistical upper control limit used for process monitoring.']
  upper_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'Maximum acceptable value defined by product or process specifications.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the parameter record for change management.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the set-point and control parameter configurations for manufacturing processes managed by automation systems. Captures parameter name, parameter type (set-point, limit, alarm threshold, PID tuning constant), associated tag definition, associated control system, process area, nominal value, upper control limit (UCL), lower control limit (LCL), upper specification limit (USL), lower specification limit (LSL), alarm high, alarm high-high, alarm low, alarm low-low, engineering unit, last modified timestamp, approved-by reference, and effective date range. Represents the authoritative configuration of how a process should be controlled — distinct from real-time tag readings which are captured in process_data_point.'
}

Table automation.alarm_definition {
  alarm_definition_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the alarm definition record.']
  parent_alarm_definition_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on alarm_definition (parent_alarm_definition_id)']
  acknowledgment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether operator acknowledgment is required to clear the alarm.']
  alarm_category varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of the alarm (e.g., process, equipment, environmental).']
  alarm_definition_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to reference the alarm definition.']
  alarm_definition_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the alarm purpose and behavior.']
  alarm_definition_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the alarm.']
  alarm_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the alarm based on its origin.']
  auto_reset boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alarm automatically resets after the condition clears.']
  class varchar [note: 'ISA‑18.2 classification of the alarm (A‑D).']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Reference to the compliance standard governing the alarm definition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the alarm definition record was created.']
  deadband decimal(12,4) [note: 'Deadband applied around the setpoint to prevent chatter.']
  deadband_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the deadband value.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to detailed documentation or SOP for the alarm.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the alarm definition is valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the alarm definition is no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  escalation_policy varchar [note: 'Textual description of escalation steps if the alarm is not addressed.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when the alarm definition was last reviewed for relevance.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the alarm definition.']
  notification_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for notifying operators about the alarm.']
  off_delay_seconds varchar [note: 'Time the condition must clear before the alarm is cleared.']
  on_delay_seconds varchar [note: 'Time the condition must persist before the alarm becomes active.']
  operator_group varchar [note: 'Operator group responsible for acknowledging and handling the alarm.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority indicating the impact of the alarm.']
  process_area varchar [note: 'Logical area of the plant or process to which the alarm belongs.']
  rationalization_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the alarm rationalization process.']
  related_equipment_tag varchar [note: 'Tag identifier of equipment associated with the alarm.']
  reset_delay_seconds varchar [note: 'Delay before the alarm automatically resets when auto_reset is true.']
  setpoint_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the setpoint value (e.g., psi, °C, %).']
