  setpoint_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Numeric setpoint that defines the alarm threshold.']
  shelving_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the alarm can be temporarily shelved by operators.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that originated the alarm definition (e.g., Aveva SCADA).']
  suppression_rules varchar [note: 'Textual description of any suppression logic applied to the alarm.']
  trigger_condition varchar [note: 'Condition that causes the alarm to fire.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the alarm definition.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the alarm definition.']

  Note: 'Master record for the alarm and event management catalog defining all configured alarms within SCADA, DCS, and PLC systems. Captures alarm tag reference, alarm name, alarm type (process alarm, equipment alarm, safety alarm, system alarm), priority (critical, high, medium, low), alarm class (ISA-18.2 classification), trigger condition (high, low, deviation, rate-of-change), setpoint value, deadband, delay time (on-delay, off-delay), suppression rules, shelving allowed flag, associated process area, responsible operator group, alarm rationalization status, and last reviewed date. Supports ISA-18.2 alarm management lifecycle including rationalization, documentation, and performance monitoring.'
}

Table automation.network_segment {
  network_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the OT network segment.']
  parent_network_segment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on network_segment (parent_network_segment_id)']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant or facility where the segment resides.']
  addressing_scheme varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the segment uses static, DHCP, or other IP allocation methods.']
  allowed_protocols varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of network protocols permitted on the segment (e.g., OPC-UA, MQTT, TCP).']
  backup_connectivity varchar [note: 'Description of any backup link or secondary path for the segment.']
  change_control_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the change control or engineering change order governing modifications to the segment.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Applicable compliance framework(s) (e.g., IEC 62443, NIST) for the segment.']
  connected_device_count varchar [note: 'Number of OT devices currently attached to the segment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the network segment record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the segment became operational.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the segment is scheduled for decommissioning (null if indefinite).']
  firewall_reference varchar [note: 'Reference or name of the firewall protecting the segment.']
  ip_subnet varchar [note: 'IP subnet in CIDR notation that defines the address space of the segment.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent security audit performed on the segment.']
  maintenance_window varchar [note: 'Scheduled maintenance window description (e.g., Sun 02:00‑04:00).']
  monitoring_tool varchar [note: 'Name of the SCADA/monitoring system that collects telemetry from the segment.']
  network_segment_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the segment purpose and characteristics.']
  network_segment_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the network segment or zone.']
  network_segment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the segment.']
  owner_contact varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person responsible for the segment.']
  redundancy_configuration varchar [note: 'Description of redundancy (e.g., dual‑homing, hot‑standby).']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Risk rating assigned to the segment based on security assessments.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Security level assigned to the segment per IEC 62443.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Enterprise‑wide code used to reference the segment in documentation and systems.']
  topology varchar [note: 'Physical or logical topology of the segment.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the record.']
  vlan_number varchar [note: 'Virtual LAN identifier assigned to the segment.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'ISA/IEC 62443 security zone classification for the segment.']

  Note: 'Master record for OT (Operational Technology) network segments and zones within the industrial automation network architecture. Captures segment name, zone type (ISA/IEC 62443 security zone: Level 0 field, Level 1 control, Level 2 supervisory, Level 3 operations, DMZ), VLAN ID, IP subnet, associated plant/facility, firewall/conduit reference, allowed protocols, connected device count, network topology (ring, star, linear), redundancy configuration, and security level (SL-1 through SL-4 per IEC 62443). Provides the network topology context for device placement and cybersecurity zone management. Distinct from IT network infrastructure managed by corporate IT.'
}

Table automation.alarm_event {
  alarm_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each alarm event record.']
  acknowledged_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Regulatory incident logs require linking each alarm acknowledgment to the employee who performed it for traceability.']
  alarm_definition_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the alarm definition that describes the condition, severity, and setpoint.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the PLC, sensor, or equipment that generated the alarm.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant, zone, or area where the alarm originated.']
  preceding_alarm_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on alarm_event (preceding_alarm_event_id)']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to production.work_center.work_center_id — Production operators need to correlate automation alarms to specific work centers for root cause analysis during production stoppages. Without this link, alarm events are disconnected from production ']
  acknowledged_by varchar [note: 'Name of the operator who acknowledged the alarm.']
  acknowledgement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the operator acknowledged the alarm.']
  alarm_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the alarm (process, equipment, environment, safety, quality).']
  alarm_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time in seconds that the alarm stayed in the active state before being cleared or returned to normal.']
  alarm_message varchar [note: 'Text message describing the alarm condition as presented to the operator.']
  alarm_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority (lower number = higher priority) used for alarm sorting and display.']
  alarm_severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the alarm according to ISA‑18.2 (critical, high, medium, low, info).']
  alarm_source_system varchar [note: 'Control system type that originated the alarm (e.g., SCADA, DCS, PLC, HMI).']
  alarm_state varchar [note: 'Lifecycle state of the alarm (e.g., active, acknowledged, shelved, suppressed, returned to normal, cleared).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alarm was generated by the control system.']
  is_nuisance boolean [note: 'True if the alarm is classified as a nuisance/alarm flood, otherwise false.']
  operator_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form text entered by the operator providing context or actions taken.']
  process_value double [note: 'Measured process variable (e.g., temperature, pressure) at the time the alarm triggered.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the alarm event record was ingested into the Silver layer.']
  setpoint_value double [note: 'The configured setpoint or threshold that the process value exceeded to cause the alarm.']
  shelve_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Time in seconds that the alarm remained shelved before being re‑activated or cleared.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing every alarm occurrence and state transition event generated by SCADA, DCS, and PLC systems. Each record represents a discrete alarm lifecycle event (alarm active, acknowledged, shelved, suppressed, returned to normal, cleared). Captures alarm definition reference, device reference, event timestamp, alarm state (active, acknowledged, RTN, shelved), operator who acknowledged, acknowledgement timestamp, shelve duration, alarm message text, process value at alarm time, setpoint at alarm time, alarm duration (seconds), and whether the alarm was a nuisance alarm. Supports ISA-18.2 alarm performance KPI calculation (alarm rate, flood detection, standing alarms) and operator response analysis.'
}

Table automation.control_mode_event {
  control_mode_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each control mode change event.']
  asset_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order associated with the mode change, if any.']
  batch_execution_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production batch (lot) active at the time of the event.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the PLC/Controller that generated the mode change.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator (human) who initiated the mode change.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment (e.g., machine, robot) whose controller changed mode.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the manufacturing plant where the event occurred.']
  preceding_control_mode_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on control_mode_event (preceding_control_mode_event_id)']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production line or cell that contains the controller.']
  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order linked to the event, when applicable.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production shift during which the event occurred.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text notes entered by the operator or system.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the mode change complies with ISA‑106 and internal SOPs.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Elapsed time the controller remained in the new mode before the next change.']
  event_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level indicating the operational impact of the mode change.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Processing status of the event record within the data pipeline.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the control mode change was recorded in the automation system.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of the event; most commonly a mode change, but may include overrides or resets.']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'True if the mode change was performed manually by an operator, otherwise false.']
  is_safety_critical boolean [note: 'True if the mode change impacts a safety‑critical control loop.']
  new_mode varchar [note: 'Control mode of the loop after the transition.']
  previous_mode varchar [note: 'Control mode of the loop before the transition.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standard code describing why the mode change was performed.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text explanation of the reason for the mode change.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the event record.']
  source_module varchar [note: 'Specific module or application within the source system that generated the event.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating automation system (e.g., Siemens Opcenter MES, Aveva SCADA).']
  tag_reference varchar [note: 'Human‑readable tag name or address used in the control system for the mode signal.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing control mode change events for automation loops and controllers — transitions between automatic, manual, cascade, and remote modes. Captures controller/loop tag reference, control system reference, event timestamp, previous mode, new mode, operator ID who initiated the change, reason code, associated work order or production order reference, and duration in new mode. Critical for process safety analysis, operator behavior auditing, and identifying manual overrides that deviate from standard operating procedures. Supports ISA-106 procedural automation and operator effectiveness analysis.'
}

Table automation.device_config_snapshot {
  device_config_snapshot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each configuration snapshot record.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the automation device (PLC, HMI, drive, controller) whose configuration is being snapshot.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operator who initiated or approved the snapshot (if applicable).']
  previous_device_config_snapshot_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on device_config_snapshot (previous_device_config_snapshot_id)']
  project_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the change request or engineering change order associated with this snapshot.']
  backup_job_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the scheduled backup job that generated the snapshot.']
  checksum_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to compute the configuration checksum.']
  compression_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to compress the configuration file, if applicable.']
  config_file_path varchar [note: 'Blob storage path or URI where the configuration file is stored.']
  configuration_checksum varchar [note: 'Hash value (e.g., SHA‑256) used to verify file integrity.']
  configuration_format varchar [note: 'File format of the stored configuration (e.g., XML, JSON, CSV).']
  configuration_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the configuration schema or layout.']
  device_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number of the device.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of the automation device (Programmable Logic Controller, Human‑Machine Interface, drive, controller).']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the stored configuration file in bytes.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version of the device firmware at the time of snapshot.']
  is_compressed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the stored configuration file is compressed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form information or comments about the snapshot.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the snapshot record was first inserted into the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the snapshot record (e.g., after validation).']
  retention_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how long the snapshot is retained (e.g., keep 30 days, archival).']
  snapshot_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes describing the purpose or context of the snapshot.']
  snapshot_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the snapshot record.']
  snapshot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the configuration snapshot was captured from the device.']
  snapshot_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the snapshot is a full backup, delta change, scheduled capture, pre‑change, or post‑change.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the snapshot (e.g., Siemens Opcenter MES, SAP S/4HANA).']
  triggered_by varchar [note: 'Mechanism that initiated the snapshot: scheduled job, manual operator action, or an automatic change event.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the snapshot has been verified against expected configuration standards.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name of the operator or system that created the snapshot record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing point-in-time configuration snapshots of automation devices (PLCs, HMIs, drives, controllers) for change management, backup, and audit purposes. Each record represents a complete or delta configuration backup taken from a device. Captures device reference, snapshot timestamp, snapshot type (full backup, delta, scheduled, pre-change, post-change), configuration file reference (blob storage path), file size, checksum/hash, firmware version at snapshot time, triggered-by (scheduled, manual, change event), operator reference, associated change request reference, and validation status (verified, unverified). Supports configuration management and rollback capabilities for OT change control.'
}

Table automation.firmware_update {
  firmware_update_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each firmware update event.']
  device_config_snapshot_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the backup snapshot taken before applying the new firmware.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the automation device or edge gateway receiving the firmware update.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or system that initiated or approved the update.']
  project_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Link to the engineering change request that authorized the firmware change.']
  rollback_firmware_update_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on firmware_update (rollback_firmware_update_id)']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the firmware update finished successfully.']
  compliance_patch_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the IEC 62443 compliance patch record linked to this firmware change.']
  device_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the device (e.g., PLC‑01‑LineA).']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Category of the device receiving the update.']
  firmware_version_after varchar [note: 'Version string of the firmware installed after the update.']
  firmware_version_before varchar [note: 'Version string of the firmware installed prior to the update.']
  is_critical_update boolean [note: 'True if the update addresses a security vulnerability or safety issue requiring immediate deployment.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall record status for retention and archival purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations captured by the operator.']
  post_update_validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of automated validation checks after the firmware was applied.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this firmware update record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this record.']
  rollback_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why a rollback was required.']
  rollback_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when a rollback to the previous firmware version was performed, if applicable.']
  scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time when the update was scheduled to start.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact time the firmware update began execution.']
  update_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time from start_timestamp to completion_timestamp (or rollback_timestamp).']
  update_method varchar [note: 'Mechanism used to deliver the firmware (remote push, manual USB, or OTA).']
  update_package_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the firmware package applied during the update.']
  update_reference_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code used to reference the firmware update in reports and tickets.']
  update_size_mb decimal(10,2) [note: 'Size of the firmware package transferred to the device.']
  update_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the firmware update.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Time when post‑update validation was performed.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking firmware and software update events applied to automation devices and edge gateways. Captures device reference, update event timestamp, previous firmware version, new firmware version, update package identifier, update method (remote push, manual USB, OTA), update status (scheduled, in-progress, completed, failed, rolled-back), applied-by operator reference, change request reference, pre-update backup snapshot reference, post-update validation status, and rollback timestamp if applicable. Provides a complete audit trail of all firmware changes across the OT device estate, supporting vulnerability management and compliance with IEC 62443 patch management requirements.'
}

Table automation.scada_session {
  scada_session_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the SCADA operator session record.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the edge device or PLC that the workstation is connected to during the session.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the operator who logged into the SCADA system.']
  previous_scada_session_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on scada_session (previous_scada_session_id)']
  alarm_ack_count varchar [note: 'Number of alarm acknowledgements performed by the operator during the session.']
  control_action_count varchar [note: 'Number of manual control actions (e.g., start/stop) executed by the operator.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Network IP address of the workstation at session start.']
  login_method varchar [note: 'Authentication mechanism used for the session.']
  login_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the operator successfully logged into the SCADA system.']
  logout_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the session ended, either by logout, timeout or forced termination.']
  os_version varchar [note: 'Operating system version of the workstation hosting the SCADA client.']
  plant_area varchar [note: 'Logical area or zone of the manufacturing plant where the session took place (e.g., Assembly, Packaging).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the session record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the session record.']
  session_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total length of the session measured in seconds (logout_timestamp – login_timestamp).']
  session_identifier varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier or code for the session, often displayed in audit logs.']
  session_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by the operator or system during the session.']
  session_source varchar [note: 'Physical source of the session interface.']
  session_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the session.']
  session_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the session purpose.']
  setpoint_change_count varchar [note: 'Number of setpoint modifications made by the operator.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Version of the SCADA application running on the workstation.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the session was terminated.']
  user_role varchar [note: 'Role of the operator within the organization for this session.']
  workstation_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SCADA workstation or HMI panel used for the session.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing operator SCADA and HMI session activity for operational auditing and human factors analysis. Each record represents a discrete operator login session on a SCADA workstation or HMI panel. Captures operator ID, workstation/HMI device reference, session start timestamp, session end timestamp, session duration, login method (local, domain, biometric), associated plant area, number of alarm acknowledgements during session, number of control actions during session, number of setpoint changes during session, and session termination reason (normal logout, timeout, forced logout). Supports operator effectiveness analysis and ISA-101 HMI human factors compliance.'
}

Table automation.setpoint_change {
  setpoint_change_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each setpoint change event.']
  approval_workflow_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the approval record when dual‑authorization is required for safety‑critical setpoint changes.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate key referencing the control system (e.g., PLC, DCS) that executed the setpoint change.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person (operator) or system that initiated the setpoint change.']
  process_parameter_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific process parameter (e.g., temperature, pressure) whose setpoint was changed.']
  production_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production order associated with the setpoint change, linking the event to manufacturing execution.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the recipe or procedure that defines the intended setpoint values for the operation.']
  reversal_setpoint_change_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on setpoint_change (reversal_setpoint_change_id)']
  tag_definition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the OPC-UA or SCADA tag that represents the process variable being adjusted.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the approval workflow for the setpoint change.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing the business reason for the setpoint modification.']
  change_status varchar [note: 'Result of the setpoint change operation (e.g., completed, failed, pending).']
  comment varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for operators or systems to add contextual notes about the change.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the setpoint change was applied on the control system.']
  initiated_by_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the change was triggered by a human operator or an automated system.']
  is_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the setpoint change has received required approvals.']
  new_setpoint_value decimal(20,5) [note: 'Numeric value of the setpoint after the change.']
  previous_setpoint_value decimal(20,5) [note: 'Numeric value of the setpoint before the change was applied.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the setpoint change record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the setpoint change record.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit associated with the setpoint values (e.g., °C, bar, psi, rpm).']
  within_normal_limits boolean [note: 'True if the new setpoint remains within predefined normal operating limits; otherwise false.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing every operator or system-initiated setpoint change event on a controlled process variable. Each record represents a discrete setpoint modification event. Captures tag definition reference, process parameter reference, control system reference, change timestamp, previous setpoint value, new setpoint value, change initiated by (operator ID or automated system), change reason code, associated production order reference, associated recipe/procedure reference, approval reference (for safety-critical setpoints requiring dual authorization), and whether the change was within normal operating limits. Provides a complete audit trail of process setpoint modifications for process safety management (PSM) and MOC (Management of Change) compliance.'
}

Table automation.batch_execution {
  batch_execution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the batch execution record.']
  batch_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Link to the planned schedule record for this batch.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the DCS/PLC control system that managed the batch.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Batch cost accounting: each batch run consumes resources charged to a cost center, required for the Batch Cost Summary report.']
  parent_batch_execution_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on batch_execution (parent_batch_execution_id)']
  primary_batch_approval_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the batch for release.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Batch revenue tracking: batches produce sellable output linked to profit centers, needed for the Production Revenue Allocation report.']
  recipe_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the recipe (formula) used for this batch execution.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Work‑shift during which the batch was executed.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'FK to production.work_order.work_order_id — Batch execution records must link to production work orders for complete manufacturing traceability — ISA-95 Level 3 to Level 4 integration. Without this, batch genealogy cannot be traced to productio']
  actual_yield_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Measured quantity of good product produced by the batch.']
  batch_capa_action_count varchar [note: 'Count of corrective and preventive actions generated from this batch.']
  batch_co2_emissions_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated CO₂ emissions attributable to the batch.']
  batch_cycle_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time of the batch execution measured in seconds.']
  batch_disposition varchar [note: 'Final disposition of the batch after execution.']
  batch_end_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the batch was terminated.']
  batch_energy_consumption_kwh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total electrical energy consumed during the batch.']
  batch_execution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the batch execution as defined by ISA‑88.']
  batch_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments entered by operators or engineers.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the batch run, often used on shop‑floor displays and reports.']
  batch_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the batch for scheduling purposes.']
  batch_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch was released for downstream processes.']
  batch_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch entered review status.']
  batch_safety_incident_count varchar [note: 'Number of safety incidents recorded during the batch.']
  batch_scrap_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quantity of material scrapped during the batch.']
  batch_scrap_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for scrap quantity (e.g., kg, pcs).']
  batch_start_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the batch was started.']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the batch run (e.g., standard production, rework).']
  batch_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the recipe or batch configuration used.']
  batch_water_usage_liters decimal(12,2) [note: 'Volume of water used in the batch process.']
  batch_yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Yield expressed as a percentage of target.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch execution record was first created in the data lake.']
  downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Total minutes of equipment downtime recorded during the batch.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the batch execution finished or was terminated.']
  equipment_train_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment train (set of machines) that executed the batch.']
  exception_count varchar [note: 'Number of exception events (alarms, faults) recorded during the batch.']
  maintenance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether maintenance activities were performed during the batch.']
  overall_equipment_effectiveness decimal(5,2) [note: 'OEE percentage captured for the batch execution.']
  plant_area varchar [note: 'Physical area or cell of the plant where the batch ran.']
  quality_check_passed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch passed all mandatory quality checks.']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Overall quality assessment result for the batch.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the batch execution started on the control system.']
  target_yield_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'Planned quantity of good product expected from the batch.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the batch execution record.']
  yield_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for yield quantities (e.g., kg, pcs).']

  Note: 'Transactional record representing the execution of a discrete batch or recipe-driven production run managed by a Batch Management System (BMS) or DCS batch engine. Captures batch ID, recipe reference, equipment train reference, control system reference, batch start timestamp, batch end timestamp, batch status (created, running, paused, completed, aborted), actual yield quantity, target yield quantity, yield unit of measure, batch disposition (released, rejected, under-review), operator reference, and exception count. Implements ISA-88 batch execution model. Distinct from production.work_order (which is the manufacturing execution layer) — this entity captures the automation-layer batch control execution record.'
}

Table automation.recipe {
  recipe_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the recipe.']
  master_recipe_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on recipe (master_recipe_id)']
  sku_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku_master. Business justification: Each recipe is tied to a specific SKU; the product_reference column is replaced by a FK for traceability.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the recipe was approved.']
  approval_user varchar [note: 'User identifier who approved the recipe.']
  batch_size decimal(12,2) [note: 'Target batch size for the recipe execution.']
  batch_size_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for batch size.']
  changeover_time decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated time to change over equipment to this recipe.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulatory standard applicable to the recipe.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recipe record was created.']
  critical_parameter_setpoints varchar [note: 'Key process parameters with setpoint values and units, stored as JSON.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to detailed recipe documentation.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the recipe is valid for production.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the recipe remains valid; null if indefinite.']
  equipment_class varchar [note: 'Class of equipment required to execute the recipe.']
  ingredient_list varchar [note: 'List of ingredients with target quantities and tolerances, stored as JSON.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified the recipe.']
  max_yield decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum expected yield percentage for the recipe.']
  oee_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness for this recipe.']
  phase_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordered list of phase identifiers defining the recipe flow, stored as JSON.']
  process_time_unit varchar [note: 'Time unit for total_process_time.']
  recipe_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code for the recipe.']
  recipe_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name of the recipe.']
  recipe_type varchar [note: 'ISA-88 classification of the recipe.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recipe.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number of the recipe.']
  safety_integrity_level varchar [note: 'Safety integrity level required for equipment executing the recipe.']
  total_process_time decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total expected duration of the recipe execution.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the recipe record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the recipe.']
  version_history varchar [note: 'Historical changes of the recipe stored as JSON.']
  yield_tolerance decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable deviation from max yield.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created the recipe record.']

  Note: 'Master record for ISA-88 compliant process recipes defining the procedural instructions, ingredient quantities, process parameters, and equipment requirements for batch and continuous manufacturing processes managed by automation systems. Captures recipe name, recipe type (master recipe, control recipe, site recipe, general recipe per ISA-88), product reference, recipe version, revision number, release status (draft, approved, released, obsolete), total process time, equipment class requirements, phase sequence definition, ingredient list with target quantities and tolerances, critical process parameters with setpoints, and approval metadata. Serves as the automation-layer procedural definition consumed by batch execution systems.'
}

Table automation.equipment_phase {
  equipment_phase_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the equipment phase record.']
  abort_logic_reference_plc_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PLC program that defines abort handling for the phase.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the control system (e.g., PLC, DCS) that executes the phase.']
  parent_equipment_phase_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on equipment_phase (parent_equipment_phase_id)']
  plc_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PLC program or function block that implements the phase logic.']
  abortable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the phase can be safely aborted by the control system.']
  average_execution_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Statistically derived average runtime for the phase, used for scheduling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the phase record was first created.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to external documentation or engineering drawing for the phase.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the phase definition becomes valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the phase definition is no longer valid (null for open‑ended).']
  energy_consumption_kwh decimal(12,3) [note: 'Typical energy usage of the phase per execution, measured in kilowatt‑hours.']
  equipment_class_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment class to which this phase applies.']
  equipment_phase_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the phase purpose and behavior.']
  equipment_phase_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the phase definition.']
  input_parameters varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded list of required input parameters (name, data type, allowed range).']
  is_default_phase boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this phase is the default for its equipment class.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle stage of the phase definition within the engineering process.']
  max_concurrent_instances varchar [note: 'Maximum number of equipment units that may run this phase simultaneously.']
  normal_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Typical execution time for the phase under normal conditions, expressed in seconds.']
  operator_role varchar [note: 'Role required to manually start or intervene in the phase.']
  output_parameters varchar [note: 'JSON‑encoded list of output parameters produced by the phase.']
  phase_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to reference the phase in control logic.']
  phase_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the phase (e.g., "Charge", "Heat").']
  phase_type varchar [note: 'Categorical type of the phase as defined by ISA‑88.']
  pressure_setpoint_bar decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target pressure for the phase when pressure control is required.']
  required_skill_level varchar [note: 'Skill level needed by personnel to safely execute the phase.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for change control tracking.']
  safety_critical boolean [note: 'True if the phase is designated as safety‑critical per IEC 61508/IEC 62443.']
  temperature_setpoint_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target temperature for the phase when temperature control is required.']
  timeout_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed execution time before the phase is forced to abort, in seconds.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the phase record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version label of the phase definition (e.g., "v1.0").']

  Note: 'Master record defining ISA-88 equipment phases — the lowest-level procedural elements that can be commanded by a batch control system to perform a specific action on a piece of equipment (e.g., CHARGE, HEAT, MIX, TRANSFER, COOL, DRAIN). Captures phase name, phase type, associated equipment class, associated control system, phase logic reference (PLC program or function block), required input parameters with data types and ranges, output parameters, normal duration, timeout duration, abort logic reference, and phase status. Provides the procedural building blocks for recipe construction and batch execution in ISA-88 compliant automation architectures.'
}

Table automation.opc_server {
  opc_server_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the OPC server record.']
  network_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to automation.network_segment. Business justification: OPC server resides in a network segment; FK to network_segment normalizes segment data and replaces the free‑text field.']
  redundant_opc_server_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on opc_server (redundant_opc_server_id)']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier. Business justification: OPC server hardware is sourced from a vendor; linking supports warranty management and regulatory compliance documentation.']
  alarm_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of concurrent alarms the server can handle.']
  authentication_mode varchar [note: 'Method used by clients to authenticate with the server.']
  certificate_thumbprint varchar [note: 'SHA‑1 thumbprint of the server\'s X.509 certificate (if certificate authentication is used).']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the server was placed into production service.']
  connected_client_count varchar [note: 'Current number of active client sessions connected to the server.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the OPC server record was first created in the data lake.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the server was retired from service (null if still active).']
  endpoint_url varchar [note: 'Network address (URL) through which clients connect to the server.']
  engineering_workstation varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineering workstation used for server configuration.']
  host_machine varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the physical/virtual host running the OPC server.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date the OPC server software was first installed on the host.']
  last_config_backup_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent backup of the server configuration.']
  last_connectivity_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent health‑check ping to the server.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle phase of the server within asset management.']
  mtbf_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average time between server failures, calculated by maintenance team.']
  mttr_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average time required to restore the server after a failure.']
  opc_server_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the server\'s purpose, location, or special characteristics.']
  opc_server_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the OPC server instance.']
  plant_area varchar [note: 'Physical plant area or zone where the server is deployed.']
  polling_interval_ms varchar [note: 'Default client polling interval in milliseconds for data change monitoring.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Commercial product name of the OPC server software.']
  redundancy_configuration varchar [note: 'High‑availability setup for the OPC server.']
  safety_integrity_level varchar [note: 'Safety integrity classification required for the server in safety‑critical environments.']
  security_policy varchar [note: 'Security policy applied to the OPC communication channel.']
  server_code varchar [note: 'Enterprise‑wide unique code assigned to the server for reference.']
  server_status varchar [note: 'Real‑time operational state of the OPC server.']
  server_type varchar [note: 'Protocol family implemented by the server.']
  subscription_count varchar [note: 'Number of active subscription objects on the server.']
  tag_count varchar [note: 'Total number of OPC tags (variables) the server makes available.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the OPC server record.']
  uptime_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative operational hours since last start or commissioning.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version string of the OPC server software.']

  Note: 'Master record for OPC-UA and OPC-DA server instances deployed within the automation infrastructure, serving as the communication middleware between field devices and supervisory systems. Captures OPC server name, server type (OPC-UA, OPC-DA, OPC-HDA, OPC-AE), vendor and product name, server version, host machine reference, endpoint URL, security policy (None, Basic128Rsa15, Basic256Sha256), authentication mode (anonymous, username, certificate), certificate thumbprint, connected client count, tag count served, polling interval, subscription count, server status, and last connectivity check timestamp. Provides the communication topology map for data flow from field to supervisory layers.'
}

Table automation.historian_config {
  historian_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each historian configuration record.']
  superseded_historian_config_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on historian_config (superseded_historian_config_id)']
  tag_definition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the tag definition that this configuration applies to.']
  alarm_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether alarm generation is active for tags under this configuration.']
  alarm_priority varchar [note: 'Default priority assigned to alarms generated from this configuration.']
  archive_group varchar [note: 'Logical grouping used to route data to specific archive storage tiers.']
  archive_group_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the archive group purpose.']
  collection_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether tag data collection is currently active for this configuration.']
  collection_interval_ms varchar [note: 'Desired sampling interval in milliseconds for tag data collection.']
  compression_algorithm varchar [note: 'Algorithm used for data compression when compression_enabled is true.']
  compression_deviation double [note: 'Maximum deviation (percentage) allowed before a new compressed data point is written.']
  compression_enabled boolean [note: 'Specifies whether collected data should be compressed before storage.']
  compression_timeout_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum time in seconds to wait before forcing a compressed write, regardless of deviation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the configuration record was first created.']
  data_quality_code varchar [note: 'Quality classification of the collected data for this configuration.']
  exception_deviation double [note: 'Deviation threshold that triggers an exception event instead of normal compression.']
  historian_config_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the configuration purpose and scope.']
  historian_config_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name identifying the historian configuration.']
  historian_config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the historian configuration.']
  instance_name varchar [note: 'Name of the historian server or instance to which this configuration applies.']
  last_config_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last time the historian settings were applied to the runtime system.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Operational lifecycle stage of the historian configuration.']
  max_data_rate_per_sec double [note: 'Upper bound on data ingestion rate (records per second) for this configuration.']
  max_tag_count varchar [note: 'Maximum number of tags that can be collected under this configuration.']
  owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or team responsible for this configuration.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Underlying historian technology platform used for time‑series storage.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days that collected data is retained before archival or deletion.']
  retention_policy varchar [note: 'Policy governing how data is retained and purged.']
  scaling_factor double [note: 'Multiplicative factor applied to raw tag values before storage.']
  storage_resolution_ms varchar [note: 'Effective resolution at which data is stored after any compression or aggregation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the configuration.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version string of the configuration, updated on each change.']

  Note: 'Master record for process historian configuration defining which tags are collected, at what resolution, with what compression settings, and for what retention period within the process data historian (OSIsoft PI, Aspentech IP.21, Wonderware Historian, InfluxDB). Captures historian instance name, historian platform, tag definition reference, collection enabled flag, collection interval (ms), compression enabled flag, compression deviation, compression timeout, exception deviation, storage resolution, retention period (days), archive group, and last configuration update timestamp. Governs the data collection policy for process time-series data and ensures historian configuration is auditable and version-controlled.'
}

Table automation.automation_change_request {
  automation_change_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the automation change request record.']
  affected_plc_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the PLC program that will be changed or replaced.']
  asset_work_order_id varchar [note: 'Work order that will execute or has executed the change.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the control system impacted by the change.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Change requests are often initiated by customers; linking provides traceability for compliance and SLA impact.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific PLC, sensor, or edge device being modified.']
  ecn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.ecn. Business justification: Engineering Change Notice drives automation change request; required for Regulatory Compliance Change Log.']
  eco_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to engineering.eco. Business justification: Engineering Change Order triggers automation change request; essential for Change Impact Report linking ECO to automation actions.']
  primary_automation_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or stakeholder who originated the change request.']
  superseded_automation_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on automation_change_request (superseded_automation_change_request_id)']
  tertiary_automation_change_review_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer or auditor who performed the review.']
  actual_downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Measured downtime that occurred during implementation.']
  actual_implementation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the change was performed on the field.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the required approvals for the change request.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the approval decision was recorded.']
  automation_change_request_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the change request.']
  change_audit_log_reference varchar [note: 'Link or identifier to the immutable audit log entry for this change request.']
  change_closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the change request was formally closed.']
  change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of the modification, including purpose and scope.']
  change_effective_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the change became operationally effective.']
  change_origin varchar [note: 'Source of the change request: internal team, external partner, or vendor.']
  change_priority varchar [note: 'Business‑defined priority level for scheduling the change.']
  change_rationale varchar [note: 'Business justification for why the change is required.']
  change_request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the change request for tracking and communication.']
  change_review_status varchar [note: 'State of the post‑implementation review process.']
  change_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the review was completed.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Category of automation modification being requested.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes from requestor or reviewers.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard governing the change (e.g., ISA‑84, IEC 62443).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the record was first created in the data lake.']
  estimated_downtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Projected production downtime associated with the change.']
  is_emergency_change boolean [note: 'True if the change is classified as an emergency requiring expedited handling.']
  planned_implementation_date date [note: 'Target calendar date for executing the change.']
  post_change_validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of validation activities after the change is applied.']
  pre_change_backup_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier or location of the backup taken before the change (e.g., file path, archive ID).']
  production_impact varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of potential impact on production output or quality.']
  request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the change request was initially submitted.']
  risk_score varchar [note: 'Numeric risk rating derived from safety and production impact assessments (0‑100).']
  rollback_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the documented rollback procedure in case the change fails.']
  safety_impact varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of potential safety consequences of the change.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  validation_test_results varchar [note: 'Summary of test outcomes performed after the change (e.g., pass/fail details).']

  Note: 'Transactional record governing the Management of Change (MOC) process for automation system modifications including PLC program changes, setpoint limit changes, control system configuration changes, network topology changes, and device replacements. Captures change request number, change type (program change, configuration change, hardware change, network change, setpoint limit change), affected system/device references, change description, risk assessment (safety impact, production impact), required approvals, approval status, planned implementation date, actual implementation date, pre-change backup reference, post-change validation status, and change closure date. Implements ISA-84 and IEC 62443 change management requirements for safety-critical automation systems.'
}

Table automation.device_connectivity_event {
  device_connectivity_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each connectivity event record.']
  alarm_event_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the alarm event that may have been generated by this connectivity issue.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the automation device, edge gateway, or OPC server that generated the event.']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the device resides.']
  preceding_device_connectivity_event_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on device_connectivity_event (preceding_device_connectivity_event_id)']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production line or cell containing the device.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work center or station associated with the device.']
  auto_recovery_attempted boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether an automatic recovery action was triggered.']
  communication_protocol varchar [note: 'Network protocol used by the device for communication.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'Method or channel that detected the connectivity change.']
  device_connectivity_event_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the event record within the data pipeline.']
  device_model varchar [note: 'Model designation of the device (e.g., PLC‑3000, Edge‑Gateway‑X).']
  device_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer‑assigned serial number uniquely identifying the hardware unit.']
  error_code varchar [note: 'Standardized error identifier returned by the device or network layer.']
  error_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the error condition.']
  event_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this event record was first inserted into the lakehouse.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the connectivity state change was detected.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of connectivity event indicating the new state of the device.']
  event_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this event record (e.g., after recovery information is added).']
  last_successful_comm_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful communication before this event.']
  network_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device on the OT network.']
  new_state varchar [note: 'Device connectivity state after the transition.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for additional context or operator comments.']
  previous_state varchar [note: 'Device connectivity state before the transition.']
  recovery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the auto‑recovery (or manual recovery) completed, if applicable.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that reported the connectivity change (e.g., heartbeat monitor, OPC server, network probe).']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing device connectivity state change events — when automation devices, edge gateways, or OPC servers go online, offline, or enter degraded communication states. Each record represents a discrete connectivity transition event. Captures device reference, event timestamp, previous connectivity state, new connectivity state (online, offline, degraded, timeout, error), last successful communication timestamp, error code, error description, detection source (heartbeat monitor, OPC server status, network probe), auto-recovery attempted flag, recovery timestamp, and associated alarm event reference. Supports OT network health monitoring and mean-time-to-restore (MTTR) tracking for automation infrastructure.'
}

Table automation.safety_function {
  safety_function_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the safety function record.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Safety Instrumented System (SIS) that hosts this function.']
  parent_safety_function_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on safety_function (parent_safety_function_id)']
  process_hazard_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the hazard that triggers this safety function.']
  associated_sensor_tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of OPC‑UA or SCADA tag identifiers for sensors feeding the SIF.']
  bypass_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the safety function may be bypassed during maintenance or abnormal operation.']
  change_control_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the change request or engineering change order that created/modified the SIF.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Reference to the governing safety standard (e.g., IEC 61511) applicable to this function.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the safety function record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the safety function is retired or superseded (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the safety function becomes operationally effective.']
  final_element_tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of tags for final elements (valves, actuators) that execute the safe state.']
  initiating_cause_description varchar [note: 'Narrative of the hazardous condition that initiates the safety function.']
  last_proof_test_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent proof test of the SIF was performed.']
  logic_solver_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PLC/logic solver that implements the SIF logic.']
  mean_time_to_failure_on_demand decimal(5,4) [note: 'Average probability of failure on demand for the SIF (PFDavg).']
  process_demand_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average number of times per hour the safety function is demanded by the process.']
  proof_test_interval_months varchar [note: 'Planned interval, in months, between mandatory proof tests of the SIF.']
  risk_reduction_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated factor representing the reduction in risk achieved by the SIF.']
  safe_state_description varchar [note: 'Description of the process state that the SIF drives the system into when activated.']
  safety_function_description varchar [note: 'Extended free‑text description of the safety function purpose and scope.']
  safety_function_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the safety function as used in engineering documentation.']
  safety_function_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the safety function.']
  safety_integrity_level varchar [note: 'Designated SIL for the function (SIL1‑SIL4) indicating required risk reduction.']
  sif_number varchar [note: 'Unique code assigned to the SIF within the safety instrumented system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the safety function record.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the SIF meets its SIL (e.g., LOPA, Fault Tree).']

  Note: 'Master record for Safety Instrumented Functions (SIFs) defined within Safety Instrumented Systems (SIS) per IEC 61511. Each SIF represents a discrete safety function designed to bring a process to a safe state upon detection of a hazardous condition. Captures SIF name, SIF number, associated SIS/control system reference, process hazard reference, Safety Integrity Level (SIL 1/2/3/4), SIL verification method (LOPA, fault tree), required risk reduction factor (RRF), initiating cause description, safe state description, process demand rate, proof test interval (months), mean time to failure on demand (PFDavg), associated sensor tags, logic solver reference, final element tags, bypass allowed flag, and last proof test date. Critical for functional safety lifecycle management.'
}

Table automation.proof_test_record {
  proof_test_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the proof test record.']
  component_final_element_device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the final element component involved in the SIF.']
  component_logic_solver_device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the logic solver component involved in the SIF.']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sensor component involved in the SIF.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment used to conduct the proof test.']
  previous_proof_test_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on proof_test_record (previous_proof_test_record_id)']
  primary_proof_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the technician who performed the proof test.']
  safety_function_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Safety Instrumented Function (SIF) being tested.']
  test_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location where the proof test was performed.']
  test_procedure_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the documented test procedure used for this proof test.']
  approval_signature_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the digital signature approving the test results.']
  as_found_condition varchar [note: 'Condition of the SIF components as found before any corrective action.']
  as_left_condition varchar [note: 'Condition of the SIF components after the proof test and any repairs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the proof test record was first created in the system.']
  detected_failures varchar [note: 'List of failures identified during the proof test.']
  next_test_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next required proof test.']
  proof_test_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the proof test record.']
  repair_actions_taken varchar [note: 'Description of corrective actions performed to address detected failures.']
  safety_integrity_level varchar [note: 'Designated SIL of the safety instrumented function being tested.']
  test_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test result was formally approved.']
  test_comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments captured by the technician.']
  test_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the proof test was performed.']
  test_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference (e.g., file name or URL) to the detailed test procedure document.']
  test_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total duration of the proof test in seconds.']
  test_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the proof test ended.']
  test_environment varchar [note: 'Operational environment condition during the test.']
  test_failed_criteria varchar [note: 'List of criteria that were not met during the test.']
  test_failure_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual failures detected during the proof test.']
  test_humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage recorded during the test.']
  test_is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the proof test is classified as critical for safety compliance.']
  test_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the proof test event.']
  test_operator_notes varchar [note: 'Additional observations entered by the operator.']
  test_passed_criteria varchar [note: 'List of criteria that were successfully met during the test.']
  test_pressure_bar decimal(7,2) [note: 'Process pressure measured during the test, in bar.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the proof test.']
  test_result_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the test result outcome.']
  test_result_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test result was recorded.']
  test_review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the test record was reviewed.']
  test_revision_number varchar [note: 'Revision number of the test execution.']
  test_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system where the proof test record originated.']
  test_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the proof test started.']
  test_temperature_c decimal(6,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature recorded during the test, in degrees Celsius.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Type of proof test performed: full, partial stroke, or partial.']
  test_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the test procedure applied.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the proof test record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the execution and results of periodic proof tests performed on Safety Instrumented Functions (SIFs) and their associated safety instrumented system components. Each record documents a discrete proof test event. Captures safety function reference, test date, test type (full proof test, partial stroke test, partial proof test), test procedure reference, tested components (sensor, logic solver, final element), test result (pass, fail, partial pass), as-found condition, as-left condition, detected failures, repair actions taken, next test due date, test duration, technician reference, and approval signature reference. Supports IEC 61511 proof test documentation and SIL verification maintenance requirements.'
}

Table automation.automation_project {
  automation_project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the automation engineering project.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the control system that is the target of the automation project.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Project budgeting: projects are funded from specific cost centers, required for the Project Cost Allocation Ledger.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Project budgeting, status reporting, and profitability analysis require associating each automation project with its customer.']
  parent_automation_project_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on automation_project (parent_automation_project_id)']
  primary_automation_engineer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the lead automation engineer for the project.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Revenue attribution: automation projects generate revenue tracked against profit centers, needed for the Project Profitability Dashboard.']
  project_header_id varchar [note: 'FK to project.project_header.project_header_id — Automation engineering projects must link to the enterprise project management domain for cost tracking, resource allocation, and milestone governance. Without this, automation projects are ungoverned']
  tertiary_automation_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person who approved the project budget or scope.']
  actual_duration_days varchar [note: 'Actual total duration of the project in days.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when project was completed.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual expenditure incurred to date.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when project work started.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the project was approved.']
  automation_project_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of scope, objectives, and deliverables.']
  automation_project_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the automation project.']
  automation_project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the automation project.']
  automation_project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the automation project based on its nature.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget for the project in the primary currency.']
  budget_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the budget amount.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance assessment of the project against regulatory standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the project record was first created in the system.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the central project documentation repository.']
  estimated_duration_days varchar [note: 'Estimated total duration of the project in days.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the project is classified as critical to operations.']
  is_cybersecurity_hardening boolean [note: 'Indicates if the project includes cybersecurity hardening activities.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Planned date for project completion.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Planned date for project commencement.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the project based on business impact.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the automation project.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating for the project.']
  safety_integrity_level varchar [note: 'Safety Integrity Level assigned to the control system within the project.']
  sla_actual_hours varchar [note: 'Actual hours achieved against SLA targets.']
  sla_target_hours varchar [note: 'Target hours for service level agreements related to project deliverables.']
  stakeholder_group varchar [note: 'Primary stakeholder group for the project.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the project record.']

  Note: 'Master record for automation engineering projects representing discrete scopes of work for designing, implementing, commissioning, or upgrading automation systems. Captures project name, project type (greenfield installation, brownfield upgrade, migration, expansion, cybersecurity hardening), associated plant/facility, project manager reference, automation engineer reference, target control system reference, project status (concept, design, procurement, installation, commissioning, FAT, SAT, handover, closed), planned start date, planned completion date, actual start date, actual completion date, budget reference, and associated change requests. Distinct from project.project_header (capital projects) — this entity is the automation-domain-specific project record for OT engineering work.'
}

Table automation.io_mapping {
  io_mapping_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the I/O mapping record.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'FK to automation.control_system']
  device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the I/O module hardware.']
  project_change_request_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the change request that drove the latest modification.']
  remapped_io_mapping_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on io_mapping (remapped_io_mapping_id)']
  access_level varchar [note: 'Permission level for reading or writing the I/O point.']
  address varchar [note: 'Logical address of the I/O point within the controller (e.g., %I0.0).']
  alarm_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether alarms are generated for this I/O point.']
  alarm_priority varchar [note: 'Severity level assigned to alarms from this I/O point.']
  cable_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the cable linking the field device to the I/O module.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for the most recent change to the I/O mapping.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Unique code assigned to the I/O channel within the control system.']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the I/O channel used by engineers and operators.']
  channel_number varchar [note: 'Numeric channel index within the I/O module.']
  commissioning_date date [note: 'Date the I/O channel was commissioned into service.']
  commissioning_status varchar [note: 'Current commissioning state of the I/O channel.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the I/O mapping record was created.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record for the I/O mapping (e.g., Siemens Opcenter MES).']
  deadband decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum change in signal value required to trigger an update.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date the I/O channel was removed from service, if applicable.']
  device_name varchar [note: 'Name of the PLC/DCS device to which the I/O channel is assigned.']
  engineering_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used for the signal (e.g., psi, °C, bar).']
  field_instrument_tag varchar [note: 'Tag number from Piping & Instrument Diagram that identifies the field device.']
  io_mapping_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the I/O channel purpose or special notes.']
  io_mapping_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the I/O mapping.']
  io_module_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the I/O module based on its capabilities.']
  io_type varchar [note: 'Category of the I/O point indicating its function and signal direction.']
  is_historian_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this I/O point is logged to the process historian.']
  junction_box varchar [note: 'Reference to the junction box used in the wiring path.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent audit of this I/O mapping record.']
  module_slot varchar [note: 'Slot identifier within the I/O rack where the module resides.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational or engineering notes.']
  quality_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the quality status of the I/O data (e.g., good, suspect).']
  raw_range_high decimal(12,4) [note: 'Maximum raw signal value (e.g., mA or V) before scaling.']
  raw_range_low decimal(12,4) [note: 'Minimum raw signal value (e.g., mA or V) before scaling.']
  retention_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the I/O data is retained in the historian.']
  safety_critical boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the I/O point is safety‑critical per IEC 61508/IEC 62443.']
  scaling_factor decimal(12,6) [note: 'Multiplicative factor applied during signal conversion.']
  scaling_high decimal(12,4) [note: 'Upper bound of the engineering unit range after scaling.']
  scaling_low decimal(12,4) [note: 'Lower bound of the engineering unit range after scaling.']
  scan_rate_ms varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the I/O point is scanned by the controller, in milliseconds.']
  signal_type varchar [note: 'Physical signal standard used for the I/O point.']
  tag_name varchar [note: 'Name of the historian/tag definition linked to this I/O point.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the I/O mapping record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the I/O mapping configuration.']
  wiring_terminal varchar [note: 'Terminal identifier on the I/O module where the wire connects.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the physical and logical Input/Output (I/O) mapping between field instruments/actuators and PLC/DCS controller I/O modules. Each record represents a single I/O channel assignment. Captures I/O channel identifier, associated device (PLC/DCS), I/O module slot and channel number, I/O type (digital input, digital output, analog input, analog output, pulse input), signal type (4-20mA, 0-10V, 24VDC, thermocouple, RTD, HART), associated tag definition, field instrument tag number (P&ID reference), engineering unit, scaling low/high, wiring terminal reference, cable number, junction box reference, and commissioning status. Provides the definitive I/O list linking physical field wiring to software tag definitions.'
}

Table automation.fat_sat_record {
  fat_sat_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each FAT/SAT test execution record.']
  automation_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the automation engineering project to which this test belongs.']
  control_system_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the control system (PLC, DCS, etc.) under test.']
  equipment_register_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific equipment or panel under test.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the plant or site area where the test was performed.']
  primary_fat_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the engineer or technician who performed the test.']
  retest_fat_sat_record_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fat_sat_record (retest_fat_sat_record_id)']
  tertiary_fat_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the authority who approved the test record.']
  test_case_id varchar [note: 'Unique code for the specific test case within the test protocol.']
  test_protocol_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved test protocol document governing this test.']
  actual_observed_result varchar [note: 'Measured value or condition observed during the test.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the test record received final approval.']
