  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or observations related to the test execution.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a corrective action must be taken to resolve a deviation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the test record was first created in the system.']
  deviation_description varchar [note: 'Explanation of any difference between actual and expected results.']
  expected_result varchar [note: 'Target value or condition defined in the test protocol.']
  fat_sat_record_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the test record.']
  record_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable sequential number assigned to the test record for traceability.']
  retest_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test must be repeated after corrective action.']
  test_case_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the test case purpose and steps.']
  test_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the test was performed.']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Overall outcome of the test execution.']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Category of the test execution (Factory Acceptance Test, Site Acceptance Test, Integrated FAT, Loop Check, Functional Test).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the test record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing Factory Acceptance Test (FAT) and Site Acceptance Test (SAT) execution results for automation systems and control panels. Each record represents a discrete test execution event against a specific test case within a FAT or SAT test protocol. Captures test record number, test type (FAT, SAT, IFAT, loop check, functional test), associated automation project reference, control system reference, test case identifier, test case description, test date, test result (pass, fail, conditional pass, not tested), actual observed result, expected result, deviation description, corrective action required, retest required flag, tested-by reference, witnessed-by reference, and test protocol document reference. Supports formal commissioning documentation and handover packages.'
}

Table automation.batch_schedule {
  batch_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for batch_schedule']
  line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the production line associated with the schedule.']
  parent_batch_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on batch_schedule (parent_batch_schedule_id)']
  plant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing plant where the schedule is applied.']
  audit_comment varchar [note: 'Free‑form comment for audit or change‑control purposes.']
  batch_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard quantity produced per execution of the schedule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the schedule purpose and scope.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the schedule expires; null if open‑ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the schedule becomes valid.']
  enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the schedule is currently enabled for execution.']
  estimated_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Typical runtime expected for a batch execution.']
  frequency_minutes varchar [note: 'Interval in minutes between successive executions when recurrence is time‑based.']
  last_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of this schedule.']
  max_runtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Maximum allowed runtime for a batch execution before it is flagged.']
  min_runtime_minutes varchar [note: 'Minimum expected runtime for a batch execution.']
  next_run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned timestamp for the next scheduled execution.']
  owner_email varchar [note: 'Contact email for the schedule owner.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority used by the scheduler to resolve conflicts; higher value = higher priority.']
  recurrence_pattern varchar [note: 'Pattern (e.g., cron expression) that defines how the schedule repeats.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the schedule in operational systems.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the batch schedule.']
  schedule_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the person or team responsible for the schedule.']
  schedule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the schedule execution model.']
  scheduled_start_time timestamp [note: 'Planned start time for the next execution of the schedule.']
  shift varchar [note: 'Work shift during which the batch is scheduled to run.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the schedule.']
  tolerance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable deviation percentage from the estimated duration.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the batch quantity.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the last update.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the schedule record.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the schedule record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for batch_schedule. Referenced by batch_schedule_id.'
}

Table automation.test_case {
  test_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for test_case']
  automation_script_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the automation script that implements the test case logic.']
  parent_test_case_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on test_case (parent_test_case_id)']
  related_device_device_registry_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the PLC, sensor, or edge device that the test case targets.']
  actual_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Measured execution time of the most recent run, in seconds.']
  category varchar [note: 'Classification of the test case by testing focus.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the test case in documentation and tooling.']
  compliance_requirement varchar [note: 'Regulatory or safety standard that the test case helps satisfy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test case record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the test case purpose, steps, and expected outcomes.']
  environment varchar [note: 'Logical environment where the test case is intended to run.']
  execution_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent execution of the test case.']
  expected_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Planned maximum execution time for the test case, in seconds.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test case is executed automatically (true) or manually (false).']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'Marks the test case as critical for safety or compliance; true if failure has high impact.']
  last_executed_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the most recent execution.']
  last_executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test case was last executed.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified the test case.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the test case.']
  owner_team varchar [note: 'Team or functional group responsible for maintaining the test case.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority indicating the importance of executing the test case.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk associated with a failure of this test case.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the test case.']
  steps_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual steps or actions defined in the test case.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of free‑form tags for categorization and search.']
  test_case_type varchar [note: 'Granular type of test case based on testing scope.']
  test_data_set varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the data set used during test execution.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the test case record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version label of the test case definition (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that created the test case.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for test_case. Referenced by test_case_id.'
}

Table automation.test_procedure {
  test_procedure_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for test_procedure']
  parent_test_procedure_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on test_procedure (parent_test_procedure_id)']
  author varchar [note: 'Name of the person or team that authored the test procedure.']
  code varchar [note: 'External code or catalog number used to reference the test procedure.']
  compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry standard that the test procedure satisfies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test procedure record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the test procedure steps and objectives.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the test procedure becomes valid for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the test procedure is retired or no longer valid (null if open‑ended).']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the test procedure can be executed automatically by an edge gateway or control system.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the test procedure was last reviewed for relevance and accuracy.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the test procedure.']
  procedure_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the purpose of the test procedure.']
  required_equipment varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of equipment or devices needed to execute the test procedure.']
  safety_rating varchar [note: 'Safety risk classification associated with performing the test procedure.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the test procedure.']
  test_duration_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical or maximum duration of the test procedure expressed in seconds.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the test procedure record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the test procedure definition.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for test_procedure. Referenced by test_procedure_id.'
}

Table automation.automation_script {
  automation_script_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for automation_script']
  called_by_automation_script_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on automation_script (called_by_automation_script_id)']
  author_name varchar [note: 'Name of the engineer or developer who created the script.']
  average_execution_time_ms decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mean duration of script execution measured in milliseconds.']
  checksum_sha256 varchar [note: 'SHA‑256 hash of the script file for integrity verification.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or industry compliance standards the script must satisfy (e.g., IEC 62443).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the script record was first created in the registry.']
  deprecation_date date [note: 'Date on which the script was marked as deprecated.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the script\'s purpose and functionality.']
  documentation_url varchar [note: 'Link to the external documentation or wiki page for the script.']
  error_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of execution failures recorded for the script.']
  execution_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the script has been executed.']
  execution_mode varchar [note: 'How the script is invoked: manually, on a schedule, or by an event.']
  input_parameters_schema varchar [note: 'JSON schema describing expected input parameters for the script.']
  is_thread_safe boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the script can safely run concurrently on multiple threads.']
  language varchar [note: 'Programming language or scripting language used to author the script.']
  last_error_message varchar [note: 'Error message captured from the most recent failed execution.']
  last_executed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent successful execution.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle phase of the script within the development‑to‑production pipeline.']
  max_execution_time_ms decimal(10,2) [note: 'Longest observed execution duration for the script.']
  min_execution_time_ms decimal(10,2) [note: 'Shortest observed execution duration for the script.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the script used to identify it in catalogs and UI.']
  output_parameters_schema varchar [note: 'JSON schema describing output data produced by the script.']
  required_permissions varchar [note: 'List of security permissions or roles required to run the script.']
  runtime_environment varchar [note: 'Target execution environment for the script.']
  schedule_cron varchar [note: 'Cron expression defining the periodic schedule when execution_mode is \'scheduled\'.']
  script_type varchar [note: 'Category of automation script based on its functional role.']
  security_classification varchar [note: 'Data security level assigned to the script according to corporate policy.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the upstream system where the script originated (e.g., Engineering Workbench).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the script.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of user‑defined tags for categorization and search.']
  trigger_event varchar [note: 'Name of the event that triggers the script when execution_mode is \'event_triggered\'.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the script metadata.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version string following the internal versioning scheme (e.g., v1.2).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for automation_script. Referenced by automation_script_id.'
}

Ref: automation.fat_sat_record.test_protocol_document_id > project.project_document.project_document_id
Ref: automation.batch_schedule.line_id > order.line.line_id
Ref: automation.batch_schedule.parent_batch_schedule_id > automation.batch_schedule.batch_schedule_id
Ref: automation.batch_schedule.plant_id > production.plant.plant_id
Ref: automation.test_case.automation_script_id > automation.automation_script.automation_script_id
Ref: automation.test_case.parent_test_case_id > automation.test_case.test_case_id
Ref: automation.test_case.related_device_device_registry_id > automation.device_registry.device_registry_id
